Description: AirClub has recalled its convertible bassinets used as bedside sleepers because they create a gap between the bassinet and adult mattress, posing a serious fall hazard. This condition violates mandatory safety standards for bedside sleepers. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: AirClub Convertible Bassinets Recalled for Fall Hazard Due to Safety Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact AirClub at 833-977-6667 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email support@airclub-baby.com, or visit https://airclub-baby.com/pages/recall. You can also access their website at https://airclub-baby.com/ and click on "Recall" for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled AirClub Convertible Bassinet – Front View light grey
Recalled AirClub Convertible Bassinet - Rear View
"Product Model: QX-831" is printed on a label located on bottom of the convertible bassinets underneath the mattress pad
"Product Model: QX-831” is printed on a label located on bottom of the convertible bassinets underneath the mattress pad
Recalled AirClub Convertible Bassinets L-Shaped Straps Attachment Mechanism

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from October 2023 through December 2025 for between $110 and $130.

Distributors.Name: Zhangzhou Qiangxing Supply Chain Management Co., Ltd. dba AirClub of China

Hazards.Name: The convertible bassinets pose a serious fall hazard due to a gap created between the bedside sleeper and the adult mattress, violating safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must immediately stop using the recalled bassinets and contact AirClub for a replacement attachment mechanism. They will need to cut off the original L-shape straps and follow instructions to install the new T-shaped buckle straps.

metaDescription: AirClub recalls convertible bassinets for fall hazard risk caused by gaps between sleeper and adult mattress violating safety standards.

Description: CCCEI power strips with 6ft, 10ft, and 15ft cords are recalled due to a fire hazard caused by missing overcurrent protection. The power strips have a black metal casing, six outlets with individual switches, and a warning label advising indoor use only. Two reports of sparking and melting have been received, but no fires or injuries have been reported.
Title: CCCEI Power Strips Recalled for Fire Hazard Risk Due to Lack of Overcurrent Protection
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Middle Way Electronics via email at CCCEIpowerstrips@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled CCCEI Power Strip front
Recalled CCCEI Power Strip back with white warning label
Recalled CCCEI Power Strip side
Recalled CCCEI Power Strip packaging

Injuries.Name: Middle Way Electronics has received two reports of the power strips sparking and melting. No fires or injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from April 2024 to January 2026, priced between $23 and $30.

Distributors.Name: Middle Way Electronics, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to lack of overcurrent protection, potentially leading to serious injury or death from smoke inhalation and burns.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the CCCEI power strips immediately and contact Middle Way Electronics for a full refund.

metaDescription: CCCEI power strips recalled for fire hazard risk from lack of overcurrent protection. Stop use immediately to prevent serious injury or death.

Description: GLMZZ has recalled its Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets because the high-powered magnets inside can be swallowed, posing a serious risk of internal injury. The magnets may attract each other or metal objects inside the body, causing severe harm. No injuries have been reported. The recall addresses compliance with mandatory toy safety standards.
Title: GLMZZ Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets Recalled for Risk of Serious Injury from Magnet Ingestion
ConsumerContact: GLMZZ via email at GLMZZrecall@outlook.com.

Products.Name: GLMZZ Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Set

Images.Caption: Recalled GLMZZ Fidget Magnet Ball Toys
Recalled GLMZZ Fidget Magnet Ball Toys (In Small Parts Test Cylinder)

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from February 2025 to October 2025 for about $15.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Jiaying E-commerce Co., Ltd., dba GLMZZ, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to high-powered magnets, leading to severe internal injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys, keep them away from children, and contact GLMZZ for a full refund.

metaDescription: GLMZZ recalls Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets due to risk of serious injury from magnet ingestion. High-powered magnets pose internal injury hazards.

Description: MPINOI adult portable bed rails have been recalled because they pose a serious risk of entrapment and asphyxiation. The product does not meet mandatory safety standards for adult portable bed rails. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: MPINOI Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled for Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, reach out via email at MPINOIproductrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled MPINOI Bed Rail
MPINOI is printed on the mesh pocket of the recalled bed rail

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from December 2025 to February 2026 for approximately $30.

Importers.Name: Guangzhou Aoran Trading Co., Ltd., dba Bioenrrty, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risk due to non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the recalled bed rails and contact Bioenrrty for a full refund. Consumers must destroy the bed rails by cutting the black safety strap in half and marking "RECALLED" on both the upper and lower rails with a permanent marker. A photo of the destroyed rails should be emailed to MPINOIproductrecall@outlook.com.

metaDescription: MPINOI adult portable bed rails recalled due to entrapment and asphyxiation hazards from non-compliance with safety standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Vive Health is recalling specific models of adult portable bed rails due to risks of entrapment and asphyxiation. Two fatalities have been reported in connection with these bed rails. The recalled products include the Compact Bed Rail (model LVA2009SLV), Bed Rail Collection V (model LVA2097SLV), and certain units of model LVA1024 purchased before August 21, 2023.
Title: Vive Health Recalls Adult Portable Bed Rails Due to Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Vive Health at 800-487-3808 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recalls@vivehealth.com, or visit vivehealth.com/pages/recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vive Health Compact Bed Rail (model number LVA2009SLV)
Recalled Vive Health Bed Rail Collection V (model number LVA2097SLV)
Recalled Vive Health Compact Bed Rail (model number LVA1024)

Injuries.Name: Vive Health has received two reports of entrapment deaths associated with one model of the recalled bed rails. The deaths occurred in September 2024 and involved a 97-year-old man at an assisted living facility in Texas and a 93-year-old man at his home in Florida.

Retailers.Name: Vive Health sold approximately 122,000 units of the recalled bed rails through medical supply stores nationwide, as well as online at vivehealth.com, Amazon, and other medical supply websites. The bed rails were available for purchase from September 2019 to December 2025, priced between $45 and $90.

Importers.Name: Vive Health LLC, of Naples, Florida

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risks when bed rails are attached to a bed.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the recalled bed rails and contact Vive Health for a full refund. Mark the bed rails as 'RECALLED' with a permanent marker, take a photo with the buyer's name, and email it to recalls@vivehealth.com. Dispose of the product according to local waste disposal regulations.

metaDescription: Vive Health recalls adult portable bed rails due to serious entrapment and asphyxiation hazards linked to two reported fatalities.

Description: Aisstxoer adult bicycle helmets have been recalled because they do not meet mandatory safety standards for impact protection and stability. These helmets pose a risk of serious head injury in the event of a crash. The helmets are designed for large head sizes and feature specific markings including the model number GH018L. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Aisstxoer Adult Bicycle Helmets Recalled for Failing Mandatory Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact YXTDZ Store via email at yxtdzamz@126.com and visit the "Recalls and Product Safety Alerts" section on their website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Aisstxoer Adult Bike Helmet (pink)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from October 2025 to November 2025 for approximately $25.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshiyongxintaidianziyouxiangongsi (Shenzhen Yongxintai Electronics Co., Ltd.), dba, Yxtdz Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with mandatory safety standards for impact protection and stability, posing a risk of serious head injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the helmets right away and reach out to YXTDZ Store for a full refund. They will need to destroy the helmet by cutting the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to yxtdzamz@126.com.

metaDescription: Aisstxoer adult bicycle helmets recalled for non-compliance with safety standards, posing a risk of serious head injury during impact.

Description: Amazon has recalled the Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knife due to a risk of laceration. The folding mechanism may fail to keep the blade securely closed, posing a safety hazard. One report was received of the blade not remaining properly secured, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Amazon Recalls Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knives for Laceration Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are advised to contact Amazon at 855-215-5857 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at https://amazonfoldingkniferecall.expertinquiry.com/ or by visiting www.Amazon.com and selecting "Recalls and Product Safety Alerts" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knife

Images.Caption: Recalled Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knife
Recalled Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knife

Injuries.Name: Amazon received one report of the blade not remaining properly secured in the closed position. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from August 2025 through February 2026 for approximately $3.

Importers.Name: Amazon.com Services LLC, of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration due to potential failure of the folding mechanism to secure the blade.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled knife immediately and register for the recall at https://amazonfoldingkniferecall.expertinquiry.com/. A prepaid return kit and instructions will be provided for the safe return of the knife to Amazon. Upon receipt, a full refund will be issued to the original payment method or as an Amazon gift card.

metaDescription: Amazon recalls Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knives due to laceration hazard from blade not securing properly during use.

Description: Electrolux Group has recalled specific Frigidaire, Frigidaire Gallery, and Frigidaire Professional gas ranges due to a burn hazard caused by delayed ignition of the oven's bake burner. This defect can cause unexpected flames, posing a risk of burns. The recall includes multiple models with certain serial numbers. Electrolux and the CPSC have received reports of burn injuries linked to this issue.
Title: Electrolux Group Recalls Frigidaire Gas Ranges for Burn Hazard from Delayed Oven Ignition
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Electrolux Group at 866-291-7633 from 8:30 am to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email gasovenburnerrecall@electrolux.com, or visit www.GasOvenBurnerRecall.com or www.frigidaire.com and navigate to Recall Information for further details.

Products.Name: Frigidaire Gas Ranges
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 174,800 (In addition, about 5,300 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Frigidaire Range Model PCFG3080AF
Recalled Frigidaire Range Model GCFG3070BF
Recalled Frigidaire Range Model GCRG3060B*, where * can be F or D
Recalled Frigidaire Range Model FCRG3051B*, where * can be S, W, or B

Injuries.Name: Electrolux Group and the CPSC are aware of 62 reports of the oven's bake burner delayed ignition, including 30 reports of burn injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: Electrolux Consumer Products, Inc., of Charlotte, North Carolina

Retailers.Name: Lowe's, The Home Depot, and other retail stores nationwide and online at Frigidaire.com from June 2025 through January 2026 for between $630 and $2,700.

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to delayed ignition of the oven's bake burner

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the ovens immediately and reach out to Electrolux Group for a complimentary repair, which includes professional in-home installation of a new bake burner at no cost. The cooktop burners can still be used.

metaDescription: Electrolux recalls Frigidaire gas ranges due to burn hazard from delayed ignition of the oven's bake burner. Free repairs are available for affected models.

Description: Goregent has recalled its infant walkers because they pose a fall hazard that can cause serious injury or death. The walkers do not meet mandatory safety standards as they can pass through doorways and fail to stop at steps. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Goregent Infant Walkers Recalled for Fall Hazard Risk Due to Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Goregent Official Store via email at GoregentInfantWalkersRecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Goregent Infant Walker

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com in January 2026 for about $90.

Distributors.Name: Dongguanshi Aokaolan Trading Co., Ltd., dba Goregent Official Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from fall hazard due to non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled walkers and contact Goregent Official Store for a full refund. Disassemble the walker, remove the seat, mark 'Recalled' on the tray, and send a photo to GoregentInfantWalkersRecall@outlook.com.

metaDescription: Goregent infant walkers recalled for fall hazard risk from non-compliance with safety standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Lifetime Brands is recalling BUILT LUUM light up tumblers in 16 oz. and 18 oz. sizes due to risks of choking and battery ingestion. The tumblers contain LED lights powered by button cell batteries in the bottom compartment, which can become accessible if the tumbler breaks. One report involved a child accessing the battery, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Lifetime Brands Recalls BUILT LUUM Light Up Tumblers for Choking and Battery Ingestion Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Lifetime Brands at 888-561-2269, Monday to Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at Recall@Builtny.com. Additional details can be found online at https://www.builtny.com/pages/recall or by visiting www.builtny.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - FESTIVE FOREST
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - MERRY GLOW
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - LET IT SNOW
Recalled Built LUUM Tumbler - WINTER BLOOM
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - MERRY MAGIC
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - JOLLY JOURNEY
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - HEART N STARS
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - USA
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - STARS
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - FLAG
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - WINDMILL
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - BLUE FIREWORKS
"lumm," the tumbler's theme name, item number and UPC code are printed on a label located on the tumblers' underside or hangtag

Injuries.Name: Lifetime Brands has received one report of a child gaining access to the tumbler's button cell battery and putting it in the mouth. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Kroger, Hy-Vee, Meijer, Nordstrom Rack, and Winn-Dixie stores nationwide, as well as online at Builtny.com from October 2024 through December 2025, priced between $10 and $15.

Importers.Name: Lifetime Brands, Inc., owner of the BUILT brand, of Garden City, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of choking and ingestion from accessible button cell batteries due to potential breakage of the tumblers.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the tumblers, keep them out of children's reach, and properly dispose of the battery. Contact Lifetime Brands for a full refund, providing a photo of the discarded product when submitting the Product Recall Refund Form at https://www.builtny.com/pages/recall. Note: Dispose of button cell batteries according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

metaDescription: Lifetime Brands recalls BUILT LUUM light up tumblers due to choking and battery ingestion hazards from accessible button cell batteries.

en.Title: Lifetime Brands Recalls BUILT LUUM Light Up Tumblers for Choking and Battery Ingestion Hazards
en.Description: Lifetime Brands is recalling BUILT LUUM light up tumblers in 16 oz. and 18 oz. sizes due to risks of choking and battery ingestion. The tumblers contain LED lights powered by button cell batteries in the bottom compartment, which can become accessible if the tumbler breaks. One report involved a child accessing the battery, but no injuries have been reported.
en.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Lifetime Brands at 888-561-2269, Monday to Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at Recall@Builtny.com. Additional details can be found online at https://www.builtny.com/pages/recall or by visiting www.builtny.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.
en.metaDescription: Lifetime Brands recalls BUILT LUUM light up tumblers due to choking and battery ingestion hazards from accessible button cell batteries.

Injuries.Name: Lifetime Brands has received one report of a child gaining access to the tumbler's button cell battery and putting it in the mouth. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Kroger, Hy-Vee, Meijer, Nordstrom Rack, and Winn-Dixie stores nationwide, as well as online at Builtny.com from October 2024 through December 2025, priced between $10 and $15.

Importers.Name: Lifetime Brands, Inc., owner of the BUILT brand, of Garden City, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of choking and ingestion from accessible button cell batteries due to potential breakage of the tumblers.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the tumblers, keep them out of children's reach, and properly dispose of the battery. Contact Lifetime Brands for a full refund, providing a photo of the discarded product when submitting the Product Recall Refund Form at https://www.builtny.com/pages/recall. Note: Dispose of button cell batteries according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

Description: Specialized is recalling all Turbo Como SL electric bicycles because of a risk of fall caused by potential fork failure. The recall includes all Como SL models, identified by the name on the seat tube or top tube. One incident involving a steerer tube failure has been reported, but no injuries have occurred. Specialized is providing a free fork replacement through authorized dealers.
Title: Specialized Recalls Turbo Como SL Electric Bicycles for Fall Hazard from Fork Failure
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Specialized Rider Care at 877-808-8154 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email ridercare@specialized.com, or visit www.specialized.com for more details and to access the safety recall notices.

Products.Name: Specialized Turbo Como SL 4.0 & 5.0 Electric Bicycles

Como SL e-bike with recalled fork steerer tube - Model 5.0 in Smoke

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an incident involving a steerer tube failing and breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.specialized.com and through Authorized Specialized Retailers from March 2021 to January 2026, priced between $3,250 and $4,800.

Importers.Name: Specialized Bicycle Components Inc., of Morgan Hill, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential fork failure

Remedies.Name: Free replacement of the fork at an authorized dealer

Description: Tainoki Fine Furniture is recalling certain height-adjustable swivel office chairs because the chair base may bend, creating a fall hazard. The recall covers multiple models including Noah, Owen, and Warren office chairs in various colors. No injuries have been reported related to this issue.
Title: Tainoki Fine Furniture Recalls Height-Adjustable Office Chairs Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Tainoki Fine Furniture at 888-698-2466 from 10 a.m. to 7 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, via email at hello@tainoki.com, or visit https://www.tainoki.com/recall. You can also go to https://www.tainoki.com and click on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Noah Office Chair (Model number: M70160)
Recalled Owen Office Chair (Model number: M70040)
Recalled Warren Office Chair (Model number: M70740)

Retailers.Name: Available at HomeGoods stores nationwide from August 2025 through December 2025, priced between $180 and $200.

Importers.Name: Tainoki Fine Furniture, of Brea, California

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to potential bending of the chair's base

Remedies.Name: Stop using the chairs immediately and visit https://www.tainoki.com/recall for recall instructions. Participants will need to submit photos of the chairs, model number, and proof of destruction for a full refund.

metaDescription: Tainoki recalls height-adjustable office chairs due to fall hazard risk from potential base bending. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: ProRider has recalled multiple bicycle helmet models that fail to meet mandatory safety standards for impact protection and stability. The affected helmets pose a risk of serious head injury or death in a crash. No injuries have been reported in connection with these helmets.
Title: ProRider Bicycle Helmets Recalled for Failing Mandatory Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact ProRider at 800-642-3123 from 8 a.m. to 1 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email org@prorider.com, or visit www.prorider.com and click on "Recall" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Toddler Bike Helmet
Recalled BMX Helmet
Recalled Economy Bicycle Helmet
Recalled Bike Helmet with Turn Ring
Recalled Bike Helmet Black Foam
Model, date of manufacture and serial number are shown on label inside the helmet

Retailers.Name: Distributed by Wisconsin Bike Federation, Kiwanis Club, and Foster Love nationwide; available online at ProRider.com from June 2022 through May 2023, priced between $5 and $18.

Importers.Name: ProRider, Inc., of Kent, Washington

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to non-compliance with mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets and contact ProRider for a full refund. Destroy the helmet by cutting the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to org@prorider.com to receive the refund.

metaDescription: ProRider recalls bicycle helmets due to risk of serious head injury from non-compliance with mandatory safety standards. No injuries reported.

Description: 17 Stories Furniture is recalling 14-drawer dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that can cause serious injury or death, especially to children. The dressers have a metal frame with a wooden top and come in black, white, and brown. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: 17 Stories Furniture 14-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Hong Kong Baojia International via email at Baojia_recall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled 17 Stories Furniture 14-Drawer Dresser (front)
Recalled 17 Stories Furniture 14-Drawer Dresser (back)
Recalled 17 Stories Furniture 14-Drawer Dresser (side)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Wayfair.com from September 2023 to January 2026 for approximately $135.

Importers.Name: Hong Kong Baojia International Limited, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment, potentially leading to serious injuries or death to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the dressers if not anchored to the wall, keep them out of children's reach, and contact Hong Kong Baojia International for disposal instructions and a full refund.

Description: LIVEHOM is recalling its 11-Drawer Dressers because they can tip over if not anchored, creating entrapment and serious injury risks. These dressers do not comply with mandatory safety standards for clothing storage units. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: LIVEHOM 11-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Simplehome via email at livehomerecall@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled LIVEHOM 11-Drawer Dresser – brown
Recalled LIVEHOM 11-Drawer Dresser – white
Recalled LIVEHOM 11-Drawer Dresser – black
Recalled LIVEHOM 11-Drawer Dresser – gray
Recalled LIVEHOM 11-Drawer Dresser – pink

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from December 2025 to January 2026, priced around $110.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Lvmukeji Co., Ltd., dba Simplehome, of China

Hazards.Name: The dressers can tip over if not securely anchored to the wall, leading to potential entrapment and serious injury or death, particularly to children. This product does not meet the mandatory safety standards set by the STURDY Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the dressers immediately if they are not anchored to the wall and store them out of children's reach. To obtain a full refund, contact Simplehome. You will need to write 'RECALL' on the dressers with a permanent marker and send a photo of the marked dressers to livehomerecall@163.com before disposing of the product.

Description: The Cubimana Island Storm 3 In 1 Building Sets are recalled because the battery compartment in the LED light piece is accessible to children, posing a risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion. The sets include 781 building blocks and feature a pirate base and ship on the packaging. No injuries have been reported. The product violates mandatory toy safety standards related to battery access.

Description: HALO Dream is recalling Magic Sleepsuits due to a choking hazard from zipper heads that can detach. The recall involves specific batch codes and affects sleepsuits made with cotton and polyester materials. The firm has received reports of zipper head detachment but no injuries have been reported.
Title: HALO Dream Recalls Magic Sleepsuits Due to Zipper Head Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact HALO Dream at 833-791-0420 between 9 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, via email at customerservice@sleepsuitrecall.com, or visit www.sleepsuitrecall.com. More details can also be found at www.halosleep.com under the 'Recalls' section.

Images.Caption: Recalled HALO Magic Sleepsuit
The sewn in label with the batch code and "Made in India" circled
The hang tag with the batch code and "Made in India" circled

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of the zipper head detaching from the garment. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Halosleep.com, Amazon.com, Walmart.com and Target.com from September 2025 through February 2026 for about $50.

Importers.Name: HALO Dream, Inc., of New York City

Hazards.Name: Zipper head detachment posing a choking risk to infants.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled sleepsuit immediately and visit http://www.sleepsuitrecall.com to register for the recall. After registration, you will receive a coupon code for a replacement sleepsuit or a $50 credit for other items. Please do not discard the recalled sleepsuit until you have received your coupon code.

Description: Tomum Minoxidil Hair Growth Treatment spray bottles have been recalled because their packaging does not meet child-resistant standards required by law. The lack of child-resistant features creates a poisoning risk for young children, which can result in serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Tomum Minoxidil Hair Serum Spray Bottles Recalled for Failing Child-Resistant Packaging Standards
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Belleka at 862 244-1785 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email tomumcs@gmail.com, or visit https://itomum.com/contact-us/ or https://itomum.com.

Products.Name: Tomum Minoxidil Hair Growth Treatment Spray Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Tomum Minoxidil Hair Regrowth Treatment (Front view)
Recalled Tomum Minoxidil -Hair Regrowth Treatment (Rear view)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2025 to September 2025 for approximately $20.

Distributors.Name: Belleka Inc, dba TOMUM, of China
Chifengrongxiangshangmaoyouxiangongsi, dba Haircare Solution, of China
SOLUVIA LLC, of Brooklyn, New York, US
Xingchang Technology Co., Ltd., dba Xingchang, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the recalled bottles away from children and contact Belleka for a free replacement, including two child-resistant bottles (60 mL each). They will need to dispose of the recalled bottle and send a photo of it in the trash to Belleka at tomumcs@gmail.com.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the recalled bottles away from children and contact Belleka for a free replacement, including two child-resistant bottles (60 mL each). They will need to dispose of the recalled bottle and send a photo of it in the trash to Belleka at tomumcs@gmail.com.

Description: Andersen Windows is recalling window opening control devices sold as kits or installed on 100 Series casement windows manufactured before November 19, 2025. These devices can break or detach, posing a fall hazard. The company has received reports of device failures but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Andersen Windows Recalls 100 Series Casement Window Control Devices for Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Andersen Windows at 844-815-7332 from 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also email 100Seriescasementwocdrecall@andersencorp.com or visit www.andersenwindows.com/100seriescasementwocdrecall for more details.

Hazards.Name: Risk of falls and serious injuries due to potential breakage or detachment of control devices

Description: Bicystar has recalled its TB-HC6900 high chairs because they lack a required crotch restraint, posing a fall hazard. There is also a risk of head entrapment between the seat and chair side. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Bicystar TB-HC6900 High Chairs Recalled for Fall and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bicystar Store via email at bicystarhighchair@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bicystar High Chair - model TB-HC6900
"BICYSTAR" and "Model No.: TB-HC6900" are printed on labels stitched to the back of the recalled Bicystar high chair seat cover

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from September 1, 2025 through September 30, 2025 for about $76.

Distributors.Name: Hebei Beihong Kejiyouxiangongsi, dba Bicystar Group Co., Ltd. and Bicystar Store, of China

Hazards.Name: These high chairs lack the necessary attached crotch restraint, creating a significant fall hazard for children. Additionally, there is a risk of a child's head becoming trapped between the seat and the chair's side, leading to a dangerous entrapment situation.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled high chairs immediately and reach out to Bicystar Store for a full refund. They should disassemble the chair, cut the restraints and fabric seat cover, label the backseat with "Recall," and email photos of the destroyed high chairs to bicystarhighchair@outlook.com.

metaDescription: Bicystar recalls TB-HC6900 high chairs due to fall and entrapment hazards that violate safety standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Grtard has recalled magnetic stick figure toy sets that contain small magnets in the hands and feet. These magnets can become loose and pose a serious ingestion hazard to children, potentially causing severe internal injuries. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Grtard Magnetic Stick Figure Toy Sets Recalled for Magnet Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please email Grtard at Grtardrecall@outlook.com.

Products.Name: Grtard Magnetic Stick Figure Toy Sets

Images.Caption: Recalled Grtard Magnet Men Stick Figure Toy Set

Retailers.Name: Available online at Walmart.com from May 2025 to November 2025 for approximately $12.

Distributors.Name: Shanghaijinlianwangluokejiyouxiangongsi (Shanghai Jinlian Network Technology Co., Ltd.), dba Grtard, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to loose magnets, which can cause severe internal injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys immediately and keep them away from children. Contact Grtard for a full refund and provide a photo of the disposed product via email.

metaDescription: Grtard recalls magnetic stick figure toys due to risk of serious injury from magnet ingestion. These toys contain loose magnets that pose a hazard to children.

Description: The Babysense Max View Baby Monitor (model VBM55RX) is recalled because its display unit can overheat or spark during charging, posing a fire hazard. The recall addresses risks linked to the lithium-ion battery in the display. Hisense has received reports of incidents but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Babysense Max View Baby Monitor Recalled for Fire Hazard in Display Unit
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Hisense at (833) 689-2652 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at support@recallsecure.com, or visit www.recallsecure.com or www.babysensemonitors.com and select "Product Recalls" for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Babysense Max View 5.5" Baby Monitor display/parent unit
Recalled Babysense Max View 5.5" Baby Monitor display/parent unit - back view
Recalled Babysense Max View Baby Monitor with included cameras

Injuries.Name: Hisense has received 11 reports of incidents involving the display unit for the Babysense Max View Baby Monitors. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Hisense Ltd., of Israel

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and babylist.com from January 2023 through December 2025 for between $90 and $180.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating or sparking of the display unit during charging

Remedies.Name: Stop using the display unit and request a free replacement from Hisense

Description: The Somgem Pig and Yomin Toilet lighters have been recalled because they do not meet mandatory child-resistant safety standards. These lighters pose a risk of fire and burn injuries due to the absence of required safety mechanisms. No injuries have been reported in connection with these products.
Title: Recall of Somgem Pig and Yomin Toilet Lighters for Fire and Burn Hazards Due to Missing Child-Resistant Features
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Elepdv via email at elepdv@163.com.

Products.Name: Somgem Lightweight Tiny Pinky Pig Lighter

Images.Caption: Recalled Somgem Lightweight Tiny Pinky Pig Lighter
Recalled Yomin Toilet Lighter

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase on Amazon.com from July 2022 through November 2025, priced between $8 and $13.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Elephant Outdoor Information Technology Inc. dba Elepdv, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury or death from fire and burn hazards due to lack of child-resistant features.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lighters immediately and contact Elepdv for a full refund by sending a photo of the disabled lighter marked 'Recalled' to elepdv@163.com. Dispose of the lighters according to local waste disposal regulations.

metaDescription: Somgem Pig and Yomin Toilet lighters recalled for fire and burn hazards as they lack required child-resistant safety features.

Description: Royal Oak Enterprises recalled Flame Saber lighters because they do not meet mandatory child-resistant safety standards and labeling requirements. These defects create serious fire and burn hazards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Royal Oak Flame Saber Lighters Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards Due to Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Royal Oak at 877-567-9324 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at RoyalOak5955@sedgwick.com, or visit www.royaloakflamesaberrecall.expertinquiry.com or royaloak.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Royal Oak Flame Saber Lighter
Recalled lighter warning label "WARNING: Only store in locked position"
Recalled lighter warning label "DANGER: Extremely Flammable, Content Under Pressure"

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's, Home Depot, Tractor Supply, Rural King, and Ace Hardware stores across the nation, as well as online at Lowes.com, HomeDepot.com, TractorSupply.com, RuralKing.com, and AceHardware.com from November 2023 to October 2025 for approximately $3.

Distributors.Name: Royal Oak Enterprises, LLC, dba Royal Oak of Roswell, Georgia

Hazards.Name: The lighters lack the necessary child-resistant features, violating safety standards and posing serious fire and burn risks. They also fail to meet labeling requirements under the Federal Hazardous Substances Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the recalled lighters immediately and contact Royal Oak for a full refund. Instructions will be provided on how to properly destroy the lighter and submit proof of destruction to receive the refund.

metaDescription: Royal Oak Flame Saber lighters recalled for fire and burn hazards due to lack of child-resistant features and labeling violations.

Description: Sanven Technology has recalled Vevor baby gates because of an entrapment hazard. The gap between the gate and the floor can allow a child's torso to become trapped, posing a serious injury risk. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Sanven Technology Recalls Vevor Baby Gates for Entrapment Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, or via email at recalling@vevor.com. Additional details can be found online at https://www.vevor.com/pages/Vevor-Retractable-Baby-Gates-Recall or by visiting www.vevor.com and selecting 'Product Recall' at the top of the page.

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com, Homedepot.com, Vevor.com, Walmart.com and Wayfair.com from December 2023 through December 2025 for between $32 and $53.

Distributors.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., dba Vevor, of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to a child's torso fitting through the gap between the gate and the floor.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the gates immediately and contact Sanven Technology for a full refund. Additionally, they should destroy the gate by cutting through the mesh and email a photo of the destroyed gate to recalling@vevor.com.

Description: TheKiddoSpace has recalled its children's Christmas tree toy play sets because the zipper pull on the music box contains lead levels exceeding federal limits, posing a toxicity risk. The play sets also fail to meet required mechanical and electrical safety standards for battery-operated toys. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Children's Christmas Tree Play Sets Due to Lead Hazard and Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com or visit www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/christmas-tree. Alternatively, you can go to www.thekiddospacestore.com and click on "Product Safety Recalls" at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Children's Christmas Tree Toy Play Sets

Images.Caption: Recalled TheKiddoSpace Children's Christmas Tree Toy Play Set

Retailers.Name: Online at TheKiddoSpaceStore.com from November 2023 through February 2025 for between $13 and $40.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: The zipper pull on the music box compartment contains lead levels that exceed federal safety standards, posing a risk of toxicity if ingested by young children. Additionally, the Christmas tree does not meet essential mechanical and electrical safety requirements for battery-operated toys.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately cease using the recalled play sets, remove them from children, and contact TheKiddoSpace for a complimentary replacement toy of equal or greater value, including free shipping. To facilitate the process, consumers must mark the tree with the word "RECALLED" and send a photo of the marked item to info@thekiddospace.com. Afterward, the tree and storage box should be disposed of according to local waste regulations.

metaDescription: TheKiddoSpace recalls children's Christmas tree play sets for lead exposure risk and failure to meet toy safety standards. No injuries reported.

Description: TheKiddoSpace has recalled children's fingerpainting kits that contain hazardous chemicals including methanol and ethylene glycol, as well as banned phthalates. These substances pose health risks such as toxicity if ingested by children under 12. The recall follows reports of strong paint odor, skin irritation, and swollen lymph nodes associated with the kits.
Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Children's Fingerpainting Kits Due to Hazardous Chemicals and Phthalate Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com, or visit www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/finger-painting-kit. Additional information is available by clicking 'Product Safety Recalls' at the bottom of www.thekiddospacestore.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled TheKiddoSpace Finger Painting Kit - 25-color Paint Palette
Recalled TheKiddoSpace Finger Painting Kit - 12-color Paint Palette
Recalled TheKiddoSpace Finger Painting Kit - 36-color Paint Palette

Injuries.Name: TheKiddoSpace has received 18 reports worldwide, including one in the United States, of strong paint odor, skin irritation and swollen lymph nodes.

Retailers.Name: Online at TheKiddoSpaceStore.com and Amazon.com from June 2023 through February 2025 for between $20 and $30.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: The fingerpainting kits contain hazardous substances such as methanol and ethylene glycol, posing risks to children under 12 years old. Additionally, the presence of a regulated phthalate violates federal safety standards, as these substances can be toxic if ingested by young children, leading to serious health concerns.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to immediately stop using the recalled fingerpainting kits, keep the products away from children, and contact TheKiddoSpace for free replacement kits, including shipping. They will need to dispose of the old products according to local regulations and provide a photo of the disposed items to info@thekiddospace.com.

metaDescription: TheKiddoSpace recalls children's fingerpainting kits for containing hazardous substances and banned phthalates posing health risks to young children.

Description: TheKiddoSpace has recalled its children's sensory swing due to a strangulation hazard caused by fabric loops that can entangle a child's head or neck. The swing is a soft, cocoon-style hammock intended for indoor use and comes in various colors and themed prints. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.

Description: TheKiddoSpace has recalled its LED soccer hover balls because the batteries can overheat, creating a burn hazard. The affected product is a smaller hover ball about 5.5 inches in diameter with foam bumpers and marked "HOVER LED SOCCER BALL." There have been reports of a burning smell during use, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls LED Soccer Hover Balls Due to Burn Hazard from Battery Overheating
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com or visit their website at www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/soccer-hover-ball. Alternatively, visit www.thekiddospacestore.com and click on "Product Safety Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled TheKiddoSpace LED Soccer Hover Ball

Injuries.Name: TheKiddoSpace has received three reports of a burning smell coming from the hover ball when in use. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Received by consumers as promotional giveaways from December 2024 through February 2025.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Excessive surface temperature of batteries, posing a burn injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled hover balls immediately, keep them away from children, and contact TheKiddoSpace for a free replacement. They will need to mark the ball as "RECALLED" with permanent marker, send a photo of it to info@thekiddospace.com, and dispose of the ball according to local waste guidelines.

metaDescription: TheKiddoSpace LED soccer hover balls are recalled due to burn hazards from battery overheating. Use poses a serious risk of injury from excessive surface temperature.

Description: TheKiddoSpace has recalled its Montessori egg puzzle toys because the egg halves pose a choking hazard for children under three. The toys consist of plastic eggs that split into halves with unique geometric shapes, which can block a child's airway. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Montessori Egg Puzzle Toys for Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled toys immediately and remove them from children's reach. For further assistance, contact TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com, or visit their website at www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/geometry-egg. More information can also be found by clicking on "Product Safety Recalls" at the bottom of their homepage.

Description: TheKiddoSpace is recalling reusable water balloons because the storage bags contain a regulated phthalate that violates federal safety standards. Phthalates can be harmful if ingested by young children, posing a health risk. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Reusable Water Balloons Due to Phthalate Exposure Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com or visit www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/water-balloons. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.thekiddospacestore.com and click on "Product Safety Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: The storage bags for the water balloons contain a regulated phthalate, violating federal regulations. Phthalates can be harmful if ingested by young children, leading to serious health issues.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled water balloons and remove the storage bag from children's access. They can contact TheKiddoSpace for a free replacement bag, including shipping. To process the replacement, consumers must cut the storage bag in half and send a photo of the destroyed bag to info@thekiddospace.com. The destroyed bags should be disposed of according to local waste disposal regulations.

Description: Todson has recalled Concord 360 Degree Rechargeable Light-Up bicycle helmets because they do not meet mandatory safety standards. These helmets may fail to provide adequate protection in a crash, posing a risk of serious head injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Todson Recalls Concord 360 Degree Rechargeable Light-Up Bicycle Helmets for Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately cease using the recalled helmets and reach out to Todson for a full refund. Contact Todson at 800-278-2565 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at 360concordhelmet@todson.com. More information is available at todson.com/360-concord-recall or by visiting www.Todson.com and selecting 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Description: Trankerloop is recalling baby bath seats labeled "PLASTIC STOOL" and "Model: YD-1958" due to instability that can cause tipping and increase drowning risk. The seats have two detachable arms and four suction cups but do not meet mandatory safety standards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Trankerloop Baby Bath Seats Recalled for Drowning Hazard and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Trankerloop at 405-204-8540 from 9 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. CT Monday through Friday or via email at hkkll147@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Trankerloop Baby Bath Seat – gray
"PLASTIC STOOL" and "Model: YD-1958" on tracking label on the back of the seat

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from August 2025 to October 2025 for about $36.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshirongmanshangmaoyouxiangongsi (Shenzhen Shi Ronmang Trading Co., Ltd.) dba Trankerloop, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to drowning from unstable bath seats.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bath seats immediately and contact Trankerloop for a full refund. Mark the front of the seat as 'Recalled', disassemble it, cut the suction cups, and email a photo of the disassembled seat to hkkll147@outlook.com.

ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Joly's at 240-412-8720 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at jolysllc@gmail.com.

Description: Meijer has recalled certain one-piece footed sleepwear for children under the Lullaby Lane and MCS brands. These sleepwear items fail to meet mandatory flammability standards, creating a potential burn hazard. The recall affects specific sizes and styles identified by labels on the neck and side seams. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Meijer Recalls Lullaby Lane and MCS Children's Sleepwear for Flammability Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Meijer at 800-927-8699 or visit https://www.meijer.com/recalls/KB0020513. Additional details can be found by clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the Meijer homepage.

Description: JJGoo LED balloon lights have been recalled because their button cell batteries are easily accessible, creating a risk of ingestion. The lights do not meet mandatory safety standards for products containing button cell batteries. No injuries have been reported. The ingestion of these batteries can cause serious harm or death.
Title: JJGoo LED Balloon Lights Recalled for Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact JJGoo via email at JJGooLEDBalloonLightsrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled JJGoo LED Balloon Lights
The recalled JJGoo balloon lights were sold in packs of 100 LED color-changing lights
Each of the multi-color recalled JJGoo balloon lights has 200 preinstalled LR41 batteries
"MY1005E-Colorfu1-100" is printed on a label on the product packaging of the recalled JJGoo LED balloon lights

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from May 2025 through November 2025 for about $23.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Yimu Technology Co., Ltd. dba JJGoo, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to accessible button cell batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lights, keep them away from children, and contact JJGoo for a refund

metaDescription: JJGoo LED balloon lights are recalled due to risk of serious injury from accessible button cell batteries posing ingestion hazards.

Description: SAMIT has recalled its youth multi-purpose helmets because they do not comply with mandatory bicycle helmet safety standards. The helmets fail to provide required positional stability, increasing the risk of serious head injury or death during an accident. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: SAMIT Youth Multi-Purpose Helmets Recalled for Failing Bicycle Helmet Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Samit Outdoors via email at usjialebei@foxmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled SAMIT Youth Multipurpose Helmet in crack red
Recalled SAMIT Youth Multipurpose Helmet in crack blue
Recalled SAMIT Youth Multipurpose Helmet in crack black
Recalled SAMIT Youth Multipurpose Helmet in pure black

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Amazon.com from March 2025 to November 2025, priced between $28 and $33.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshilonghuaqujialebeibaihuoshanghang (Jialebei Department Store, Longhua District, Shenzhen) dba Samit Outdoor, of China

Hazards.Name: These helmets do not meet the mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, failing to ensure positional stability and certification. This non-compliance increases the risk of serious injury or death in the event of an accident.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled helmets immediately and contact Samit Outdoor for a full refund. To dispose of the helmet safely, cut off the straps. Refund requests can be submitted by sending photos of the helmet with the straps removed to usjialebei@foxmail.com.

metaDescription: SAMIT recalls youth multi-purpose helmets due to failure to meet safety standards, posing a serious head injury hazard in accidents.

Description: Vive Health has recalled specific models of adult portable bed rails due to risks of entrapment and asphyxiation. These products do not meet mandatory safety standards and lack required hazard warning labels. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Vive Health Recalls Adult Portable Bed Rails for Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Vive Health at 800-487-3808 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recalls@vivehealth.com, or visit vivehealth.com/pages/recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Vive Health Bed Rail Model LVA1024

Manufacturers.Name: Ningbo Shenyu Medical Equipment Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com and ViveHealth.com from August 2023 to December 2025, priced between $45 and $80.

Importers.Name: Vive Health LLC, of of Naples Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of entrapment and asphyxiation due to violation of safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bed rails immediately and contact Vive Health for a full refund. Mark the bed rails as 'RECALLED' and send a photo with your name to recalls@vivehealth.com. Dispose of the product following local regulations. Only bed rails purchased after August 21, 2023, are affected.

Description: Beloems has recalled its adult portable bed rails, model BL-BR201, due to risks of entrapment and asphyxiation caused by structural instability and improper sizing of components. The foldable rails include metal tubing, foam grips, support legs, and a fabric pouch with the brand logo. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Beloems Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled for Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Beloems via email at leioujiapin@163.com for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled Beloems Adult Portable Bed Rail – black handles
Recalled Beloems Adult Portable Bed Rail – gray handles

Manufacturers.Name: Leioujiapin Technology Co., Ltd. dba Beloems, of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from June 2025 to October 2025 for approximately $90.

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risks due to structural instability, improper sizing of push pins, and potential for lacerations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use immediately and contact Beloems for a full refund. Destroy the bed rails by cutting the foam padding and marking them as 'RECALLED' with a permanent marker, then send a photo of the destroyed product to leioujiapin@163.com.

metaDescription: Beloems recalls adult portable bed rails due to entrapment and asphyxiation hazards from structural and sizing defects. No injuries reported.

Description: Aroeve has recalled MK04 model air purifiers in black or white due to fire and burn hazards caused by overheating and ignition. The affected units have serial numbers starting with 'BN' and a label on the bottom. There have been reports of overheating and one fire incident, but no injuries or property damage have been confirmed.

ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out via email at Aroeve-airpure-recall@outlook.com, or visit https://aroeve.com/pages/product-recall-information or https://aroeve.com/ and select 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Description: LShome is recalling a 3-pack of photoelectric smoke detector fire alarms due to a high sensing threshold that can delay alarm activation during a fire. The affected model is XG-7D04-KZ9Z with SKU CX-50YP-A5VN. This defect poses a serious risk of injury or death by failing to provide timely warning. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: LShome 3-Pack Photoelectric Smoke Detector Fire Alarms Recalled for Delayed Alarm Activation
ConsumerContact: Email lmm15957491237@163.com for refund instructions.

Description: SOOWERY has recalled its 6-Drawer Dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards when the units are not anchored to a wall. Description: Andy & Evan has recalled children's pajama sets that do not meet mandatory flammability standards, posing a risk of serious burn injuries or death. The recalled sets include long-sleeved shirts with buttons and matching flared pants in specific patterns and sizes. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Andy & Evan Recalls Children's Pajama Sets for Burn Hazard Violation
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Andy & Evan via email at recall@andyandevan.com or visit their website at https://www.andyandevan.com/pages/recall.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious burn injuries or death due to violation of mandatory flammability standards.

Description: Clark Associates is recalling Lancaster Table & Seating powder-coated aluminum outdoor chairs and barstools because their legs can bend or break during use, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes arm chairs, side chairs, and barstools sold under numerous item numbers in various colors. The company has received reports of broken or bent legs causing falls and injuries. Outdoor tables sold separately are not affected by this recall.
Title: Clark Associates Recalls Lancaster Table & Seating Aluminum Outdoor Chairs and Barstools Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Clark Associates at 800-285-8172, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at pcachairsandbarstools@lancastertableandseating.com. Additional details are available at www.lancastertableandseating.com/recalls/powder-coated-aluminum-chairs or by visiting www.lancastertableandseating.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Lancaster Table & Seating Powder-Coated Aluminum Outdoor Chairs

Images.Caption: Recalled arm chair - gray
Recalled side chair - black
Recalled bar stool - silver

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 36 reports of broken or bent chair legs, resulting in four falls and three injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available through Clark Pro and Clark National Accounts across the U.S., as well as online at webstaurantstore.com, therestaurantstore.com, and quicksupply.com from April 2020 to October 2025, priced between $55 and $133.

Importers.Name: Clark Core Services LLC, dba Clark Associates, of Lancaster, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Risk of legs bending or breaking during use, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled items immediately and contact Clark Associates for a full refund or store credit. They are also advised to register their product online at www.lancastertableandseating.com/recalls/powder-coated-aluminum-chairs, label the product as 'Recalled' in a visible area, and dispose of it according to local regulations.

metaDescription: Clark Associates recalls Lancaster Table & Seating aluminum outdoor chairs and barstools due to fall hazard from broken or bent legs.

Description: Dresbe has recalled LED Christmas Headbands because the button cell batteries inside can be easily accessed by children, posing a risk of serious injury or death from ingestion. The headbands do not meet mandatory safety standards and lack required warnings. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Dresbe LED Christmas Headbands Recalled for Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Dresbe via email at dresberecall@shineprom.com for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled Dresbe Christmas Headbands (with candy cane and Christmas tree attachments)
Recalled Dresbe Christmas Headbands (with lights)
Label affixed to the recalled headband package

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from March 2024 through June 2025 for approximately $13.

Distributors.Name: Wenxi Xuefeng Technology Co., Ltd, dba Dresbe, of China

Hazards.Name: The headbands pose a risk of battery ingestion, as children can easily access the button cell batteries. This violates safety standards and lacks the necessary warnings under Reese's Law, potentially leading to serious injuries or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the headbands immediately and reach out to Dresbe for disposal instructions. A photograph of the disposed product must be emailed to dresberecall@shineprom.com to receive a refund. Note that button cell batteries are hazardous and should be disposed of following local hazardous waste guidelines.

metaDescription: Dresbe LED Christmas Headbands recalled due to risk of serious injury or death from button cell battery ingestion. Immediate safety concern identified.

Description: EnHomee has recalled its 10-drawer dressers because they can tip over if not anchored, creating serious tip-over and entrapment hazards. These dressers do not meet mandatory safety standards and pose risks primarily to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: EnHomee 10-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact EnHomee at 833-668-8899 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or email enhomeestore@gmail.com. More information is available at https://enhomee.store/recall.html or by visiting https://enhomee.store/ and clicking 'Recall' at the top.

Distributors.Name: HunanSan Tang International Trading Co., Ltd. dba EnHomee Direct, of China

Hazards.Name: Unstable dressers can tip over if not anchored, posing serious risks of injury or death to children, violating the STURDY Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the dressers if unanchored and move them out of children's reach. Contact EnHomee for guidance on identifying affected units and disposal for a full refund. A photo of the disposed product must be sent to enhomeestore@gmail.com.

Description: GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil is recalled because its packaging does not comply with child-resistant requirements mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The essential oil contains methyl salicylate, posing a poisoning hazard to young children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil Recalled for Child Poisoning Hazard Due to Non-Compliant Packaging
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Linkers at 347-775-2310 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email admin@linkersecomm.com, or visit us.shoprythm.com and click on "Recall" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil-Bottle and Box Front
Recalled GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil-Bottle Side Label
Recalled GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil-Bottle Label with UPC Code

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com by Gumili Beauty from October 2023 through November 2025 for approximately $8.

Distributors.Name: Guangzhou Feifan Kuajing Supply Chain Co., Ltd. dba Gumili Beauty, of China

Hazards.Name: The essential oil contains methyl salicylate, which must be packaged in child-resistant containers as mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (PPPA). The current packaging does not meet this requirement, creating a poisoning hazard for young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately keep the recalled essential oil out of children's reach and contact the company for a full refund or a complimentary replacement bottle. To process the refund or replacement, consumers will need to send a photo of the product disposed of in the trash to gumilius@outlook.com.

metaDescription: GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil is recalled for child poisoning risk because its packaging fails to meet child-resistant standards required by law.

Description: Little Cow Company is recalling its 9 oz candles in glass jars because the jars can crack or break during use, posing fire, burn, and laceration hazards. The candles are labeled with the brand name 'Little Cow Co.' and described as '100% natural soy wax candle, laced with organic butter.' The company has received two reports of glass jar breakage but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Little Cow Company Recalls 9 oz Glass Jar Candles for Fire, Burn, and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Little Cow Company via email at info@littlecowco.com or visit their website at https://littlecowco.com/pages/recall. Click on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page for detailed information.

Products.Name: Little Cow Co. 9 oz Glass Jar Candle

Images.Caption: Recalled Little Cow Co. 9 oz Candle in Glass Jar

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of glass jar breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Little Cow Company LLC, of Portland, Oregon

Retailers.Name: Available at New Seasons Market and Town and Country stores across the nation from December 2025 to January 2026 for approximately $25.

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire, burns, and lacerations due to potential glass jar breakage

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled candles and contact Little Cow Company for a full refund. They will need to mark the jar with an 'X', trim the wicks, and email a photo of the modified jar and cut wicks.

Description: Lola + The Boys has recalled five styles of youth hooded clothing containing drawstrings that pose a strangulation hazard. The affected garments include several hoodie sets available in youth sizes 1 through 12. No injuries have been reported. This recall addresses a violation of federal safety standards for children's upper outerwear.
Title: Lola + The Boys Recalls Youth Hooded Clothing with Drawstrings Due to Strangulation Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can contact Lola + The Boys at 312-285-6053 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email customerservice@lolaandtheboys.com. More details can be found online at https://lolaandtheboys.com/pages/recall or by visiting https://lolaandtheboys.com/ and clicking on 'Recall Policy' at the bottom of the homepage.

Description: Semfri is recalling children's multi-purpose helmets that do not meet mandatory safety standards. These helmets may fail to protect against serious head injuries or death in a crash. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Semfri Recalls Children's Helmets for Failing Mandatory Bicycle Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Semfri via email at Semfri_recall@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Semfri Multi-Purpose Helmet - front view
NorthStar Gas Washer Serial number
Recalled Semfri Multi-Purpose Helmet - inside view

Retailers.Name: Available online at Walmart.com from June 2025 through November 2025, priced between $19 and $28.

Distributors.Name: Jinjiangshi XunMai Network Technology Co., Ltd., dba Semfri, of China

Hazards.Name: These helmets do not meet mandatory safety standards, failing to provide adequate protection in crashes, which could lead to severe head injuries or fatalities.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the helmets immediately and contact Semfri for a full refund. It is recommended to destroy the helmet by cutting the straps and sending a photo of the destroyed helmet to Semfri_recall@163.com.

Description: Scepter B62 gas and oil fuel containers are recalled for lacking required safety features, increasing the risk of flash fires and child poisoning. These containers have a dual-chamber design for gasoline and oil and are marked with "Scepter" and "Essence Danger." No injuries have been reported. The recall addresses serious fire and poisoning hazards associated with these products.
Title: Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Containers Recalled for Fire and Child Poisoning Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Mameix Retail at 587-816-2804 from 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email scepterrecall@gmail.com.

Importers.Name: Mameix Retail, Inc. of Canada

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire due to missing flame mitigation devices and potential child poisoning from non-child-resistant spouts.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the containers, keep them away from children, and contact Mameix Retail for a refund.

Description: Shintenchi is recalling its white 6-drawer dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that can cause serious injury or death, especially to children. The dressers do not comply with mandatory safety standards for clothing storage units. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Shintenchi 6-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Shintenchi via email at ShintenchiRecall@hotmail.com or visit their website at http://www.shintenchi.com.cn/recall.html. Alternatively, they can go to http://www.shintenchi.com.cn and click on the "Recall" section at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Shintenchi 6-Drawer Dresser, model SHX12077-WH (front)
Recalled Shintenchi 6-Drawer Dresser, model SHX12077-WH (side)

Manufacturers.Name: Huizhou Sunhouse Home Furnishings Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from July 2025 to August 2025 for approximately $130.

Distributors.Name: Shanghai Kase International Trade Co., Ltd., dba Shintenchi, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment, especially if not anchored to the wall, posing serious injury or death risks to children. Non-compliance with STURDY Act standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the dressers if unanchored and store them out of children's reach. Contact Shintenchi for disposal instructions to receive a full refund, including submitting a photo to ShintenchiRecall@hotmail.com as proof of disposal.

Description: YITA has recalled multiple dresser brands including Dextrus, ModFusion, Uforic, Yintatech, and Yitahome due to tip-over and entrapment hazards. These dressers feature metal frames with fabric drawers and come in various colors. The recall addresses risks of serious injury or death, particularly to children. No injuries have been reported.
Title: YITA Recalls Multiple Brands of Dressers Due to Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach YITA at 877-672-0878 from 8 a.m. to 12 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at yitamotor@outlook.com. More information is available online at https://www.yitahome.com/article/voluntary-safety-recall-16-drawer-dresser-fabric-dresser.html?aid=152 or by visiting https://www.yitahome.com/ and selecting 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Description: AiTuiTui Pull String Teething Toys have been recalled because they pose a choking hazard and do not meet mandatory toy safety standards. The toys feature a disc shape with colorful pull string tentacles and spinning rings. There have been reported incidents of choking and children biting off pieces of the silicone strings. This recall addresses the risk of serious injury or death.
Title: AiTuiTui Pull String Teething Toys Recalled for Choking Hazard Due to Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Vanfun via email at recallmls@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Teether Toy – Red
Recalled Teether Toy – Blue

Injuries.Name: CPSC is aware of 15 choking incidents and two incidents of children able to bite pieces off the silicone strings.

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from August 2022 to September 2025 for about $10.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Mailesi Technology Co., Ltd., dba Vanfun, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to non-compliance with toy safety standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy immediately and request a full refund from Vanfun. Cut and discard all string tentacles, mark the toy as "DESTROYED," and email a photo of the destroyed item to recallmls@outlook.com.

metaDescription: AiTuiTui Pull String Teething Toys recalled for choking hazard from non-compliance with toy safety standards. Risk of serious injury or death reported.

Description: Bazic Products has recalled its Silicone Glue Model 2030 because it contains methanol and is not packaged in child-resistant containers as required by law. The product is also mislabeled as non-toxic and lacks proper flammability warnings, violating federal safety standards. No injuries have been reported related to this recall.
Title: Bazic Products Recalls Silicone Glue Model 2030 for Poisoning Hazard and Labeling Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Bazic Products at 877-772-2942 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday or via email at compliance@bazic.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bazic Silicone Glue – Front and Side
Recalled Bazic Silicone Glue – Location of Batch Code

Retailers.Name: Available at Shims Bargain Inc., Henry Lamond Co. Ltd., Universal Wholesale, and D'notions & crafts stores across the nation, as well as online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com from May 2025 through October 2025 for approximately $3.

Importers.Name: Bangkit USA Inc, dba Bazic Products, of El Monte, California

Hazards.Name: The glue contains methanol and is not packaged in child-resistant containers as mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. This poses a significant risk of serious injury or death from poisoning if ingested by children. Furthermore, the product does not meet labeling requirements under the Federal Hazardous Substances Act, as it is incorrectly labeled "Non-Toxic" and lacks a warning for its flammability.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Bazic Silicone Glue and contact Bazic Products for guidance on submitting a photo of the product to obtain a full refund. It is important to dispose of the recalled glue according to local and state regulations. Consumers should not dispose of methanol in sewers, rainwater pipes, or regular trash to prevent fire hazards and environmental damage.

metaDescription: Bazic Products recalls Silicone Glue Model 2030 due to poisoning risk from methanol and failure to meet child-resistant packaging and labeling standards.

Description: Gazelle USA is recalling Urban Arrow FamilyNext Pro Cargo E-Bikes because the Fidlock buckle may not fully latch, posing an injury hazard to children in the cargo area. The company has received reports of buckle failures but no injuries have been reported. The recall involves e-bikes with an aluminum frame and reinforced foam cargo box in green or black.
Title: Gazelle USA Recalls Urban Arrow FamilyNext Pro Cargo E-Bikes for Buckle Failure Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Gazelle USA at 800-838-5388, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email service.na@urbanarrow.com or visit https://urbanarrow.com/en-na/recall for further details.

Products.Name: Urban Arrow FamilyNext Pro Cargo E-Bikes

Images.Caption: Recalled FamilyNext Pro Cargo Bike
Recalled E-bike Old Buckle (left) and E-bike Replacement Buckle (right)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of buckles not fully latching. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at select specialty stores from March 2025 to December 2025 for approximately $9,000.

en.Title: Gazelle USA Recalls Urban Arrow FamilyNext Pro Cargo E-Bikes for Buckle Failure Hazard
en.Description: Gazelle USA is recalling Urban Arrow FamilyNext Pro Cargo E-Bikes because the Fidlock buckle may not fully latch, posing an injury hazard to children in the cargo area. The company has received reports of buckle failures but no injuries have been reported. The recall involves e-bikes with an aluminum frame and reinforced foam cargo box in green or black.

Description: Hobby Lobby is recalling Giraffe and Llama plush toys with clips because their legs can detach, releasing small beads that pose a choking hazard. The Giraffe toy has a yellow body and colorful legs, while the Llama toy features a white body with a mirror and teether. One report of a leg detaching has been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Hobby Lobby Recalls Giraffe and Llama Plush Toys with Clips Due to Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Hobby Lobby Stores at 800-326-7931 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email customer.service@hobbylobby.com, or visit www.hobbylobby.com/customer-service/recalls.

Images.Caption: Recalled Giraffe Plush Toy w/Clip
Recalled Llama Plush Toy w/Clip

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the leg detaching from the body of the plush toy, allowing the small beads to detach from the leg. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Hobby Lobby Stores nationwide and online at hobbylobby.com from May 2025 through December 2025 for between $6 (Giraffe) and $8 (Llama).

Importers.Name: Hobby Lobby Stores, Inc. of Oklahoma City, Oklahoma

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to detachable legs releasing small beads

Remedies.Name: Return for a full refund
