Description: Mindbodygreen is recalling Ultimate Multivitamin+ dietary supplement bottles because they do not meet child-resistant packaging requirements under the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The lack of child-resistant caps creates a poisoning risk if accessed by children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Mindbodygreen Recalls Ultimate Multivitamin+ Bottles for Non-Child-Resistant Packaging Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach out to Mindbodygreen via email at multi@mindbodygreen.com, or visit shop.mindbodygreen.com/pages/multi-update for further details. Additional information is also available at www.shop.mindbodygreen.com under "Important Multi packaging update".

Retailers.Name: Available at Mindbodygreen.com from November 2021 to November 2025, priced between $40 and $70 based on subscription.

Distributors.Name: Mindbodygreen, of Miami, Florida

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging leading to potential poisoning risk for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the bottles out of children's reach and request a free child-resistant cap from Mindbodygreen.

The set includes five glow-in-the-dark crayons and coloring sheets. No injuries have been reported in connection with this product.

Description: LuxJet is recalling its Submersible LED Lights because the lithium coin batteries inside are easily accessible, posing a risk of ingestion. The lights do not comply with mandatory safety standards for products containing coin batteries. No injuries have been reported. The ingestion of these batteries can cause serious injury or death.
Title: LuxJet Submersible LED Lights Recalled for Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to LuxJet via email at info@luxjet.com.cn.

Images.Caption: Violative LuxJet Submersible LED Lights
Violative LuxJet Submersible LED Lights

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from January 2022 through November 2025 for approximately $23.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Shimei Lighting Co., Ltd dba as Luxjet, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard from accessible lithium coin batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the LED lights and contact LuxJet for disposal instructions and a full refund. Consumers must send a photo of the destroyed product to LuxJet at info@luxjet.com.cn. Follow local hazardous waste procedures for battery disposal.

metaDescription: LuxJet recalls Submersible LED Lights due to ingestion hazard from accessible lithium coin batteries posing serious injury risks.

Description: Trek and Electra have recalled the rear wheel bolts on certain 2026 model electric bicycles due to a risk of the bolts breaking and causing the rear wheel to detach. The affected models include Trek FX+ 1 Midstep, FX+ 1S Midstep, FX+ 1 Stepover, FX+ 1S Stepover, and Electra Townie Go! Step Thru and Townie Go! S Step Thru bicycles sold before October 28, 2025. The black bolts connecting the motor to the rear frame may fail, creating a fall hazard. Seven reports of bolt breakage have been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Recall of Rear Wheel Bolts on Trek FX+ and Electra Townie Go! Electric Bicycles Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Trek at 800-373-4594 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/company/legal_policies/safety_recalls/ for more details.

Products.Name: Rear wheel bolts for 2026 Trek FX+ 1 electric bicycles

Trek FX+1S Midstep with recalled rear wheel bolts
Trek FX+ 1 Stepover with recalled rear wheel bolts
Trek FX+ 1S Stepover with recalled rear wheel bolts
Electra Townie Go! Step Thru with recalled rear wheel bolts
Electra Townie Go! S Step Thru with recalled rear wheel bolts

Injuries.Name: The firm has received seven reports of the rear wheel bolts breaking on affected bicycles. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Hyena, Inc., of Taiwan

Retailers.Name: Available at Trek stores and authorized retailers across the nation, as well as online, from April 2025 to October 2025 for approximately $2,100.

Hazards.Name: Risk of wheel separation due to breaking bolts

Remedies.Name: Free replacement of black bolts with new silver bolts

metaDescription: Trek and Electra recall rear wheel bolts on select electric bicycles due to risk of wheel separation and fall hazard. Free bolt replacement offered.

Description: Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kits are recalled because their packaging does not meet child-resistant standards. The serum contains minoxidil, which can cause serious injury or death if ingested by children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kits Recalled for Non-Child-Resistant Packaging Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Nengmiaokeji at sesuall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kit for Men
Recalled Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kit for Men Packaging (Box Side)
Recalled Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kit for Men Packaging (Box Back)
Recalled Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kit for Men Packaging (Bottle Back)

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Amazon.com from December 2023 to August 2025 for approximately $15.

Distributors.Name: Ganzhounengmiaokejiyouxiangongsi dba Nengmiaokeji, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging of the hair growth serum, which contains minoxidil, increases the risk of serious injury or death from poisoning in children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately store the recalled serum bottles out of children's reach and contact Nengmiaokeji for a complimentary replacement. They will need to dispose of the serum by placing the empty bottle in the trash, taking a photo of it, and emailing it to sesuall@outlook.com.

metaDescription: Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kits recalled due to non-child-resistant packaging posing poisoning risks to children.

Description: Aborder Products has recalled Cumbor retractable safety gates due to a risk of child entrapment caused by gaps between the gate and floor that violate mandatory safety standards. The gates have a metal frame with a mesh screen and adjustable width. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Aborder Products Recalls Cumbor Retractable Safety Gates Due to Child Entrapment Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Aborder Products at 800-539-9594 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email recall@cumbor.cn, or visit https://cumborgates.com/recall/ for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Cumbor retractable safety gate - gray
Suction cup sold with gate
Label affixed to the retractable safety gate fabric

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com in September 2025 for about $40.

Importers.Name: Aborder Products of Irving, Texas

Hazards.Name: Entrapment risk due to gap between gate and floor, violating safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gates immediately, contact Aborder Products for a full refund, and destroy the gate by cutting the mesh, then email a photo of the destroyed gate to recall@cumbor.cn.

metaDescription: Cumbor retractable safety gates recalled for child entrapment hazard caused by unsafe gaps violating safety standards.

Description: Johnson Health Tech has expanded its recall to include several Matrix Retail and Vision treadmill models due to a fire hazard caused by loose power cord connections. The affected treadmills lack a power cord bracket, which can cause the cord to loosen and overheat. The company has received reports of overheating but no injuries. A free replacement power cord with a locking-style plug is provided to address the hazard.
Title: Johnson Health Tech Expands Recall of Matrix Retail and Vision Treadmills for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Johnson Health Tech North America at 800-211-4590 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email retailpowercord@johnsonfit.com. More information is available online at https://matrixhomefitness.com/recalls or https://www.visionfitness.com/eng/recalls under the 'Recalls' section.

Recalled Matrix Retail T75 (TM703, TM703B, TM703C, TM703E) Treadmill
Recalled Vision T9800 (TM87) Treadmill
Recalled Vision TF20 (TM432, TM432C) Treadmill
Recalled Vision T40 (TM433, TM433C) Treadmill
Recalled Vision T80 (TM445) Treadmill
Location of model and serial number on lower front crossbar

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 27 reports of overheating at the power cord-socket connection. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: JFW

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to loose power cord connection

Remedies.Name: Free replacement power cord with automatic locking-style plug for self-installation

Description: Gagaku has recalled ice cream shop toy playsets containing high-powered magnets that can detach and pose a serious ingestion hazard to children. Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys, keep them away from children, and contact Brandworl.USA for a full refund. Destroy the toy by marking each piece "RECALLED" and send a photo of the destroyed toy to Brandworl@gmail.com before disposing of it.

Description: Mikario Trading has recalled its 5-in-1 baby gyms because they pose a suffocation hazard by violating mandatory infant support cushion safety standards. The product includes a convertible activity center and tummy-time pillow mat with a toy bar and plush toys. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Mikario Trading 5-in-1 Baby Gyms Recalled for Suffocation Hazard Due to Safety Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Mikario Trading at 209-232-8655 from 7 a.m. to 1 p.m. ET Monday through Friday or via email at mikariorecall@gmail.com.

Description: Based Online is recalling Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets because they contain loose high-powered magnets that violate mandatory toy safety standards. These magnets pose a serious ingestion hazard, as swallowing them can cause severe internal injuries or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Based Online Fidget Magnet Ball Toys Recalled for Magnet Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled toys immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Based Online for a full refund. Dispose of the toys and email a photo of the disposal to Based Online at recall@mail.goclimber.cn.

Description: Topkay Torch Lighters sold in two-packs are recalled for failing to meet mandatory safety standards. The lighters lack child-resistant features, creating a risk of serious fire and burn injuries. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Topkay Torch Lighters Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards Due to Lack of Child-Resistant Features
ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Xiyude at BCLighterService@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Topkay Torch Lighter

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon from September 2025 to October 2025 for approximately $15.

Distributors.Name: Xinyude Shenzhen Keji Youxiangongsi, dba Xiyude, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from fire and burn hazards due to lack of child-resistant mechanisms.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the lighters and contact Xiyude for a full refund. To destroy the lighters, pull out the black adjuster knob on top, then email a photo of proof of destruction to BCLighterService@outlook.com to receive a refund. Dispose of the lighters following local waste disposal regulations.

metaDescription: Topkay Torch Lighters recalled for fire and burn hazards caused by missing child-resistant mechanisms. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: The Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy has been recalled because its silicone strings pose a choking hazard. These strings can become lodged in a child's throat, potentially causing respiratory distress or death. The Consumer Product Safety Commission is aware of multiple choking incidents related to this toy.
Title: Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy Recalled for Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, please reach out to Longyanguiheng via email at yetonamr_recall@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy – blue
Recalled Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy – blue
Recalled Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy – red
Recalled Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy – red

Injuries.Name: CPSC is aware of 32 choking incidents.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from June 2025 to October 2025, priced between $10 and $16.

Importers.Name: Longyan Xinluo District Guiheng E-Commerce Co. Ltd., dba Longyanguiheng, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to silicone strings that can become lodged in the throat, leading to respiratory distress or death.

Remedies.Name: Immediately discontinue use, remove the toy from children, and contact Longyanguiheng for a full refund. Cut and discard all silicone strings, mark the toy as "DESTROYED," and email a photo of the destroyed toy to yetonamr_recall@163.com.

metaDescription: Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy recalled due to choking hazard from silicone strings that can block the airway and cause serious injury or death.

Description: BBWOO baby loungers have been recalled because they do not meet mandatory safety standards for infant sleep products. The loungers have low sides and oversized openings that create risks of entrapment and falls. They also lack a stand, increasing the risk of falls when placed on elevated surfaces. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: BBWOO Baby Loungers Recalled for Entrapment and Fall Hazards Violating Infant Sleep Product Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should email LSY Direct at bbwoorecall@163.co.

Images.Caption: Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Gray Dinosaur
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Gray Line
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Gray Feather
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Pink Deer
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Pink Elephant
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Green Line
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Blue Car
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Khaki
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Blue
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Gray
Recalled BBWOO Baby Lounger in Red
Location of Tracking Label with Model Number
Tracking label with model number About the U.S. CPSC

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from September 2025 through November 2025 for about $23.

Distributors.Name: 91440300MA5HFP8E78, dba LSY Direct, of China

Hazards.Name: The loungers fail to meet safety standards for Infant Sleep Products, with low sides that cannot contain an infant and oversized openings that create fall and entrapment risks. Additionally, the lack of a stand presents a fall hazard when used on elevated surfaces, making these products unsafe for infant sleep.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the loungers immediately and reach out to LSY Direct for a full refund. They should also remove the foam and pads from the cover, then cut the cover, foam, and pad in half. To process the refund, consumers need to email photos of the destroyed items to bbwoorecall@163.com.

metaDescription: BBWOO baby loungers recalled due to entrapment and fall hazards that pose serious injury risks and fail infant sleep product safety standards.

Description: Curtis International has expanded its recall to include the Frigidaire 6-can minifridge model EFMIS121 due to fire and burn risks caused by electrical short-circuiting. This model is marked with the Frigidaire logo and specific serial numbers on the back label. The company has received multiple reports of these units catching fire, resulting in property damage.
Title: Curtis International Expands Recall of Frigidaire Minifridges Model EFMIS121 for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more details, consumers can reach out to Curtis International Ltd. at 888-727-0198 from 8 a.m. to 12 a.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@curtiscs.com, or visit www.recallrtr.com/minifridge.

Products.Name: Frigidaire 6-can Minifridge Model EFMIS121
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 330,000, Curtis International previously recalled 634,000 minifridges in July 2024

Images.Caption: Recalled Curtis International Minifridge Model EFMIS121 – serial numbers between A2001 to A2310

Injuries.Name: Curtis International has received at least six reports of the model EFMIS121 minifridges catching fire, with property damages.

Manufacturers.Name: ShangYu North Electron Manufacture Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Sold exclusively at Target stores nationwide and online at Target.com from January 2020 through October 2023 for around $30.

Importers.Name: Curtis International Ltd., of Canada

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burns due to short-circuiting electrical components

Remedies.Name: Stop using the minifridge, register for a refund, and dispose of the unit safely

metaDescription: Curtis International recalls Frigidaire minifridges model EFMIS121 due to fire and burn hazards from electrical short-circuiting. Stop use immediately.

Description: Energizer is recalling its Large Power Indicator Lanterns because the lithium-ion batteries can overheat, posing a burn hazard. The recall includes lanterns with model number BGAAL9 and specific SKU numbers, marked with a June 2024 date code on the base. The company reported one overheating incident but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Energizer Recalls Large Power Indicator Lanterns Due to Lithium-Ion Battery Overheating Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Energizer at 800-383-7323, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email customersupport@energizer.com or visit https://energizer.com/lightings/lanternrecall or www.energizer.com and select the "Lantern Recall" tab.

Images.Caption: Recalled Energizer® Large Power Indicator Lantern, model BGAAL9
Date code located on the base of recalled lantern

Injuries.Name: The firm reported one incident of the lithium-ion battery in a lantern overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Meijer, Blain, and Fleet Wholesale stores nationwide and online at Amazon.com between January and November 2025 for between $35 to $45. In addition, approximately 100 lanterns were distributed to reviewers through the Bazaarvoice marketing program.

Importers.Name: Energizer Holdings, Inc., of Clayton, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Overheating of lithium-ion batteries posing burn risks

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lantern and contact Energizer for a full refund

metaDescription: Energizer recalls Large Power Indicator Lanterns due to lithium-ion battery overheating risk that may cause burns. No injuries reported.

Description: Greatale has recalled self-feeding pillows that hold a bottle in front of an infant, posing a risk of aspiration and suffocation. The pillows keep the bottle at an unsafe angle, preventing infants from pulling away. These white pillows feature angel wing decorations and the Greatale brand name on the seam. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Greatale Recalls Self-Feeding Pillows Due to Aspiration and Suffocation Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Greatale via email at greatale-recall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Greatale Self-Feeding Pillow

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from September 2024 through July 2025 for between $10 and $20.

Importers.Name: Xiamen Weiqiaoerwangluokeji Co., Ltd., dba Greatale, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of aspiration and suffocation due to unsafe bottle positioning

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pillows immediately and contact Greatale for a full refund. Consumers must cut the pillow and send a photo of the destroyed item, including its tag, to greatale-recall@outlook.com. If the tag is missing, proof of purchase from Amazon can be provided.

metaDescription: Greatale recalls self-feeding pillows due to aspiration and suffocation hazards from unsafe bottle positioning. No injuries reported.

Description: Morgan Lane is recalling the Asher Pajama Sets for children because they do not comply with mandatory flammability standards, posing a serious fire hazard. The recalled pajamas feature a rose print with blue piping and imitation mother-of-pearl buttons and are available in sizes 2T to 14. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

Description: R.X.Y has recalled adult bicycle helmets that do not meet mandatory safety standards, increasing the risk of serious head injuries during accidents. The recalled helmets fit head sizes between 22.44 and 24.41 inches and have a blue and black design with gray padding and reflective straps. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: R.X.Y Adult Bicycle Helmets Recalled for Non-Compliance with Safety Standards and Head Injury Risk
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to R.X.Y via email at RXY-recall@outlook.com or visit their Amazon.com Seller Profile: R.X.Y.

Description: Ruahouine is recalling its Hair Growth Serum because the packaging does not meet mandatory child-resistant standards. The serum is sold in amber dropper bottles labeled with "Ruahouine" and "5% Minoxidil With Biotin." This non-compliant packaging creates a poisoning risk if accessed by children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

Description: Yixing Trading has recalled KEAWIS crib mattresses because they may not fit securely in some play yards or non-full-sized cribs. This can create gaps that pose entrapment and suffocation hazards to infants. The recalled mattresses are white, measure about 38 by 24 inches, and have model number XX-BM-01 on a side label. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Yixing Trading Recalls KEAWIS Crib Mattresses Due to Entrapment and Suffocation Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled mattresses and reach out to Yixing Trading for a complete refund. Contact them via email at support@keawis.com.

Model number printed on tracking label of recalled crib mattress. The importer printed on the tracking label may be an entity other than "Yixing Trading"

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from August 2024 through July 2025 for between $50 and $6.

Importers.Name: Yixing Trading of Richmond, Kentucky

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and Suffocation Risks

Remedies.Name: Refund Process for Recalled Mattresses

Description: Infant Innovations has recalled BabyBond retractable safety gates because of a risk that a child's torso can fit through the gap between the gate and the floor, posing an entrapment hazard. This condition violates mandatory safety standards for gates and enclosures. The gates are metal-framed, about 33 inches tall, and adjustable up to 55 inches wide. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Infant Innovations Recalls BabyBond Retractable Safety Gates for Entrapment Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please reach out to Infant Innovations at 844-406-3569 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at elsie@BabyBond-global.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled BabyBond retractable safety gate - black
Recalled BabyBond retractable safety gate - gray
Recalled BabyBond retractable safety gate - white
"Retractable Safety Gate" and model number "SH20.006DB" are printed on a white label stitched to the mesh fabric of the recalled safety gate

Retailers.Name: Online at Target.com from November 2024 through October 2025 for about $50.

Distributors.Name: Infant Innovations, dba BabyBond, of Sheridan, Wyoming

Hazards.Name: Entrapment risk due to a child's torso fitting through the gap between the gate and the floor, violating safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled gates immediately and contact Infant Innovations for return instructions to receive a full refund at any Target store.

Description: Isla Rae magnetic wireless chargers have been recalled due to fire and burn hazards caused by an explosion risk during use. The chargers, sold in multiple colors and identified by model number RM5PBM, contain lithium-ion batteries that pose a fire risk if not handled properly. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Chargers Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact TJX at 888-256-1564 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email powerbank@realtimeresults.net, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/powerbank. For Marshalls, visit https://m.marshalls.com/us/m/jump/topic/Product-Recalls/2400019 or Marshalls.com and scroll to the bottom for 'Product Recalls.' For T.J. Maxx, go to https://m.tjmaxx.tjx.com/m/jump/topic/product-recalls/2400019 or TJmaxx.com and check the bottom of the page for 'Product Recalls.'

Products.Name: Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 13,200 (In addition, about 7,000 units were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger (white)
Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger (pink)
Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger (purple)
Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger Model: RM5PBM

Retailers.Name: Marshalls and T.J. Maxx stores nationwide between June 2024 and November 2025 for $15.

Importers.Name: Hello to Green dba Press Play Products of Bell, California

Distributors.Name: The TJX Companies Inc., of Framingham, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Explosion risk during use leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the chargers immediately and visit https://www.recallrtr.com/powerbank to register for the recall and receive a full refund. After registration, dispose of the charger according to local regulations and do not throw it in the household trash.

Description: Joyful Journeys has recalled its baby loungers because they pose entrapment and fall hazards that do not meet infant sleep product safety standards. The loungers include a foam sleeping pad, padded bumpers, and a cloth cover with a zipper. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Joyful Journeys Recalls Baby Loungers for Entrapment and Fall Hazards Violating Infant Sleep Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Joyful Journeys via email at joyfuljourneysrecalls@myjoyfuljourneys.com or visit https://myjoyfuljourneys.com and click on "Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Joyful Journeys Baby Lounger in blue zoo animals
Recalled Joyful Journeys Baby Lounger in green minky
Recalled Joyful Journeys Baby Lounger in white minky
Recalled Joyful Journeys Baby Lounger in green avocado
Recalled Joyful Journeys Baby Lounger – Bag Mode
Recalled Joyful Journeys Baby Lounger Styles
"Joyful Journeys" can be found on a tag stitched on the outside of the lounger cover

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com from December 2023 through October 2025 for between $40 and $60.

Distributors.Name: Sarnia Sales LLC, dba Joyful Journeys, of Issaquah, Washington

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and fall hazards due to non-compliance with infant sleep product safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the loungers immediately and contact Joyful Journeys for a full refund. Dispose of the foam and pads by cutting them in half and send photos of the destroyed items to joyfuljourneysrecalls@myjoyfuljourneys.com.

Description: Kalencom Corporation is recalling SARO Braided Crib Bumpers, Model 1374, in salmon, gray, and mint colors. These bumpers violate the federal ban on crib bumpers because they pose a suffocation risk by obstructing an infant's breathing. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Kalencom Recalls SARO Braided Crib Bumpers for Suffocation Hazard Violating Federal Ban
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Kalencom Corporation at 800-344-6699 from 9 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.kalencombaby.com/recall. Alternatively, they can visit www.kalencombaby.com and click on the recall tab.

Images.Caption: Recalled SARO Braided Crib Bumpers in gray, mint and salmon
Location of product tags showing "Ref. 1374"

Retailers.Name: MacroBaby, of Orlando, Florida and online at Kalencombaby.com and Target.com from May 2024 through October 2025 for about $60.

Importers.Name: The Kalencom Corporation, of New Orleans, Louisiana

Hazards.Name: Risk of suffocation due to obstruction of infant's breathing, violating the federal crib bumper ban.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the crib bumpers immediately and request a full refund. Those who purchased in-store at MacroBaby in Orlando, Florida, can return the item for a refund. Online purchasers should email info@kalencom.com with the subject line 'Crib Bumper Recall - Refund Request,' including their name, phone number, and a photo of the crib bumper cut in half as proof of destruction.

metaDescription: Kalencom recalls SARO Braided Crib Bumpers due to suffocation hazard from obstructed infant breathing, violating federal crib bumper regulations.

Description: NFSVLB is recalling baby bath seats that fail to meet mandatory safety standards, posing risks of drowning and entrapment. The seats can tip over or allow a child to slip through the leg openings, creating a serious hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: NFSVLB Baby Bath Seats Recalled for Drowning and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled bath seats immediately and contact NFSVLB via email at amzmaihuayd@163.com for a full refund.

Images.Caption: Recalled NFSVLB Baby Bath Seat - blue
Label on the side of the recalled baby bath seat

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from May 2025 through October 2025 for between $25 and $40.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Maihua Trading Co., Ltd., dba NFSVLB, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of drowning and entrapment due to instability and improper leg openings.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use, request a refund, mark the seat as 'Recalled', disassemble it, and send a photo to NFSVLB.

metaDescription: NFSVLB recalls baby bath seats due to drowning and entrapment hazards from instability and improper leg openings. No injuries reported.

Description: PNW Components is recalling the Loam Pedal Gen 2 aluminum alloy mountain bike pedals due to a risk of axle cracking that can cause the pedal to detach, posing a crash hazard. The pedals feature a 'Tectonic' logo and come in multiple colors. Four reports of axle cracking and detachment have been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: PNW Components Recalls Loam Pedal Gen 2 Aluminum Alloy Bicycle Pedals Due to Crash Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to PNW Components, Inc. at 833-902-3381 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, via email at info@pnwcomponents.com, or visit their website at https://www.pnwcomponents.com/pages/voluntary-recall-loam-pedal-gen-2. Alternatively, visit www.pnwcomponents.com and click the Recall link in the footer.

Products.Name: Loam Pedal Gen 2 Aluminum Alloy Mountain Bike Pedals

Images.Caption: Recalled Loam Pedal Gen 2 aluminum alloy bicycle pedal
Recalled Loam Pedals Gen 2 aluminum alloy bicycle pedals

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of the pedal axle cracking and detaching from the crank. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle dealers and online through PNWComponents.com, REI.com, Backcountry.com, and JensonUSA.com from June 2025 to November 2025, priced around $160 per pair.

Importers.Name: PNW Components, Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Risk of pedal detachment due to axle cracking, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the Loam Pedal Gen 2 immediately and contact PNW Components for free replacement axles when available. In the meantime, PNW Components will provide the Range Pedal at no cost. Replacement axles are expected to start shipping on January 5, 2026.

metaDescription: PNW Components recalls Loam Pedal Gen 2 bicycle pedals due to risk of axle cracking and detachment causing a crash hazard.

Description: Rattan is recalling its 6-Drawer Dressers, model YD001, because they can tip over if not anchored to the wall, creating a risk of serious injury or death. The dressers have a natural wood finish with woven rattan drawer fronts and gold handles. This recall addresses violations of mandatory safety standards for clothing storage units. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Rattan 6-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please email Rattan at FUQARHYrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Rattan 6-Drawer Dresser - Front
Recalled Rattan 6-Drawer Dresser - Back
Recalled Rattan 6-Drawer Dresser - Side

Manufacturers.Name: Fuzhou Sunrise Creation Corporation Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from September 2025 through November 2025 for approximately $150.

Distributors.Name: Ningbo Honghui E-Commerce Co., Ltd., dba Rattan, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment leading to potential serious injuries or death.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the dressers if they are not wall-anchored, and keep them away from children. Mark the dressers as 'RECALLED' with a permanent marker, take photos, and send them to FUQARHYrecall@outlook.com. Dispose of the product according to local waste guidelines.

metaDescription: Rattan recalls 6-Drawer Dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that pose serious injury risks if not properly anchored.

Description: Agrish has recalled its adult portable bed rails, including models 2512, 2513, and 2516, due to risks of entrapment and asphyxiation. These bed rails do not meet mandatory safety standards. The recall follows a report of a consumer fall involving one of the recalled models.
Title: Agrish Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled for Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Agrish Direct at 800-613-5998 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at agrish.us@outlook.com. Additional information is available online at http://www.agrish.online/recall or by visiting www.agrish.online and selecting 'More' followed by 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Agrish Adult Portable Bed Rail Model Number 2512
Recalled Agrish Adult Portable Bed Rail Model Number 2513
Recalled Agrish Adult Portable Bed Rail Model Number 2516

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a consumer fall involving an Agrish adult bed rail with model number 251.

Manufacturers.Name: Zhongshan Lebo Medical Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from April 2025 to October 2025, priced between $50 and $80.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Yunshangqinyang E-commerce Ltd. dba Agrish Direct, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risks due to violation of mandatory safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bed rails immediately and contact Agrish Direct for a full refund. Visit http://www.agrish.online/recall to register for the recall and get disposal instructions.

Description: RH is recalling its Natural Antler Chandeliers, available in 48" and 60" diameters, due to an impact hazard caused by canopy hardware failure. The hardware can break, causing the chandelier to detach and fall. The firm has received reports of hardware breaking and chandeliers falling, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: RH Recalls Natural Antler Chandeliers Due to Impact Hazard from Canopy Hardware Failure
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact RH toll-free at 833-784-1852, available from 6 a.m. to 7 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, and 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekends. You can also reach them via email at recall@rh.com or visit https://rh.com/us/en/customer-experience/safety-recalls for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled RH 48” Natural Antler Chandelier
Recalled RH 60” Natural Antler Chandelier

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of canopy hardware components breaking and chandeliers detaching, including one report in which the chandelier fell to the floor. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: RH galleries and online at https://rh.com/us/en/ from September 2021 to October 2024, and RH Outlets nationwide from September 2021 to September 2025, priced between $1,700 and $7,700.

Distributors.Name: RH US, LLC, of Corte Madera, California

Hazards.Name: Impact hazard due to chandelier detachment

Remedies.Name: Free in-home repair to replace canopy hardware

Description: Sangohe has recalled its adult portable bed rails due to risks of entrapment and asphyxiation linked to non-compliance with mandatory safety standards. The recalled product is a black, trapezoidal bed rail with an extendable handle and mesh storage pocket, identified by model number KDB504A01FT. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

Description: Amp Fit has recalled the AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machine, a wall-mounted device used for full-body resistance workouts. The machine's adjustable arm may fail to lock securely, causing unexpected swinging that poses a risk of lacerations and other injuries. Reports include lacerations to the head and bruising to the shoulder and body.
Title: Amp Fit Recalls AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machine for Laceration and Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled fitness machine immediately. For assistance, contact Amp Fit at 888-708-9560, available from Monday to Sunday, 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT. Alternatively, reach out via email at support@ampfit.com or visit http://joinamp.com/blog/product-safety-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled MP2 Model of Amp Smart Fitness Machine
Recalled MP2 Model of Amp Smart Fitness Machine

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of the arm on the fitness machine not locking properly, resulting in lacerations to the head and bruising to the shoulder and other body.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Ampfit.com from January 2025 to August 2025 for approximately $1,800

Importers.Name: Amp Fit, Inc., of Middletown, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration or serious injury due to improper locking of the arm

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and repair by an authorized technician at the consumer's residence

metaDescription: Amp Fit recalls the AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machine due to a laceration and serious injury hazard from an arm that may not lock properly.

Description: ANNQUAN has recalled two power strip models, EX-D112-05 and EX-D106-25, due to a fire hazard caused by the absence of overcurrent protection. The defect could result in serious injury or death from fire. Seven reports of blown fuses have been received, but no fires or injuries have been reported.
Title: ANNQUAN Power Strips Recalled for Fire Hazard Due to Lack of Overcurrent Protection
ConsumerContact: For further details, contact Hefei Juyuan Sporting Development at 401-998-3366 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall_annquan@163.com. More information is also available online at https://annquanrecall.com/.

Images.Caption: Recalled ANNQUAN Power Strip Model EX-D112-05
Recalled ANNQUAN Power Strip Model EX-D106-25

Injuries.Name: Hefei Juyuan Sporting Development has received seven reports of the power strips blowing a fuse. No fires or injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from December 2023 to October 2025, priced between $15 and $40.

Distributors.Name: Hefei Juyuan Sporting Development Co., Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to lack of overcurrent protection, potentially leading to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the power strips immediately and contact Hefei Juyuan Sporting Development for a full refund.

Description: Currey & Company is recalling Electra and Electra Three Tier Chandeliers because they may fall unexpectedly due to a defective ceiling mount connection. These chandeliers, made of glass, iron, and brass, pose an impact injury hazard if they detach. The company has received reports of the fixtures falling, including one case of property damage, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Currey & Company Recalls Electra Chandeliers Due to Falling Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Currey & Company at 800-899-7047 from 8:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@curreyco.com, or visit https://www.curreyandcompany.com/legalities/recall/ for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Electra Chandelier, Model No. 9000-1234
Recalled Electra Three Tier Chandelier, Model No. 9000-1235

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the chandelier light fixtures falling from the ceiling, with one incident involving property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Currey & Company showrooms and various lighting stores nationwide, as well as online at www.curreyandcompany.com and other home e-commerce retailers

Importers.Name: Currey & Company, Inc., of Atlanta, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury from falling due to improper ceiling mount connection

Remedies.Name: Free in-home repair kit and professional installation reimbursement

metaDescription: Currey & Company recalls Electra Chandeliers due to risk of falling from faulty ceiling mount connection, posing an impact injury hazard.

Description: Feel The Beard is recalling its Minoxidil Beard Growth Oil because the packaging does not comply with child-resistant standards. The product contains 5% minoxidil with biotin and is sold in a one-ounce dark amber bottle with a black dropper cap. This packaging flaw creates a serious risk of poisoning if accessed by children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Feel The Beard Recalls Minoxidil Beard Growth Oil for Child Poisoning Hazard Due to Non-Child-Resistant Packaging
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please email Feel The Beard at ziyad@feelthebeard.com.

Products.Name: Feel The Beard Minoxidil Beard Growth Oil

Images.Caption: Recalled Feel The Beard Growth Oil Bottle
Recalled Feel The Beard Growth Oil Bottle (side view)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from April 2025 to September 2025 for approximately $10.

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging leading to potential poisoning risks for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the recalled product away from children and contact Feel The Beard for disposal instructions and a replacement.

metaDescription: Feel The Beard recalls Beard Growth Oil because its packaging is not child-resistant, posing a poisoning hazard to children if ingested.

Description: Furnulem is recalling its 8-Drawer Dressers because they pose a risk of tip-over and entrapment, which can cause serious injury or death. The dressers do not comply with the STURDY Act's mandatory safety requirements. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Furnulem 8-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the dressers immediately if they are not secured to a wall. For further assistance, contact Furnulem at 310-227-9560 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email support@furnulem.com, or visit https://furnulem.com/pages/voluntary-safety-recall for more details.

Recalled Furnulem Fabric 8-Drawer Dresser in black (back)
Recalled Furnulem Fabric 8-Drawer Dresser Packaging with Model Number DS-0148D00

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from September 2023 through November 2025 for approximately $80.

Distributors.Name: Shanghai Taiye Furniture Co., Ltd., dba Furnulem, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment, potentially leading to serious injuries or death, especially for children. These dressers do not comply with the STURDY Act's mandatory safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the dressers if they are not wall-anchored and keep them out of children's reach. Contact Furnulem for disposal instructions to receive a full refund. A photo demonstrating the disposal must be sent to support@furnulem.com.

Description: JOKOSIS has recalled its Portable Bed Rails, model HC0262, because they pose serious entrapment and asphyxiation hazards. The product does not meet mandatory safety standards for adult portable bed rails. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: JOKOSIS Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled for Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, email JOKOSIS at jokosisbedrailrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled JOKOSIS Bed Rail (front view)
Recalled JOKOSIS Bed Rail (back view)
Recalled JOKOSIS Bed Rail (top view)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from August 2025 to November 2025, priced between $40 and $50.

Distributors.Name: Guangshui Xie Lan Zheng Trading Co., Ltd., dba JOKOSIS, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation hazards due to non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the bed rails immediately and contact JOKOSIS for a refund. Mark the bed rails with 'RECALLED' using a permanent marker, photograph the marked rails, and email the image to jokosisbedrailrecall@outlook.com. Finally, dispose of the product according to local disposal regulations.

metaDescription: JOKOSIS recalls Portable Bed Rails due to entrapment and asphyxiation hazards from non-compliance with safety standards. No injuries reported.

Description: KKL Fabric 9-Drawer Dressers have been recalled because they pose tip-over and entrapment hazards if not anchored to the wall. These dressers do not meet mandatory safety standards and can cause serious injury or death, especially to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

Description: Pacific Cycle has recalled the Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carrier due to a potential fall hazard caused by buckle failure. The carrier attaches to a rear bike rack and features black harness straps with red buckles. One incident of buckle release is known, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Pacific Cycle Recalls Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carriers for Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled carriers and reach out to Pacific Cycle at 877-564-2261 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, via email at customerservice@pacific-cycle.com, or visit www.pacific-cycle.com/safety-notices-recalls/ for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carrier, Model SW80356
Recalled Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carrier, Model SW80356 attached to rear bike rack (sold with carrier)

Injuries.Name: Pacific Cycle is aware of one incident where the buckles released. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com and schwinnbikes.com from May 2025 to September 2025, priced between $190 and $230

Importers.Name: Pacific Cycle Inc., of Madison, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to buckle failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the carrier and request a full refund from Pacific Cycle

metaDescription: Pacific Cycle recalls Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carriers due to buckle failure posing a fall hazard. No injuries reported.

Description: Pedego Electric Bikes is recalling its Fat Tire Trike adult three-wheeled electric bicycles due to potential hairline fractures in the aluminum frame. These fractures can cause the frame to break, posing fall and laceration hazards. The recall follows reports of frame failures, including one injury involving a leg laceration.
Title: Pedego Recalls Fat Tire Trikes Due to Frame Fracture Risk Causing Falls and Lacerations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Pedego at 800-646-8604 from 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@pedego.com, or visit https://pedegoelectricbikes.com/pages/product-recalls for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pedego Fat Tire Trike

Injuries.Name: Pedego has received 16 reports of the trike frame breaking, including one report of a laceration to the leg.

Retailers.Name: Available at Pedego stores across the country and online at PedegoElectricBikes.com from March 2024 to March 2025, priced between $3,500 and $4,000.

Importers.Name: Pedego, LLC, of Fountain View, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and laceration due to potential hairline fractures in the trike frame.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled Fat Tire Trikes immediately and contact Pedego to arrange for a free repair, which includes replacing a section of the frame through a Pedego dealer.

metaDescription: Pedego recalls Fat Tire Trikes due to fall and laceration hazards from potential frame fractures. Free repairs are available for affected models.

Description: Plantimex is recalling Mamisan Lidocaine Ointment containers because the packaging does not meet mandatory child-resistant standards. This increases the risk of serious injury or death from accidental ingestion by children. No injuries have been reported. The recall includes a free replacement lid designed to secure the ointment and reduce the poisoning hazard.
Title: Plantimex Recalls Mamisan Lidocaine Ointment Containers for Child Poisoning Risk
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Plantimex at 855-752-6869 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email customercare@plantimexusa.com with the subject "RECALL". Visit http://plantimexusa.com/contact.php or www.plantimexusa.com for further assistance.

Distributors.Name: Plantimex Distributors, Inc., of San Diego, California

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging posing a poisoning risk to children

Remedies.Name: Free replacement lid for secure packaging

Description: 17 Stories Furniture is recalling its 18-Drawer Dressers because they pose tip-over and entrapment hazards if not anchored to a wall. The dressers have a metal frame, wooden top, and 18 collapsible fabric drawers, available in black, brown, and gray. These products do not comply with mandatory safety standards and can become unstable, creating a risk of serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: 17 Stories Furniture 18-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact 17 Stories Furniture via email at Drawer18Recall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled 17 Stories Furniture Dresser in black (front)
Recalled 17 Stories Furniture Dresser in black (side)
Recalled 17 Stories Furniture Dresser in black (back)

Manufacturers.Name: Jiangsu Quanronghe Smart Home Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Wayfair.com from September 2023 to November 2025 for approximately $100.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Jinhongzhen Technology Co., Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment if not anchored, which can cause serious injuries or fatalities.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the dressers if they are not anchored, keep them out of children's reach, and contact 17 Stories Furniture for disposal instructions to obtain a full refund. A photo showing the disposal of the product must be sent to Drawer18Recall@outlook.com.

Description: Luyuan is recalling the X-Pro Eagle 40cc and FRP Sahara 40cc Youth ATVs because they fail to meet mandatory safety standards. The ATVs have mechanical suspension defects and handlebar designs that can cause lacerations during crashes, creating a serious injury or death hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Luyuan Recalls X-Pro Eagle and FRP Sahara 40cc Youth ATVs for Crash Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Luyuan at 855-663-2121, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at luyuanusa@gmail.com or visit www.luyuanusa.com and click on "Recall" for further details.

Importers.Name: Luyuan Inc., of City of Industry, California

Description: Primark is recalling the Little Bear 2-pack Soother Clips because the wooden button can detach, exposing a sharp screw that poses choking and laceration hazards. There have been reports of the button detaching, including incidents where the screw was found in an infant's mouth and a child sustained cuts. These clips are designed to attach pacifiers to children's clothing but present safety risks.
Title: Primark Recalls Little Bear 2-Pack Soother Clips for Choking and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Primark at 617-946-3236 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or visit www.primark.com/recall. You can also access details by clicking 'Recalls' at the bottom of the Primark homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Primark Little Bear 2-pack Soother Clip
Recalled Primark Little Bear 2-pack Soother Clip (product code location)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the wooden buttons on the soother clip detaching, including one report where the screw was found inside an infant's mouth and a second report of cuts to a child's arm.

Retailers.Name: Primark stores located in the northeast and in Florida, Maryland and Illinois from June 2025 through August 2025 for about $5.

Importers.Name: Primark US Corp., of Boston, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Choking and laceration hazards due to the potential detachment of the wooden button, exposing a sharp screw.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the soother clips immediately and return them to any Primark store for a full refund in the original payment method.

metaDescription: Primark recalls Little Bear Soother Clips due to choking and laceration hazards from detachable wooden buttons with exposed screws.

Description: SKCAIHT is recalling children's Mermaid costumes because they contain a banned phthalate that poses a health risk if ingested. The costumes include a colorful dress, headband, and mermaid tail designed for size XL children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: SKCAIHT Mermaid Children's Costumes Recalled for Phthalate Exposure Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please reach out to SKCAIHT Direct via email at pafiney@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled SKCAIHT Children's Mermaid Costume

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2022 through July 2025 for approximately $30.

Distributors.Name: Yiwu Qiangyao E-Commerce Co., Ltd., dba SKCAIHT Direct, of China

Hazards.Name: Presence of a toxic phthalate that violates federal regulations, posing health risks if ingested by children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the costumes immediately and keep them away from children. Contact SKCAIHT Direct for a full refund. Consumers should cut the dress and send a photo of the destroyed costume to pafiney@outlook.com.

metaDescription: SKCAIHT recalls children's Mermaid costumes due to hazardous phthalate levels that violate federal safety standards posing health risks.

Description: Uuoeebb Infant Walkers have been recalled because they do not meet mandatory safety standards, posing risks of falls and head entrapment. The walkers can pass through standard doorways and fail to stop at stairs, increasing the risk of serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Uuoeebb Infant Walkers Recalled for Fall and Entrapment Hazards Violating Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact BaoD via email at Uuoeebbrecalls@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Uuoeebb Infant Walker in gray
"Production Batch: 7654" on tracking label under the seat

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from December 2024 through September 2025 for between $60 and $90.

Distributors.Name: Hunan Suihuo E-commerce Co., Ltd., dba BaoD, of China

Hazards.Name: These infant walkers do not comply with safety standards, allowing them to pass through standard doorways and failing to stop at stairs, which can lead to serious falls. Additionally, the design permits a child's legs to slip down, risking head entrapment and potential injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled walkers immediately and reach out to BaoD for a full refund. They should disassemble the walker, remove the fabric seat, label the tray with 'Recalled' using a permanent marker, and send a photo of the recalled product to BaoD at Uuoeebbrecalls@outlook.com. Proper disposal of the walkers should follow local waste management regulations.

metaDescription: Uuoeebb Infant Walkers recalled due to fall and entrapment hazards that violate safety standards. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: Sanven Technology is recalling VEVOR Garment Steamers because the water tank cap may leak, detach, or fail to stay closed, causing hot water to spill or spit. These defects have led to reports of serious burn injuries, including permanent scarring and second-degree burns. The steamers also pose a risk of minor steam burns despite the included glove. Consumers should stop using the product immediately due to the burn hazard.
Title: Sanven Technology Recalls VEVOR Garment Steamers Due to Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email recalling@vevor.com. More details are available at vevor.com/pages/vevor-steamer-recall or by visiting www.vevor.com and selecting 'Product Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer – side view
Recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer – front view
Recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer – handle view
Label on underside of recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer
Glove sold with recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer

Injuries.Name: The Firm has received six reports of hot water spitting, pouring out, leaking or dripping from the steamers, and 12 reports of issues with the water tank cap detaching, leaking, not staying closed, fitting loosely, or melting. The reported cap issues resulted in two burn injuries, including one consumer who received a permanent scar and a second-degree burn from the hot water, after the steamer's cap suddenly detached and one consumer who received a minor burn on their hand and fingers from leaked water. Additionally, there is one report of a minor steam burn through the provided glove.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Vevor.com, Amazon.com, and HomeDepot.com from December 2023 to September 2025, priced between $10 and $33.

Distributors.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., dba VEVOR, of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious burn injuries from leaking or spitting hot water; water tank cap may leak or detach.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled steamers immediately and contact Sanven Technology for a full refund. They will need to provide their contact details and send a photo of the steamer with its unplugged cut power cord.

metaDescription: Sanven Technology recalls VEVOR Garment Steamers due to risk of serious burn injuries from leaking or spitting hot water and faulty water tank caps.

Description: Sanven Technology is recalling Vevor ice crushers, including models BY-300 and BY-300 FS, due to a fire hazard risk. These home-use machines, marketed as ice shavers, crushers, and snow cone makers, have caused thermal events, including one fire. The recall addresses potential safety issues related to overheating during operation.
Title: Sanven Technology Recalls Vevor Ice Crushers Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at recalling@vevor.com. Additional information is available online at https://www.vevor.com/pages/vevor-ice-shaver-recall or by visiting www.vevor.com and selecting 'Product Recall.'

Products.Name: Vevor Ice Crushers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 11,650 (In addition, about 1,060 were sold in Canada)

Injuries.Name: Sanven has received two reports of thermal events, including one fire.

Retailers.Name: Online at Vevor.com, Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Wayfair.com, HomeDepot.com and Lowes.com from July 2021 through July 2025 for between $40 and $86.

Distributors.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., dba Vevor, of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential thermal events

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ice crusher and contact Sanven for a full refund. Email a photo of the ice crusher with the unplugged power cord cut in half to recalling@vevor.com.

Description: TREK is recalling several 2026 electric bicycle models because loose chainring bolts can cause the chainring to separate, increasing the risk of falls or crashes. The affected models include Domane+ ALR 5, Domane+ ALR 6 AXS, Checkpoint+ SL 6, and Checkpoint+ SL 7. TREK has received reports of the chainring coming loose but no injuries have been reported. The recall addresses this safety hazard to prevent potential accidents.
Title: TREK Recalls 2026 Electric Bicycles Due to Loose Chainring Bolts Causing Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected bicycles immediately and contact Trek at 800-373-4594 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/recalls or by visiting https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/ and clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled MY26 Domane+ ALR 5 Electric Bicycle
Recalled MY26 Domane+ ALR 6 AXS Electric Bicycle
Recalled MY26 Checkpoint+ SL 6 Electric Bicycle
Recalled MY26 Checkpoint+ SL7 Electric Bicycle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the bicycle chainring coming loose on the recalled bikes. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle retailers and authorized Trek retailers nationwide and online from July 2025 through September 2025, priced between $5,000 and $8,000.

Importers.Name: Trek Bicycle Corporation, of Waterloo, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Loose chainring bolts may lead to separation, posing a fall or crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the bicycles and contact an authorized Trek dealer for a complimentary repair. The dealer will ensure the chainring bolts are properly tightened. Additionally, participants in the recall will receive a $20 in-store credit for Trek, Electra, or Bontrager products, valid until December 31, 2026.

metaDescription: TREK recalls 2026 electric bicycles due to loose chainring bolts that pose a fall hazard. No injuries reported in connection with this issue.

Description: YCXXKJ baby bath seats, sold in multiple colors and identified by model YD-1958, are recalled for posing a serious drowning hazard. The seats fail to meet mandatory safety standards, making them unstable and prone to tipping during use. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: YCXXKJ Baby Bath Seats Recalled for Drowning Hazard Due to Instability
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BenTalk at 213-579-2588 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or email bentalkhere@outlook.com.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to drowning from unstable bath seats.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bath seats and request a full refund from BenTalk. Send two photos of the bath seat to bentalkhere@outlook.com: one with 'Recalled' marked on the front and another showing the seat disassembled.

Description: INIU is recalling its 10,000mAh BI-B41 portable power banks because of fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The recalled units have black or blue casings with the INIU logo and a paw-print LED light, and specific serial numbers printed on the back. Reports include incidents of overheating, fires, minor burn injuries, and property damage. Consumers should stop using these power banks immediately.
Title: INIU Recalls 10,000mAh BI-B41 Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach INIU at 888-886-3606 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@iniu.shop. More information is available online at https://iniushop.com/pages/recall-b41# or by visiting https://iniushop.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the top.

Images.Caption: Recalled INIU Power Bank – model BI-B41

Injuries.Name: INIU has received 15 reports of power banks overheating, including 11 reports of fires that resulted in three minor burn injuries and property damage totaling over $380,000.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Topstar Industry Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Amazon.com between August 2021 and April 2022 for approximately $18.

Distributors.Name: INIU, of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the power banks and contact INIU for disposal guidance.

metaDescription: INIU recalls 10,000mAh BI-B41 power banks due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Check model and serial numbers for safety.

Reports include incidents of overheating, fires, minor burn injuries, and property damage. Consumers should stop using these power banks immediately.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach INIU at 888-886-3606 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@iniu.shop. More information is available online at https://iniushop.com/pages/recall-b41# or by visiting https://iniushop.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the top.
en.metaDescription: INIU recalls 10,000mAh BI-B41 power banks due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Check model and serial numbers for safety.

Images.Caption: Recalled INIU Power Bank – model BI-B41

Injuries.Name: INIU has received 15 reports of power banks overheating, including 11 reports of fires that resulted in three minor burn injuries and property damage totaling over $380,000.

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Amazon.com between August 2021 and April 2022 for approximately $18.

Distributors.Name: INIU, of Seattle, Washington

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Topstar Industry Co., Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the power banks and contact INIU for disposal guidance.

Description: Casaottima has recalled its 13-Drawer Dressers due to risk of tip-over and entrapment hazards that could cause serious injury or death. These dressers feature a metal frame, wooden top, and 13 collapsible fabric drawers. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Casaottima 13-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can contact Casaottima via email at support@casaottima.store or visit https://casaottima-store.com/ and select "Recall" at the top of the page.

Description: Cubimana Treehouse building toy sets are recalled because the battery compartment in the LED light is accessible to children, posing a risk of battery ingestion. Ingesting button cell batteries can cause severe internal injuries or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Cubimana Treehouse Building Toy Sets Recalled for Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Vatos Toys via email at shein.us@vatostoys.com for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled Cubimana Treehouse Building Set
Recalled Cubimana Treehouse Building Set - brown building piece with a LED light that uses a button cell battery
Recalled Cubimana Treehouse Building Set - front of the packaging
Recalled Cubimana Treehouse Building Set - back of the packaging

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Shein.com from July 2025 through October 2025 for approximately $20.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Excellence Qichuang Technology, dba Vatos Toys, of China

Hazards.Name: The toy sets do not meet mandatory safety standards, as the battery compartment in the LED light is accessible to children. Ingesting button cell or coin batteries can lead to severe injuries, internal chemical burns, or even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled toy sets immediately, keep them away from children, and safely remove and dispose of the batteries. To obtain a full refund, contact Vatos Toys. Additionally, consumers should discard the LED light piece and send a photo of it in the trash to shein.us@vatostoys.com. Note: Button cell and coin batteries are dangerous and should be disposed of or recycled according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

metaDescription: Cubimana treehouse toy sets recalled due to risk of serious injury from accessible button cell batteries posing ingestion hazard to children.

Description: KTEBO is recalling Writing Tablet Toys because the battery compartment screws can detach, exposing batteries that pose a risk of serious injury or death if swallowed. The toys, sold in pairs with matching styluses, do not comply with mandatory toy safety standards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys Recalled for Battery Ingestion Hazard Due to Unsafe Battery Compartments
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact KTEBO at writing-tablet-recall@ktebo.com.

Description: Ningbo Tianqi Electronic has recalled FUFU&GAGA queen size Murphy wall beds with a white wood frame due to impact, crush, and laceration hazards. The 215-pound wall-mounted beds can fall during assembly or disassembly if not anchored to the wall, posing serious injury risks. The recall follows reports of injuries including lacerations and fractures caused by the beds falling. Consumers should be aware of these safety risks associated with the product.
Title: Ningbo Tianqi Electronic Recalls FUFU&GAGA Murphy Wall Beds Due to Impact and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Ningbo Tianqi Electronic at 208-820-0799 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or via email at adelahu@fufugaga.com or support@fufugaga.zendesk.com. More information is also available online at www.fufugaga.com under the "RECALL INFORMATION" section.

Products.Name: FUFU&GAGA Queen Size Murphy Wall Bed

Images.Caption: FUFU&GAGA White Wood Frame Queen Size Murphy Wall Bed (unfolded)
FUFU&GAGA White Wood Frame Queen Size Murphy Wall Bed (folded)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the wall beds falling during assembly when the wall beds were not anchored to the wall, resulting in two injured consumers. One consumer sustained a laceration, a bruise, and a fractured growth plate in her finger, and another consumer sustained a laceration to his face that required eight stitches.

Manufacturers.Name: Jiangsu Liangjiang Intelligent Furniture Co., Ltd, of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at HomeDepot.com and Lowes.com from January 2025 to April 2025 for approximately $550.

Importers.Name: Ningbo Tianqi Electronic Inc., of Denver, Colorado

Hazards.Name: Risk of impact, crush, and laceration injuries during assembly or disassembly

Remedies.Name: Stop using the wall beds and contact Ningbo Tianqi Electronic for a full refund, including a disassembly kit with necessary tools and instructions.

metaDescription: FUFU&GAGA Murphy wall beds recalled for impact and laceration hazards during assembly or disassembly. Heavy frame poses injury risks if not anchored properly.

Description: Primark is recalling Water Balloon Pumps because they can rupture under pressure, posing a risk of cuts and physical injury. The pumps, sold in blue and pink, have caused reported injuries to users' hands and fingers. The recall addresses safety concerns related to product failure during use.
Title: Primark Recalls Water Balloon Pumps Due to Risk of Rupture and Injury
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Primark at 617-946-3236 between 8 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, visit www.primark.com/recall or navigate to www.primark.com and select 'Recall' at the bottom of the page. You can also fill out the Primark Contact Us form for inquiries or to request a callback from Customer Service.

Images.Caption: Recalled Primark Water Balloon Pump (front)
Recalled Primark Water Balloon Pump (back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports in the United States (and nine reports internationally) of the water balloon pumps rupturing, resulting in two reports of cuts on users' hands and fingers in the United States (and nine reports of cuts internationally).

Retailers.Name: Primark stores in the northeast and in Florida, Maryland and Illinois from March 2023 through July 2023 for about $6.

Importers.Name: Primark US Corp., of Boston, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of physical injury due to excessive pressure causing product rupture

Remedies.Name: Return to Primark for a full refund in original payment method

metaDescription: Primark recalls Water Balloon Pumps due to risk of rupture causing cuts and injury. Consumers should stop use immediately to avoid physical harm.

Description: Little Pea Shop has recalled two styles of padded crib bumpers due to a suffocation hazard that violates federal crib bumper regulations. The recalled products include the Waffle Bedding Accessory and Plush Crib Bumper styles, which pose a risk of obstructing an infant’s breathing. No injuries have been reported in connection with these products.
Title: Little Pea Shop Recalls Crib Bumpers for Suffocation Hazard Violating Federal Ban
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Little Pea Shop via email at support@littlepeashop.com or visit https://littlepeashop.com/pages/recall. Additional details can be found on their website by clicking "Recall" at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Little Pea Shop Waffle Bedding Accessory Crib Bumpers

Images.Caption: Recalled Little Pea Shop Crib Bumpers – "Waffle Bedding Accessory" in gray
Recalled Little Pea Shop Crib Bumpers - "Waffle Bedding Accessory"
Recalled Little Pea Shop Crib Bumper - "Plush Crib Bumper"

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from September 2025 through October 2025, priced between $8 and $30.

Distributors.Name: Little Pea Shop, of France

Hazards.Name: Risk of suffocation due to obstruction of an infant's breathing, creating an unsafe sleeping environment.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the crib bumpers immediately and contact Little Pea Shop for a full refund. Cut the padding from the bumpers and email a photo of the destroyed items to support@littlepeashop.com.

metaDescription: Little Pea Shop recalls crib bumpers due to suffocation hazard. These products violate federal crib bumper regulations and pose serious risks to infants.

Description: Rotinyard has recalled convertible baby high chairs sold in beige or pink that can convert into an infant swing and recline. The chairs lack an attached restraint system, posing a serious fall risk, and have a hazard of head entrapment between the seat and tray. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Rotinyard Convertible Baby High Chairs Recalled for Fall and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Rotinyard via email at trailblazeramz@outlook.com for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled High Chair in different modes
Tracking label on the seatback showing "Product No.: HA-028"

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from March 2025 through June 2025 for about $90.

Distributors.Name: Rotinyard,of Zhejiang, China

Hazards.Name: These high chairs do not meet mandatory safety standards due to the absence of an attached restraint system, creating a significant fall risk. Additionally, there is a danger of a child's head getting trapped between the seat and tray.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled high chairs immediately and reach out to Rotinyard for a complete refund. They should disassemble the chairs, cut the restraints and fabric seat cushion insert, and send photos of the destroyed items to trailblazeramz@outlook.com. A full refund will be processed upon receipt of the photos.

metaDescription: Rotinyard recalls convertible baby high chairs due to fall and entrapment hazards that violate mandatory safety standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Ebern Designs has recalled the Ojaswi 12-Drawer Dressers because they pose a tip-over and entrapment hazard. These dressers do not comply with mandatory safety standards, increasing the risk of serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Ebern Designs Recalls Ojaswi 12-Drawer Dressers for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ebern Designs via email at Ojaswi12Drecall@outlook.com.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Wayfair.com from January 2025 through October 2025 for approximately $75.

Hazards.Name: Unstable dressers pose a risk of tip-over and entrapment, potentially leading to serious injuries or fatalities, especially for children. This product does not meet the mandatory safety standards set by the STURDY Act.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease using the dressers if they are not wall-anchored and move them to an inaccessible area for children. Reach out to Ebern Designs for disposal instructions to obtain a full refund. A photo demonstrating the disposal must be sent to Ojaswi12Drecall@outlook.com.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease using the dressers if they are not wall-anchored and move them to an inaccessible area for children. Reach out to Ebern Designs for disposal instructions to obtain a full refund. A photo demonstrating the disposal must be sent to Ojaswi12Drecall@outlook.com.

Description: Ikuso has recalled its pressure-mounted safety gates because they pose a risk of entrapment that could cause serious injury or death to children. The gates are made of white steel and come in two adjustable sizes with model numbers Door-Gen1-123 and Door-Gen1-159. They are marked with the Ikuso label on the latch and model numbers on the side-post beneath the latch. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Ikuso Pressure-Mounted Safety Gates Recalled for Child Entrapment Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Ikuso via email at ikuso_door_gate@163.com for assistance.

Description: KingPavonini has recalled six models of adult portable bed rails because they pose risks of entrapment and asphyxiation. These products fail to meet mandatory safety standards designed to prevent serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: KingPavonini Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled for Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach KingPavonini at 888-281-8150 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email service@kingpavonini.com. Additional details are available online at https://www.kingpavonini.com.cn by clicking on "Recall" at the top of the page.
