Description: Ozark Trail Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stoves, model BG2247A1, are recalled due to risks of explosion, fire, and serious burn injuries. The stoves have caused multiple incidents, including reports of second-degree burns. These hazards present a significant safety risk to consumers.
Title: Ozark Trail Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stoves Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further information, contact Walmart at 800-925-6278, available from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT, or visit www.walmart.com/help or https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls.

Injuries.Name: China Window Industry Co., Ltd. has received 26 reports of the camping stoves exploding or catching fire, including 16 reports of injuries such as second-degree burns.

Hazards.Name: Risk of explosion or fire

Remedies.Name: Return the recalled stoves to any Walmart store for a full refund

Description: Sofoliana and Glotika baby loungers have been recalled due to entrapment and fall hazards. These products do not meet mandatory safety standards for infant sleep items, featuring low sides and wide openings that increase the risk of falls and entrapment. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Recall of Sofoliana and Glotika Baby Loungers for Entrapment and Fall Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further information, please contact Bosen US at BosenRecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sofoliana Baby Lounger
Recalled Glotika Baby Lounger in white-elephant
Recalled Glotika Baby Lounger in grey-rocket
Recalled Glotika Baby Lounger in pink-heart

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from August 2025 through October 2025. Sofoliana baby loungers were sold for between $30 and $40. Glotika baby loungers were sold for between $40 and $50.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Bosen Trading Co., Ltd., dba Bosen US, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and fall hazards due to low sides and wide openings, violating infant sleep product safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the loungers immediately and contact Bosen US for a full refund. Destroy the loungers by cutting the cover, foam, and pad in half, and send photos of the destroyed items to BosenRecall@outlook.com to receive your refund.

Description: Bearlala baby loungers, sold under the Granlass brand, have been recalled for failing to meet mandatory infant sleep product safety standards. The loungers have low sides and wide openings that increase the risk of infant falls and entrapment. The absence of a stand also creates hazards when used on elevated surfaces. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Bearlala Baby Loungers Recalled for Entrapment and Fall Hazards Violating Infant Sleep Product Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should stop using the loungers immediately and reach out to Nuoxuann via email at Recall-nuoxuann@outlook.com or visit http://callback.yankunwei.top for further instructions.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bearlala Baby Lounger Top View
Recalled Bearlala Baby Lounger Bottom View
"Bearlala" is printed on an attached label on the outside of the recalled baby lounger's cover

Retailers.Name: Online at Walmart.com from August 2025 through October 2025 for between$35 and$45.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshiruoxuandianzishangwuyouxiangongsi doing business as Nuoxuann, of China

Hazards.Name: These baby loungers do not meet mandatory safety standards for infant sleep products. Their sides are too low to safely contain an infant, and the openings at the foot are excessively wide, increasing risks of falls and entrapment. Additionally, the lack of a stand makes them hazardous when used on elevated surfaces, creating an unsafe sleeping environment that could lead to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the loungers and contact Nuoxuann for a full refund. They should remove the foam and pads from the cover, then cut the cover, foam, and pad in half. To receive a refund, consumers are required to email photos of the destroyed items to Recall-nuoxuann@outlook.com.

metaDescription: Bearlala baby loungers recalled for entrapment and fall hazards due to noncompliance with infant sleep product safety standards.

Description: Demlar has recalled MoonSoll and Magic Chems fuel bottles used with heating appliances due to a risk of flash fire. The bottles lack required flame mitigation devices and have misleading safety labels, violating federal standards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Demlar Recalls MoonSoll and Magic Chems Fuel Bottles for Flash Fire Risk and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Demlar at 943-212-3054 from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email info@demlar.com. More details can be found online at https://demlar.com/recall-1 or by visiting Demlar.com and clicking on the 'RECALL' link at the top of the page.

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire due to lack of flame mitigation devices and misleading safety claims.

Description: Little Partners is recalling Grow 'N Stow Folding Learning Towers due to a fall hazard caused by potential platform collapse. The wooden towers, identified by model LP01711, have been reported to collapse, resulting in at least one child injury. Consumers should be aware of the risk associated with continued use.
Title: Little Partners Recalls Grow 'N Stow Folding Learning Towers for Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Little Partners at 800-704-9058 from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, and 12 p.m. to 5 p.m. ET Saturday. You can also reach them via email at recall@LittlePartners.com or visit https://littlepartners.com/pages/product-recalls for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Grow 'N Stow Folding Learning Tower (front and folded views)
Recalled Grow 'N Stow Folding Learning Tower (model and date code location)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 14 reports of the folding towers collapsing, including one report of a child with bruises.

Retailers.Name: Walmart stores nationwide and online at Walmart.com, Amazon.com, Littlepartners.com, Etsy.com, Wayfair.com, Goodbyegear.com, Buybuybaby.com and Bedbathandbeyond.com from December 2024 through September 2025 for about $100.

Importers.Name: LPA1, LLC dba Little Partners, of Exton, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to potential platform collapse

Remedies.Name: Stop using the tower immediately, keep it away from children, and contact Little Partners for a free repair kit, which includes a new crossbar and installation instructions.

metaDescription: Little Partners recalls Grow 'N Stow Folding Learning Towers due to fall hazard from platform collapse. Immediate use poses risk of injury.

Retailers.Name: Walmart stores nationwide and online at Walmart.com, Amazon.com, Littlepartners.com, Etsy.com, Wayfair.com, Goodbyegear.com, Buybuybaby.com and Bedbathandbeyond.com from December 2024 through September 2025 for about $100.

Importers.Name: LPA1, LLC dba Little Partners, of Exton, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to potential platform collapse

Remedies.Name: Stop using the tower immediately, keep it away from children, and contact Little Partners for a free repair kit, which includes a new crossbar and installation instructions.

Description: Anna Queen has recalled play yards with black fabric sides, gray mesh panels, and white fabric top rails featuring a bear print. These play yards, including model P700, fail to meet mandatory safety standards, creating a risk of suffocation and entrapment. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Anna Queen Play Yards Recalled for Suffocation and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Anna Queen at tingerservice@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Play Yard
Removable tag with model number "P700"
Packaging with model number "P700"

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon from March 2025 for between $100 and $110.

Distributors.Name: Guangzhou Tinger Trading Co. Ltd., of China doing business as Anna Queen

Hazards.Name: Suffocation and entrapment risks due to non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled play yards and contact Anna Queen for a full refund. Disassemble the fabric cover from the frame, cut it up along with the mattress pad, and email a photo of the destroyed items to tingerservice@outlook.com to receive a full refund. Dispose of the destroyed play yard responsibly.

metaDescription: Anna Queen recalls play yards due to suffocation and entrapment hazards from non-compliance with safety standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Mallimoda is recalling children’s pajama sets that do not meet mandatory safety standards, posing a serious burn hazard. Description: Romorgniz has recalled its 12- and 13-drawer fabric dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that pose a serious risk of injury or death. These dressers do not comply with mandatory safety standards for clothing storage units. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Romorgniz 12- and 13-Drawer Fabric Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please reach out to Romorgniz via email at Romorgnizrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Romorgniz Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser in black (front)
Recalled Romorgniz Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser in black (side)
Recalled Romorgniz Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser Label (back)
Recalled Romorgniz 13-Drawer Dresser in rustic brown (front)
Recalled Romorgniz 13-Drawer Dresser in rustic brown (side)

Manufacturers.Name: Baituo Innovation Technology Co. Ltd., dba Romorgniz, of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from July 2025 through October 2025, priced around $140 for the 13-drawer and $80 for the 12-drawer models.

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment hazards, especially if not anchored to the wall, posing a serious threat to children.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the dressers if they are not anchored. Keep them out of children's reach and contact Romorgniz for disposal instructions to claim a full refund. A photo showing the disposal of the product must be sent to Romorgnizrecall@outlook.com.

metaDescription: Romorgniz recalls 12- and 13-drawer fabric dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards posing serious injury risks, especially to children.

Description: Belkin is recalling certain portable power banks and wireless charging stands due to fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The recall includes charging stands with model MMA008 and power banks with models BPB002 and PB0003. There has been one reported fire incident in the U.S. and two minor burn injuries internationally related to these products.
Title: Belkin Recalls Portable Power Banks and Wireless Charging Stands for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Belkin at 800-223-5546 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit http://www.belkin.com/MMA008recall and http://www.belkin.com/BPB002recall for more information. Additional details can be found on the Belkin website under 'Product Recalls.'

Products.Name: Wireless Charging Stand MMA008
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 83,500 (In addition, about 2,385 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled MMA008 Portable Wireless Charging Stand
Location of model number on the recalled MMA008 Portable Wireless Charging Stand (in red box)
Recalled BPB002 Portable Power Bank
Location of model number on the recalled BPB002 Portable Power Bank (in red box)
Recalled PB003 Portable Power Bank

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a fire incident in the U.S. (and 15 reports internationally). Two minor burn injuries and property damage totaling $37,765 have been reported outside of the U.S.

Manufacturers.Name: Belkin International Inc., of El Segundo, California

Retailers.Name: Best Buy, Target, Walmart and other stores nationwide and online at Belkin.com, Amazon.com and on Tiktok from August 2020 through August 2025 for between $29 and $180.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled products and contact Belkin for a full refund or store credit. Submit photos of the product with your recall form. Refunds are available with or without a receipt, and proper disposal instructions for lithium-ion batteries must be followed.

metaDescription: Belkin recalls portable power banks and wireless charging stands due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries.

Description: The Bettina Doll Set with Magic Light Unicorn is recalled because its battery compartment can be easily accessed by children, posing a risk of button cell battery ingestion. This hazard can cause serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Bettina Doll Set with Magic Light Unicorn Recalled for Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Shantou Chenghai Xingzigu Toy Industry via email at bettinaaftersales@outlook.com.

Products.Name: Bettina Doll Set with Magic Light Unicorn

Images.Caption: Recalled Bettina Doll Set with Magic Light Unicorn
Recalled Bettina Doll Set with Magic Light Unicorn (front of the packaging)
Recalled Bettina Doll Set with Magic Light Unicorn (back of the packaging)

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from August 2025 to September 2025 for approximately $28.

Distributors.Name: Shantou Chenghai Xingzigu Toy Industry Co., Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion due to accessible button cell batteries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should remove the doll set from children, cease usage, and safely dispose of the batteries. They will need to discard the unicorn and send a photo of the disposed item to bettinaaftersales@outlook.com to obtain a full refund.

metaDescription: Bettina Doll Set with Magic Light Unicorn recalled due to risk of serious injury from accessible button cell batteries posing a battery ingestion hazard.

Description: Great Lakes Wholesale International is recalling select Great Lakes Select battery packs because they do not have child-resistant packaging or required warning labels. These deficiencies increase the risk of battery ingestion, which can cause serious injuries. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Great Lakes Wholesale International Recalls Select Great Lakes Select Battery Packs for Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Great Lakes Select Battery Packs and keep them out of children's reach. For more information and to request a full refund, contact Great Lakes Wholesale International at 708-597-6000 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, or email Recall@Glwholesale.com. Additional details can be found at www.glwholesale.com under the "Recall" section.

Products.Name: Great Lakes Select Battery Pack CR2032

Recalled Mixed Super Alkaline Button Cell Batteries, Item 00272
Recalled LR44 Button Cell Batteries, Item 00276

Retailers.Name: Downeast Wholesalers Renys, International Wholesale, Lots & More, Northwoods Wholesale Outlet, a Ben Franklin store in Tennessee, and other stores nationwide from September 2024 through August 2025 for between $0.65 and $2.25.

Distributors.Name: Great Lakes Wholesale International of Lockport, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injuries from battery ingestion due to lack of child-resistant packaging and warning labels.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the battery packs, keep them away from children, and contact Great Lakes Wholesale International for a refund. Dispose of batteries following local hazardous waste guidelines.

Description: Tesla is recalling certain Powerwall 2 AC Battery Systems because of fire and burn hazards linked to potential overheating of lithium-ion battery cells. The affected units may emit smoke or flames during normal use. Tesla has reported minor property damage but no injuries related to these incidents.
Title: Tesla Powerwall 2 AC Battery Systems Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact the Tesla Support team via email at powerwallsupportna@tesla.com or call toll-free at 877-961-7652, available Monday to Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT. More information can also be found online at https://tesla.com/support/energy/powerwall/own/powerwall-2-replacement or by visiting https://tesla.com, navigating to Energy, then selecting Support > Powerwall > Owners.

Images.Caption: Recalled Powerwall 2 AC Battery System
Recalled Powerwall 2 AC Battery System (Product Nameplate Sticker)
Recalled Powerwall 2 AC Battery System (Product Nameplate Sticker)
Recalled Powerwall 2 AC Battery System (installed)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 22 reports of overheating, including six reports of smoking and five reports of fire, resulting in minor property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Tesla, Inc., of Reno, Nevada

Retailers.Name: Available at Tesla.com/powerwall and through certified Tesla installers across the country from November 2020 to December 2022, priced around $8,000.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential overheating and smoke or flame from lithium-ion battery cells.

Remedies.Name: Check the Tesla App for recall notifications and ensure the Powerwall 2 system is online. Tesla has remotely discharged affected units to prevent overheating until replacements are available.

metaDescription: Tesla recalls Powerwall 2 AC Battery Systems due to fire and burn hazards from potential overheating of lithium-ion battery cells.

Description: Trek is recalling specific Trek and Electra bicycles equipped with coaster brakes, along with certain replacement rear wheels. The recall addresses a brake failure hazard that can lead to loss of control and crashes. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Trek Recalls Bicycles with Coaster Brakes and Replacement Rear Wheels Due to Brake Failure Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Trek at 800-373-4594 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/company/legal_policies/safety_recalls/ or www.trekbikes.com and click on 'Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: Trek and Electra Coaster Brake Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 68,000 (In addition, about 6,820 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Precaliber 20 Bicycle in Miami Green (MY 2024)
Recalled Trek Precaliber 12 Bicycle with Coaster Brakes in Viper - Red
Recalled Precaliber 16 Bicycle with Coaster Brakes in Volt - Yellow
Close Up of Replacement Wheel for Recalled Bicycles
Replacement Wheel for Recalled Bicycles

Retailers.Name: Authorized Trek and Electra retailers across the nation, available both in-store and online, including Trek.com, from August 2023 to September 2025. Prices ranged from $300 to $660 for bicycles and $66 to $77 for replacement wheels.

Importers.Name: Trek Bicycle Corporation, of Waterloo, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Brake failure leading to loss of control and potential crashes

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bicycles and rear wheels immediately and contact an authorized Trek or Electra dealer for a free repair.

Description: Zigjoy is recalling children's one-piece sleep sacks with feet in specific designs and sizes because they do not meet mandatory flammability standards for children's sleepwear. This violation creates a serious burn hazard that could result in injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this product.
Title: Zigjoy Recalls Children's Sleep Sacks with Feet Due to Burn Hazard Violation
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the sleep sacks immediately and reach out to Zigjoy-US via email at zjoy-us@outlook.com. Additional information can be found online at https://www.zigjoy.com/pages/zigjoy-sleep-sacks-recalled-due-to-risk-of-serious-injury-or-death-from-burn-hazard-violates-mandatory-standard-for-children-s-sleepwear-sold-on-amazon-by-zigjoy-us or by visiting www.zigjoy.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to violation of flammability standards for children's sleepwear

Description: Astemrey has recalled Y'all Can DIY bioethanol fireplace fuel containers because they lack required flame mitigation devices, creating a risk of flash fire. These containers do not meet mandatory safety standards for portable fuel containers. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Astemrey Recalls Y'all Can DIY Bioethanol Fireplace Fuel Containers for Flash Fire Risk
ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach out to Astemrey via email at contact@astemrey.com for assistance.

Products.Name: Y'all Can DIY Bioethanol Fireplace Fuel Containers

Images.Caption: Recalled Y'all Can DIY Liquid Bioethanol 1000ml fuel container
Recalled Y'all Can DIY Liquid Bioethanol 1000ml fuel full product label

Manufacturers.Name: STARKİM KİMYA SAN.TİC. A.Ş, of Turkey

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from April 2025 to July 2025, priced around $19 for single packs and $31 for three-packs.

Distributors.Name: STARKIM KIMYA SAN.TIC. A.S, of Turkey
Astemrey LLC, of Casper, Wyoming

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire due to lack of required flame mitigation devices.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled fuel, mark the container 'DO NOT USE', and contact Astemrey for a free replacement.

metaDescription: Astemrey recalls Y'all Can DIY bioethanol fireplace fuel containers due to flash fire hazard from missing safety features in portable fuel containers.

Description: Mengna is recalling Criblike aftermarket play yard and non-full-sized crib mattresses because they pose entrapment and suffocation hazards. The mattresses do not meet mandatory safety standards, including flammability requirements. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Mengna Recalls Criblike Mattresses Due to Entrapment and Suffocation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Mengna via email at Criblike-service_2025@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Criblike Pack and Play Mattress XCD-002
Recalled Criblike Pack and Play Mattress Label XCD-002
Criblike Mini-crib Mattress MNCL-001
Recalled Criblike Mini-Crib Mattress Label MNCL-001
Recalled Criblike Pack and Play Mattress XCD-001
Recalled Criblike Pack and Play Mattress Label XCD-001

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from May 2023 through September 2025, priced between $20 and $60.

Distributors.Name: Zhujisibeixuxinxizixunfuwubu dba Mengna, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and suffocation risks due to improper fit in play yards or cribs, as well as flammability issues.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattresses immediately and contact Criblike for a full refund. Consumers should cut the mattresses in half, photograph the destroyed product, and email the image to Criblike-service_2025@outlook.com with 'Recall Proof' and their name in the subject line.

metaDescription: Mengna recalls Criblike mattresses for entrapment and suffocation risks due to improper fit and flammability hazards in play yards and cribs.

Description: F&F Fine Wines is recalling Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG bottles sold at Costco because of a laceration hazard from bottles breaking or shattering. The recall follows reports of ten incidents, including one injury caused by broken glass. The affected bottles have green glass, purple foil tops, and purple labels. Consumers should be aware of the risk associated with these bottles.
Title: F&F Fine Wines Recalls Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG Bottles Sold at Costco for Laceration Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to contact Ethica Wines at 786-810-7132, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. EST, or via email at customercare@ethicawines.com.

Products.Name: Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG bottle

Injuries.Name: F&F Fine Wines International, Inc. has received ten reports of the bottles shattering or breaking, resulting in one laceration injury.

Retailers.Name: Costco locations across IA, IL, IN, KY, MI, MN, MO, ND, NE, OH, SD, and WI from April 2025 to August 2025, priced around $8.

Importers.Name: F&F Fine Wines International, Inc. dba Ethica Wines, of Miami, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration due to breaking or shattering bottles

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the recalled bottles, do not attempt to open them, discard them in household trash, and reach out to Ethica Wines for refund instructions.

metaDescription: F&F Fine Wines recalls Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG bottles sold at Costco due to risk of laceration from breaking or shattering glass.

Description: Konges Sløjd is recalling three-wheeled children's scooters with a foot brake because the left front wheel can detach, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes two models with cherry and lemon designs, both marked with batch number 062024. There have been reports of injuries, including a broken arm and a lost tooth, related to the wheel detachment.
Title: Konges Sløjd Recalls Three-Wheeled Children's Scooters Due to Fall Hazard from Wheel Detachment
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Konges Sløjd via email at webshop@kongessloejd.com for consumer inquiries or Recall@kongessloejd.com for business-related questions. Additional information can be found online at https://kongessloejd.com/en-us/pages/product-recall or on their website at https://kongessloejd.com/ under the 'product recall' section.

Products.Name: Three-Wheeled Children's Scooter with Foot Brake

Images.Caption: Recalled Konges Sløjd Scooter - Cherry, model KS101544-P00005
Close-up of Cherry print on the Recalled Konges Sløjd Scooter – model KS101544-P00005
Recalled Konges Sløjd Scooter – Lemon, model KS101544-P00038
Close-up of Lemon print on the Recalled Konges Sløjd Scooter – model KS101544-P00038

Injuries.Name: Konges Sløjd has received 18 reports of the left front wheel detaching, resulting in three injuries, including one report of a broken arm and a child's lost tooth.

Retailers.Name: Available at specialty stores across the country and online at kongessloejd.com from September 2024 through July 2025 for a price of $115.

Importers.Name: Konges Sløjd, of Copenhagen, Denmark

Hazards.Name: Potential for left front wheel detachment leading to fall risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the scooter immediately and contact Konges Sløjd for a full refund or voucher. This can be done by providing a photo of the scooter disassembled (with the stem removed from the base) or by returning the scooter to the place of purchase.

metaDescription: Konges Sløjd recalls three-wheeled children's scooters due to a fall hazard caused by potential front wheel detachment. Stop use immediately.

Description: Lezyne has recalled two models of bicycle floor pumps due to a serious injury hazard. The pumps' canisters may eject from the base when pressurized, posing a risk of injury. One incident involving serious facial injuries has been reported. The recall applies to pumps with a bleed valve at the front of the base.
Title: Lezyne Bicycle Floor Pumps Recalled for Serious Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Lezyne at 888-998-8881 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, email recall@lezyne.com, or visit ride.lezyne.com/recall for more details.

Products.Name: Lezyne Pressure Over Drive Bicycle Floor Pump
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 7,500 (In addition, about 680 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Lezyne Pressure Over Drive Bicycle Tire Pump with analog gauge
Recalled Lezyne Digital Pressure Over Drive Bicycle Tire Pump with digital gauge
Only Lezyne bicycle floor pumps with a bleed valve on the front of the base are included in this recall

Injuries.Name: Lezyne has received one report of a canister ejecting from its base, resulting in serious injuries to the fac.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle retailers nationwide, including Art's Cyclery, Incycle, Bike Bling, Sports Basement, and Over the Edge Sports, as well as online at ride.lezyne.com, Jensonusa.com, Backcountry.com, and Velotech.com from May 2016 to December 2021, priced between $150 and $180.

Importers.Name: Lezyne USA Inc., of San Luis Obispo, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury due to the pump's canister potentially ejecting from the base when pressurized.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected pumps immediately and contact Lezyne for a complimentary replacement, including shipping. They should register their recalled product at ride.lezyne.com/recall for disposal instructions. No proof of purchase is necessary.

metaDescription: Lezyne recalls specific bicycle floor pumps due to risk of canister ejecting and causing serious injury. Stop use immediately for safety.

Description: Neaude has recalled its adult portable bed rails, model BR0221SL001, because they do not meet mandatory safety standards. The bed rails pose a risk of entrapment and asphyxiation, which can result in serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Neaude Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled for Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Neaude via email at ruiye1234@outlook.com or visit their website at https://neaudehealth.com/products/neaude-adult-portable-bed-rails. For more details, click on 'Free Refund - Register Here' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Neaude Adult Portable Bed Rail with silver top rail – top view
Recalled Neaude Adult Portable Bed Rail with silver top rail – front view
Recalled Neaude Adult Portable Bed Rail with faux wood top rail – front view
Recalled Neaude Adult Portable Bed Rail with faux wood top rail – top view

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from August 2023 through March 2025, priced between $55 and $65.

Distributors.Name: Ruiyewenhuachuanboshenzhenyouxiangongsi, dba Neaude, of China

Hazards.Name: These bed rails do not comply with mandatory safety standards and pose serious risks of entrapment and asphyxiation, potentially leading to severe injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled bed rails immediately and contact Neaude for a full refund. Mark the rails with 'RECALLED' using a permanent marker, note 'Bought on or after August 21, 2023' on paper, take a photo of this alongside the marked rails, and email it to ruiye1234@outlook.com. Dispose of the product according to local waste guidelines.

metaDescription: Neaude recalls adult portable bed rails due to entrapment and asphyxiation hazards that risk serious injury or death. No injuries reported.

Description: Peloton is recalling its Original Series Bike+ exercise bikes because the seat post assembly can break and detach during use, posing a fall hazard. There have been reports of injuries resulting from these falls. The recall affects bikes with model number PL02 and specific serial numbers. Consumers should stop using the affected bikes until repaired.
Title: Peloton Recalls Original Series Bike+ Exercise Bikes Due to Seat Post Breakage Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled bikes and reach out to Peloton at 866-679-9129, available from 6 a.m. to 12 a.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional support can be found online at https://support.onepeloton.com/s/article/Peloton-Recall-Information?language=en_US or by visiting www.onepeloton.com and clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page to request a free seat post and installation instructions.

Products.Name: Peloton Original Series Bike+ Model PL02

Images.Caption: Recalled Peloton Original Series Bike+ Model PL02
Recalled Peloton Original Series Bike+ Seat Post

Injuries.Name: Peloton has received three reports of the seat post breaking and detaching from the bike during use, including two reports of injuries due to a fall.

Retailers.Name: Available at Peloton and Dick's Sporting Goods stores across the nation, as well as online at onepeloton.com, ebay.com, Amazon.com, and Dickssportinggoods.com from January 2020 through April 2025, priced around $2,495.

Importers.Name: Peloton Interactive Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of seat post assembly breaking, leading to falls and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the affected bikes and contact Peloton for a complimentary repair. A free seat post is available for self-installation.

metaDescription: Peloton recalls Original Series Bike+ exercise bikes due to seat post breakage risk that can cause falls and injuries. Stop use and seek repair.

Description: Napei is recalling collapsible infant bath tubs because the built-in thermometer contains accessible button cell batteries that pose a risk of serious injury or death if ingested. The tubs feature detachable legs, a removable cushion, and a star-shaped drain plug. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Napei Infant Bath Tubs Recalled for Button Cell Battery Hazard in Built-In Thermometer
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should email Sefon Store at Napeiservice00@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Front view of Recalled Napei Collapsible Infant Bath Tub
Side view of Recalled Napei Collapsible Infant Bath Tub
Bottom view of the Recalled Napei Collapsible Infant Bath Tub showing location of battery compartment
Bottom view of the Recalled Napei Collapsible Infant Bath Tub showing location of battery compartment
Label of Recalled Napei Collapsible Infant Bath Tub
Label of Recalled Napei Collapsible Infant Bath Tub

Retailers.Name: Amazon from July 2025 through September 2025 for about $35.

Distributors.Name: Sefon Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to accessible button cell batteries in the built-in thermometer.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the bath tubs immediately and contact Sefon Store for a free replacement battery compartment. To obtain the replacement, provide your order number or a photo of the tracking label found on the tub by emailing Napeiservice00@outlook.com. After verification, a replacement battery compartment will be sent to you. Remember, button cell batteries are hazardous and should be disposed of or recycled according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

metaDescription: Napei recalls infant bath tubs due to risk of serious injury from accessible button cell batteries in the built-in thermometer.

Description: Umeyda has recalled children's short-sleeve nightgowns made of 100% cotton because they do not comply with mandatory flammability standards for children's sleepwear. This noncompliance creates a risk of serious burn injuries. The nightgowns were sold in multiple colors and sizes and feature front buttons and a pocket. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Umeyda Children's Nightgowns Recalled for Flammability Hazard and Burn Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Umeyda Company via email at ameyda123@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Baby Pink Stripe
Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Baby Pink
Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Black
Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Blue Grey
Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Dark Green
Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Dark Pink
Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Hot Pink
Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Mint Green
Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Purple
Recalled Umeyda Shortsleeve Nightgown – Red
Label on side seam of nightgown

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from June 2025 to October 2025 for approximately $19.

Distributors.Name: Xindu District Miuwola Clothing Store dba Umeyda Company, of China

Hazards.Name: Violation of flammability standards for children's sleepwear, posing a burn injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the nightgowns right away and reach out to Umeyda Company for a full refund. Cut the nightgowns in half and send a photo of the destroyed items to ameyda123@163.com to process your refund. Dispose of the cut garments.

metaDescription: Umeyda recalls children's nightgowns that violate flammability standards, posing a serious burn hazard. No injuries reported in this recall.

This noncompliance creates a risk of serious burn injuries. The nightgowns were sold in multiple colors and sizes and feature front buttons and a pocket. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

Description: Sanven Technology has recalled Vevor Baby Swings because their incline angle exceeds safety limits, creating a suffocation hazard. The recalled models include white/gray swings (BB501K and BB702A) and black/gray swings (BB005) marked with the VEVOR trademark. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Sanven Technology Recalls Vevor Baby Swings for Suffocation Hazard and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recalling@vevor.com, or visit https://www.vevor.com/pages/vevor-baby-swing-recall. Additional information can be found by clicking "Product Recall" on the Vevor website.

Description: Crate & Barrel is recalling Ana Dining Chairs because the chair legs can break, posing a fall hazard. The company has received reports of injuries related to this issue. The recall includes specific SKU numbers found on the underside of the chair seat. Chairs sold after January 23, 2025, are not affected.
Title: Crate & Barrel Recalls Ana Dining Chairs Due to Chair Leg Breakage and Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled chairs immediately and reach out to Crate & Barrel at 800-451-8217, available from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT on weekdays and 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT on weekends. Alternatively, contact them via email at recall@crateandbarrel.com or visit https://www.crateandbarrel.com/customer-service/product-recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Ana Dining Chair
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 54,930 (In addition, about 3,160 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Crate & Barrel Ana Dining Chairs
SKU Label location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 149 reports of chair legs breaking, including 10 reports of injuries to the head, back, arms and face.

Retailers.Name: Available at Crate & Barrel stores and on Crateandbarrel.com from January 2021 to January 2025, priced between $300 and $350.

Importers.Name: Euromarket Designs, Inc., dba Crate & Barrel, of Northbrook, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of chair legs breaking, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers can receive a free replacement chair and arrange for the free pickup of the recalled chair.

metaDescription: Crate & Barrel recalls Ana Dining Chairs due to risk of chair legs breaking, creating a fall hazard. Injuries have been reported from this defect.

ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to DINBooonLUX via email at Dingbangrecall2025@outlook.com.

Hazards.Name: Flammability violation posing serious fire risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattress, mark it as 'Recalled', email a photo to DINBooonLUX, and certify disposal for a full refund.

Description: Grundens has recalled the Youth Clipper 282 Jacket and Youth Zenith 282 Jacket because the drawstrings in the hoods present a strangulation hazard. These jackets are available in youth sizes 8 to 12 and feature the Grundens logo on the left chest. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Grundens Recalls Youth Clipper and Zenith Jackets Due to Strangulation Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Grundens at 800-323-7327 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT Monday through Thursday, and 8 a.m. to 12 p.m. on Friday. You can also email recall@Grundens.com or visit https://grundens.com/pages/recall for details.

Description: Harppa has recalled its 5-in-1 convertible high chairs, model BHC001, due to risks of serious injury or death from falls and entrapment. Description: Knog is recalling Blinder 900 and Blinder 1300 bicycle lights because their lithium-ion batteries may overheat, posing fire and burn hazards. The affected units have specific batch codes located on the underside of the lights. The company has received reports of batteries catching fire or exploding, but no injuries have been reported. Proper disposal of the lithium-ion batteries is essential to prevent fire risks.
Title: Knog Recalls Blinder 900 and 1300 Bicycle Lights for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Knog at recall@knog.com, visit http://www.knog.com/recall, or navigate to https://us.knog.com/ and select 'Blinder Recall' under the Support section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Knog Blinder 900 Bicycle Light
Recalled Knog Blinder 900 Bicycle Light
Recalled Knog Blinder 1300 Bicycle Light
Recalled Knog Blinder 1300 Bicycle Light

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the battery catching on fire or exploding. No injuries reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle retailers nationwide and online through Amazon from January 2023 to August 2025, priced between $60 and $100.

Importers.Name: Knog Pty Ltd, of Grapevine, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bicycle light immediately and register at http://www.knog.com/recall for a free replacement. Dispose of lithium-ion batteries according to local regulations and do not discard the light until confirmation from Knog is received.

metaDescription: Knog recalls Blinder 900 and 1300 bicycle lights due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. No injuries reported.

Description: Kroger has recalled Halloween-themed Skeleton Wax Candles because the plastic container and decorative ornaments are flammable, posing fire and burn hazards. The candle is white, set in a black plastic bowl with skeleton and pumpkin decorations, and includes a warning label with a misspelling. No injuries have been reported in connection with this product.
Title: Kroger Recalls Halloween Skeleton Wax Candles Due to Fire Hazard from Flammable Ornaments
ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach The Kroger Co. at 800-576-4377 from 7 a.m. to Midnight ET on weekdays and 7 a.m. to 9:30 p.m. ET on weekends. For more information, leave a message at https://www.kroger.com/hc/help/contact-us/customer-comments or visit www.kroger.com/i/recall-alerts or click on "Recall Alerts" at the bottom of the Kroger homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Halloween-themed Skeleton Wax Candle

Retailers.Name: Available at Kroger stores nationwide, including Dillons, Fred Meyer, Fry's, Smith's, and King Soopers from September 2025 to October 2025 for approximately $7.

Importers.Name: Carole Accessories, Inc., of Los Angeles, California

Distributors.Name: The Kroger Co., of Cincinnati, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Flammable ornaments posing fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled candle and return it to any Kroger store for a full refund.

Description: Modera has recalled its Pack N Play Play Yard Mattresses because they pose a risk of entrapment that could cause serious injury or death. The mattresses do not meet mandatory crib mattress safety standards due to improper fit. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Modera Recalls Pack N Play Mattresses for Entrapment Hazard and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Modera at 877-450-0320, available from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET Monday through Thursday and 10 a.m. to 1 p.m. on Friday. Alternatively, inquiries can be sent via email to recall@moderababy.com or through their website at https://moderababy.com/recall.

Description: Avenco and Novilla mattresses have been recalled because they do not meet mandatory flammability standards, creating a serious fire hazard. The recall includes various mattress sizes and thicknesses manufactured by PT Champion. No injuries have been reported. A free fitted cover is provided to address the fire risk and ensure compliance with safety standards.
Title: Avenco and Novilla Mattresses Recalled for Fire Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violation

Description: HOMEAL is recalling its children's portable bed rails because they pose a risk of entrapment that can result in serious injury or death. The product features a white steel frame with gray oxford cloth and is labeled with the brand name and model number. These bed rails do not comply with mandatory safety standards designed to prevent entrapment. No injuries have been reported.
Title: HOMEAL Children's Bed Rails Recalled for Entrapment Hazard Violating Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to HOMEAL via email at homeal_recalls@outlook.com for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled HOMEAL Bed Rail
Model number 338 is printed on a label stitched to the bed rail

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from June 2025 to September 2025, priced between $45 and $50.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Speeding Culture Communication Co. Ltd., dba HOMEAL, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment risk leading to serious injury or death

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bed rails, contact HOMEAL for a refund, destroy the cloth covering, and dispose of the product properly.

Description: WLIVE has recalled its fabric 16-drawer dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards if the units are not anchored to a wall. These dressers feature a metal frame with collapsible fabric drawers and do not comply with mandatory safety standards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: WLIVE Fabric 16-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach WLIVE at 833-367-7572 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email wliveproductrecall@wlivehome.com, or visit http://wlivehome.com/ and select "Recall" at the top of the page.

Products.Name: WLIVE Fabric 16-Drawer Dressers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 34,730 16-drawer dressers. (About 76,500 12-drawer dressers were previously recalled)

Description: 3M has recalled the Scotch™ TL909-50 Thermal Laminator because it poses a risk of overheating that could result in burns. The affected units are light gray with gray accents and have serial numbers between 2406000001 and 2501001920. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: 3M Recalls Scotch™ TL909-50 Thermal Laminators Due to Overheating Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact 3M Company at 800-772-4337, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT. You can also reach out via email through the "Contact Us" link on the Scotch Brand Website at https://www.scotchbrand.com/3M/en_US/scotch-brand/contact-us/ or visit https://www.scotchbrand.com/laminatorrecallTL909-50/ for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Scotch™ brand TL909-50 thermal laminator

Injuries.Name: No incidents or injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available in-store and online at Office Depot, Office Smart, and School Specialty, Inc. from August 2024 through September 2025, priced between $100 and $130.

Importers.Name: 3M Company, of St. Paul, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating that may lead to burns.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the laminators immediately, unplug them, and contact 3M Company for a full refund. A short form must be completed, confirming the laminator's inclusion in the recall, and a photo of the unplugged machine with the cut power cord must be uploaded. Refunds will be issued via check once the form is processed and the power cord confirmation is received.

metaDescription: 3M recalls Scotch™ TL909-50 Thermal Laminators due to risk of overheating that may cause burns. No injuries reported in this safety recall.

Description: Bealife has recalled its 5-Drawer Dressers because they pose tip-over and entrapment hazards that can cause serious injury or death. The dressers are white and measure about 32 inches high, 28 inches wide, and 16 inches deep. The model number AP23-W appears on the packaging but not on the dresser. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Bealife 5-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers should reach out to Bealife via email at info@bealifehome.com, or visit https://bealifehome.com/pages/bealife-recall. Alternatively, consumers can navigate to https://bealifehome.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page for additional details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bealife 5-Drawer Dresser (front)
Recalled Bealife 5-Drawer Dresser (back)
Recalled Bealife 5-Drawer Dresser (side)

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from June 2025 to August 2025 for approximately $76.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Quanyuanfa Trading Co., Ltd. dba Bealife, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment that can cause serious injuries or fatalities, especially to children.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the dressers if they are not wall-anchored and relocate them to an inaccessible area for children. Contact Bealife for disposal instructions and provide a photo of the disposed dresser to info@bealifehome.com to obtain a full refund.

metaDescription: Bealife recalls 5-Drawer Dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that pose serious injury risks. No injuries reported.

Description: Cranach Hardware has recalled its plastic tip restraint kits used to secure furniture to walls because the plastic parts can weaken or break over time. This failure increases the risk of furniture tipping over, creating a safety hazard. The Consumer Product Safety Commission is aware of multiple incidents involving breakage but no reported injuries. The kits do not meet industry safety standards for tip restraint.
Title: Cranach Hardware Recalls Plastic Tip Restraint Kits Due to Tip-Over Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Cranach Hardware at 888-798-9487 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email info@cranach-hardware.com, or visit www.cranach-hardware.com/recalls.html for further details.

Products.Name: Cranach Hardware Plastic Tip Restraint Kits

Images.Caption: Recalled Cranach Hardware Plastic Tip Restraint Kit
Recalled Cranach Hardware Plastic Tip Restraint Kit
Replacement Stainless Steel Tip Restraint Kit

Injuries.Name: CPSC is aware of at least 115 incidents where consumers reported that the defective plastic tip restraint kits broke, including six reported tip-over incidents. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Cranach Hardware, of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon since September 2019, priced between $6 and $30 based on package size.

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment due to potential breakage or degradation of the plastic components.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the kits immediately and contact Cranach Hardware for a complimentary replacement kit made of stainless steel. While awaiting the replacement, keep children away from the furniture. To obtain the replacement, consumers must provide photographic proof of the destroyed or disposed product.

metaDescription: Cranach Hardware recalls plastic tip restraint kits due to tip-over hazard from potential plastic breakage posing safety risks.

Description: Lifepro Fitness has recalled Bioremedy Infrared Sauna Blankets due to an overheating hazard that can cause burn injuries. The recall includes multiple models with a gray-faced control pad, available in various sizes and colors. The company received reports of the blankets overheating, resulting in burn injuries. Consumers are advised to stop using the blankets immediately.
Title: Lifepro Fitness Recalls Bioremedy Infrared Sauna Blankets Due to Overheating and Burn Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Lifepro at 888-491-1690 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recalls@lifeprofitness.com, or visit https://lifeprofitness.com/recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Lifepro Sauna Blanket
Controller Pad on Recalled Lifepro Sauna Blanket

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 65 reports of the sauna blankets overheating, including 32 reports of burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Lifepro.com, Amazon.com, Walmart, QVC, and Dick's Sporting Goods from September 2022 through June 2025, priced between $179 and $199.

Importers.Name: Lifepro Fitness, of Howell, NJ

Hazards.Name: Overheating risk leading to burn injuries

Remedies.Name: Stop using and unplug the sauna blanket immediately. Visit the company's website for recall participation instructions and to obtain a replacement. Dispose of the recalled blankets according to local regulations.

Description: Olympia Tools is recalling the Pack-N-Stroll Premium Folding Utility Wagon because it poses a risk of serious injury or death from potential entrapment of a child's head and falls due to the absence of a restraint system. The wagon features a removable tray and cup holders and folds for storage. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Olympia Tools Recalls Pack-N-Stroll Utility Wagons for Entrapment and Fall Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can contact Olympia Tools at 877-461-8665 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email info@olympiatools.com, or visit www.cohooutdoors.com and click on "Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pack-N-Stroll Premium Folding Utility Wagon

Retailers.Name: Costco.com and cohooutdoors.com from June 2025 through July 2025 for between $200 and $400.

Importers.Name: Olympia Tools International, Inc. of Covina, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to potential entrapment of a child's head and falls from lack of a restraint system.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the wagon immediately and return it to Costco for a full refund.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to potential entrapment of a child's head and falls from lack of a restraint system.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the wagon immediately and return it to Costco for a full refund.

Description: Sauna360 is recalling Tylö Halmstad and Kiruna Hybrid sauna models because the bench seating may collapse, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes Halmstad 2, 3, and 4 sauna rooms and Kiruna Hybrid 2 and 3 models. There have been reports of bench failures resulting in injuries, including head and neck harm. The issue involves potential bench collapse that can lead to falls.
Title: Sauna360 Recalls Tylö Halmstad and Kiruna Hybrid Saunas Due to Bench Collapse Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sauna360 toll-free at 888-780-4427 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email us_techsupport@sauna360.com. More information is also available online at https://sauna360.com/product-recall or by visiting www.sauna360.com and selecting 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Recalled Sauna Model Tylö Halmstad 3
Recalled Sauna Model Tylö Halmstad 4
Recalled Sauna Model Kiruna Hybrid 2
Recalled Sauna Model Kiruna Hybrid 3

Injuries.Name: Sauna360 has received seven reports of the benches breaking, including one consumer with head and neck injuries.

Retailers.Name: Various distributors across the country from July 2024 to December 2024, priced between $6,000 and $12,000.

Importers.Name: Sauna360, Inc., of Cokato, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Potential bench collapse leading to fall risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the sauna benches immediately and contact Sauna360 for a complimentary repair. A professional installer will retrofit the benches with additional hardware, and consumers will receive an updated product manual that specifies the weight limit.

metaDescription: Sauna360 recalls Tylö Halmstad and Kiruna Hybrid saunas due to bench collapse hazard that can cause falls and injuries.

Description: Scepter B62 gas and oil fuel containers are recalled for failing to meet mandatory safety standards. These containers lack required flame mitigation devices and child-resistant closures, increasing the risk of flash fires and burns. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Scepter B62 Fuel Containers Recalled for Flash Fire and Child Safety Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Pro Recycle at 514-887-7309 between 8 a.m. and 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at prorecycle@hotmail.ca.

Products.Name: Scepter Gas and Oil Combination Fuel Containers

Images.Caption: Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – front
Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – side

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from September 2024 through July 2025 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: Pro Recycle of Montreal, Quebec, Canada

Hazards.Name: These fuel containers do not comply with safety regulations, lacking essential flame mitigation devices and child-resistant closures, which increases the risk of flash fires and burns to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the containers immediately, keep them away from children, and reach out to Pro Recycle for a free return and full refund.

The dressers have a metal frame with a wooden top and fabric drawers, available in multiple colors. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: AOWOS 12-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact AOWOS at 888-919-9734 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or email AOWOSRecall@outlook.com.

Description: Belivium has recalled baby loungers that fail to meet mandatory infant sleep product safety standards. The loungers pose suffocation and entrapment risks due to low side heights, thick sleeping pads, and wide openings at the foot. These design flaws create hazardous sleeping conditions that could lead to serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Belivium Baby Loungers Recalled for Suffocation and Entrapment Hazards Due to Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Belivium via email at BeliviumRecall@163.com.

Recalled Belivium Baby Lounger - Blue Rainbow

Recalled Belivium Baby Lounger - Blue and Grey Star
Recalled Belivium Baby Lounger - Yellow Zoo Animal
"BABY NEST" is printed on an attached label inside the baby lounger’s cover.

Retailers.Name: Amazon from November 2024 through August 2025 for between $25 and $50.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshiyingjiayueshangmaoyouxiangongsi, dba Belivium, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation and entrapment risks due to inadequate side heights and excessive pad thickness; unsafe openings leading to falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the loungers and contact Belivium for a full refund. They should also destroy the loungers and email a photo of the destroyed product to BeliviumRecall@163.com.

metaDescription: Belivium recalls baby loungers for suffocation and entrapment hazards caused by inadequate side heights and unsafe openings. No injuries reported.

Description: iFIT is recalling NordicTrack RW900 rowing machines because the screen console can overheat, creating a fire risk. There have been reports of fires, smoking, and melting, resulting in property damage but no injuries. The recall affects specific models identified by model numbers NTRW19147.0 through NTRW19147.3.
Title: iFIT Recalls NordicTrack RW900 Rowing Machines Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 7 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email ntrw19147-recall@ifit.com, or visit www.nordictrack.com/product-recalls or www.nordictrack.com and select 'Voluntary Recalls' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: NordicTrack Rowing Machine Model NTRW19147
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 44,800 (In addition, about 700 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled NordicTrack RW900 Rowing Machine

Injuries.Name: There have been eight reported incidents including two reports of fire, six reports of smoking or melting, and at least $6,000 in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, Dick's Sporting Goods, Nebraska Furniture, and ABT stores nationwide, as well as online at nordictrack.com and Amazon.com from November 2018 to April 2022, priced around $1,700.

Importers.Name: iFIT Inc., of Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Overheating screen console posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using and unplug the rowing machines; contact iFIT for a free home repair with a replacement screen console.

metaDescription: iFIT recalls NordicTrack RW900 rowing machines due to overheating screen consoles that pose a fire hazard. No injuries reported.

Description: Super Wheels Inc. is recalling Vaast A/1 bicycles and frames because of a risk of falling caused by potential hairline fractures in the frame. The recall includes multiple models identified by specific serial numbers and color options. There have been reports of frame fractures and tube separations, including one incident resulting in a minor injury. Consumers should stop using the affected bicycles and frames immediately to prevent fall hazards.
Title: Super Wheels Recalls Vaast A/1 Bicycles and Frames Due to Frame Fracture Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Super Wheels Inc. at 855-430-6240 between 8 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email info@vaastbikes.com, or visit www.vaastbikes.com/vaast-model-a1-recall for further details.

Products.Name: Vaast A/1 Bicycles and Frames
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,860 (in addition, about 4 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Vaast A/1 bicycle

Injuries.Name: The firm has had 22 consumers report frame fractures and three consumers report frame tube separation, one of which resulted in a minor injury after falling from the bike.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from November 2024 through August 2025, priced between $25 and $50.

Importers.Name: Super Wheels Inc. f/k/a Allite Inc., of Miamisburg, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to potential hairline fractures in the bicycle frame.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the bicycles and frames immediately and reach out to Super Wheels Inc. for a free replacement. After registering their bike at www.vaastbikes.com/contact-us/, they will receive a replacement frame and reimbursement (up to $200) for a bike shop technician to rebuild their bicycle.

metaDescription: Super Wheels recalls Vaast A/1 bicycles and frames due to risk of falls from potential frame fractures. Immediate use cessation is advised for safety.

Description: Wilteexs has recalled its bioethanol fuel bottles used in tabletop fire pits and similar devices due to fire hazards. The bottles lack required flame mitigation devices, increasing the risk of flash fires, and include misleading "Non Toxic" claims. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Wilteexs Bioethanol Fuel Bottles Recalled for Fire Hazard and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Wilteexs via email at hongyaodemg@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Wilteexs Bioethanol Fuel Bottle
"X004IQ9ZCH" is printed below the barcode on the back of the recalled Wilteexs bioethanol fuel bottles

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from April 2025 to July 2025 for approximately $17.

Importers.Name: Hongyaodekejiyouxiangongsi, dba online seller Wilteexs, of China

Hazards.Name: The ethanol fuel bottles do not comply with federal safety standards for portable fuel containers, lacking necessary flame mitigation devices, which increases the risk of flash fires. Additionally, they contain misleading claims of being "Non Toxic," violating federal regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the recalled bottles immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Wilteexs for a full refund. They should mark the bottles with "Do not use" and their Amazon order number, then send a photo to hongyaodemg@outlook.com. Proper disposal should follow local and state regulations.

metaDescription: Wilteexs recalls bioethanol fuel bottles due to fire hazard risk from noncompliance with safety standards and misleading labeling.

Description: WLIVE is recalling its Fabric 12-drawer dressers because they pose a risk of serious injury or death from tip-over and entrapment if not anchored to the wall. These dressers do not comply with mandatory safety standards for clothing storage units. The firm has received one report of a dresser tipping over, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: WLIVE Fabric 12-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach WLIVE at 833-367-7572 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email wliveproductrecall@wlivehome.com. Additional details are available online at http://wlivehome.com/ by clicking on "Recall" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled WLIVE Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser in Rustic Brown Wood Grain Print
Recalled WLIVE Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser in Black Rustic Brown
Recalled WLIVE Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser in Black
Recalled WLIVE Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser in Charcoal Black Wood Grain
Recalled WLIVE Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser in Dark Grey
Recalled WLIVE Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser in Light Grey
Recalled WLIVE Fabric 12-Drawer Dresser in White

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the dresser tipping over. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Jiangsu Pengcheng Weiye Furniture Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from September 2023 through August 2025 for approximately $83.

Distributors.Name: WLIVE, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from tip-over and entrapment if not anchored to the wall, violating the STURDY Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the dressers if they are not secured to the wall and keep them out of children's reach. Contact WLIVE for disposal instructions and to request a full refund. A photo showing the disposal of the product must be sent to wliveproductrecall@wlivehome.com.

Description: Bellabu Bear has recalled its bamboo sherpa children's robes because they do not meet mandatory flammability standards for children's sleepwear. These robes present a risk of burn injuries due to their failure to comply with fire safety requirements. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

Description: Bmrwtg has recalled LED Tutu Skirts designed for children aged 2 to 8 due to the risk of serious injury or death from ingestion of lithium coin batteries. The skirts contain CR2032 batteries that are easily accessible and do not meet mandatory safety standards for products with coin batteries. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Bmrwtg LED Tutu Skirts Recalled for Risk of Battery Ingestion Injury to Children
ConsumerContact: Contact Bmrwtg at bmrwtgrecall@outlook.com for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bmrwtg Children's Tutu Skirt in Pink
The recalled Bmrwtg children's tutu skirts have a battery compartment with lithium coin batteries

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon between March 2025 and August 2025 for approximately $10

Distributors.Name: Bmrwtg, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from ingestion of lithium coin batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the skirt, remove batteries, and contact Bmrwtg for a refund

metaDescription: Bmrwtg recalls LED Tutu Skirts due to risk of serious injury from lithium coin battery ingestion by children. No injuries reported.

Description: Calico Brands is recalling Scripto Premium Torches because they pose fire and burn hazards by violating mandatory safety standards for multipurpose lighters. The torches feature an adjustable metal nozzle and flame control but do not meet required safety criteria. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Calico Brands Recalls Scripto Premium Torches for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Calico Brands at 800-544-4837 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at premiumtorchrecall@calicobrands.com. More details are available at www.calicobrands.com/premiumtorchrecall.html or by visiting www.calicobrands.com and selecting 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Scripto® Premium Torch

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart and Home Depot stores across the country and online at Walmart.com and Homedepot.com from November 2024 through August 2025, priced around $15.

Importers.Name: Calico Brands, of Ontario, California

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to violation of safety standards for multipurpose lighters.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the torches immediately and return them to the retailer for a full refund or store credit. Contact Calico Brands for return instructions and receive a shipping label and hazmat packaging at no cost.

metaDescription: Calico Brands recalls Scripto Premium Torches due to fire and burn hazards from safety standard violations. No injuries reported.

Description: EcoFlow Technology has recalled the Delta Max 2000 Model EFD310 Power Stations because of a risk of overheating that may result in serious burn injuries or fire. The recall follows reports of fires and significant property damage. The power stations have a black and silver design with six outlets and an LCD display.
Title: EcoFlow Delta Max 2000 Power Stations Recalled for Overheating and Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach EcoFlow Technology at 833-424-4137 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at deltamax2000@ecoflow.com. More details can be found online at https://www.ecoflow.com/us/blog/ecoflow-delta-max-2000-recall-firmware-update or by visiting https://us.ecoflow.com and selecting "IMPORTANT RECALL INFORMATION and Firmware Update" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled EcoFlow Delta Max 2000 Model EFD310 Power Station
Nameplate on underside of recalled EcoFlow Delta Max 2000 Model EFD310 Power Station

Injuries.Name: EcoFlow Technology has received six reports of the recalled Power Stations catching fire, with property damage totaling over $850,000.

Retailers.Name: Available at Costco.com, Amazon.com, and Ecoflow.com from July 2022 to May 2025, priced around $1,600.

Importers.Name: EcoFlow Technology, Inc., of San Francisco, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating, burn injuries, and fire hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled power stations and contact EcoFlow Technology for guidance on a free firmware update repair.

metaDescription: EcoFlow recalls Delta Max 2000 Power Stations due to overheating risks that can cause serious burns and fire hazards. Firmware update available.

Description: The recall now covers ESR HaloLock wireless power bank model 2G505 in addition to previously recalled models 2G520, 2G505B, and 2G512B. These devices contain lithium-ion batteries that can overheat, posing fire and burn hazards. The firm has reported multiple incidents of battery fires causing property damage but no injuries. Consumers should stop using the recalled power banks to prevent risk.
Title: Expanded Recall of ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Banks Model 2G505 Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Waymeet at 888-990-0280 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email support@esrtech.com, or visit https://www.esrtech.com/pages/recall or www.esrtech.com and select "Product Recalls" at the top of the page.

Products.Name: ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 19,500 (In addition, about 2,015 were sold in Canada). Waymeet previously recalled 24,000 power banks on August 14, 2025 (In addition, 9,900 were sold in Canada that were covered in the August recall)

Images.Caption: Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505
Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G520
Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505B
Recalled ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Bank Model 2G512B

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 20 reports of the lithium-ion battery catching fire and exploding, resulting in property damage of about $30,000. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com (all models), Esrtech.com (all models) and Homedepot.com (models 2G520, 2G505B and 2G512B only), from September 2022 through July 2025 for between $32 and $50.

Distributors.Name: Waymeet Limited, of Hong Kong

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Waymeet for a full refund. Send a photo of the recalled power bank with the model number marked as "Recalled" to support@esrtech.com. Dispose of the power banks according to local hazardous waste regulations.

metaDescription: ESR HaloLock wireless power banks recalled for fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Includes model 2G505 and earlier models.

Description: Mom Genius is recalling its retractable safety gates because they pose a risk of serious injury or death from entrapment. The gates feature a retractable mesh screen with metal framing and are available in black and gray. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Mom Genius Retractable Safety Gates Recalled for Entrapment Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Mom Genius at 844-266-6436, email product-recall@momgenius.com, or visit https://momgenius.com/pages/product-recall. You can also go to https://momgenius.com/ and click on "Recall" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Mom Genius retractable safety gate-black
Recalled Mom Genius retractable safety gate-gray

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Purchasingpower.com and Perpay.com from March 2024 through June 2025 for between $50 and $7.

Distributors.Name: Mom Genius of Alpharetta, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to entrapment

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gates immediately and contact Mom Genius for a full refund. Destroy the gate by cutting the mesh and send a photo of the destroyed gate to product-recall@momgenius.com to receive your refund.

metaDescription: Mom Genius retractable safety gates recalled due to entrapment hazard that risks serious injury or death. No injuries reported.

Description: Living Glow portable waist fans (model CP-WFLED01) are recalled because their lithium-ion batteries can overheat and cause a fire hazard. The fans include multiple features such as adjustable speeds, a flashlight, and power bank functionality. There have been reports of the fans melting or catching fire, but no injuries or property damage have been confirmed.
Title: Living Glow Portable Waist Fans Recalled for Fire Hazard from Overheating Batteries
ConsumerContact: For more details, contact Guangzhou Jiguang Lighting at support@waistfanrecall.info or visit https://www.waistfanrecall.info.

Products.Name: Living Glow five-in-one portable waist fan

Images.Caption: Recalled Rechargeable Portable Waist Fan (green)
Recalled Rechargeable Portable Waist Fan (white)
Recalled Rechargeable Portable Waist Fan (red)
Recalled Rechargeable Portable Waist Fan (aqua)
Recalled Rechargeable Portable Waist Fan (sage green)
Recalled Rechargeable Portable Waist Fan (coral)
Label on Back of Recalled Rechargeable Portable Waist Fan

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of the portable waist fan melting or catching fire. No injuries or property damage have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Guangzhou Jiguang Lighting Co. Ltd., of Guangzhou, China

Retailers.Name: Available on HSN.com and through the HSN television network from May 2025 to July 2025, priced between $20 and $25.

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion batteries posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the waist fan immediately and register at https://www.waistfanrecall.info for a full refund. Follow the instructions to disable the fan and dispose of it according to local regulations.

metaDescription: Living Glow portable waist fans recalled due to overheating lithium-ion batteries posing a fire hazard. Users should stop use immediately.

Description: YGJT is recalling baby loungers that do not comply with mandatory infant sleep product safety standards. The loungers have low sides and wide foot openings, increasing the risk of infant entrapment and falls. The lack of a stand also raises the danger of falls from elevated surfaces. No injuries have been reported.
Title: YGJT Baby Loungers Recalled for Entrapment and Fall Hazards Violating Infant Sleep Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact YGJT Local Warehouse via email at YGJTrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled YGJT Baby Lounger
Recalled YGJT Baby Lounger
"YGJT" is printed on an attached label on the outside of the baby lounger's cover

Retailers.Name: SHEIN.com from June 2025 through August 2025 for between $18 and $21.

Distributors.Name: YGJT Local Warehouse, of China

Hazards.Name: The loungers do not meet safety standards, with sides too low to contain infants and foot openings wider than permissible, leading to fall and entrapment risks. Additionally, the absence of a stand increases the danger of falls from elevated surfaces, creating an unsafe sleeping environment that can result in serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the loungers immediately and reach out to YGJT Local Warehouse for a full refund. They should dismantle the foam and pads from the cover and cut all components in half. To process the refund, consumers need to email photos of the destroyed items to YGJTrecall@outlook.com.

metaDescription: YGJT recalls baby loungers that pose serious entrapment and fall hazards, failing to meet infant sleep product safety standards.
