Description: Tempo USA is recalling Ambiano electric pressure cookers, including models 93550, 96775, and 98527, due to a risk of burn injuries. The hazard involves premature lid opening before steam pressure is fully released, causing hot contents to be expelled. The recall follows reports of multiple incidents, including severe burn injuries.
Title: Tempo USA Recalls Ambiano Electric Pressure Cookers Due to Serious Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled pressure cookers immediately. For assistance, contact ALDI at 800-325-7894 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://help.aldi.us/contact-form-product. Alternatively, reach out to Tempo via email at serviceusa@tempo.org or check aldi.us for more details on product recalls.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 93550 (KY-318B)
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 93550 (KY-318B) Rating Label
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 96775 (KY-318A
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 96775 (KY-318A) Rating Label
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 98527 (6L-PC)
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 98527 (6L-PC) Rating Label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 11 reports of incidents, in which the contents were expelled under pressure, including eight reports of severe burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: ALDI retail stores nationwide between January 2016 and December 2019 for about $40.

Importers.Name: Tempo International (USA) Inc., of Aventura, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn injuries due to premature lid opening before steam pressure is released.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the cookers and contact Tempo or ALDI for a refund. They can return the cooker to any ALDI store for a full refund or email Tempo with a photo of the unplugged cooker and its model number for further instructions.

metaDescription: Tempo USA recalls Ambiano electric pressure cookers due to burn hazard from premature lid opening. Multiple severe burn injuries reported.

en.Title: Tempo USA Recalls Ambiano Electric Pressure Cookers Due to Serious Burn Hazard
en.Description: Tempo USA is recalling Ambiano electric pressure cookers, including models 93550, 96775, and 98527, due to a risk of burn injuries. The hazard involves premature lid opening before steam pressure is fully released, causing hot contents to be expelled. The recall follows reports of multiple incidents, including severe burn injuries.
en.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled pressure cookers immediately. For assistance, contact ALDI at 800-325-7894 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://help.aldi.us/contact-form-product. Alternatively, reach out to Tempo via email at serviceusa@tempo.org or check aldi.us for more details on product recalls.
en.metaDescription: Tempo USA recalls Ambiano electric pressure cookers due to burn hazard from premature lid opening. Multiple severe burn injuries reported.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 93550 (KY-318B)
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 93550 (KY-318B) Rating Label
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 96775 (KY-318A
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 96775 (KY-318A) Rating Label
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 98527 (6L-PC)
Recalled Pressure Cooker, Model Number 98527 (6L-PC) Rating Label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 11 reports of incidents, in which the contents were expelled under pressure, including eight reports of severe burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: ALDI retail stores nationwide between January 2016 and December 2019 for about $40.

Importers.Name: Tempo International (USA) Inc., of Aventura, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn injuries due to premature lid opening before steam pressure is released.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the cookers and contact Tempo or ALDI for a refund. They can return the cooker to any ALDI store for a full refund or email Tempo with a photo of the unplugged cooker and its model number for further instructions.

Description: Almpal has recalled its dissolved oxygen test kits because the sulfuric acid bottle inside lacks child-resistant packaging, violating safety standards. This creates a poisoning hazard for young children if accessed. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Almpal Dissolved Oxygen Test Kits Recalled for Poisoning Hazard to Children
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Almpal via email at lehapet@hotmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Almpal Dissolved Oxygen Test Kit - Closed
Recalled Almpal Dissolved Oxygen Test Kit - Opened

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from May 2025 to August 2025, priced around $25.

Distributors.Name: Ningbo Leha E-Commerce Co., Ltd., of China doing business as Amazon seller Almpal

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from poisoning due to non-child-resistant sulfuric acid packaging.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the kits out of children's reach, contact Almpal for disposal instructions, and request a full refund after submitting a photo of the product.

metaDescription: Almpal recalls dissolved oxygen test kits due to poisoning hazard from non-child-resistant sulfuric acid packaging posing risk to children.

Description: Blossom is recalling children’s two-piece loungewear sets because they fail to meet mandatory flammability standards, posing a serious fire hazard. The recall includes short- and long-sleeved button-up shirts with matching shorts or pants in various designs and colors. No injuries have been reported related to this hazard.
Title: Blossom Recalls Children’s Loungewear for Fire Hazard Violation
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Blossom at 256-481-8838 from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email sales@blossomocc.com, or visit https://www.blossomocc.com/pages/recall. Additionally, consumers can go to www.blossomocc.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Blossom Short Sleeve Button Up Short Set – Pink, Light Blue, Lilac, White, Navy
Recalled Blossom Short Sleeve Button Up Short Set – Fireworks, Sailboats, Stripes, Tulip
Recalled Blossom Long Sleeve Button Up Pant Set – Letter to Santa, Santa Paws
"Blossom" and the size are printed on the sewn-in neck label

Retailers.Name: Little Pineapple in Colfax, North Carolina; Once Upon a Time in Birmingham, Alabama; The Pink Pineapple in Clayton, North Carolina; Lily Jane Columbia in Columbia, Tennessee; Sweet Dreams in Madison, Mississippi; Joggling Board in Pawleys Island in South Carolina and Little Chunky Monkeys in Southside, Alabama; and online at blossomocc.com, littlepineappleboutique.com, onceuponatimechildrens.com, pinkpineapplenc.com, lilyjanecolumbia.com and shoplittlechunkymonkeys.com from September 2023 through May 2025 for between $18 and $35.

Distributors.Name: Blossom Blanks, LLC dba Blossom, of Gadsden, Alabama

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to violation of flammability standards for children's sleepwear

Remedies.Name: Stop using the loungewear immediately and contact Blossom for a full refund or store credit. Destroy the garments by cutting them in half, take a photo, and email it to sales@blossomocc.com to receive the refund. No proof of purchase is required.

metaDescription: Blossom recalls children’s loungewear due to fire hazard risk from flammability standard violations. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: Gunaito is recalling its 10-drawer dressers sold in brown and white due to tip-over and entrapment hazards. These dressers do not meet mandatory safety standards and pose a risk of serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Gunaito 10-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Gunaito via email at gunaito10recall@outlook.com or visit https://gunaito.com/pages/recall. More details can also be found by navigating to the "Recall" section on their website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Brown Gunaito 10-Drawer Dresser (front)
Recalled Brown Gunaito 10-Drawer Dresser (back)
Recalled Brown Gunaito 10-Drawer Dresser (side)
Recalled White Gunaito 10-Drawer Dresser (front)
Recalled White Gunaito 10-Drawer Dresser (back)
Recalled White Gunaito 10-Drawer Dresser (side)

Manufacturers.Name: Changzhou Hengze Home Furnishing Co., Ltd, of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Walmart.com from December 2024 through August 2025 for approximately $70.

Distributors.Name: Gunaito Direct, of China

Hazards.Name: Unstable dressers pose serious risks of tip-over and entrapment, especially for children, violating the STURDY Act's mandatory safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the dressers if they aren't anchored to a wall and keep them out of children's reach. Contact Gunaito for disposal instructions to obtain a full refund, and submit a photo of the disposed product to gunaito10recall@outlook.com.

metaDescription: Gunaito recalls 10-drawer dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that pose serious injury risks, especially to children.

Description: Zyntony has recalled Kogalla power banks, including models BP125, BatPak 2F, and BatPak 3F, because of fire and burn risks from lithium-ion battery overheating. The batteries can ignite even when not in use. There have been reports of overheating incidents causing one minor burn injury and property damage.
Title: Zyntony Recalls Kogalla Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Kogalla via email at bpreplace@kogalla.com, or visit https://rtn.kogalla.com/pages/kogalla-recalls-batpak1-batpak-2f-batpak-3f or https://kogalla.com/ and click on “Recall Information” for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kogalla rechargeable USB power banks - front
Recalled Kogalla rechargeable USB power banks - back

Injuries.Name: Zyntony has received two reports of lithium-ion battery overheating and catching fire, even when not in use, resulting in one minor burn injury and property damage, totaling about $3,300.

Retailers.Name: Available on Kogalla.com from June 2024 to January 2025 for the BP125 and BatPak 2F models, as well as the trail lights sold with them; and from August 2024 to January 2025 for the BatPak 3F model and its associated trail lights. Prices ranged from $45 to $90 for the power banks and from $180 to $230 for the trail lights with power banks.

Importers.Name: Zyntony Inc. of Draper, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition of lithium-ion batteries, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled power banks and contact Kogalla for a free replacement, including shipping. An online form at https://rtn.kogalla.com/products/request-batpak-replacement must be completed. Proper disposal of the recalled power banks is also required per local regulations.

metaDescription: Zyntony recalls Kogalla power banks due to fire and burn hazards from lithium-ion battery overheating. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: AMTRADING is recalling Hoovy Cooling Gel Insert Pads designed for use in strollers and car seats because they can overheat when exposed to sunlight or left in hot vehicles. This overheating poses a burn hazard to young children. The company has received a report of a child sustaining second-degree burns from the product.
Title: AMTRADING Recalls Hoovy Cooling Gel Insert Pads for Car Seats and Strollers Due to Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact AMTRADING at 888-259-1411 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email info@hoovyproducts.com. Additional information is available at https://hoovyproducts.com; click on 'Recall' at the bottom or visit https://hoovyproducts.com/pages/product-recall directly.

Images.Caption: Recalled Hoovy Cooling Gel Insert Pads shown in stroller
Recalled Hoovy Cooling Gel Insert Pads

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one consumer report where the child experienced second degree burns to his back, requiring medical attention.

Retailers.Name: Target stores nationwide and online at Target.com and Walmart.com from March 2025 through July 2025 for between $20 and $25.

Importers.Name: AMTRADING LLC, of Brielle, NJ

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to overheating in sunlight or hot vehicles

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled pads and contact AMTRADING for a full refund. Visit https://hoovyproducts.com/pages/product-recall for registration and disposal instructions.

metaDescription: AMTRADING recalls Hoovy Cooling Gel Insert Pads for strollers and car seats due to burn hazard from overheating in sunlight or hot vehicles.

Description: Mad Rabbit is recalling its RELIEVE lidocaine numbing cream because the packaging does not meet child-resistant standards, posing a poisoning risk to young children. The product’s container is a short, wide-mouthed jar that violates the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. No injuries have been reported. The recall addresses the serious safety hazard related to packaging that could allow children to access the cream.
Title: Mad Rabbit Recalls RELIEVE Lidocaine Numbing Cream for Poisoning Risk Due to Non-Child-Resistant Packaging
ConsumerContact: For more information, reach out to Mad Rabbit via email at product@madrabbit.com or visit their website at https://www.madrabbit.com/pages/product-recalls. You can also contact them through https://www.madrabbit.com/pages/contact-us and select "Product Recall".

Images.Caption: Recalled RELIEVE Numbing Cream – Outer Packaging
Recalled RELIEVE Numbing Cream

Retailers.Name: Available at trade shows, tattoo shops, and online at MadRabbit.com from October 2024 through August 2025 for approximately $26.

Distributors.Name: Mad Rabbit Inc., of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging of lidocaine numbing cream poses poisoning risk to young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the product and contact Mad Rabbit for a free child-resistant lid.

metaDescription: Mad Rabbit recalls RELIEVE lidocaine numbing cream due to poisoning hazard from non-child-resistant packaging posing risk to young children.

Description: Sunbeam Products is recalling Oster French Door Countertop Ovens because the spring-loaded glass doors can close unexpectedly, posing a burn hazard. The recall includes multiple models identified by specific model numbers and the Oster brand marking. There have been reports of burn injuries related to this issue, including cases of second-degree burns.
Title: Sunbeam Recalls Oster French Door Countertop Ovens for Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sunbeam Products Inc. at 800-334-0759 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://recall.oster.com or www.oster.com and select 'Product Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: Oster French Door Countertop Oven
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,290,000 (In addition, about 104,195 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Model TSSTTVFDMAF Oster French Door Countertop Oven
Recalled Model TSSTTVFDDAF Oster French Door Countertop Oven
Recalled Model TSSTTVFDXL Oster French Door Countertop Oven
Recalled Model TSSTTVFDDG Oster French Door Countertop Oven

Injuries.Name: Sunbeam Products Inc. has received 95 reports of the doors unexpectedly closing, resulting in burn injuries to consumers, including two reports of second-degree burns.

Retailers.Name: Available at Bed Bath and Beyond, Costco, Walmart, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com and Overstock.com from August 2015 to July 2025, priced between $140 and $250.

Importers.Name: Sunbeam Products Inc., of Atlanta, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn due to doors closing unexpectedly

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled ovens and contact Sunbeam Products Inc. for a complimentary repair kit, which includes a clip-on device to secure the doors open, along with installation instructions and a QR code for a video tutorial. No tools are needed for installation.

metaDescription: Sunbeam recalls Oster French Door Countertop Ovens due to burn hazard from doors closing unexpectedly. A free repair kit is available to address the risk.

Description: EnHomee has recalled its Fabric 13-Drawer Dressers because they pose tip-over and entrapment hazards if not properly anchored to the wall. These dressers fail to meet mandatory safety standards and present a risk of serious injury or death, particularly to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: EnHomee Fabric 13-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach EnHomee at 833-668-8899 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email enhomeestore@gmail.com, or visit https://enhomee.store/recall.html for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled EnHomee 13-drawer dresser (front)
Recalled EnHomee 13-drawer dresser (back)
Recalled EnHomee 13-drawer dresser (side)

Manufacturers.Name: Changzhou Jiaxuan Intelligent Furniture Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon, Walmart, Wayfair, Shein, TikTok, Shopify, and Temu from September 2023 to March 2025 for rustic brown units, and from September 2023 to May 2024 for white, black, and pink units, priced between $75 and $120.

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment, especially to children, if not anchored to the wall.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the dressers if unanchored, keep them away from children, and contact EnHomee for disposal instructions to receive a full refund.

metaDescription: EnHomee recalls Fabric 13-Drawer Dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards posing serious injury risks, especially to children if unanchored.

Description: Five Below is recalling two styles of tabletop fire pits because of a risk of flash fire caused by fuel leakage, which can result in serious burn injuries. The recalled models include one with a smooth black base and another with a beveled gray base, both featuring a stainless-steel fuel cup, lid, and flame cover. There has been one report of flames escaping from a fire pit, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Five Below Recalls Tabletop Fire Pits Due to Risk of Serious Burn Injuries
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Five Below at 844-452-3569 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email Recalls@FiveBelow.com, or visit https://www.fivebelow.com/info/five-below-recalls-tabletop-fire-pit. More details can also be found by clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of https://www.fivebelow.com/.

Recalled Five Below Tabletop Fire Pit packaging – smooth model

Recalled Five Below Tabletop Fire Pit – beveled model
Recalled Five Below Tabletop Fire Pit packaging – beveled model

Injuries.Name: Five Below has received one report of flames escaping from a tabletop firepit. No injuries have been reported.

en.Title: Five Below Recalls Tabletop Fire Pits Due to Risk of Serious Burn Injuries
en.Description: Five Below is recalling two styles of tabletop fire pits because of a risk of flash fire caused by fuel leakage, which can result in serious burn injuries. The recalled models include one with a smooth black base and another with a beveled gray base, both featuring a stainless-steel fuel cup, lid, and flame cover. There has been one report of flames escaping from a fire pit, but no injuries have been reported.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Five Below at 844-452-3569 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email Recalls@FiveBelow.com, or visit https://www.fivebelow.com/info/five-below-recalls-tabletop-fire-pit. More details can also be found by clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of https://www.fivebelow.com/.
en.metaDescription: Five Below recalls tabletop fire pits due to risk of serious burn injuries from flame jetting and fire hazards. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: IcyBreeze has recalled the Buddy portable misting fan due to a risk of overheating and fire during charging. The fan uses a rechargeable lithium-ion battery and features a 1.5-liter water tank for misting. There have been reports of the fan overheating, including instances that caused fire, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: IcyBreeze Buddy Portable Misting Fans Recalled for Fire Hazard During Charging
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact IcyBreeze at 817-900-2664 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email support@icybreeze.com, or visit https://www.icybreeze.com and click on "Recalls" at the bottom of the page or go to https://www.icybreeze.com/buddy-recall-information.

Images.Caption: Recalled IcyBreeze Buddy Portable Misting Fan in slate blue

Injuries.Name: The firm has received seven reports of the misting fan overheating while charging, with two of those reports resulting in fire. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at www.icybreeze.com and Amazon.com from November 2023 through August 2024 for approximately $150.

Importers.Name: IcyBreeze Cooling LLC, of Grapevine, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and fire during charging

Remedies.Name: Stop using the fan immediately and follow the online instructions to participate in the recall for a $150 credit with Solo Brands.

Description: Arizer has recalled the Solo II portable vaporizers due to risks of overheating, smoke, and material ejection from the lithium-ion battery, which can cause fire and burn hazards. The recall affects units with serial numbers starting with 'M2'. The company has received reports of battery malfunctions but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Arizer Solo II Portable Vaporizers Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the Arizer Solo II immediately and reach out to 7111495 Canada at 888-291-0521 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email recall@arizer.com or visit https://arizer.com/solo-ii-voluntary-recall-submission-form/ or www.arizer.ca and click on 'Product Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: Arizer Solo II Portable Vaporizer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5,460 (in addition, about 2,820 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Arizer Solo II portable electronic vaporizer devices in Blue and Black

Injuries.Name: The firm has received nine reports of the battery overheating, producing smoke, or ejecting material. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at specialty, health and wellness, and adult novelty stores across the country, as well as on arizer.com from June 2024 through July 2025 for approximately $170.

Importers.Name: 111495 Canada Inc., dba Arizer Tech, of Waterloo, Ontario

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating, smoke production, and material ejection from the lithium-ion battery, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the vaporizer and contact 7111495 Canada for a complimentary replacement. Affected customers will receive a Solo II MAX and wax for safe disposal. The recalled unit must be disabled with the provided wax in the charging port and disposed of at a hazardous waste facility. Do not discard this lithium-ion battery or device in regular trash or recycling bins. Check with local hazardous waste centers for proper disposal options.

metaDescription: Arizer Solo II vaporizers are recalled due to fire and burn hazards from lithium-ion battery overheating and smoke. No injuries reported.

Description: LXDHSTRA has recalled baby loungers and crib bumpers that fail to meet federal safety standards for infant sleep products. The loungers have low sides, thick sleeping pads, and wide openings that pose suffocation and fall risks. The crib bumpers are banned under federal law due to suffocation hazards. No injuries have been reported in connection with these products.
Title: LXDHSTRA Baby Loungers and Crib Bumpers Recalled for Suffocation and Fall Hazards Violating Federal Infant Sleep Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to LXDHSTRA via email at lxdhstrarecall@gmail.com.

Recalled LXDSTRA Baby Lounger -Tan Star

Recalled LXDSTRA Baby Lounger - Gray Rainbow
Crib bumper that came with the recalled LXDSTRA baby lounger – animal characters
Crib bumper that came with the recalled LXDSTRA baby lounger – alphabet

Retailers.Name: Amazon from May 2025 through August 2025 for between $30 and $34.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshilongxingdashangmaoyouxiangongsi, dba LXDHSTRA, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation and fall hazards due to noncompliance with federal safety standards for infant sleep products

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use immediately and contact LXDHSTRA for a full refund. They must also destroy the loungers and bumpers as instructed.

Description: Youbeien crib mobiles are recalled because their battery compartments pose a risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion. The mobiles include a music box, remote control, and four animal characters. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Youbeien Crib Mobiles Recalled for Battery Ingestion Hazard Sold by GKKBSJ on Amazon
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact GKKBSJ via email at gkkbsjtoys@outlook.com.

Recalled Youbeien Crib Mobile in purple

Retailers.Name: Amazon from May 2024 through April 2025 for about $20.

Distributors.Name: Shantou Xihong Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion due to accessible battery compartment.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product and contact GKKBSJ for a refund. Dispose of the product and send a photo for a full refund or send a photo of the disposed remote for an $8 refund.

Description: Wuensche USA is recalling Ambiano Cotton Candy Makers, model 836098, due to a fire hazard linked to the heating element. Using the device without the designated sugar receptacle can cause sugar to ignite. The recall follows reports of sparking and fire, though no injuries have been reported.
Title: Wuensche USA Recalls Ambiano Cotton Candy Makers Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled cotton candy maker immediately. For assistance, contact ALDI at (800) 325-7894 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, visit https://help.aldi.us/contact-form-product, or email Wuensche USA at service@801service.net. Additional information can be found by visiting aldi.us and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Ambiano Cotton Candy Maker – Red
Recalled Ambiano Cotton Candy Maker – Teal
Recalled Ambiano Cotton Candy Maker – Rating Label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 12 reports of sparking or fire. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: ALDI retail stores nationwide between August 2024 and September 2024 for $15.

Importers.Name: Wuensche USA, Chicago, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential ignition of sugar when used without the sugar receptacle.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use and contact Wuensche or ALDI for a refund. Return the intact cotton candy maker to any ALDI store for a full refund or email Wuensche with a photo of the unplugged device and model details.

metaDescription: Wuensche USA recalls Ambiano Cotton Candy Makers for fire hazard risk from sugar ignition when used without the sugar receptacle.

Description: YooxArmor is recalling multi-purpose kids' helmets that do not comply with mandatory bicycle helmet safety standards. The helmets fail in impact attenuation, positional stability, labeling, and certification, increasing the risk of serious head injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: YooxArmor Multi-Purpose Kids' Helmets Recalled for Failing Bicycle Helmet Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For further details, reach out to YooxArmor via email at service@yooxarmor.com or through their Amazon Seller Profile: YooxArmor.

Images.Caption: Recalled YooxArmor Multi-Purpose Helmet (front view)
Recalled YooxArmor Multi-Purpose Helmet (side view)
Recalled YooxArmor Multi-Purpose Helmet (side view)
Recalled YooxArmor Multi-Purpose Helmet (rear view)

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from October 2024 through July 2025 for approximately $30.

Distributors.Name: Yiwu Shi Yuxing Keji Youxiangongsi, dba YooxArmor, of China

Hazards.Name: These helmets do not meet the mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, failing in impact attenuation, positional stability, labeling, and certification. This non-compliance increases the risk of serious injury or death in the event of an accident.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the helmets immediately and contact YooxArmor for a full refund. To facilitate the refund, consumers should destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps and can send photos of the altered helmet to service@yooxarmor.com.

metaDescription: YooxArmor recalls kids' helmets that fail safety standards, posing a serious head injury hazard. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: Blueroot Health is recalling certain Bariatric Fusion Iron Multivitamin bottles because their packaging does not meet child-resistant standards, posing a risk of child poisoning. The recall includes specific 90 and 270-count bottles of high ADEK multivitamins and 90-count One Per Day bariatric multivitamins containing 45 mg of iron. The affected bottles have white and orange designs with smooth caps lacking the required child-resistant features. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Blueroot Health Recalls Bariatric Fusion Iron Multivitamins for Child Poisoning Hazard Due to Non-Compliant Packaging
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Blueroot Health at 866-259-0602 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email support@bariatricfusion.com or visit bariatricfusion.com/product-safety for further details.

Products.Name: Bariatric Fusion High ADEK Multivitamin Capsules

Images.Caption: Recalled Bottle of Bariatric Fusion High ADEK Multivitamin
Recalled Bottle of Bariatric Fusion One Per Day Bariatric Multivitamin
Lot number 0066J4, 0065J4, 0453B5 or 0370B5 is printed on the bottom of the bottle

Manufacturers.Name: VitaQuest International, LLC of Caldwell, New Jersey

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com, bariatricfusion.com, and other online platforms from January 2025 to June 2025, priced between $23 and $57.

Distributors.Name: Blueroot Health, Inc., of Middletown, Connecticut

Hazards.Name: Risk of child poisoning due to non-compliant packaging lacking child-resistant features.

Remedies.Name: Secure the recalled bottles away from children and contact Blueroot Health for a free child-resistant cap.

Description: Epoca International has recalled Paris Hilton Mini Beauty Fridges due to an electrical switch defect that can short circuit and cause overheating, posing fire and burn hazards. The recall covers 4-liter and 10-liter models in multiple colors. The company has received reports of units overheating and catching fire, resulting in property damage but no reported injuries.
Title: Epoca International Recalls Paris Hilton Mini Beauty Fridges Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Epoca International at 888-262-3905 from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@epoca.com, or visit www.epoca.com/minifridgerecall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Paris Hilton Mini Beauty Fridge (pink)
Recalled Paris Hilton Mini Beauty Fridge (white)
Recalled Paris Hilton Mini Beauty Fridge (aqua)

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of 27 reports of the mini fridge overheating and catching on fire. No injuries have been reported. Property damage has included damage to the unit and surrounding surfaces.

Retailers.Name: Walmart, Ross and online at Walmart.com and Amazon.com and other e-commerce sites from November 2022 through July 2025. The 4-liter model sold for approximately $30, and the 10-liter model sold for approximately $60.

Importers.Name: Epoca International LLC, of Boca Raton, Florida

Hazards.Name: Electrical switch can short circuit, leading to overheating and posing fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the fridge, unplug it, and register for the recall online. To obtain a refund, complete the online form and submit three photos as specified.

metaDescription: Paris Hilton Mini Beauty Fridges recalled for fire and burn hazards caused by electrical switch short circuit. No injuries reported, risk of overheating.

Description: URMYWO baby loungers with a gray feather design are recalled for failing to meet mandatory infant sleep product safety standards. The loungers have short sides and a thick sleeping pad that pose suffocation risks, and infants may fall or become trapped. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: URMYWO Baby Loungers Recalled for Suffocation and Fall Hazards Violating Infant Sleep Product Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Pomona via email at urmyworecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled URMYWO Baby Lounger – Grey Feather
Tracking label located on side of recalled baby lounger
Label with “URMYWO” brand name on side of recalled baby lounger

Retailers.Name: Amazon from January 2024 through April 2025 for between $30 and $50.

Distributors.Name: Dong Guan Bo Mo Na Ke Ji You Xian Gong Si, of China, doing business as Pomona

Hazards.Name: The loungers do not meet mandatory safety standards for Infant Sleep Products, as their sides are shorter than required, the sleeping pad is too thick, creating a suffocation risk, and infants may fall out or get trapped. Additionally, the lack of a stand increases the fall hazard, making these loungers unsafe for infant use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the loungers immediately and reach out to Pomona for a full refund. To process the refund, remove the sleeping pad, cut the sides of the loungers and the pad, and email a photo of the destroyed product to urmyworecall@outlook.com.

metaDescription: URMYWO baby loungers recalled due to suffocation and fall hazards from noncompliance with infant sleep product safety standards.

Description: CT-ENERGY has recalled its Lithium Coin Battery Chargers, model nc-02, because the lithium coin batteries are accessible to children and the packaging does not meet child-resistant standards. This poses a risk of serious injury or death if batteries are ingested. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: CT-ENERGY Lithium Coin Battery Chargers Recalled for Risk of Injury from Battery Ingestion
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to CT New Energy Technology via email at info@ct-energy.cn, or visit their website at www.ct-energy.cn/about/22.html?land=en-us. Click on "Urgent Product Safety Recall Notice" at the top of the page for additional details.

Products.Name: CT-ENERGY Lithium Coin Battery Charger Set

Images.Caption: Recalled CT-ENERGY Lithium Coin Battery Charger with Rechargeable 2032 Batteries

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon.com and Walmart.com from March 2024 to May 2025 for approximately $17.

Distributors.Name: CT (Foshan) New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. (dba CT-Energy), of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion due to non-child-resistant packaging and accessibility of batteries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the charger, remove batteries, and contact for a full refund or replacement kit.

Description: DT Swiss is recalling specific carbon fiber road wheels due to a defect that may compromise their structural integrity, creating a potential crash hazard. The recall includes ERC, CRC, HEC, and ARC models with DT Swiss ID codes of 2740000 or higher. The company has received reports of the outer carbon layer separating near the rim flange, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: DT Swiss Recalls Carbon Fiber Road Wheels for Potential Crash Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DT Swiss toll-free at 800-000-1994 from 8:30 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email Recall@dtswiss.com. More information is available online at https://www.dtswiss.com/recall or by visiting www.dtswiss.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: DT Swiss Carbon Fiber Road Wheels
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 6,000 (In addition, about 150 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled DT Swiss Carbon Fiber Road Wheels

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the outermost carbon layer separating around the rim flange. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: DT Swiss, of Grand Junction, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available through independent bicycle dealers across the nation and as original equipment on various bicycle brands from September 2024 to July 2025, priced between $1,000 and $1,880.

Hazards.Name: Potential crash hazard due to structural integrity defect

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled wheels and contact DT Swiss for a replacement. Register the recalled wheels at www.dtswiss.com/recall.

metaDescription: DT Swiss recalls carbon fiber road wheels due to a structural defect that poses a crash hazard. No injuries reported but use caution if you own affected wheels.

Description: Lulive has recalled its 12-drawer dressers because they pose tip-over and entrapment hazards that can cause serious injury or death, especially to children. These dressers are unstable unless anchored to a wall, which violates mandatory safety standards under the STURDY Act. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Lulive 12-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards Violating STURDY Act
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Lulive at 866-610-4348 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email lulive12recall@sina.com, or visit www.lulive.com/recalls. More information can also be found by clicking the "Recall" link on the Lulive homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Lulive 12-Drawer Dresser (front)
Recalled Lulive 12-Drawer Dresser (side)
Recalled Lulive 12-Drawer Dresser (rear)

Manufacturers.Name: Changzhou Hengze Home Furnishing Co., of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from March 2025 to June 2025 for prices ranging from $90 to $100.

Distributors.Name: Lulive Home US, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment, posing serious injury or death to children; violates STURDY Act standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the dressers if they are not wall-anchored and keep them out of children's reach. Contact Lulive for disposal instructions to receive a full refund, including sending a disposal photo to lulive12recall@sina.com.

metaDescription: Lulive recalls 12-drawer dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards posing serious injury risks to children. Dressers must be anchored to prevent tip-over.

Description: Electric motors used in gable and roof-mounted attic fans have been recalled due to a fire hazard caused by potential short circuits and failure of the safety cutoff. The affected motors include specific models identifiable by a unique vent pattern on the motor end bells. The recall follows multiple reports of burning or fire incidents, though no injuries have been reported.
Title: Recall of Electric Motors for Gable and Roof-Mounted Attic Fans by Air Vent Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Air Vent at 877-247-4878 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://powermaxmotorrecall.expertinquiry.com or https://gibraltarbuildingproducts.com/ventilation-category/ and select 'Important Safety Information' for further details.

Images.Caption: Sample Roof-Mounted Attic Fan with Recalled Electric Motor
Sample Gable-Mounted Attic Fan with Recalled Electric Motor

Recalled Electric Motor Vent Patterns and Unaffected Motor Vent Patterns (Recalled electric motor vents (depicted on the left) align on the outer ring of the motor end bell, as highlighted in yellow. Recalled electric motor vents use an alternating pattern of two long vents then two short vents. Unaffected motor vents (depicted on the right) align on the inner ring of the motor end bell, as highlighted in yellow. Unaffected motor vents use an alternating vent pattern of one long, then one short vent)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 159 reports of incidents related to the recalled attic fan motors involving burning or fire. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at various retailers nationwide, including ABC Supply, Lowe's, and Beacon, both in-store and online from August 2003 to December 2013, priced between $74 and $92.

Distributors.Name: Air Vent Inc., of Dallas, Texas

Hazards.Name: Potential short circuit and fire hazard due to safety cutoff failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the motors and contact Air Vent for a refund after verifying the recall

metaDescription: Electric motors for gable and roof-mounted attic fans recalled for fire hazard risk from potential short circuit and safety cutoff failure.

Description: IBC Technologies has recalled certain CX Series Combi Boilers due to a risk of overheating water, which can cause burns. The recall includes specific models and serial numbers produced between April 2023 and April 2025. The company has received reports of fluctuating hot water temperatures but no injuries have been reported. Affected boilers will be repaired free of charge by authorized technicians.
Title: IBC Technologies Recalls CX Series Combi Boilers for Burn Hazard from Overheating Water
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact IBC at 844-432-8422 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email sales@ibcboiler.com, or visit www.ibcboiler.com/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: CX Series Combi Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,100 (In addition, about 880 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled IBC CX 199 Combi Boiler
Location of Model Number and Serial Number on the Rating Plate Inside the Recalled CX Combi Boiler

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the hot water temperature fluctuating while in use. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through home improvement distributors nationwide from May 2023 to June 2025, priced between $4,700 and $5,100

Importers.Name: IBC Technologies USA Inc., of Lawnside, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to overheating water

Remedies.Name: Free in-home repair by an authorized technician, including installation of a mixing valve if necessary

metaDescription: IBC Technologies recalls CX Series Combi Boilers due to burn hazard from overheating water. Affected units will receive free repairs by authorized technicians.

Description: STWUQIKONG has recalled three-tiered spiral tower toys designed for children under three due to a choking hazard from small balls included with the toy. Title: STWUQIKONG Children's Spiral Tower Toys Recalled for Choking Hazard Due to Small Balls
ConsumerContact: Parents and guardians should contact STWUQIKONG via email at STWUQIKONG_RECALL@hotmail.com for assistance.

Description: Firefly Fuel Inc. has recalled Safe & Green 32 oz. fuel bottles because they do not meet child-resistant packaging standards, creating a poisoning hazard for young children. The bottles contain hydrocarbons and are labeled as 'Non-Toxic' and 'Readily Biodegradable,' but the packaging fails to prevent child access. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Firefly Safe & Green 32 oz. Fuel Bottles Recalled for Poisoning Hazard to Young Children
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Firefly Fuel Inc. at 888-472-6740 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email support@fireflyfuel.com, or visit www.fireflyfuel.com and click on 'Recall' for further details.

Products.Name: Firefly Safe & Green 32 oz. Fuel Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Firefly Safe & Green 32 oz. fuel bottles
Recalled Firefly Kosher Safe & Green 32 oz. bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Firefly Fuels Inc., of Sarasota, Florida

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and www.fireflyfuel.com from August 2019 through May 2025 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: The fuel bottles contain hydrocarbons that are not in child-resistant packaging, violating safety standards and posing a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should replace the nozzle with the original child-resistant closure and secure the bottle out of children's reach. Firefly Fuel offers a free corrected label and replacement child-resistant lid upon request.

metaDescription: Firefly Safe & Green 32 oz. fuel bottles recalled due to poisoning hazard from non-child-resistant packaging posing risk to young children.

Description: ESR HaloLock wireless power banks, including models 2G520, 2G505B, and 2G512B, are recalled due to fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The recall follows reports of battery fires and explosions resulting in property damage. No injuries have been reported in connection with these incidents.
Title: ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Banks Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately cease using the affected power banks and reach out to Waymeet at 888-990-0280 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday. They can also email support@esrtech.com or visit https://www.esrtech.com/pages/recall for more information.

Products.Name: ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Banks
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 24,000 (In addition, about 9,900 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G520
Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505B
Recalled ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Bank Model 2G512B

Injuries.Name: The firm has received nine reports of the lithium-ion battery catching fire and exploding, resulting in property damage of about $20,000. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com, Homedepot.com, and Esrtech.com from September 2023 to July 2025, priced between $32 and $40.

Distributors.Name: Waymeet Limited, of Hong Kong

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the power banks and contact Waymeet for a full refund

metaDescription: ESR HaloLock wireless power banks recalled due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Use poses serious safety risks.

Description: Synergy Housewares is recalling Wolfgang Puck Petite Tea Kettles because the removable infuser basket lid can detach during use, posing a burn hazard. The stainless-steel kettles come in multiple colors and have specific lot codes on the underside. Reports include incidents where the lid fell off, causing burns or scalding injuries.
Title: Synergy Housewares Recalls Wolfgang Puck Petite Tea Kettles Due to Burn Hazard from Detaching Lid
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Synergy Housewares at 855-837-4111, available Monday to Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or visit their website at https://claims.synergyhousewares.com/recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled Wolfgang Puck Petite Kettle
Wolfgang Puck Petite Kettle appears on the kettle's underside

Injuries.Name: Synergy Housewares has received at least 10 reports of incidents involving the kettle lids, including 5 resulting in injuries such as burns or scalding from the lid falling off the kettle during use.

Retailers.Name: Available on HSN.com and through the HSN television network from August 2020 to January 2025 for approximately $20.

Importers.Name: Synergy Housewares LLC, of Coconut Creek, Florida

Hazards.Name: Loose infuser basket lid can detach during use, creating a burn hazard.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the kettle and contact Synergy Housewares for a complimentary replacement lid.

Description: Viewrail is recalling the E2-WR electronic LED modules used in floating stairs and rails because they can overheat and pose a fire hazard. These rectangular white modules are installed inside the metal stringer tube at the back of the stairs. The company has received reports of modules overheating or melting, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Viewrail Recalls E2-WR LED Modules for Floating Stairs and Rails Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Viewrail at their toll-free number (888) 500-2151, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, inquiries can be sent via email to info@viewrail.com or through their website at https://www.viewrail.com/recall or https://www.viewrail.com/support by selecting 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: LED Modules for Floating Stairs and Rails

Images.Caption: Recalled LED module
The recalled module is located inside the metal tubing supporting the stairs

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 20 reports of modules overheating or melting. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Viewrail.com from August 2023 to June 2025 for approximately $80

Importers.Name: Viewrail, of Goshen, Indiana

Hazards.Name: Overheating LED module posing a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Free replacement of the module or a refund check for the purchase price

Description: AliExpress has recalled LeymanKids children's two-piece pajama sets because they do not meet mandatory flammability standards. The pajamas pose a risk of burn injuries or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: AliExpress Recalls LeymanKids Children's Pajama Sets for Flammability Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to AliExpress via their online service at https://helpcenter.aliexpress.com/s/BuyerHelp/, email at us_product_recall@aliexpress.com, or visit www.aliexpress.com and click on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Recalled LeymanKids Pajama Set – Green

Retailers.Name: AliExpress.com from March 2024 through May 2025 for about $6.

Importers.Name: AliExpress, of San Mateo, California

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Jubilant Network Technology Co., LTD dba Little Girl Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Violation of mandatory flammability standards for children's sleepwear, leading to potential burn injuries or fatalities.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled pajamas immediately and contact AliExpress for a full refund. They must destroy the pajamas by cutting them in half, take a photo of the destroyed items, and send it to us_product_recall@aliexpress.com or respond to the recall email from AliExpress.

Description: Deale International is recalling Altafit af28 smartwatches because their wireless charging pads can overheat, posing fire and burn hazards. Reports include incidents of the smartwatches melting, burning, and catching fire during charging, resulting in burn injuries and property damage. The watches feature a 1.68-inch touchscreen and come with interchangeable bands in gold and silver finishes.
Title: Deale International Recalls Altafit af28 Smartwatches Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Deale International at 877-880-6137 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email altafitrecall@thedeale.com. More information is available at www.thedeale.com/pages/altafit-safety-recall or visit www.thedeale.com and select "RECALL INFORMATION" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Altafit af28 Smartwatch
Recalled Altafit af28 Smartwatch wireless charging pad

Injuries.Name: Deale International has received 39 reports of smartwatches melting, burning and catching on fire while charging, including six reports of burns to hands and property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available on HSN.com from March 2025 through May 2025 for approximately $50.

Importers.Name: Deale International LLC, of Fort Lauderdale, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition from the smartwatch's wireless charging pad, leading to potential burn injuries and fire hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately disconnect the smartwatch's charging pad and cease charging the device. For a free replacement, contact Deale International for further instructions. Consumers will need to cut the power cable of the unplugged charging pad and send a photo of it to altafitrecall@thedeale.com.

metaDescription: Deale International recalls Altafit af28 smartwatches due to fire and burn hazards from overheating wireless charging pads.

Description: Fieldsheer Apparel Technologies has recalled heated socks due to risks of burns and blisters caused by heat, friction, moisture, and pressure during intense activities. The recall includes multiple models with lithium-ion battery packs and remote or Bluetooth controls. The company received reports of pain and discomfort, including burn injuries. Proper disposal of the lithium-ion batteries is essential to avoid fire hazards.
Title: Fieldsheer Heated Socks Recalled for Burn and Blister Injury Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Fieldsheer Apparel Technologies at 888-908-6024, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, email cs@fieldsheer.com or visit https://fieldsheer.com/pages/recall-information for more details.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 11 reports of socks causing pain and discomfort, resulting in 4 burns and blisters.

Retailers.Name: Available at Scheels All Sports, Fred Meyer, Home Depot, Meijer, Bomgaars Supply, Buchheits, Running Supply, Fleet Farm Wholesale Supply, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com from August 2021 through June 2025, priced between $80 and $130.

Importers.Name: Tech Gear 5.7 Inc., dba Fieldsheer Apparel Technologies, of Carlsbad, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of burns and blisters due to heat, friction, moisture, and pressure during intense activities.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heated socks and contact Fieldsheer for a full refund or a replacement. Dispose of lithium-ion battery packs according to local regulations.

Description: La Ligne is recalling Enfant Bonne Nuit children's pajamas made of 100% cotton because they do not meet mandatory flammability standards, posing a fire hazard. The two-piece, long-sleeve sets come in multiple colors and sizes for children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: La Ligne Recalls Enfant Bonne Nuit Pajamas for Fire Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For further information, contact La Ligne via email at lou@lalignenyc.com or visit https://lalignenyc.com/pages/enfant-bonne-nuit-pajamas-recall. You can also find details by clicking 'Recall' at the bottom of the homepage.

Products.Name: La Ligne Enfant Bonne Nuit Pajamas

Images.Caption: Recalled La Ligne Enfant Bonne Nuit Pajamas in Navy with White Trim
Recalled La Ligne Enfant Bonne Nuit Pajamas in Red with White Trim

Recalled La Ligne Enfant Bonne Nuit Pajamas in Light Blue with Red Trim
Recalled La Ligne Enfant Bonne Nuit Pajamas Neck Label and Side Seam Label

Retailers.Name: Available at La Ligne stores nationwide and online at https://lalignenyc.com/ from November 2023 through March 2025 for approximately $75.

Importers.Name: La Ligne LLC, of New York

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to non-compliance with children's sleepwear flammability standards, risking burn injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the pajamas immediately and keep them away from children. To obtain a full refund, contact La Ligne. You will need to destroy the pajamas by cutting them in half and disposing of them, then email a photo of the destroyed item to lou@lalignenyc.com to receive a refund of $75 or a store credit of $100.

Description: Monica Vinader has recalled select 14k Yellow Gold Mini Heart Chain Bracelets, Necklaces, and Stud Earrings due to excessive cadmium levels. Cadmium exposure can pose toxicity risks, particularly to children, and may cause adverse health effects from prolonged skin contact. No injuries have been reported in connection with these products.
Title: Monica Vinader Recalls Select 14k Yellow Gold Mini Heart Jewelry Due to Cadmium Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Monica Vinader at 888-580-8582 from 4 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email productrecall@monicavinader.com or visit www.monicavinader.com/recall-hearts for more details.

Products.Name: 14k Yellow Gold Mini Heart Chain Bracelet

Recalled 14k Yellow Gold Mini Heart Necklace (SKU YG-NK-HART-NON)
Recalled 14k Yellow Gold Mini Heart Stud Earrings (SKU YG-EA-HARS-NON)

Retailers.Name: Available at Monica Vinader and Nordstrom retail locations, as well as online at www.monicavinader.com/us and www.nordstrom.com from January 20, 2025, to March 2025 for Monica Vinader, and from November 26, 2024, to May 2025 for Nordstrom, priced between $250 and $350.

Importers.Name: Monica Vinader Inc., of New York, New York
Monica Vinader Retail LLC, of Wilmington, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Excess cadmium levels posing toxicity risks, especially to young children, and potential adverse health effects from prolonged skin contact.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease wearing the recalled items immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Monica Vinader for a replacement or refund. Returns can be made using a prepaid shipping label for a full refund or replacement. Nordstrom customers may also return the items directly to Nordstrom.

Description: Petmate is recalling Pet Zone Laser Pointer and LED Ball pet toys because broken LED balls can expose button cell batteries, creating a serious ingestion hazard. These toys violate mandatory safety standards for products containing button cell batteries. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Petmate Recalls Pet Zone Laser Pointer and LED Ball Pet Toys for Button Cell Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using these toys immediately and reach out to Petmate at 888-847-8716 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information is available online at https://recallrtr.com/pettoys or https://petmate.com by clicking 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pet Zone Laser Pointer and LED Ball Pet Toys
Item #:15712EAM3 appears on the back of the recalled pet toys

Retailers.Name: Menards stores nationwide and online from September 2024 through March 2025 for about $4.

Importers.Name: Doskocil Manufacturing Company Inc., dba Petmate, of Arlington, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to accessible button cell batteries from broken LED ball, violating safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys and contact Petmate for a full refund. Dispose of button cell batteries following local hazardous waste guidelines.

Description: Amazon is recalling its Basics Premium Foam Eurotop Mattresses because they do not meet federal mattress flammability standards, creating a fire hazard. The recall covers mattresses in multiple sizes with a white top panel, gray side panel, and an Amazon Basics tag on the foot panel. No injuries have been reported related to this issue.
Title: Amazon Basics Premium Foam Eurotop Mattresses Recalled for Fire Hazard Violation
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can contact Amazon at 800-913-4350 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit http://amazoneurotoprecall.expertinquiry.com/ or www.Amazon.com and navigate to the "Recalls and Product Safety Alerts" section.

Products.Name: Amazon Basics Premium Foam Eurotop Mattresses

Images.Caption: Recalled Amazon Basics Premium Foam Eurotop Mattress
The Amazon Basics tag is located on the foot panel of the recalled mattress
The date of manufacture, SKU and prototype ID are on a white label on the center seam of the foot panel

Manufacturers.Name: 3Z Brands, of Glendale, Arizona

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from January 2023 to June 2024, priced between $82 and $278.

Distributors.Name: Amazon.com Services LLC, of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to violation of mattress flammability standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattress immediately and follow Amazon's instructions for obtaining a full refund. Mark the mattress as "Recalled" with a permanent marker, submit a photo of it to http://amazoneurotoprecall.expertinquiry.com, and certify its disposal. A full refund will be issued upon receipt of the photo and certification, either to a valid payment method in the consumer's Amazon wallet or as an Amazon gift card. Dispose of the mattress according to local regulations.

Description: Backyard Kids has recalled the KidKraft Farm to Table play kitchen because of a strangulation hazard. The hazard involves clothing getting caught on hooks located at the back of the unit, posing a risk to children. A fatal incident occurred when a young child became trapped and asphyxiated after clothing caught on one of these hooks. No other injuries have been reported.
Title: Backyard Kids Recalls KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchens Due to Strangulation Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers should reach out to Backyard Kids at 800-882-0234, available Monday through Friday from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at recall@kidkraft.com. More details can also be found online at www.kidkraft.com/pages/recall.

Products.Name: KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchen
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 192,000 (In addition, 12 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchen
Recalled KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchen (the hooks are circled)

Injuries.Name: In February 2023 in Oregon, a 23-month-old child was asphyxiated after his shirt got caught on one of the hooks while climbing and crawling through the opening at the back of the play kitchen where the toy kitchen accessories are held. The child was unable to rescue himself and died from strangulation and asphyxia.

Manufacturers.Name: KidKraft Inc., of Dallas, Texas

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at KidKraft.com, Amazon.com, and Walmart.com from 2018 until July 2025, priced between $120 and $270.

Distributors.Name: Backyard Kids, LLC, of Plano, Texas

Hazards.Name: Strangulation hazard due to clothing getting caught on hooks

Remedies.Name: Free replacement hooks provided by Backyard Kids

metaDescription: Backyard Kids recalls KidKraft Farm to Table play kitchens due to a strangulation hazard from hooks, following a fatal incident involving a young child.

Description: Tegu has recalled Magnetic Floating Stackers toys because magnets can detach, creating a risk of ingestion that may lead to serious injury or death. The recall covers specific models featuring wooden stacking pieces connected by magnets. The company has received reports of magnets separating but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Tegu Recalls Magnetic Floating Stackers Toys Due to Magnet Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately remove the toys from children and reach out to Tegu at 877-Tegu-Toy (877-834-8869) from 10 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. For further details, email service@tegu.com or visit https://tegu.com/pages/recall or www.tegu.com and select 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Magnetic Floating Stackers
Products.NumberOfUnits: 22,850 (In addition, about 1,977 in Canada and about 190 in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled Magnetic Floating Stackers toy, model STA-BGY-801T (Rainbow)
Recalled Magnetic Floating Stackers toy, model STA-BTP-806T (Big Top)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 31 reports of magnets separating from the toy stacker. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Tegu Holdings Inc., of Naples, Florida

Retailers.Name: Available at toy, children's, and specialty stores across the nation, as well as online at Tegu.com and Amazon.com from August 2018 through April 2025 for approximately $40.

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to magnets that can detach, posing serious health risks including intestinal blockage and potential death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use immediately and contact Tegu for a free replacement.

metaDescription: Tegu recalls Magnetic Floating Stackers toys due to magnet ingestion hazard that can cause serious injury or death. No injuries reported so far.

Description: Aiho is recalling its five-drawer dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that can cause serious injury or death, particularly to children. The dressers may become unstable if not anchored to a wall. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Aiho Five-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers should contact Aiho via email at support@aiholife.com or visit hiapes.com/pages/aiho-recall. Alternatively, go to https://hiapes.com and click on "Recall" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Aiho Five-Drawer Dresser (front)
Recalled Aiho Five-Drawer Dresser (back)
Recalled Aiho Five-Drawer Dresser (side)
Model number "AP23-W" is printed on the product packaging

Retailers.Name: Available on Walmart.com from Aiho between September 2023 and March 2025 for approximately $80.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Yiqun Technology Co. Ltd. dba Aiho Inc., of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment, especially for children, if not anchored to a wall.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the dressers if they are not wall-anchored and keep them away from children. Contact Aiho for disposal instructions to receive a full refund, and submit a photo of the disposed dresser to support@aiholife.com.

Description: Apollo is recalling certain 2024 City electric scooters because weld line cracking in the stem may cause it to break, posing fall and injury hazards. The company has received reports of stem weld cracking, including incidents where riders fell and one injury with abrasions was reported. The affected scooters are dual-motor models with specific serial numbers. Users should stop using the scooters until the stem is replaced.
Title: Apollo Recalls 2024 City Electric Scooters Due to Stem Weld Cracking Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Apollo at 888-501-8862 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email support@apolloscooters.co, or visit https://apolloscooters.co/pages/apollo-city/recall for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Apollo Electric Scooter

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of the weld cracking on the stem of the scooter, including four riders who reported coming off the scooter; one reported injury including abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy stores across the country and online at Apolloscooters.co, Amazon.com, and BestBuy.com from June 2024 to April 2025 for approximately $1,800.

Importers.Name: Apollo Imports Inc., of Newark, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Weld line cracking may lead to stem breakage, resulting in fall and injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled scooters and reach out to Apollo for a complimentary replacement stem. To initiate the recall process, visit https://apolloscooters.co/pages/apollo-city/recall and complete the online form. Apollo advises all customers to take their scooters to a service repair location for a free stem replacement.

metaDescription: Apollo recalls 2024 City electric scooters for weld line cracking in the stem that can cause falls and injuries. Stop use and arrange free stem replacement.

Description: Creekwood Naturals is recalling Pure Gum Spirits of Turpentine because the product does not comply with mandatory child-resistant packaging standards. The clear glass bottles with brown labels pose a poisoning risk to young children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Creekwood Naturals Recalls Pure Gum Spirits of Turpentine for Child Poisoning Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Creekwood Naturals at 877-754-1776 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, via email at Sales@CreekwoodNaturals.com, or visit https://CreekwoodNaturals.com/pages/recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Creekwood Naturals 100% Natural Pure Gum Spirits Turpentine bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Creekwood Naturals, of Corona, California

Retailers.Name: Amazon, Etsy, Ebay and Creekwood Naturals from June 2023 through May 2025 for between $20 and $40.

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to lack of child-resistant packaging

Remedies.Name: Free replacement with child-resistant closure

Description: Curtis International is recalling certain Frigidaire minifridge models due to fire and burn hazards caused by potential short circuiting of internal electrical components. The recall involves specific models and serial numbers of 6- and 9-can capacity units. Reports include incidents of smoking, sparking, overheating, and fires, resulting in property damage and some smoke inhalation injuries.
Title: Curtis International Recalls Frigidaire Minifridges for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Curtis International Ltd. at 888-727-0198 from 8 a.m. to 12 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@curtiscs.com, or visit www.recallrtr.com/minifridge.

Products.Name: Frigidaire Minifridge Model EFMIS129 (specific serial numbers only)

Images.Caption: Recalled Curtis International Minifridge Model EFMIS129 - serial numbers between A2001 to A2308 – except for model numbers containing "EFMIS129-B" or "EFMIS129-C"
Recalled Curtis International Minifridge Model EFMIS137 - serial numbers between A2001 to A2312
Recalled Curtis International Minifridge Model EFMIS149 - serial numbers between A2001 to A2308
Recalled Curtis International Minifridge Model EFMIS175 - serial numbers between A2001 to A2310

Injuries.Name: Curtis International has received at least 26 reports of the minifridges smoking, sparking, burning, melting, overheating and catching fire, with property damages totaling more than $700,000. Two consumers reported smoke inhalation injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: ShangYu North Electron Manufacture Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Walmart and other stores nationwide and online at Walmart.com and Amazon.com from January 2020 through December 2023 for between $36 and $40.

Importers.Name: Curtis International Ltd., of Canada

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential short circuiting of internal electrical components.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled minifridges and follow refund instructions at www.recallrtr.com/minifridge. Unplug the unit, cut the power cord, and mark 'Recall' on the front door before disposing of the minifridge according to local regulations.

metaDescription: Curtis International recalls Frigidaire minifridges due to fire and burn hazards from electrical short circuits. Check affected models and serial numbers.

Description: Diamond G Forest Products is recalling 4-ounce bottles of 100% pure gum spirits of turpentine because they do not have child-resistant packaging as required by law. The lack of safety closures creates a risk of serious injury or death if children ingest the product. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Diamond G Forest Products Recalls Turpentine Bottles for Child Poisoning Hazard Due to Non-Child-Resistant Packaging
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Diamond G Forest Products at 912-647-7463 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email diamondgforestproducts@hotmail.com or visit www.diamondgforestproducts.com/recall-information for further details.

Products.Name: Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine

Images.Caption: Recalled Diamond G Forest Products Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine Bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Diamond G Forest Products LLC, of Patterson, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Nature's Corner Market stores in Marietta, Georgia; Cornerstone Retreat stores in Gap, Pennsylvania; Nickel Mine Health Foods stores in Paradise, Pennsylvania; Leaves of The Trees in Memphis, Tennessee; Country Boy's Mini Mall in Bristol, Georgia and online at Amazon from January 2021 through April 2025 for about $17.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the turpentine bottles away from children and contact Diamond G Forest Products for a free replacement child-resistant closure.

en.Title: Diamond G Forest Products Recalls Turpentine Bottles for Child Poisoning Hazard Due to Non-Child-Resistant Packaging
en.Description: Diamond G Forest Products is recalling 4-ounce bottles of 100% pure gum spirits of turpentine because they do not have child-resistant packaging as required by law. The lack of safety closures creates a risk of serious injury or death if children ingest the product. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Diamond G Forest Products at 912-647-7463 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email diamondgforestproducts@hotmail.com or visit www.diamondgforestproducts.com/recall-information for further details.

Description: FENGQS is recalling F7 Pro electric bikes due to a risk of overheating and ignition from the lithium-ion battery, which can cause fire and burn hazards. The recall follows reports of e-bikes catching fire, including incidents causing property damage. These bikes are identified by the FENGQS brand on the downtube and model number F7 Pro on the chain stay near the rear wheel.
Title: FENGQS F7 Pro Electric Bikes Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards from Battery Defect
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled e-bikes and reach out to FENGQS at 607-307-2127 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday. They can also email recall@fengqs.work or visit www.fengqs.com/pages/recalls for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled FENGQS F7 Pro E-bike
The battery's capacity (21.5Ah) and the production date (2024/4/21) are printed on a label on the front of the battery

Injuries.Name: FENGQS has received 13 reports of e-bikes igniting, two resulting in fires with property damage totaling $4,000.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Fengqisi Car Industry Co., Ltd., dba FENGQS, of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from May 2024 to December 2024, priced between $700 and $1,200.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition from the lithium-ion battery, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease use of the recalled e-bikes and contact FENGQS for a full refund. They must mark their name, the word "recalled," and the current date next to the battery's production date with a permanent marker, then submit a photo of this information to recall@fengqs.work. Proper disposal of the battery is crucial; it should not be discarded in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, check with local hazardous waste centers for safe disposal options.

metaDescription: FENGQS recalls F7 Pro electric bikes due to lithium-ion battery fire hazards posing serious injury risks. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: Innovative Designs is recalling Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelets because their battery compartments can be easily opened by children, exposing button cell batteries. These batteries pose a serious ingestion hazard that can cause severe injury or death. One report of a battery separating from the toy has been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Innovative Designs Recalls Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelets for Button Cell Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please email info@innovativenyc.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelet, Model 718508MCR - Front
Recalled Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelet, Model 718508MCR – Back
Recalled Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelet, Model 718075MCR - Front
Recalled Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelet, Model 718075MCR - Back

Injuries.Name: Innovative Designs has received one report of a battery separating from the toy. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Walmart stores nationwide from March 2025 through May 2025 for about $2.

Importers.Name: Innovative Designs LLC, of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion of button cell batteries due to easily accessible battery compartments.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bracelets immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Innovative Designs for disposal instructions and a full refund. To receive the refund, email a photograph of the disposed item to info@innovativenyc.com. Dispose of button cell batteries following local hazardous waste guidelines.

metaDescription: Innovative Designs recalls Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelets due to button cell battery ingestion hazard posing serious injury risks to children.

Description: LIVACTI has recalled retractable safety mesh gates, model LT-BG01-3, due to a risk of serious injury or death from entrapment. The gap between the gate and the floor can allow a child's torso to pass through, posing a significant hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: LIVACTI Retractable Safety Gates Recalled for Entrapment Hazard Violating Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Qeyan and Yanyanny at 833-980-0055 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at support@livactihome.com. More information is available at https://livactihome.com/pages/product-recall or by visiting www.livactihome.com and selecting 'Product Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled LIVACTI Retractable Safety Gate - Black
Recalled LIVACTI Retractable Safety Gate - White
Recalled LIVACTI Retractable Safety Gate - Gray
Model number printed on a white label on the side post of the recalled gate

Retailers.Name: Amazon from December 2024 through April 2025 for between $55 and $75.

Importers.Name: Lichang E-Commerce, Inc, of Hacienda Heights, California

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Yiqiyuan Technology Co. Ltd., dba Qeyan, of China
Shenzhen Yanyan Network Co. Ltd., dba Yanyanny, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to entrapment as a child's torso can fit through the gap between the gate and the floor.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled gates and contact Qeyan and Yanyanny for a full refund. Gates should be destroyed by cutting through the mesh, and a photo of the destroyed gate must be submitted to support@livactihome.com.

metaDescription: LIVACTI retractable safety gates recalled due to entrapment hazard risking serious injury or death. Immediate discontinuation recommended for safety.
