Description: Minongad LED gloves, model ST01, are recalled because their battery compartments can be easily accessed by children, posing a serious ingestion hazard. The gloves include lithium coin batteries that are not packaged with child-resistant features or proper warnings. This violation of mandatory toy safety and battery packaging standards increases the risk of severe injury or death from swallowing batteries. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Minongad LED Gloves Recalled for Battery Ingestion Hazard and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Minongad via email at yummyet2022@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled LED Gloves – lights on
Recalled LED Gloves – lights off
Recalled LED Gloves, Model ST01 – Packaging
Recalled LED Gloves, Model ST01 – Bottom of Package

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com by Minongad from January 2025 through April 2025 for approximately $14.

Distributors.Name: Jinanmingzhongdashangmaoxiaoshouyouxiangongsi, dba Minongad, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gloves, keep them away from children, and dispose of them. Contact Minongad for a full refund.

Description: Transpro is recalling the A3 Hub Motor, A11F Spark, and R1 Commuter electric scooters because their lithium-ion batteries have unauthorized certification labels, creating fire and burn hazards. The batteries are located beneath the scooter deck, increasing the risk of fire. One fire incident has been reported, resulting in substantial property damage but no injuries. The company is offering a full refund or free replacement for affected scooters.
Title: Transpro Recalls A3 Hub Motor, A11F Spark, and R1 Commuter Electric Scooters for Fire Hazard from Unauthorized Battery Labels
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Transpro at 888-616-0328 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email sales@transprous.com, or visit https://transprous.com/recall-escooters.

Images.Caption: Recalled Transpro A3 Hub Motor Electric Scooter
Recalled Transpro A11F Spark Electric Scooter
Recalled Transpro R1 Commuter Electric Scooter

Injuries.Name: Transpro US Inc. has received one report of a scooter catching fire, causing at least $200,000 in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Bike and electronics retailers in the Northeast from August 2024 to April 2025

Importers.Name: Transpro US Inc., of Paterson, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to unauthorized battery certification

Remedies.Name: Full refund or free replacement scooter; proper disposal instructions provided

Description: Bexco Enterprises is recalling Babyletto Kiwi Electronic Recliners and Swivel Gliders equipped with USB charging ports due to an overheating hazard from the USB module sparking. The recall includes specific models identified by labels under the footrest. The company has received multiple reports of overheating incidents, including one minor injury, but no fires or property damage have been reported.
Title: Bexco Enterprises Recalls Babyletto Kiwi Electronic Recliner and Swivel Gliders Due to Overheating Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Babyletto at 866-621-9483 from 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or email kiwi.recall@babyletto.com. Additional information is available online at babylettorecall.com/ or www.babyletto.com under the 'Product Recalls' section.

Products.Name: Babyletto Kiwi Electronic Recliner and Swivel Gliders
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 9,030 (Additionally, about six were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Kiwi Electronic Recliner and Swivel Glider with USB port module shown in chair arm (Performance Cream Eco-Weave)
Closeup of USB port module
Batch/Lot number is found on the label under the footrest

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 37 reports of incidents of overheating, smoking or sparking including one minor injury. No fires or property damage have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Guangzhou Plenty Bicycle Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Bikes with the recalled batteries were sold online at amazon.com, walmart.com, ebay.com, viviebikes.com, wish.com, sears.com, wayfair.com and aliexpress.com from December 2020 through November 2023 for between $365 and $950.

Importers.Name: Bexco Enterprises, Inc. of Pico Rivera, California

Hazards.Name: Overheating risk due to USB module sparking

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recliners and contact Babyletto for a free repair kit, then dispose of the original USB module.

metaDescription: Babyletto Kiwi Electronic Recliner and Swivel Gliders recalled for overheating risk caused by USB module sparking. Check product details for safety.

Description: Deale International is recalling CasaClean Handheld Steamers, model DF-958, because they pose a risk of serious burn injury from leaking steam or hot boiling water. The steamers, sold in white/gray and blue/gray, have caused reported incidents of burns. Consumers should be aware of this hazard and stop using the product immediately.
Title: Deale International Recalls CasaClean Handheld Steamers Due to Serious Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Deale International at 877-880-6137 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, via email at casacleanrecall@thedeale.com, or visit www.thedeale.com/pages/casaclean-safety-recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Model DF-958 CasaClean Handheld Steamer in White/Gray
Recalled Model DF-958 CasaClean Handheld Steamer in Blue/Gray

Injuries.Name: Deale has received 26 reports of the recalled steamers leaking water or steam, including 13 consumers receiving burns.

Retailers.Name: HSN, available from November 2024 through April 2025 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: Deale International LLC, of Fort Lauderdale, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious burn injury due to leaking steam or hot boiling water.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the steamers immediately and reach out to Deale for guidance on safely cutting the cord at the base of the machine. They will need to provide proof of destruction and will receive a refund. A photo of the cut cord must be uploaded along with contact information via the Recall Request form at www.thedeale.com/pages/casaclean-safety-recall.

metaDescription: Deale International recalls CasaClean Handheld Steamers due to risk of serious burn injury from leaking steam or hot water.

Description: The iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank 5000 mAh model IST-09991/W05 is recalled due to fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. Reports include overheating, explosions, and one minor burn injury. The power banks pose a risk of fire and property damage if used while charging.
Title: iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Banks Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact iStore at 800-268-4049 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@istore.co, or visit https://istore.co/pages/battery-recall and https://www.tomauri.com/pages/battery-recall. You can also go to http://www.tomauri.com and click on "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (Model IST-09991/W05)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8,520 (An additional 6,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (packaging)
Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (front)
Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the wireless power banks overheating and exploding while they were charging, including one incident that resulted in a minor burn to the victim's arm. The firm received reports of property damage of about $15,000.

Retailers.Name: Available at vending machines and stores in various airports nationwide, as well as online at iStore.co from February 2023 through May 2025, priced between $46 and $65.

Importers.Name: Tomauri Inc., of Ontario, Canada

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power bank immediately and visit the firm's website to participate in the recall. You will need to submit two photos via email: one showing the front of the power bank marked with "Recalled" and the date, and the other displaying the model number on the back. A purchase receipt is not mandatory but may be requested. Dispose of the power bank according to local hazardous waste regulations.

metaDescription: iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Banks recalled due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Immediate discontinuation recommended.

en.Title: iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Banks Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
en.Description: The iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank 5000 mAh model IST-09991/W05 is recalled due to fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. Reports include overheating, explosions, and one minor burn injury. The power banks pose a risk of fire and property damage if used while charging.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact iStore at 800-268-4049 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@istore.co, or visit https://istore.co/pages/battery-recall and https://www.tomauri.com/pages/battery-recall. You can also go to http://www.tomauri.com and click on "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.
en.metaDescription: iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Banks recalled due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Immediate discontinuation recommended.

Products.Name: iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (Model IST-09991/W05)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8,520 (An additional 6,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (packaging)
Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (front)
Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the wireless power banks overheating and exploding while they were charging, including one incident that resulted in a minor burn to the victim's arm. The firm received reports of property damage of about $15,000.

Retailers.Name: Available at vending machines and stores in various airports nationwide, as well as online at iStore.co from February 2023 through May 2025, priced between $46 and $65.

Importers.Name: Tomauri Inc., of Ontario, Canada

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power bank immediately and visit the firm's website to participate in the recall. You will need to submit two photos via email: one showing the front of the power bank marked with "Recalled" and the date, and the other displaying the model number on the back. A purchase receipt is not mandatory but may be requested. Dispose of the power bank according to local hazardous waste regulations.

Description: VIVI is recalling 36-volt lithium-ion rechargeable batteries sold with certain e-bike models due to fire and burn hazards caused by battery overheating. The recall includes specific battery and e-bike model numbers detailed in the official notice. There have been reports of batteries overheating and fires, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: VIVI E-Bikes Lithium-ion Battery Recall Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact VIVI at 800-375-6103 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, or visit https://viviebikes.com/pages/recall or https://viviebikes.com and click on "Important Recall Information" for further details.

Affected VIVI e-bike models label (located on the frame of e-bikes sold in 2022 and after)
Recalled VIVI e-bike 36-volt lithium-ion battery number label (located on the top or bottom of the battery casing depending on the battery model)

Injuries.Name: VIVI is aware of 14 reports of incidents of batteries overheating, including three reports of fires. No injuries reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Guangzhou Plenty Bicycle Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: These e-bikes were available for purchase online at amazon.com, walmart.com, ebay.com, viviebikes.com, wish.com, sears.com, wayfair.com, and aliexpress.com between December 2020 and November 2023, with prices ranging from $365 to $950.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Binxin Household Co., Ltd., d/b/a VIVI E-bikes, of China

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards from overheating batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled batteries immediately and contact VIVI for a free replacement battery and charger. Dispose of the recalled battery at a household hazardous waste collection center or follow local disposal guidelines, confirming disposal via email to vivirecall@163.com.

metaDescription: VIVI recalls lithium-ion batteries for e-bikes due to fire and burn hazards from overheating. Immediate replacement is advised to prevent risk.

Description: Tractor Supply is recalling Traveller Aluminum Loading Ramps because they may bend or break under the specified weight limits, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes several models designed for loading equipment such as riding lawn mowers, ATVs, and golf carts. The company has received reports of ramps failing during use, resulting in injuries. Consumers should stop using the ramps to prevent falls.
Title: Tractor Supply Recalls Traveller Aluminum Loading Ramps Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can contact Tractor Supply Company at 877-718-6750 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT Monday through Saturday, or from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. CT on Sunday. More information is available online at https://www.tractorsupply.com/tsc/cms/policies-information/customer-solutions/recall-notices or by visiting www.tractorsupply.com and selecting 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Traveller 3,000 lb. 12" x 90" Aluminum Sheet Arched Ramp
Recalled Traveller 1,800 lb. 77" Straight Ramp
Recalled Traveller 1,500 lb. 12" x 84" Aluminum Sheet Arched Ramp

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 35 reports of ramps bending or breaking, including two reports of ramps breaking and causing knee and other injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Tractor Supply Company stores nationwide and online at www.tractorsupply.com from October 2024 through July 2025, priced between $180 and $220.

Importers.Name: Tractor Supply Company, of Brentwood, Tennessee

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to ramps bending or breaking under specified weight limits

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled ramps and return them to any Tractor Supply store. Alternatively, they can send a photo of the product marked 'Recalled' to customersolutions@tractorsupply.com for a full refund.

metaDescription: Tractor Supply recalls Traveller Aluminum Loading Ramps due to fall hazard from bending or breaking under weight limits. Stop use immediately.

Description: Giant Bicycle is recalling Momentum Vida E+ E-bikes because the fork steerer tube may crack or break, creating a fall hazard. The recall covers bikes with specific serial numbers identified by the third character. No injuries have been reported, but four incidents of broken fork steerer tubes were received.
Title: Giant Bicycle Recalls Momentum Vida E+ E-Bikes for Fork Steerer Tube Failure
ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach Giant Bicycle toll-free at 866-458-2555 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email at recall@giantbicycle.com, or visit https://www.momentum-biking.com/us/recall-information, https://www.giant-bicycles.com/us/recall-information, or https://www.liv-cycling.com/us/recall-info for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (metallic green)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (metallic red)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (pearl white)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (metallic navy)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (indigo)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (panther)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (happy mint)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of broken fork steerer tubes. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized Giant Bicycle dealers across the country and online at https://www.momentum.com/us from September 2019 through May 2025, priced between $2,500 and $3,200.

Importers.Name: Giant Bicycle Inc., of Newbury Park, California

Hazards.Name: Fork steerer tube may crack or break, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Bikes with serial numbers having the third character as 'H', 'J', or 'K' will receive a free fork replacement. Those with 'G' require a visual inspection by an authorized Giant Bicycle dealer.

metaDescription: Giant Bicycle recalls Momentum Vida E+ E-bikes due to fork steerer tube cracking, posing a fall hazard. Free repairs are available for affected bikes.

Description: AUGNORYE padded crib bumpers have been recalled because they pose a suffocation hazard to infants and do not meet federal crib bumper safety regulations. The bumpers are made of polyester and come in sets of four with specific size dimensions. No injuries have been reported in connection with this product.
Title: Recall of AUGNORYE Padded Crib Bumpers for Suffocation Hazard and Federal Regulation Violation
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled crib bumpers immediately and reach out to YIXIA for a full refund by emailing Yixiakejirecall@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled AUGNORYE Padded Cushioned Crib Bumpers
Care Label on Recalled Crib Bumper

Retailers.Name: Amazon from February 2025 through May 2025 for between $40 and $45.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshi Yixiakejiyouxiangongsi, dba YIXIA, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to obstruction of infant breathing, violating federal crib bumper regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bumpers, cut the straps and padding, and email a photo of the destroyed bumpers to obtain a full refund.

metaDescription: AUGNORYE padded crib bumpers recalled due to suffocation hazard and noncompliance with federal crib bumper safety standards.

Description: Haoyunm braided crib bumpers have been recalled because they pose a suffocation risk to infants and violate the Federal Safe Sleep for Babies Act, which bans padded crib bumpers. These bumpers come in multiple lengths and colors and are sold in vacuum-sealed packaging without labels. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Recall of Haoyunm Braided Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation Hazard and Federal Safety Violation
ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using the crib bumpers immediately, take them out of the crib, and reach out to Haoyunm for a refund at hyzhongtshung@outlook.com. Additionally, they are advised to cut the bumper in half, photograph it, and email the image to the provided address.

Images.Caption: Recalled braided crib bumper
Recalled braided crib bumpers in crib

Retailers.Name: Amazon from December 2024 through April 2025 for between $25 and $11.

Distributors.Name: Fang Cheng Xian Hao Yun Shang Mao You Xian Gong Si, dba Haoyunm, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of suffocation due to obstruction of breathing, violating federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bumpers, remove them from the crib, and contact Haoyunm for a refund.

metaDescription: Haoyunm braided crib bumpers recalled for suffocation hazard and violation of federal safety rules. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: Creekliybear Play Yard Mattresses labeled "Play Mat" and "Model: m-sy2" are recalled due to excessive thickness that violates mandatory crib mattress standards. These mattresses may not fit securely in play yards, creating a serious entrapment hazard where infants can suffocate if trapped between the mattress and play yard walls. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Creekliybear Play Yard Mattresses Recalled for Entrapment Hazard and Non-Compliance with Crib Mattress Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Kbabyea via email at Kbabyearecall@outlook.com.

Products.Name: Creekliybear "Pack and Play" Aftermarket Play Yard Mattresses

Images.Caption: Recalled Creekliybear "Pack and Play" Aftermarket Play Yard Mattress
Recalled Creekliybear "Pack and Play" Aftermarket Play Yard Mattress Label

Retailers.Name: Online on Amazon from December 2024 to June 2025 for between $28 and $40.

Importers.Name: Zhongshan Hongtu Furniture Co., dba Kbabyea, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with crib mattress thickness standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattresses, contact Kbabyea for a full refund, and send a photo of the cut mattress for verification.

Description: Dearlomum retractable mesh safety gates have been recalled due to an entrapment hazard that poses a risk of serious injury or death to children. The gates, available in black, gray, and white, do not meet federal safety standards for gates and enclosures. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Dearlomum Retractable Safety Gates Recalled for Entrapment Hazard Risking Serious Injury or Death to Children
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers should reach out to Signbaby via email at regcards.team@gmail.com or visit https://kiddoryproducts.com/pages/retractable-safety-gates-recalled-page. Alternatively, go to https://kiddoryproducts.com and select the "retractable safety gates recalled page" at the top of the website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Dearlomum retractable safety gate-black
Recalled Dearlomum retractable safety gate-gray
Recalled Dearlomum retractable safety gate-white
Label on the side of the recalled safety gate

Retailers.Name: Amazon from December 2024 through May 2025 for between $33 and $38.

Distributors.Name: Shaoxing Yishan Garment Co., Ltd., dba Signbaby, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard posing serious injury or death risk to children

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gates immediately and contact Signbaby for a full refund. Consumers will need to dismantle the gates, destroy them by cutting the mesh, and send a photo of the destroyed gates to regcards.team@gmail.com. A full refund will be issued upon receipt of the photo. Signbaby and Amazon are reaching out to all known buyers directly.

metaDescription: Dearlomum retractable safety gates recalled due to entrapment hazard posing serious injury or death risk to children. No injuries reported.

Description: SPPTTY is recalling kids bicycles that contain lead levels exceeding federal limits, posing a lead poisoning hazard. The affected bicycles are pink with brown accents, available in 14-inch and 18-inch sizes, and marked with "SPPTTY" and "New Sport Bike" on the downtube. No injuries have been reported. The recall addresses the risk of lead exposure from bicycle components and paint.
Title: SPPTTY Kids Bicycles Recalled for Lead Poisoning Hazard Due to Excessive Lead Levels
ConsumerContact: Contact Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology via email at lishkjie@163.com for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled SPPTTY Kids 14-inch Bicycle in Pink
Recalled SPPTTY Kids 18-inch Bicycle in Pink

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart.com from August 2024 to December 2024 for approximately $85.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology, dba SPPTTY, of China

Hazards.Name: Lead poisoning risk due to excessive lead levels in bicycle components and paint.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles and contact Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology for a full refund by providing proof of destruction.

Description: Walmart is recalling Ozark Trail 64 oz stainless steel insulated water bottles because the lids can eject forcefully when opened, posing serious impact and laceration hazards. The bottles have a silver body with a black screwcap lid and an embedded Ozark Trail logo. There have been reports of injuries, including permanent vision loss, caused by the lid striking consumers' faces.
Title: Walmart Recalls Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless Steel Water Bottles Due to Lid Ejection Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT daily or visit www.walmart.com/help or https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls.

Products.Name: Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless Steel Insulated Water Bottle

Images.Caption: Recalled Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless-Steel Insulated Water Bottle, model 83-662

en.Title: Walmart Recalls Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless Steel Water Bottles Due to Lid Ejection Hazard
en.Description: Walmart is recalling Ozark Trail 64 oz stainless steel insulated water bottles because the lids can eject forcefully when opened, posing serious impact and laceration hazards. The bottles have a silver body with a black screwcap lid and an embedded Ozark Trail logo. There have been reports of injuries, including permanent vision loss, caused by the lid striking consumers' faces.
en.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT daily or visit www.walmart.com/help or https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls.

Description: WLIVE is recalling its Fabric 15-Drawer Dressers because they can tip over if not properly anchored, creating serious tip-over and entrapment hazards. These dressers do not meet federal safety standards and pose risks of injury or death, especially to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: WLIVE Fabric 15-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach WLIVE at 833-367-7572 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email wliveproductrecall@wlivehome.com, or visit http://wlivehome.com/ and click on "Recall" for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled WLIVE Fabric 15-Drawer Dresser in Rustic Brown
Recalled WLIVE Fabric 15-Drawer Dresser in White
Recalled WLIVE Fabric 15-Drawer Dresser in Black
Recalled WLIVE Fabric 15-Drawer Dresser in Black Wood Grain

Manufacturers.Name: Jiangsu Zhi Pai Furniture Manufacturing Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from April 2024 through May 2025 for approximately $110.

Distributors.Name: WLIVE, of China

Hazards.Name: Unstable dressers that can tip over if not anchored, posing serious risks of injury or death to children, violating federal safety standards as per the STURDY Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the dressers if they are not wall-anchored and move them out of children's reach. Contact WLIVE for disposal instructions to obtain a full refund, including submitting a photo of the disposed dresser to wliveproductrecall@wlivehome.com. WLIVE and Amazon are notifying all known buyers directly.

metaDescription: WLIVE recalls Fabric 15-Drawer Dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards posing serious injury risks. No injuries reported.

Description: 509 is recalling several Tactical 3.0 and Tactical 2.0 helmet models from 2023, along with Fidlock®-equipped helmets produced since 2020. These helmets may fail to provide adequate protection in crashes, increasing the risk of head injury. One reported incident involved a helmet detaching during a crash, resulting in a concussion.
Title: 509 Recalls Tactical and Fidlock® Helmet Models Due to Head Injury Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact 509 at 877-743-3509 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, or email support@ride509.com. More information can be found at https://ride509.com/pages/recalls or by visiting www.ride509.com and selecting 'Safety Recalls' under the 'Support' section. For Polaris Industries, call 800-765-2747 during the same hours or visit www.polaris.com and navigate to 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section. Polaris customers can also reach the owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through an online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Images.Caption: Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Youth Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Mountain Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Trail Helmet
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 3.0 Youth Helmet
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 3.0 Mountain Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 2.0 Small/Medium Helmets
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 2.0 Small/Medium Helmets
Recalled 509 and 509 for Polaris Helmets with Fidlock®
Correct Fidlock
Incorrect Fidlock Strap Fastener

Injuries.Name: The firm received one incident where the rider's helmet became detached during a crash, resulting in a concussion.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.ride509.com and www.polaris.com, and through 509 and Polaris dealers nationwide from January 2020 to May 2025, priced between $140 and $900.

Importers.Name: Teton Outfitters LLC, dba 509, a Polaris subsidiary, of Rigby, Idaho

Hazards.Name: Insufficient protection in the event of a crash, leading to head injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled helmets immediately. Contact 509 for refund, credit, or replacement options for Tactical 3.0 and Tactical 2.0 helmets. Polaris customers can also choose similar options for their helmets. No proof of purchase is necessary. Discard recalled helmets in household trash after confirming the recall and following disposal instructions on the website. Polaris will provide inspection instructions for Fidlock® helmets.

metaDescription: 509 recalls multiple Tactical and Fidlock® helmet models due to risk of insufficient protection and head injury during crashes.

Description: Harbor Freight Tools is recalling Predator 2000-Watt Power Stations because of a shock hazard caused by reversed wiring in the AC outlet receptacles during Emergency Power Supply mode. The portable generators feature multiple outlets and a lithium-ion phosphate battery. One report of reversed wiring has been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Harbor Freight Recalls Predator 2000-Watt Power Stations for Electric Shock Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Harbor Freight Tools at 800-444-3353 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recall@harborfreight.com or visit https://www.harborfreight.com/customer-service-product-support.html#5 for further details. More information is also available at www.harborfreight.com under the 'Recall & Safety' section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Predator 2000 Watt Power Station (SKU #70084; UPC # 193175488718)
Location of the SKU and UPC on the recalled Power Station

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the wires being reversed in EPS mode. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Harbor Freight Tools locations nationwide from February 2025 to April 2025 for approximately $950.

Importers.Name: Harbor Freight Tools, of Calabasas, California

Distributors.Name: Harbor Freight Tools, of Calabasas, California

Hazards.Name: Shock hazard due to reversed wires in AC outlet receptacles during Emergency Power Supply mode.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use immediately and return the power stations to any Harbor Freight Tools location for a complimentary replacement. The company will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Harbor Freight recalls Predator 2000-Watt Power Stations due to electric shock hazard from reversed wiring in AC outlets during emergency power mode.

Description: Positec is recalling certain electric corded chainsaws and pole saws because the main switch may fail, causing the saws to run continuously and posing a laceration hazard. The recalled models include Hyper Tough, Worx, and Portland branded saws with distinct color schemes. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Positec Recalls Electric Corded Chainsaws and Pole Saws for Laceration Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Positec at 800-889-0129 from Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, or visit https://www.trane.com/residential/gas-electric-packaged-unit-recall/ for more information.

Products.Name: Hyper Tough 9 Amp Electric Corded Chainsaw
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 49,000 (In addition, about 3,000 were sold in Canada)

Retailers.Name: Walmart (Hyper Tough brand)

Importers.Name: Positec Tool Corporation, of Charlotte, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to potential switch failure

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the products and contact Positec for a prepaid shipping label and a replacement saw.

Description: Trane and American Standard have recalled five models of gas/electric packaged units because of a risk of unexpected fuel gas valve openings that can cause gas leaks and fire hazards. One report of gas leakage has been received, but no injuries have been reported. The affected units can be identified by specific model and serial number ranges on the unit.
Title: Trane and American Standard Gas/Electric Packaged Units Recalled for Gas Leak and Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Trane or American Standard at 800-889-0129, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET. More information can also be found online at https://www.trane.com/residential/gas-electric-packaged-unit-recall/ or by visiting www.trane.com/residential and selecting 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the page. For American Standard, visit https://www.americanstandardair.com/gas-electric-packaged-unit-recall/ and click on 'Product Recall' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled American Standard Gas/Electric Packaged Unit
Recalled Trane Two-Stage Gas/Electric Packaged Unit
Recalled Two-Stage Gas/Electric Packaged Unit
Model and serial number location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of gas leaking from the recalled two-stage gas furnace/air conditioners. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Trane U.S. Inc., of Davidson, North Carolina

Retailers.Name: HVAC distributors and dealers nationwide from January 2024 through March 2025 for between $8,250 and $12,900.

Hazards.Name: Unexpected fuel gas valve opening leading to gas leaks and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heating mode of the recalled units and contact the installation dealer for a free inspection and repair, which includes replacing the ignition board. Trane users can find their nearest dealer at www.trane.com/residential/en/dealer-locator/. American Standard users can locate their dealer at www.americanstandardair.com/find-your-dealer/. Direct support is also available from Trane or American Standard.

metaDescription: Trane and American Standard recall gas/electric packaged units due to gas leak and fire hazards. Check model and serial numbers to identify affected units.

Description: Bell Sports is recalling Axle, Cadence, Rev, and Frenzy bicycle helmets because they do not meet federal impact safety requirements. These helmets may increase the risk of head injury in a crash. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Bell Sports Recalls Axle, Cadence, Rev, and Frenzy Bicycle Helmets for Head Injury Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bell Sports at 800-456-2355 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com, or visit https://www.thebellgarage.com/bell-sports-helmet-recall-notice-axle-cadence-rev-passage-and-frenzy-models/ or www.thebellgarage.com and click on the "Recall" banner for more information.

Products.Name: Axle Bicycle Helmet
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 31,200 (In addition, about 480 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Bell Sports Bicycle Helmet, Model B0494Y
Recalled Bell Sports Bicycle Helmet Model, B06065C or B0605Y
The model name, model number and manufacture date, in MM/YY or M/YY format, can be found on a sticker inside the helmet

Manufacturers.Name: Bell Sports Inc., of Irvine, California

Retailers.Name: Walmart, Target, Academy Sports+ Outdoors and other stores nationwide and online on www.Amazon.com, FredMeyer.com, Academy.com and Target.com from September 2024 through May 2025 for about $20.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with CPSC impact requirements, increasing the risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets immediately and contact Bell Sports for a full refund. Destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps and send photos to consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com to process the refund.

Description: Boyro Baby is recalling its baby walkers, model US-B01, because they do not meet federal safety standards for fall prevention and contain brake pads with lead levels above legal limits. These walkers pose a serious risk of injury or death to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Boyro Baby Walkers Recalled for Fall Hazard and Excessive Lead Content Violations
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Boyro Baby via email at boyrobaby@outlook.com or visit https://www.boyrobaby.com/round-walker-safety-recall/ or www.boyrobaby.com and click on "Round Walker Safety Recall".

Images.Caption: Recalled Boyro Baby Walker

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from August 2024 through December 2024 for between $70 and $90.

Distributors.Name: Hangzhou Christine Trading Co. Ltd., dba Boyro Baby, of China

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to non-compliance with federal safety standards and lead content exceeding legal limits.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the walkers, disassemble them, and contact Boyro Baby for a full refund. They will need to destroy the walker and provide three photos of the destroyed product to receive their refund.

metaDescription: Boyro Baby recalls walkers due to fall hazard risks and lead content violations that exceed federal safety standards.

Description: Coleman has recalled Converta camping cots and suspension stretchers due to a pinching hazard in the backrest fold mechanism. This defect can cause lacerations, amputations, and finger fractures. The recall includes specific model numbers with tan or white fabric and black steel frames. Seven incidents involving finger injuries, including fractures, have been reported.
Title: Coleman Converta Camping Cots and Suspension Stretchers Recalled for Finger Injury Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact The Coleman Company at 800-345-3278 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://recall.coleman.com/ to check if your model is affected and to request a free repair kit.

Images.Caption: Recalled Coleman Converta camping cot
Recalled Coleman Converta camping suspension stretcher

Injuries.Name: The firm has received seven reports of the seatback adjustment mechanism pinching consumers' fingers, including two reports of fractured fingers.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart and various retailers nationwide, as well as online on Amazon, from January 2011 through March 2025, priced between $60 and $80.

Importers.Name: Newell Brands Inc., an affiliate of The Coleman Company, Inc., of Atlanta, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Pinching hazard from backrest fold mechanism

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit with installation instructions

metaDescription: Coleman recalls Converta camping cots and suspension stretchers due to finger pinching hazards that can cause lacerations and amputations.

Importers.Name: Newell Brands Inc., an affiliate of The Coleman Company, Inc., of Atlanta, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Pinching hazard from backrest fold mechanism

Description: DR Power is recalling its 62-volt 5.0 Ah LiPRO lithium-ion battery packs used with various outdoor power equipment due to fire and burn hazards. The recall includes stand-alone battery packs and removable packs with specific outdoor equipment models. Two incidents of fire or overheating have been reported, but no injuries have occurred. These batteries pose a risk of short-circuiting and ignition if not handled properly.
Title: DR Power Recalls 62-Volt 5.0 Ah LiPRO Lithium-Ion Battery Packs for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact DR Power at 800-454-8643 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@drpower.com. Additional details can be found online at https://drpower.com/recalls and https://www.drpower.com/ under "Product Recalls."

Products.Name: DR Power LiPRO 5.0 Ah Lithium-Ion Battery Pack

DR Power Model CE77021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21" SP

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential short-circuiting and ignition

Dispose of the battery packs according to local regulations if not returning.

Description: iHerb is recalling certain California Gold Nutrition iron-containing dietary supplements because their packaging does not meet federal child-resistant standards. This poses a risk of serious injury or death from child poisoning. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: iHerb Recalls California Gold Nutrition Iron Supplements for Non-Child-Resistant Packaging Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach iHerb at 888-430-4770, email ProductRecall@iherb.com, or visit https://www.iherb.com/help/contact or https://information.iherb.com/hc/en-us/articles/37894456653972-iHerb-Recalls-California-Gold-Nutrition-Iron-Supplements-Due-to-Risk-of-Child-Poisoning.

Description: Peg Perego is recalling Tatamia 3-in-1 recliners, swings, and high chairs because their incline angle exceeds federal limits, creating a suffocation hazard. These products also lack required warnings under high chair safety standards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Peg Perego Recalls Tatamia 3-in-1 Recliners, Swings, and High Chairs for Suffocation Hazard and Federal Standard Noncompliance
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Peg Perego at 877-737-3464 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email service@pegperego.com, or visit https://www.pegperego.com/en_us/baby/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Peg Perego Tatamia 3-in-1 Recliner, Swing, and High Chair

Manufacturers.Name: Peg Perego SPA, of Italy

Retailers.Name: Online at PegPerego.com, Amazon.com, Target.com and other websites from November 2022 through January 2025 for about $450.

Importers.Name: Peg Perego USA Inc., of Fort Wayne, Indiana

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to incline angle exceeding 10 degrees and failure to meet mandatory warning requirements.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled products and contact Peg Perego for a full refund, including a prepaid shipping label for returns.

Description: Green Pastures Wholesale is recalling LED Fireplace Lanterns due to a risk of ingestion from the lithium coin battery in the remote control. The battery can be easily accessed by children, violating federal safety standards and posing serious health risks. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Green Pastures Wholesale Recalls LED Fireplace Lanterns Due to Coin Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Green Pastures Wholesale at 855-787-7429 from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email info@greenpastureswholesale.com. More details are available online at https://greenpastureswholesale.com/product-recall or by visiting https://greenpastureswholesale.com and clicking on 'Product Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled LED Fireplace Lantern – Model SP-14
Recalled LED Fireplace Lantern – Model SP-55
Recalled LED Fireplace Lantern – Model SP-66
Recalled LED Fireplace Lantern – Model SP-78
Recalled LED Fireplace Lantern – Product Packaging
Recalled LED Fireplace Lantern – Product Packaging
Recalled Remote Control
Recalled Remote Control

Retailers.Name: Green Pastures Wholesale Showroom at AmericasMart Atlanta, Atlanta, GA; The Columbus Warehouse, Columbus, OH; The Warehouse at Paxton, Paxton, IL; Clarksville Wholesale Warehouse, Clarksville, TN; and online at https://GreenPasturesWholesale.com, sold from January 2025 through April 2025, priced between $13 and $25 based on the model.

Importers.Name: Green Pastures Wholesale, of Baltimore, Maryland

Hazards.Name: The lanterns present an ingestion risk as the lithium coin battery in the remote can be easily accessed by children, violating federal safety standards. This poses serious health risks, including internal chemical burns and potential fatality.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the lanterns immediately, remove the battery from the remote control, and store it out of children's reach. Green Pastures Wholesale will provide a free UPS shipping label for product returns and issue full refunds. They are also contacting all known purchasers directly. Note: Dispose of button cell and coin batteries safely according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

Description: Sanven Technology is recalling Vevor wrought iron single post handrails used on one- or two-step stairs due to a risk of welding breakage during use. The breakage hazard has resulted in reported injuries, including one head injury. The handrails were sold in black, white, and silver with various model numbers and feature a 'Vevor' label near the bottom.
Title: Sanven Technology Recalls Vevor Wrought Iron Handrails Due to Breakage Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email recalling@vevor.com, or visit https://www.vevor.com/pages/vevor-single-post-handrail or www.vevor.com and select Product Recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vevor handrail, model number LTFSSHSDDZJSFS001V0
Recalled Vevor handrail, model number LTFSBSHXDDZJSFS01V0
Recalled Vevor handrail, model number LTFSBSDDZJSFS0001V0
Recalled Vevor handrail, model number LTFS1BBXGMLFS0001V0
Recalled Vevor handrail, model number LTFS2BBXGMLFS0001V0
Recalled Vevor handrail, model number TFBGWXLZHS1JGNHKIV0
Vevor is printed on a label located near the bottom of the handrail

Injuries.Name: Vevor has received five reports of the handrails breaking, resulting in injuries, including one head injury.

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon.com, eBay.com, HomeDepot.com, Lowes.com, Vevor.com, and Walmart.com from February 2020 to November 2024, priced between $57 and $109.

Distributors.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., dba Vevor, of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to potential breakage of welding while in use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to cease use of the recalled handrails immediately and contact Vevor for a complete refund. Vevor will supply pre-paid shipping labels for returns. Alternatively, consumers may send photographic proof of disposal to recalling@vevor.com. Vevor is reaching out to all known buyers directly.

metaDescription: Sanven Technology recalls Vevor wrought iron handrails due to risk of breakage causing injury. Users should stop use immediately to prevent harm.

Description: AstroAI is recalling its 4-Liter/6-Can minifridges due to fire and burn hazards linked to an electrical switch that may short circuit. The recall follows reports of units smoking, burning, melting, or catching fire, including two fires causing significant property damage. The affected models display the AstroAI logo and model number LY0204A with specific serial numbers on the back label.
Title: AstroAI 4-Liter/6-Can Minifridges Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach AstroAI at 877-278-7624 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, email recall@astroai.com, or visit https://www.astroai.com/product-recall or www.astroai.com and navigate to the 'Recalls' section for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled AstroAI 4-Liter/6-Can Minifridge
Recalled AstroAI 4-Liter/6-Can Minifridge label

Injuries.Name: CPSC has received at least 70 reports of the minifridges smoking, burning, melting or catching fire. Two fires resulted in extensive property damages, together totaling more than $360,000 in reported damages.

Manufacturers.Name: Shaoxing ShangYu North Electron Manufacture Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com and Astroai.com from June 2019 through June 2022 for about $40.

Importers.Name: AstroAI Corp., of Garden Grove, California

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to electrical switch short circuiting

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the minifridges and contact AstroAI for a free replacement. To obtain a replacement, send a photo of the minifridge showing the model and serial number, marked 'Recalled' in permanent marker, to AstroAI via email at recall@astroai.com or through their online portal. Dispose of the recalled units according to local waste disposal regulations.

metaDescription: AstroAI recalls 4-Liter/6-Can minifridges due to fire and burn hazards caused by electrical switch short circuits. Stop use immediately.

Description: Crayan is recalling its 10-inch and 12-inch mattresses in multiple sizes because they do not meet federal open flame flammability standards, posing a serious fire hazard. The mattresses have a white quilted top with the Crayan brand on the gray side panel and were sold compressed in a box. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Crayan Mattresses Recalled for Fire Hazard Due to Federal Flammability Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Crayan at 779-605-4458 from 5 p.m. to 11 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at crayan_service@hotmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Crayan Mattress
Recalled Crayan Mattress Labeling
Recalled Crayan Mattress Labeling
Recalled Crayan Mattress Labeling

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from July 2022 through June 2024, priced between $100 and $220.

Distributors.Name: Foshanshi Liyue Jiaju Youxian Gongsi, dba Crayan Inc., of China

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet federal open flame flammability standards, creating a serious fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must immediately stop using the recalled mattresses and contact Crayan for a full refund. They will need to permanently mark the mattress with the word "Recalled" in large letters on the white top panel and send a photo of the marked mattress to crayan_service@hotmail.com to confirm disposal before receiving the refund. The company is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Crayan recalls mattresses for fire hazard risk due to failure to meet federal flammability standards. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: MaxKare Electric Blankets have been recalled due to risks of overheating that can cause fire and burn hazards. The recall includes blankets in twin, full, and queen sizes with specific model numbers. There have been reports of burn injuries and instances where the blankets caught fire. Consumers should stop using the blankets immediately to avoid these hazards.
Title: MaxKare Electric Blankets Recalled for Overheating and Fire Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yumo at 866-620-0026 from 8 a.m. to 11:30 a.m. ET and 8 p.m. to 11:30 p.m. Monday through Thursday, or visit https://www.marnur.net/pages/important-recall-information or www.marnur.net and select "Important Recall Information" for further details.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition, leading to burn and fire hazards.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Walmart.com.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Yumo Commerce and Trade Corporation, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition, leading to burn and fire hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the blankets immediately and contact Yumo for disposal instructions. A photo of the blanket should be sent to yumoofficial@126.com to confirm disposal for a full refund.

Description: TADAKAZU is recalling baby loungers that fail to meet federal infant sleep product safety standards. The loungers pose suffocation, fall, and entrapment hazards due to inadequate side height and excessive sleeping pad thickness. No injuries have been reported in connection with these products.
Title: TADAKAZU Baby Loungers Recalled for Suffocation, Fall, and Entrapment Hazards Violating Infant Sleep Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to TADAKAZU via email at tadakazurecall@yeah.net.

Recalled TADAKAZU Baby Lounger (Snow House)
Recalled TADAKAZU Baby Lounger (Pink)
Recalled TADAKAZU Baby Lounger (Star)

Retailers.Name: Exclusively on Amazon from September 2024 through May 2025 for between $12 and $47.

Distributors.Name: Sichuanyongzhichengguojimaoyiyouxiangongsi, d/b/a TADAKAZU, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation, fall, and entrapment hazards due to violations of federal safety standards for infant sleep products.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the loungers, contact TADAKAZU for a full refund, and destroy the product as instructed.

Description: YaFiti has recalled its 12-Drawer White Dressers because they pose tip-over and entrapment hazards that can cause serious injury or death. The dressers do not comply with federal safety standards for clothing storage units. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: YaFiti 12-Drawer White Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach YaFiti toll-free at 877-452-4067 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email Yafitiservice@outlook.com, or visit https://yafiti.com/pages/recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled YaFiti 12-Drawer White Dresser (front view)
Recalled YaFiti 12-Drawer White Dresser (side view)
Recalled YaFiti 12-Drawer White Dresser (back view)

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Leader-Union Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from September 2023 through March 2025 for approximately $95.

Distributors.Name: YaFiti Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment that may lead to serious injuries or fatalities, particularly to children, due to non-compliance with federal safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the dressers if they are not securely anchored to a wall and to keep them out of children's reach. They should contact YaFiti for disposal instructions to receive a full refund, which requires submitting a photo of the disposed dresser to Yafitiservice@outlook.com. YaFiti and Amazon will reach out to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: YaFiti recalls 12-Drawer White Dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that pose serious injury risks, especially to children.

Description: Agro Superior Group is recalling Oliver & Smith 6-inch hybrid mattresses because they do not comply with federal mattress flammability standards. This non-compliance creates a fire hazard that could result in serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Agro Superior Group Recalls Oliver & Smith Mattresses for Fire Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Agro Superior Group at 800-453-5856 from 9:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at agrorecall@gmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Oliver & Smith 6-inch Mattress
Recalled Oliver & Smith 6-inch Mattress side seam labels

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.Amazon.com by "Aylas Furniture Store" and www.Walmart.com by "OLIVER & SMITH SINCE 1921" from January 2024 to July 2024, priced between $90 and $180.

Importers.Name: Agro Superior Group LLC, of Princeton, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to non-compliance with mandatory mattress flammability standards, posing a risk of serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the recalled mattresses immediately and contact Agro Superior Group for a complimentary fitted cover that will ensure compliance with federal flammability standards. The company will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Agro Superior Group recalls Oliver & Smith mattresses for fire hazard risk from failure to meet federal flammability standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Anker Innovations has recalled PowerCore 10000 power banks, model A1263, due to overheating lithium-ion batteries that can cause fire and burn hazards. The recall follows reports of fires, explosions, minor burn injuries, and property damage. These power banks are marked with the Anker brand on the front and have model and serial numbers on the bottom. Consumers should verify if their device is included in the recall.
Title: Anker PowerCore 10000 Power Banks Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Anker at 800-988-7973, available from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, or email support@anker.com. Additional support can be found at https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus, and a Live Chat option is available at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us. For more details on the recall, visit https://www.anker.com/product-recalls.

Images.Caption: Recalled Anker A1263 power bank

Injuries.Name: Anker has received 19 reports of fires and explosions. This includes two reports of minor burn injuries not requiring medical attention and 11 reports of property damage totaling over $60,700.

Manufacturers.Name: Anker Innovations Limited, of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Anker, Amazon, Newegg, and eBay from June 2016 to December 2022, priced around $27.

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion battery presenting fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Anker for a free replacement. Registration at https://www.anker.com/product-recalls is required, along with a photo of the power bank and confirmation of proper disposal.

metaDescription: Anker recalls PowerCore 10000 power banks due to overheating lithium-ion batteries posing fire and burn hazards. Check if your device is affected.

Description: Bugaboo has recalled its Giraffe high chairs because the legs can detach, creating a fall hazard that may result in serious injury or death. The recall follows reports of incidents involving detached legs and minor injuries. The chairs come in multiple colors and have an item code on the bottom of the seat for identification.
Title: Bugaboo Giraffe High Chairs Recalled for Fall Hazard Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach the Bugaboo Service Team at 800-460-2922, available from 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT Monday through Thursday and from 7 a.m. to 1 p.m. PT on Friday. They can also email service.us@bugaboo.com or visit https://service.bugaboo.com/s/bugaboo-giraffe-recall-repair-kit-checker?language=en_US&Country=US for further details. More information is available at www.bugaboo.com under the 'Product Recalls' section.

Products.Name: Giraffe High Chair
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 18,280 (In addition, about 2,325 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Bugaboo Giraffe High Chair
Location of item code on label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 22 reports of the legs of the chair detaching, including 13 reports of minor injuries.

Retailers.Name: Nordstrom and other specialty stores nationwide and online at www.bugaboo.com and www.Amazon.com from May 2023 through April 2025 for about $380.

Importers.Name: Bugaboo North America Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard leading to serious injury or death

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit with Allen key and new screws

metaDescription: Bugaboo Giraffe high chairs are recalled due to a fall hazard that can cause serious injury or death. Use of the chairs should be stopped immediately.

Description: Apollo America is recalling its Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detectors, model 51000-600, because they may fail to alert users to fires or carbon monoxide leaks. These detectors were sold exclusively by Vivint and include a 'Replace by' date between June and October 2034. No injuries have been reported. Improper disposal of the lithium battery can cause fire hazards.
Title: Apollo America Recalls Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detectors for Alert Failure Risk
ConsumerContact: For further information, contact Vivint at 800-281-9667, email recalls@vivint.com, or visit https://www.vivint.com/smoke-co-recall. Additional details can be found by clicking on 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the Vivint homepage.

Products.Name: Apollo America Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector

Images.Caption: Recalled Apollo America Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector
Back of recalled Apollo America Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector
Recalled Apollo America Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector

Manufacturers.Name: Apollo America Inc., of Auburn Hills, Michigan

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.Vivint.com and through door-to-door and telephone sales by Vivint from June to October 2024, priced between $50 and $100.

Distributors.Name: Vivint, of Lehi, Utah

Hazards.Name: Malfunctioning detectors that may fail to alert for fires or carbon monoxide leaks, leading to risks of smoke inhalation, poisoning, or death.

Remedies.Name: Vivint is providing free replacement detectors to all known customers. Users should continue to use the recalled units until the new ones arrive and should follow the provided installation guide upon receipt.

metaDescription: Apollo America recalls combination smoke and carbon monoxide detectors due to alert failures posing serious fire and carbon monoxide hazards.

Description: Direct Scaffold Supply is recalling five-inch casters used on baker scaffolding because faulty spot welds may cause the casters to break, creating a fall hazard. The recall includes specific model numbers and heat codes stamped on the casters. There have been reports of caster failures resulting in one fall injury. Consumers should verify their casters against the recall information to ensure safety.
Title: Direct Scaffold Supply Recalls Baker Scaffolding Casters Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Direct Scaffold Supply at 800-729-6118 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at bakercasters@realtimeresults.net, or online at www.recallrtr.com/bakercasters or www.dss.net by selecting 'Baker Caster Recall.'

Products.Name: Direct Scaffold Supply Baker Scaffolding Casters

Images.Caption: Recalled casters (CAS5SS4P+BFL)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISUS+BFL) (MPISUS)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISUSD+BFL)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISUST+BFL)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISU+BFL) (MPISU) (MPISU+1)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISUS+AHD)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISU+AHD)
Heat Code Example

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of casters breaking or failing, including one injury where a caster failure led to a fall and bruising injury.

Retailers.Name: Available at Direct Scaffold Supply branches, Lowe's stores nationwide, and online at Granite.com, Homedepot.com, and Lowes.com from September 2022 to March 2025, with prices ranging from $280 to $1,600 for scaffolding and approximately $110 for casters sold in four-packs.

Importers.Name: Direct Scaffold Supply LLC, of Houston, Texas

Hazards.Name: Faulty spot welds may cause casters to break, leading to unstable scaffolding and potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled casters and contact Direct Scaffold Supply for free replacement instructions. The company will provide a prepaid shipping label and packaging for returning the faulty casters, and new replacements will be sent after the return.

metaDescription: Direct Scaffold Supply recalls baker scaffolding casters due to faulty welds that can cause falls. Check model numbers and heat codes for safety.

Description: DR Power Equipment is recalling lithium-ion battery-powered chainsaws due to an overheating motor controller that can cause fire and burn hazards. The recall includes specific models labeled DR Pulse 62V with black handles and orange buttons. The company has received reports of fire, sparks, and smoke but no injuries. Consumers should stop using the chainsaws to avoid potential hazards.
Title: DR Power Equipment Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery Chainsaws Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact DR Power at 800-940-6923 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recalls@DRPower.com, or visit http://drpower.com/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Lithium-Ion Battery Chainsaws
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 7,180 (In addition, about 21 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Equipment battery-powered chainsaw (without battery)
Recalled DR Power Equipment battery-powered chainsaw (with battery)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 20 reports of incidents of fire, sparks and/or smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent power equipment retailers across the country and online at https://drpower.com from June 2018 to July 2024, priced between $200 and $350.

Importers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Overheating motor controller leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the chainsaws and contact DR Power for a pro-rated refund. Proof of destruction is required.

metaDescription: DR Power recalls lithium-ion battery chainsaws due to fire and burn hazards from overheating motor controllers. No injuries reported.

Description: Hobby Toy Hammer Ball sets have been recalled because small balls included with the toy present a choking hazard. The product features a base with buttons, a detachable door, and four small colored balls. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Hobby Toy Hammer Ball Sets Recalled for Choking Hazard Due to Small Balls
ConsumerContact: For more information, please email haochenghengkj@outlook.com or visit www.hcfjehtoy.com or www.amazon.com/stores/page/2912E5E1-628F-4384-930C-5D8CCB7B8184.

Images.Caption: Recalled Hobby Toy Hammer Ball set
Recalled Hobby Toy Hammer Ball set (front of package)
Recalled Hobby Toy Hammer Ball set (label)

Manufacturers.Name: Shantou Jinshanglu Toys Industry Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from September 2024 through April 2025 for about $30.

Importers.Name: TPDB Trade Corporation, of Welling, Colorado

Distributors.Name: Shantou Haochengheng Technology Co. Ltd, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to small balls posing a risk of serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled sets immediately and reach out to Shantou Haochengheng Technology for instructions on product disposal and to receive a full refund. Both Shantou Haochengheng Technology and Amazon are proactively contacting all known buyers.

metaDescription: Hobby Toy Hammer Ball sets recalled for choking hazard from small balls posing serious injury risk. No injuries reported.

Description: HTP has recalled ELU Elite Ultra residential wall-mounted gas boilers, models ELU-150WBN and ELU-199WBN, due to a defective door seal caused by incorrect burner door studs. This defect can allow flames and harmful gases to escape, creating fire and carbon monoxide poisoning hazards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: HTP ELU Elite Ultra Gas Boilers Recalled for Fire and Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled boilers immediately and contact HTP at 800-323-9651, available from 7 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET Monday through Friday. Additional information can be obtained via email at recall@htproducts.com or online at https://htproducts.com/safety-notice.html. Visit www.htproducts.com and click on 'Urgent Recall' for further details.

Products.Name: HTP ELU Elite Ultra 150WBN Gas Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 96 (In addition, 9 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled HTP-brand ELU Elite Ultra boiler

Manufacturers.Name: NTI Boilers Inc., of Canada

Retailers.Name: Sold through independent heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors across the U.S. from October 2024 to February 2025, priced between $3,400 and $4,100.

Importers.Name: Ariston USA LLC, of Providence, Rhode Island

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and carbon monoxide poisoning due to improper sealing of the boiler door.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the boilers and contact HTP for a free repair installation by a certified technician. If continued use is necessary before repair, ensure working smoke and carbon monoxide alarms are present.

metaDescription: HTP recalls ELU Elite Ultra gas boilers due to fire and carbon monoxide poisoning hazards from faulty door seals posing serious safety risks.

Description: Lutron is recalling the LU-PH3-B power interfaces used with LED tape lights because they pose a shock hazard from potential failure during power surges. These 96W hard-wired interfaces were sold individually and in specific LED tape light starter kits for RadioRA 3 and HomeWorks systems. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Lutron Recalls LU-PH3-B Power Interfaces for LED Tape Lights Due to Shock Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled power interfaces and any connected lighting immediately. They should turn off the power at the circuit breaker and contact Lutron at 800-239-0495 from 8 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or via email at productrecall@lutron.com. More information is available online at https://residential.lutron.com/us/en/safety/safety-recall or by visiting https://www.lutron.com/us/en and clicking on 'Safety Notices' at the bottom of the page.

Distributors.Name: Lutron Electronics Co. Inc., of Coopersburg, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Shock hazard from power interface failure during power surges

Remedies.Name: Free replacement power interface and installation

Description: Midea is recalling about 1.7 million U and U+ window air conditioners sold under multiple brand names due to risk of mold growth from pooled water. Mold exposure may cause respiratory infections and allergic reactions. The recall includes units in three cooling capacities with model numbers on the front right side. The company has received reports of mold presence and related health symptoms among consumers.
Title: Midea Recalls U and U+ Window Air Conditioners Due to Mold Growth Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Midea at 888-345-0256 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email midea4028@midea.com. More information is available online at www.MideaUrecall.expertinquiry.com or www.midea.com/us under "RECALL INFORMATION."

Products.Name: U and U+ Window Air Conditioners
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1.7 million (In addition, about 45,900 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled U Window Air Conditioner
Recalled U+ Window Air Conditioner

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of at least 152 reports of mold in the air conditioners, including 17 reports of consumers experiencing symptoms such as respiratory infections, allergic reactions, coughing, sneezing and/or sore throats from mold exposure.

Manufacturers.Name: GD Midea Air-Conditioning Equipment Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Costco, Menards, Home Depot, Best Buy and other stores nationwide and online at Midea.com, Amazon.com, Costco.com, Menards.com, HomeDepot.com, Lowes.com, Walmart.com, BJs.com, BestBuy.com and other websites from March 2020 through May 2025 for between $280 and $500.

Importers.Name: Midea America Corp., of Parsippany, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of mold growth due to pooled water not draining quickly, potentially causing respiratory issues or infections.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Midea for a free repair or a full or prorated refund based on the purchase date. For refunds, units must be returned using a free shipping label or by providing a photo of the cut power cord.

metaDescription: Midea recalls U and U+ window air conditioners due to mold growth risk that may cause respiratory issues. Free repairs or refunds available.

Description: Ravmix has recalled its portable hook-on chairs for children due to a fall hazard caused by a removable crotch restraint that does not meet federal safety standards. The chairs feature a metal frame with fabric seating and attach to dining tables with metal arms. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Ravmix Portable Hook-On Chairs Recalled for Fall Hazard and Federal Safety Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, please email Ravmix at ravmix@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Portable Hook-On Chair
Recalled Portable Hook-On Chair
Recalled Portable Hook-On Chair

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com and from September 2024 through March 2025 for between $40 and $60.

Distributors.Name: Yuanimi Trade Co. Ltd., d/b/a Ravmix, of China

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to removable crotch restraint, violating federal safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the chair and contact Ravmix for a refund process, which includes disassembling the chair and sending photos of the parts.

metaDescription: Ravmix recalls portable hook-on chairs due to fall hazard risk from removable crotch restraint violating federal safety standards.

Description: Johnson Health Tech Trading is recalling BowFlex Model 552 and Model 1090 Adjustable Dumbbells due to a risk of weight plates dislodging during use, creating an impact hazard. The recall covers both pairs and single units with specific serial numbers. Reports include incidents of plates detaching, with no injuries reported for units sold by Johnson Health Tech Trading.
Title: Johnson Health Tech Trading Recalls BowFlex Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Impact Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Johnson Health Tech Trading at 800-209-3539 from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email at recall@bowflex.com, or visit www.bowflex.com/recalls. More information is available on their website under the "Recalls" section.

Images.Caption: Recalled BowFlex 552 52.5 LB Adjustable Dumbbell
Recalled BowFlex 552 52.5 LB Adjustable Dumbbell serial number location
Recalled BowFlex 1090 90 LB Adjustable Dumbbell
Recalled BowFlex 1090 90 LB Adjustable Dumbbell serial number location

Injuries.Name: Johnson Health Tech Trading has received 12 reports of the plates dislodging during use with no injuries for units it sold. Nautilus received 337 reports of the plates dislodging during use for units it sold, including 111 resulting in injuries such as concussions, abrasions, broken toes or contusions.

Retailers.Name: Available at Johnson Fitness & Wellness, DICK'S Sporting Goods, Best Buy stores nationwide, and online through www.Bowflex.com and www.Amazon.com from 2004 to April 2024 by BowFlex Inc. and from May 2024 to May 2025 by Johnson Health Tech Trading Inc., priced between $200 and $800.

Importers.Name: BowFlex Inc., (formerly Nautilus Inc.), of Vancouver, Washington
Johnson Health Tech Trading Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging from the handle during use, leading to an impact hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled dumbbells and contact Johnson Health Tech Trading for a refund or replacement. Those with units sold by BowFlex Inc. can receive a prorated refund and a one-year JRNY digital fitness membership. A prepaid shipping label will be provided for returns.

metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech Trading recalls BowFlex Adjustable Dumbbells for risk of weight plates dislodging, posing an impact hazard during use.

Description: Funlio has recalled its 2-in-1 Convertible High Chairs due to fall and entrapment hazards caused by large openings between the seat and tray and potential child restraint system failure. These hazards violate federal safety standards for high chairs. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Funlio Convertible High Chairs Recalled for Fall and Entrapment Hazards Violating Federal Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Ecogoods at 800-251-8805 from 5:30 p.m. to 1:30 a.m. PT Sunday through Thursday, via email at funlioproductrecall@outlook.com, or visit https://funliotoys.com/pages/product-recalls or https://funliotoys.com and click on "Product Recalls" at the bottom of the page for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled 2-in-1 Funlio Convertible Baby High Chair
Recalled 2-in-1 Funlio Convertible Baby High Chair with forest-themed padded seat
Tracking label under the tray

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com and Walmart.com from March 2024 through March 2025 for about $100.

Distributors.Name: Shanghai Balejiao E-commerce Co. Ltd., d/b/a Ecogoods, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and fall hazards due to a large opening between the seat and tray, and potential failure of the child restraint system.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the high chair, disassemble it, and contact Ecogoods for a full refund. Consumers will need to cut the restraint straps and send photos of the disassembled and destroyed parts to funlioproductrecall@outlook.com. Ecogoods will directly notify all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Funlio Convertible High Chairs recalled due to fall and entrapment hazards from large openings and restraint failures. No injuries reported.

Description: NTI is recalling TRX Series II residential wall-mounted gas boilers, including models TRX150, TRX199, and TRX199C. The recall addresses a risk of fire and carbon monoxide poisoning caused by improper sealing of the boiler door. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: NTI TRX Series II Boilers Recalled for Fire and Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach NTI Boilers at 800-688-2575 from 7 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@ntiboilers.com, or visit http://www.ntiboilers.com/trx-safety-recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.ntiboilers.com and click on 'Urgent Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: NTI TRX150 Gas Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 323 (In addition, 459 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled TRX Series II boiler

Manufacturers.Name: NTI Boilers Inc., of Canada

Retailers.Name: Sold through independent heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors nationwide from September 2024 to February 2025, priced between $3,400 and $4,100.

Importers.Name: NTI Boilers Inc., of Canada

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and carbon monoxide poisoning due to improper sealing of the boiler door.

Remedies.Name: Corrective measures to ensure proper sealing of the boiler door.

RemedyOptions.Option: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled boilers and contact NTI Boilers to arrange to have a certified technician install the repair at no cost to the consumer. Consumers who must continue using the boiler while awaiting repair should have working smoke and carbon monoxide alarms in their homes.
