Description: Specialized Bicycle Components is recalling Globe-branded Haul ST and Haul LT e-bikes because the telescopic seat posts may break when fully extended, posing a fall hazard. The seat posts are 13 inches long and were included as standard equipment. The company has received reports of incidents, including minor injuries such as scrapes and bruises.
Title: Specialized Recalls Globe Haul ST and LT E-Bikes for Seat Post Breakage Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Specialized Bicycle Components at 877-808-8154, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, reach out via email at ridercare@specialized.com, use the Live Chat feature at http://www.specialized.com, or visit http://www.specialized.com/safety-recall-notices for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled HAUL-ST e-bike
Recalled Haul LT e-bike

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 13 reports of incidents, including two minor injuries consisting of scrapes and bruises.

Retailers.Name: Authorized Specialized retailers and bicycle shops across the country, as well as online at www.specialized.com, from January 2023 to March 2025, priced between $2,500 and $3,500.

Importers.Name: Specialized Bicycle Components Inc., of Morgan Hill, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of breaking seat post when fully extended, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the affected e-bikes and contact an authorized Specialized retailer for a complimentary repair, which involves replacing the seat post with one made from a different material.

metaDescription: Specialized Globe Haul ST and LT e-bikes recalled due to seat post breakage risk causing falls. Free repairs available for affected models.

Description: ZHORANGE portable bassinets, model 148012-A, have been recalled because they do not comply with federal safety standards for infant sleep products. The bassinets have low sides and no stand, creating a fall hazard for infants. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: ZHORANGE Portable Bassinets Recalled for Fall Hazard and Federal Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to DFD Trading via email at zhorange8695@gmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled ZHORANGE Baby portable bassinet
Recalled ZHORANGE Baby portable bassinet (left side)
Recalled ZHORANGE Baby portable bassinet (bottom)
Recalled ZHORANGE Baby portable bassinet (front)

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from November 2024 through April 2025 for between $0 (as a promotional item) and $76.

Distributors.Name: Nanjing Dengfengda Trading Co. Ltd., d/b/a DFD Trading, of China

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to low sides and lack of stand, violating safety standards for infant sleep products.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bassinets immediately and contact DFD Trading for a full refund. Consumers must remove the sleeping pad, cut the sides of the bassinet and sleeping pad, and email a photo of the destroyed bassinet to zhorange8695@gmail.com to receive a refund. DFD Trading and Amazon will reach out to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: ZHORANGE portable bassinets recalled due to fall hazard risk from low sides and lack of stand, violating federal infant sleep product safety standards.

en.Title: ZHORANGE Portable Bassinets Recalled for Fall Hazard and Federal Safety Standard Violations
en.Description: ZHORANGE portable bassinets, model 148012-A, have been recalled because they do not comply with federal safety standards for infant sleep products. The bassinets have low sides and no stand, creating a fall hazard for infants. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to DFD Trading via email at zhorange8695@gmail.com.
en.metaDescription: ZHORANGE portable bassinets recalled due to fall hazard risk from low sides and lack of stand, violating federal infant sleep product safety standards.

Images.Caption: Recalled ZHORANGE Baby portable bassinet
Recalled ZHORANGE Baby portable bassinet (left side)
Recalled ZHORANGE Baby portable bassinet (bottom)
Recalled ZHORANGE Baby portable bassinet (front)
Label with Model Number and Date of Manufacture

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from November 2024 through April 2025 for between $0 (as a promotional item) and $76.

Distributors.Name: Nanjing Dengfengda Trading Co. Ltd., d/b/a DFD Trading, of China

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to low sides and lack of stand, violating safety standards for infant sleep products.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bassinets immediately and contact DFD Trading for a full refund. Consumers must remove the sleeping pad, cut the sides of the bassinet and sleeping pad, and email a photo of the destroyed bassinet to zhorange8695@gmail.com to receive a refund. DFD Trading and Amazon will reach out to all known purchasers.

Description: Rinnai America is recalling i-Series Plus residential wall-mounted gas boilers because of a risk of carbon monoxide poisoning caused by a possible failure of the boiler door seal. The affected models include IP175S, IP199S, IP175199C, and IP199199C. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Rinnai America Recalls i-Series Plus Boilers Due to Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Rinnai America Corporation at 800-621-9419 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, email recall@rinnai.us, or visit https://www.rinnai.us/support/recalls. Additional information is available on their website under the "Recall" section.

Products.Name: Rinnai i-Series Plus Gas Boilers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,030 (In addition, 249 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled i-Series Plus Boiler
Recalled i-Series Plus Boiler Label

Retailers.Name: Available through independent heating and plumbing contractors, wholesale distributors nationwide, and online retailers including Amazon.com, Homedepot.com, and Lowes.com from September 2024 to February 2025, priced between $3,900 and $5,000.

Importers.Name: Rinnai America Corporation, of Peachtree City, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to potential failure of the boiler door seal.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled boilers and contact Rinnai for a free repair by a certified technician. If continued use is necessary, ensure carbon monoxide alarms are installed on every level of the home and near sleeping areas.

metaDescription: Rinnai recalls i-Series Plus boilers due to carbon monoxide poisoning risk from potential door seal failure. No injuries reported.

Description: ZRWD has recalled its infant swings, model S0008A, because the incline exceeds safety limits, creating a suffocation hazard. The swings also lack required warnings about the lithium button cell batteries, which pose ingestion risks. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: ZRWD Infant Swings Recalled for Suffocation Hazard and Federal Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to ZRWD via email at zrwdrecall@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing with Gray and White Fabric and Black Base
Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing with Gray and White Fabric, Black Base and Plush Toys
Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing with Blue and White Fabric and White Base
Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing with Blue and White Fabric, White Base and Plush Toys
Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing Remote Control

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Amazon.com from December 2023 through December 2024 for about $110.

Distributors.Name: Fuzhouwanhonggongyipinyouxiangongsi, dba ZRWD, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper incline and missing warnings for button cell batteries, which can cause serious injuries if ingested.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the swings and contact ZRWD for a full refund. Consumers must send a photo of the cut swing cover and a marked swing to zrwdrecall@163.com before disposal.

metaDescription: ZRWD recalls infant swings due to suffocation risk from improper incline and missing battery warnings. No injuries reported in this safety recall.

Description: KICIMPRO has recalled the KM800-01-06BN kitchen faucet due to lead that can leach into water, posing health risks to infants and young children. The faucet is a brushed nickel, single-handle model with a pull-down spray. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: KICIMPRO KM800-01-06BN Kitchen Faucets Recalled for Lead Exposure Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Kicimpro via email at support@kicimprohome.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled KICIMPRO Kitchen Faucet

Manufacturers.Name: Kaipingshikemuyulinweiyushiyeyouxiangongsi, of China, doing business as Amazon seller Kicimpro

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.amazon.com from January 2024 to May 2025, priced around $35.

Hazards.Name: Lead exposure that can leach into water, posing health risks to infants and young children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the faucets immediately and reach out to Kicimpro for disposal instructions and a refund. A photo of the disposed faucet and your order number will be required for the refund process. Kicimpro is directly notifying all known customers. Until replacement faucets are available, run the water for 15 seconds before use.

metaDescription: KICIMPRO recalls KM800-01-06BN kitchen faucets due to lead exposure risk that can harm young children. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: Minka Lighting is recalling Vortic Flow Series light fixtures because the ceiling mount connection can weaken, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes multiple pendant and chandelier models. There have been reports of fixtures partially detaching, but no complete falls or injuries have been reported.
Title: Minka Lighting Recalls Vortic Flow Series Fixtures Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Minka Lighting at 888-770-7027 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, inquiries can be sent via email to vortic@realtimeresults.net or through their websites at https://www.recallrtr.com/vortic and https://www.minkagroup.net by selecting the 'Recall' option at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Minka Vortic Flow 10-Light Pendant, Model No. 3679
Recalled Minka Vortic Flow 8-Light Pendant, Model No. 4677
Recalled Minka Vortic Flow 18-Light Chandelier, Model No. 4679

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the light fixtures partially detaching. No fallen light fixtures or injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at lighting and home improvement stores nationwide and online at www.Ferguson.com, www.wayfair.com, and www.lamps.com from August 2019 through October 2024, priced between $850 and $1,500.

Importers.Name: Minka Lighting LLC, of Corona, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury from falling fixture due to weakened ceiling mount connection

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and on-site repair to replace the connection component

metaDescription: Minka Lighting recalls Vortic Flow light fixtures due to risk of fixture falling from weakened ceiling mount connection.

Description: Playworld Systems is recalling Unity Teeter Tunnels with Lexan tops due to a risk of serious injury and crush hazards if a child falls underneath the seesaw. The recall covers units identified by specific part numbers. There have been reports of injuries, including a liver laceration and leg fractures, resulting from children falling under the seesaw plank.
Title: Playworld Systems Recalls Unity Teeter Tunnels with Lexan Tops Due to Serious Injury and Crush Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Playworld Systems at 800-233-8404 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recalls@playworld.com or visit https://playworld.com/recall-information for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Unity Teeter Tunnel with Lexan Top (first model)
Recalled Unity Teeter Tunnel with Lexan Top (second model)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of children falling under the end of the seesaw plank of the recalled Unity Teeter Tunnels. One child sustained a liver laceration when the end of the seesaw plank landed on her abdomen and the second child suffered fractures to his right tibia and right fibula when the end of the seesaw plank landed on him.

Manufacturers.Name: Playworld Systems Inc., of Lewisburg, Pennsylvania

Retailers.Name: Playworld Systems sales representatives and their authorized independent sales representatives nationwide from October 2014 through March 2025 for between $13,450 and $13,600.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury and crush hazard if a child falls underneath the seesaw.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the teeter tunnels immediately and contact Playworld Systems for a free repair, which will be conducted by a certified playground installer.

metaDescription: Playworld Systems recalls Unity Teeter Tunnels due to serious injury and crush hazards from falls under the seesaw. Free repairs are available.

Description: BASDEHEN kitchen faucets, model CFDTTH-000-YGH, have been recalled because of lead contamination that can leach into drinking water. This poses a serious health risk to infants and young children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: BASDEHEN Kitchen Faucets Recalled for Lead Exposure Hazard to Children
ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Chenfeng Store via email at ChenfengStoreF@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled BASDEHEN Kitchen Faucet

Manufacturers.Name: Ruianshichenfengwujinzhizaoyouxiangongsi, of China, doing business as Amazon seller Chenfeng Store

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.amazon.com from May 2024 to May 2025 for approximately $30.

Hazards.Name: Lead contamination that can leach into water, posing serious health risks to infants and young children, including neurological issues and cognitive impairments.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the faucets and contact Chenfeng Store for guidance on disposal and obtaining a refund. A photo of the disposed faucet will be required for the refund process. Amazon is notifying all known buyers directly. Until replacements are available, run the water for 15 seconds before consumption.

metaDescription: BASDEHEN kitchen faucets recalled due to lead contamination risk posing health hazards to young children. No injuries reported.

Description: iWALK Magnetic Wireless Power Banks have been recalled because their lithium-ion batteries can overheat, posing fire and burn hazards. The recall includes specific models marked with "iWALK" and identified by model and serial numbers. The firm has reported incidents of battery fires causing burn injuries. Consumers should stop using these power banks immediately and follow proper disposal procedures.
Title: iWALK Magnetic Wireless Power Banks Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to U2O Global Co. Ltd. at their toll-free number 888-279-0111, available from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. You can also email them at service@iwalk.net or visit https://www.iwalk.net/recall.html for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled iWALK Power Bank Model MXB008 (front)
Recalled iWALK Power Bank Model MXB008 (back)
Recalled iWALK Power Bank Model MXB008 (serial number on the back)
Recalled iWALK Power Bank Models DBL6000M/DBL6000P (front)
Recalled iWALK Power Bank Models DBL6000M/DBL6000P (back)
Recalled iWALK Power Bank Models DBL6000M/DBL6000P (serial number on the back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of the lithium-ion battery catching fire, resulting in 10 burn injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: U2O Global Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at iWalkMall.com, Amazon.com, and other e-commerce platforms from July 2021 through March 2024, priced between $20 and $57.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact U2O Global for a full refund. To claim your refund, email a photo of the power bank showing the model and serial number, with "Recalled" written on it, to service@iwalk.net. Dispose of the power banks following local hazardous waste disposal guidelines.

metaDescription: iWALK Magnetic Wireless Power Banks recalled due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Stop use and seek refund from U2O Global.

Description: Step2 is recalling My First Snowman toys because the removable plastic top hat can break, creating small pieces that pose a choking hazard. The toy includes detachable facial features and mittens and is about 17 inches tall. The recall affects units with production dates in December 2023 and April 2024. No injuries have been reported related to this issue.
Title: Step2 Recalls My First Snowman Toys for Choking Hazard from Breakable Top Hat
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled snowman, remove the hat, and keep it away from children. For further instructions on disposing of the hat and obtaining a free replacement, contact The Step2 Company at 800-347-8372 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, via email at step2-company@step2.net, or visit https://www.step2.com/pages/step2-my-first-snowman or www.step2.com and click on 'Product Recalls' for more details.

Products.Name: My First Snowman Toy
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 30,800 (In addition, 692 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Step2 My First Snowman
Recalled My First Snowman's top hat
Affected date codes on bottom of hat

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 47 reports of the brim of the top hat breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Online at Step2.com, Amazon.com, Walmart.com and Wayfair.com from March 2024 through December 2024 for about $40.

Importers.Name: The Step2 Company LLC, of Streetsboro, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to small pieces from the top hat breaking off.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy, remove the hat, and contact Step2 for disposal instructions and a free replacement.

metaDescription: Step2 recalls My First Snowman toys due to choking hazard from small parts breaking off the top hat. No injuries reported but risk remains.

Description: WeHwupe has recalled adult portable bed rails, models BRX-8002 and BRX-8003, due to risks of entrapment and asphyxiation. The bed rails do not meet federal safety regulations, posing serious injury hazards. One injury was reported when a BRX-8002 bed rail broke, causing a fall.
Title: WeHwupe Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled for Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact WeHwupe Store at 888-438-3220 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email wehwupestore@163.com. More information is available online at https://chunqushop.com/wehwupe-bed-rail-safety-recall/ or by visiting https://chunqushop.com and selecting 'Product Recall' at the top.

Recalled WeHwupe Adult Portable Bed Rails Model BRX-8003 Assembly Instructions

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report involving the BRX-8002 model bed rail, which broke during use, resulting in a fall injury.

Manufacturers.Name: Nanjing Chunqu Clothing Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com from September 2023 to January 2025, priced between $33 and $43.

Distributors.Name: Nan Jing Chun Qu Fu Shi You Xian Gong Si, dba WeHwupe Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of entrapment and asphyxiation due to non-compliance with federal regulations for adult portable bed rails.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bed rails immediately and contact WeHwupe Store for a free replacement. Follow specific disposal instructions provided by WeHwupe Store.

Description: Areson has recalled the Rolanstar 6-Drawer Dressers because they can tip over and cause entrapment if not anchored to the wall. These dressers do not meet federal safety standards and pose a risk of serious injury or death, particularly to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Areson Recalls Rolanstar 6-Drawer Dressers for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Areson at 888-864-7995 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email Support@Areson.shop. More information is available at https://www.rolanstar.com/pages/recall or by visiting Rolanstar.com and selecting 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Rolanstar 6-Drawer Dresser (front view)
Recalled Rolanstar 6-Drawer Dresser (back view)
Recalled Rolanstar 6-Drawer Dresser (side view)

Manufacturers.Name: Fujian Ming Jin Household Technology Limited, of China

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from September 2023 through March 2025, priced between $100 and $213.

Distributors.Name: Areson Inc., of Tustin, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment due to instability without wall anchoring, posing serious injury or death to children.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the dressers if they are not anchored to the wall and keep them away from children. Areson is reaching out to all known buyers for refund instructions. Contact Areson for details on how to receive a full refund and submit a disposal photo at Support@Areson.shop.

metaDescription: Areson recalls Rolanstar 6-Drawer Dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that pose serious injury risks, especially to children.

Description: Macy's is recalling five styles of Holiday Lane infant footed pajamas due to tight elastic on the ankles that may restrict blood circulation and cause injury. The pajamas are cotton and come in infant sizes 0-3, 3-6, and 6-9 months. Four reports of chafing, imprinting, or restricted circulation have been received. The recalled styles include Merry Mix It, Family Plaid, Winterton Plaid, Star, and Floral.
Title: Macy's Recalls Holiday Lane Infant Footed Pajamas Due to Tight Elastic Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled pajamas, keep them away from children, and reach out to Macy's at 833-963-1708 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or email footedpajamas@realtimeresults.net. More information is available online at https://www.recallrtr.com/footedpajamas or by visiting www.macys.com and clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Holiday Lane Infant Merry Mix It Cotton Matching Family Footed Pajama

Images.Caption: Recalled Holiday Lane infant footed pajamas
PID/Style number location

Injuries.Name: Macy's has received four reports of the elastic being too tight on infants' ankles, resulting in chafing, imprinting or restricting blood circulation.

Retailers.Name: Macy's stores nationwide, including Macy's Backstage, and online at www.macys.com from March 2024 through January 2025 for about $27.

Importers.Name: Macy's Merchandising Group Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Elastic on the pajamas' ankles may be too tight, risking blood circulation and causing injury.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the pajamas, keep them away from children, and contact Macy's for a refund. Do not return the pajamas to stores. A recall form can be filled out at https://www.recallrtr.com/footedpajamas, after which a pre-addressed, postage-paid envelope will be provided for returning the pajamas at no cost. A refund of $26.50 will be issued via check upon receipt of the returned product. Macy's will directly contact all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Macy's recalls Holiday Lane infant footed pajamas because tight ankle elastic poses an injury hazard to infants. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: Nezmdi is recalling electric infant swings that pose a suffocation hazard due to an incline exceeding federal safety limits. The swings also lack required warning labels and the remote control does not include necessary lithium battery safety information. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Nezmdi Electric Infant Swings Recalled for Suffocation Hazard and Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Nezmdi via email at yingpengds@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Nezmdi infant swing
A leather patch with the brand Nezmdi located on restraints
"Model Number: S0008" and "Baby Swing Chair" are printed on a label located under the seat
Recalled Nezmdi infant swing remote control

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from September 2024 through February 2025 for about $70.

Importers.Name: Ningboyingpengdianzishangwuyouxiangongsi/YingPengStore, dba Nezmdi, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard and risk of serious injury or death due to incline angle and lack of required warnings.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the swings immediately and request a full refund from Nezmdi by sending a photo of the cut swing cover marked 'Recalled' to yingpengds@163.com. Dispose of the swing according to local regulations.

metaDescription: Nezmdi recalls electric infant swings due to suffocation hazard from unsafe incline and missing safety warnings. No injuries reported.

Description: Primark is recalling the DTR Plush Bag Stitch and DTR Plush Bag Angel because the detachable zipper pull poses a choking and ingestion hazard. The bags are plush and feature a swing tag care label on the side. No injuries have been reported related to this issue.
Title: Primark Recalls DTR Plush Bag Stitch and Angel for Choking Hazard from Detachable Zipper Pull
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Primark at 617-946-3236 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.primark.com/recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.primark.com and click on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Recalled Primark DTR Plush Bag - Angel

Retailers.Name: Primark stores located in the northeast and in Florida, Maryland and Illinois from March 2024 through October 2024 for about $6.

Importers.Name: Primark US Corp., of Boston, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Detachable zipper pull poses choking and ingestion risks

Remedies.Name: Return to Primark for a full refund

metaDescription: Primark recalls DTR Plush Bag Stitch and Angel due to choking hazard from detachable zipper pulls. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: Sivan six-drawer dressers in natural oak and black finishes have been recalled due to tip-over and entrapment hazards if not properly anchored. These dressers do not comply with federal safety standards and pose a risk of serious injury, especially to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Sivan Six-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Anji Guyou Furniture via email at guyouproductrecall@163.com for further assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sivan six-drawer double dresser (front view)
Recalled Sivan six-drawer double dresser (back view)
Recalled Sivan six-drawer double dresser (side view)

Retailers.Name: Available on Wayfair.com from May 2024 to March 2025, priced between $285 and $294.

Distributors.Name: Anji Guyou Furniture Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Unstable design leading to tip-over and entrapment risks, particularly dangerous for children; non-compliance with the STURDY Act's safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the dressers if they are not wall-anchored and relocate them out of children's reach. Contact Anji Guyou Furniture for disposal instructions to obtain a full refund. A photo of the disposed product must be emailed to guyouproductrecall@163.com. Anji Guyou Furniture and Wayfair will also reach out to all known customers.

metaDescription: Sivan dressers recalled due to tip-over and entrapment hazards posing serious safety risks. Immediate attention is required to prevent injury.

Description: Boyro Baby has recalled its 4-in-1 convertible high chairs due to fall and entrapment hazards. The restraint system is improperly attached, and large openings between the seat and tray create entrapment risks. These issues violate federal safety standards. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Boyro Baby Recalls 4-in-1 High Chairs for Fall and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Boyro Baby via email at boyrobaby@outlook.com or visit www.boyrobaby.com/testimonial or www.boyrobaby.com and select "Safety Recalls".

Images.Caption: Recalled Boyro Baby convertible high chair

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com and from October 2023 through November 2024 for between $70 and $90.

Distributors.Name: Boyro Baby, of China

Hazards.Name: Fall and entrapment hazards due to improper restraint system attachment and large openings between the seat and tray.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled high chairs immediately, disassemble them, and contact Boyro Baby for a full refund. Instructions will include cutting the restraint system and seat cushions, and sending photos of the disassembled parts to boyrobaby@outlook.com. A refund will be processed upon receipt of the photos.

metaDescription: Boyro Baby recalls 4-in-1 high chairs due to fall and entrapment hazards from improper restraint attachment and seat-tray gaps.

Description: BuddyLove has recalled its children's Mini Danica two-piece pajama sets due to a burn hazard from noncompliance with federal flammability standards for sleepwear. The sets include a long-sleeved button-up top and matching pants with detachable feathers and various design styles. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: BuddyLove Recalls Children's Mini Danica Pajama Sets for Burn Hazard Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach BuddyLove at 866-516-6244 from 9:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, email Recall@buddylove.com, or visit https://buddylove.com/pages/recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Mini Danica Loungewear Set – Sugar Berry (BK1105)
Recalled Mini Danica Loungewear Set – Tartan (BK1006)
Recalled Mini Danica Loungewear Set – Frostbite (BK1107)
Recalled Mini Danica Loungewear Set – Kris Kringle (BK1108)
Recalled Mini Danica Loungewear Set – Prowl (BK1109)

Retailers.Name: Available at BuddyLove and select boutique clothing stores nationwide, as well as online at https://Buddylove.com from October 2023 through July 2024, priced around $95.

Importers.Name: BuddyLove, of Dallas, Texas

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to violation of flammability standards for children's sleepwear

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the pajamas immediately and contact BuddyLove for disposal instructions to receive a full refund or store credit. To destroy the garments, cut them in half and email a photo of the destroyed item to Recall@buddylove.com. A full refund will be processed upon receipt of the photo. BuddyLove will also reach out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: BuddyLove recalls Children's Mini Danica Pajama Sets due to burn hazard from flammability standard violations. No injuries reported.

Description: Yiwu Baili Import and Export has recalled multiple models of child safety gates sold on Amazon due to risks of entrapment and falls. The gates, made of steel and ABS plastic, include pressure-mounted and drill-mounted versions, some with secondary pet doors that increase hazard potential. These products do not comply with federal safety standards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Yiwu Baili Child Safety Gates Recalled for Entrapment and Fall Hazards Due to Federal Compliance Violations
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to the third-party seller via email for details on product disposal and to request a full refund. Specific email addresses for each seller are provided in the product details.

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at www.FidgetThings.com from October 2022 through February 2025 for about $10.

Importers.Name: Yiwu Baili Import and Export Co., of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from entrapment and fall hazards due to design flaws in the gates.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the recalled gates and contact the third-party seller by email for disposal instructions and to receive a full refund.

Description: Fat Brain Toy Co. is recalling PlayTab Rollers Tile toys used in the PlayTab Modular Activity Board due to a risk of magnet detachment. The loose magnets pose a serious ingestion hazard that can result in severe internal injuries or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Fat Brain Recalls PlayTab Rollers Tile Toys Over Magnet Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Fat Brain Toy Co. at 800-590-5987 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email recall@fatbraintoys.com, or visit https://fatbraintoys.com/info/toy_safety/play_tab_roller_tile_recall.cfm. You can also find details by clicking "Recall" on the top of fatbraintoys.com.

Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5,100 (In addition, about 380 were sold in Canada)

Recalled Rollers Tile included in PlayTab Sensory Set 3

Retailers.Name: Specialty toy, book and educational stores nationwide and online at fatbraintoys.com from July 2024 through November 2024. The individual rollers tiles sold for about $6. The Sensory Set 3 sold for about $18.

Importers.Name: Fat Brain Toy Co. LLC, of Elkhorn, Nebraska

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to loose magnets, which can lead to severe internal injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys and contact Fat Brain for disposal instructions to receive a $10 credit or a replacement tile.

Description: Leon Rading is recalling PH-05 digital wall clocks because the remote control contains a lithium coin battery that can be easily accessed by children, posing a serious ingestion hazard. This design violates federal safety regulations for products with coin batteries. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Leon Rading Recalls PH-05 Digital Wall Clocks Due to Lithium Coin Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Leon Rading via email at recall@lianaocoltd.com.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from November 2024 to January 2025 for approximately $23.

Importers.Name: Leon Rading Co. Ltd., of Owatonna, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard from accessible lithium coin battery in remote control, violating federal safety regulations.

Description: LivingCaring has recalled its adult portable bed rails because they pose entrapment and asphyxiation hazards. The product does not meet federal safety regulations for adult portable bed rails. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Shanghai Kai Rong Xin Xi Ke Ji via email at Jerryhe@sourcing-cn.com.

Importers.Name: Shanghai Kai Rong Xin Xi Ke Ji Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risks due to non-compliance with federal regulations for adult portable bed rails.

Description: DEMDACO is recalling Maril-brand seasonal scented candles because the glass containers may crack or break while burning, posing fire and laceration hazards. The affected candles include Siberian Fir, Cinnamon + Cedar, and Pumpkin + Birch fragrances. There have been reports of glass breakage, but no injuries or property damage have been confirmed.
Title: DEMDACO Recalls Maril Seasonal Scented Candles for Fire and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled candles immediately and reach out to DEMDACO for a full refund. Contact them toll-free at 888-336-3226 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at customercare@demdaco.com. More details can be found at www.demdaco.com/recall-information.

Products.Name: Maril Seasonal Scented Candle - Siberian Fir

Images.Caption: Recalled MARIL Scented Candle 8-ounce Siberian Fir (SKU number: 1008170071)
Recalled MARIL Scented Candle 8-ounce Cinnamon + Cedar (SKU number: 1008170072)

Recalled MARIL Scented Tester Candle 8-ounce Siberian Fir (SKU number: 1008170074)
Recalled MARIL Scented Tester Candle 8-ounce Cinnamon + Cedar (SKU number: 1008170075)
Recalled MARIL Scented Tester Candle 8-ounce Pumpkin + Birch (SKU number: 1008170076)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the candle's glass breaking while in use. No injuries or property damage have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at various gift shops across the country, including Bachman's in Minneapolis, Flowers Around the Corner in Bowling Green, Coopers Uptown Store in Kenosha, Haws & Co. Floral and Gifts in Lehi, and Past and Presents in Lenexa, as well as online at www.demdaco.com from July 2024 to November 2024 for approximately $30.

Distributors.Name: DEMDACO, of Leawood, Kansas

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration and fire due to potential cracking or breaking of the candle's glass container while burning.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled candles and contact DEMDACO for a full refund, including a prepaid shipping label for returns. No proof of purchase is needed.

Description: Deuter is recalling Kid Comfort, Kid Comfort Pro, and Kid Comfort Active child carriers because the hinge on the rear kickstand support leg can crack and break, posing a fall hazard. The carriers have a foldable frame with hinges and come in several colors and models. Although there have been reports of hinge failures, no injuries have been reported.
Title: Deuter Recalls Kid Comfort Series Child Carriers Due to Fall Hazard from Hinge Failure
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled child carriers immediately and reach out to Deuter for a free replacement. Contact Deuter at 877-384-9252 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email recall@deuterusa.com, or visit https://www.deuter.com/us-en/service/kid-comfort-recall for further assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kid Comfort Carrier
Recalled Kid Comfort Pro Carrier
Recalled Kid Comfort Active Carrier
Recalled Kid Comfort Carrier with production labels

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 13 reports of hinges cracking and breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: REI and Bass Pro and other stores in the United States, and online at deuter.com and Amazon.com and other websites from March 2023 through March 2025 for between $240 and $370.

Importers.Name: deuter USA, of Longmont, Colorado

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to hinge failure on the rear kickstand support leg.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use and contact Deuter for a complimentary replacement child carrier. A recall form can be filled out at https://www.deuter.com/us-en/service/kid-comfort-recall, and a prepaid return shipping label will be provided.

metaDescription: Deuter recalls Kid Comfort, Kid Comfort Pro, and Kid Comfort Active child carriers due to a fall hazard caused by hinge failure on the rear kickstand support leg.

Description: Sanven Technology has recalled Vevor stainless steel cable railing kits used indoors and outdoors because the cable support posts pose an entrapment hazard. The posts have hole placements that can trap body parts, creating a safety risk. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Sanven Technology Recalls Vevor Stainless Steel Cable Railing Kits Due to Entrapment Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Sanven Technology Ltd. at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or via email at recalling@vevor.com.

Products.Name: Vevor Stainless Steel Cable Railing Kit

Images.Caption: Recalled Vevor cable railing kit with Cable Support Post model number JZLGZHS9142UYBR4V0
Recalled Vevor cable railing kit with Cable Support Post model number JZLGZHS1067CSWRBV0

Recalled Vevor cable railing kit with Cable Support Post model number LGZY106.72.55KZF0V0
Recalled Vevor cable railing kit with Cable Support Post model numbers 1JZLGZ91455YMDM3LV0 and 1JZLGZ91455YMDM3LV0-0801
Recalled Vevor cable railing kit label

Retailers.Name: Available online at HomeDepot.com, Lowes.com, Walmart.com, Amazon.com, Wayfair.com, and vevor.com from May 2021 to September 2024, priced between $32 and $40 per kit.

Importers.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Hazards.Name: Entrapment risk due to inadequate hole placement on cable support posts.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use immediately, keep children away from the area, and contact Vevor for a full refund. Vevor will supply pre-paid shipping labels for returns, or consumers may send photographic proof of disposal to recalling@vevor.com.

metaDescription: Sanven Technology recalls Vevor stainless steel cable railing kits due to entrapment hazards from cable support post design. No injuries reported.

ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Babywants via email at recall@twoodtoy.com or visit their recall page at www.xylolintoys.com/pages/recall-page.

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to peg dolls' spherical ends

Description: Baseus is recalling its 65W 30000mAh portable chargers, model BS-30KP365, because of a fire hazard caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The chargers are black or white, marked with '65w' on the front, and have a label on the bottom with the brand, model number, and serial number starting with 'SN' and ending with a digit or 'D.' Reports include incidents of bulging batteries and fires resulting in property damage.
Title: Baseus 65W Portable Chargers Recalled for Fire Hazard Due to Overheating Batteries
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Baseus at 888-568-7216 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email care@baseus.com, or visit https://www.baseus.com/pages/recall_select_BS-30KP365 for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Baseus portable charger in black
Recalled Baseus portable charger in white
Recalled Baseus portable charger with SN ending in the letter "D" (label on bottom)
Recalled Baseus portable charger with SN ending in Arabic numerals (label on bottom)

Injuries.Name: Baseus has received 76 reports of incidents involving the portable chargers, including 72 reports of bulging and four reports of fire, including three reports of property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, AliExpress.com, Walmart.com, and Baseus.com from April 2020 to April 2025, priced between $39 and $90.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Baseus Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the charger and contact Baseus for a free replacement with a different serial number.

metaDescription: Baseus recalls 65W portable chargers due to fire hazard from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Immediate use poses risk of fire and property damage.

Description: Dlesot Snap Children's Hair Clips are recalled due to excessive lead levels in the paint, posing a lead poisoning hazard if ingested by children. The clips are metal, approximately 1.2 inches long, and sold in multipacks. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Dlesot Snap Children's Hair Clips Recalled for Lead Poisoning Hazard Due to Excessive Lead in Paint
ConsumerContact: For further information, contact YFLGOTOO via email at YGLGOTOOrecall@yeah.net.

Description: Kohl's is recalling St. Nicholas Square Christmas Tea Light Candle Holders because they can catch fire, posing a fire and burn hazard. The holders are shaped like a Christmas tree and hold six tea light candles. There have been reports of the holders catching fire, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Kohl's Recalls St. Nicholas Square Christmas Tea Light Candle Holders Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Kohl's at 855-564-5705 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Saturday, or visit www.kohls.com/faq/article/1013?icid=ft-customer-recalls. You can also find more details by clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the Kohl's homepage.

Products.Name: St. Nicholas Square Christmas Tea Light Candle Holders

Images.Caption: Recalled St. Nicholas Square Christmas Tea Light Candle Holders

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of tea light candle holders catching on fire. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Kohl's stores nationwide and online at www.kohls.com from September 2024 through April 2025 for approximately $25.

Importers.Name: Kohl's Inc., of Menomonee Falls, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazard from candle holder catching fire

Remedies.Name: Stop using the candle holders immediately and return them to any Kohl's store for a full refund via gift card or original payment method. A pre-paid shipping label will be provided for online purchases. Kohl's will reach out to all known customers.

Description: Santa Cruz Bicycles has recalled Heckler 9 electric bikes equipped with a 720W lithium-ion battery due to a risk of battery overheating that can cause fires. The recall follows reports of fires and property damage, though no injuries have been reported. The affected bikes are identified by the "SANTA CRUZ" logo on the downtube and "Heckler" markings on the top tube and left chainstay.
Title: Santa Cruz Heckler 9 Electric Bikes Recalled for Fire Hazard from Battery Overheating
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Santa Cruz Bicycles toll-free at 833-944-8335 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit their website at https://santacruzbicycles.com/pages/recall-heckler-9-lithium-ion-battery for more details.

Products.Name: Heckler 9 Electric Bikes
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,850 (In addition, about 550 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Santa Cruz Bicycles Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle
Recalled Santa Cruz Bicycles Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle serial number on battery pack

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of fires, including some property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Santa Cruz dealers across the U.S. from October 2021 to January 2025, priced between $8,750 and $13,900.

Distributors.Name: Santa Cruz Bicycles LLC, of Santa Cruz, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of battery overheating leading to fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bike, disconnect the battery, and check the manufacturer's website for registration and replacement battery details.

metaDescription: Santa Cruz recalls Heckler 9 electric bikes due to fire hazard from battery overheating. No injuries reported but property damage occurred.

Description: SharkNinja is recalling Ninja Foodi OP300 Series Multi-Function Pressure Cookers because the pressure-cooking lid can open during use, posing a burn hazard. The recall includes all models in the OP300 series and replacement lids sold separately. There have been reports of serious burn injuries associated with this issue.
Title: SharkNinja Recalls Ninja Foodi OP300 Series Pressure Cookers Due to Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach SharkNinja at 888-370-1733 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET Monday through Friday and 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Saturday, email sharkninja@rqa-inc.com, or visit https://www.rqa-inc.com/client/SharkNinja/ or www.ninjakitchen.com and select 'Recalls' under 'Support' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Ninja Foodi OP300 Series Multi-Function Pressure Cookers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,846,400 (In addition, about 184,240 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled SharkNinja Foodi OP300 Series Multi-Function Pressure Cooker
Recalled SharkNinja Foodi OP300 Series Multi-Function Pressure Cooker - Front of Packaging

Injuries.Name: SharkNinja has received 106 reports of burn injuries, including more than 50 reports of second- or third-degree burns to the face or body, with 26 lawsuits filed.

Retailers.Name: Walmart, Costco, Sam's Club, Amazon and Target stores nationwide, and online at www.Ninjakitchen.com, www.walmart.com, www.costco.com, www.samsclub.com, www.amazon.com and www.target.com from January 2019 through March 2025 for about $200.

Importers.Name: SharkNinja Operating LLC, of Needham, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn injuries due to the pressure-cooking lid being opened during use

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pressure-cooking function and contact SharkNinja for a free replacement lid; other functions can still be used.

Description: AliExpress has recalled RGB-branded LED strip lights that contain lithium coin batteries accessible to children. The lights present a serious ingestion hazard, violating federal safety regulations. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: AliExpress Recalls RGB LED Strip Lights for Ingestion Hazard from Accessible Lithium Coin Batteries
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to AliExpress through their online service at https://helpcenter.aliexpress.com/s/BuyerHelp/home?language=en_US&m_station=BuyerHelp, email us_product_recall@aliexpress.com, or visit https://helpcenter.aliexpress.com/s/BuyerHelp/knowledge?categoryId=1000110454&language=en_US&m_station=BuyerHelp&questionId=1061297473&regulation=US-product-recall for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled AliExpress LED strip light - purple
Recalled AliExpress LED strip lights
Recalled AliExpress LED strip light - green

Retailers.Name: Available on AliExpress.com from March 2024 to November 2024 for approximately $3.

Distributors.Name: Dongguan Changsheng Heng Electronic Commerce Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: The LED strip lights present a significant ingestion hazard due to easy access to lithium coin batteries, violating federal safety regulations. Swallowing these batteries can lead to severe injuries, chemical burns, and even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled LED strip lights immediately and contact AliExpress for a full refund. They should destroy the lights by cutting them in half and disposing of them properly. A photo of the destroyed lights must be sent to us_product_recall@aliexpress.com or in response to the recall email from AliExpress. Note: Proper disposal of button cell and coin batteries should follow local hazardous waste guidelines.

metaDescription: AliExpress recalls RGB LED strip lights due to ingestion hazard from accessible lithium coin batteries posing serious injury risks to children.

ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled torches immediately and reach out to Sondikodirt via email at recalltorch@outlook.com for instructions on returning the product for a full refund. Sondikodirt and Amazon are proactively contacting all known buyers.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious burn injuries or death due to lack of child-resistant mechanisms.

Remedies.Name: Return for a full refund.

Description: Casely is recalling its Power Pods 5000mAh portable MagSafe wireless chargers due to fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The chargers can overheat, expand, or catch fire while charging phones. The recall follows reports of six minor burn injuries linked to these incidents.
Title: Casely Recalls Power Pods 5000mAh Wireless Chargers for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Casely at 888-964-9331 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@getcasely.com, or visit https://www.getcasely.com/pages/2025-recall. More information is available on their website under the 'Recalls' section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Casely wireless portable power bank
Recalled Casely wireless portable power bank (back of pod)
Recalled Casely wireless portable power bank (model number)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 51 consumer reports of the lithium-ion battery overheating, expanding or catching fire while they were charging their phones, resulting in six minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available online at getcasely.com, Amazon.com, and other e-commerce platforms from March 2022 to September 2024, priced between $30 and $70.

Importers.Name: Casely Inc., of Brookyln, New York

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the power banks and request a free replacement from Casely

metaDescription: Casely recalls Power Pods 5000mAh wireless chargers due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries.

Description: COMEONROA electric infant swings have been recalled due to a suffocation hazard caused by an incline angle exceeding safety limits and missing required warnings. The swings do not comply with the federal ban on inclined sleepers. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: COMEONROA Electric Infant Swings Recalled for Suffocation Hazard and Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Eonroacoo via email at eonroacoorecall@126.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled COMEONROA infant swing
Recalled COMEONROA infant swing
"Model No.: S-Y608" and "Baby cradle swing" are printed on a label located under the seat

Retailers.Name: Walmart.com from January 2024 through February 2025 for about $80.

Distributors.Name: YiWuShiJingYuDianZiShangWuYouXianGongSi, dba Eonroacoo, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to incline angle exceeding 10 degrees and failure to meet safety warning requirements.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the swings and contact Eonroacoo for a full refund. Consumers must provide photos of the swing cover cut in half and the swing marked 'Recalled' on its base to eonroacoorecall@126.com, and properly dispose of the swing according to local regulations.

metaDescription: COMEONROA electric infant swings recalled due to suffocation hazard from unsafe incline angle and missing safety warnings. No injuries reported.

Description: American Bolt and Screw is recalling tip-over restraint kits included with certain Canyon Furniture clothing storage units because the zip ties can become brittle, creating tip-over and entrapment hazards. The affected kits come in red plastic bags marked with specific production dates and include components such as brackets, screws, and drywall anchors. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: American Bolt and Screw Recalls Tip-Over Restraint Kits for Canyon Furniture Clothing Storage Units Due to Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to American Bolt and Screw via email at recall@absfasteners.com or visit www.ABStipoverecall.com. Additional support is available from Rooms To Go at 855-688-0919, Monday through Friday, from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, or via email at productcare@roomstogo.com.

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment due to brittle zip ties

Remedies.Name: Free replacement tip-over restraint kit

Description: The North Face is recalling Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots because the top hook on one boot can catch on the lace of the other, posing a fall hazard. The boots were sold in multiple colors and sizes 5 to 11. The company has received reports of the hook catching on the lace, including incidents resulting in minor injuries.
Title: The North Face Recalls Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled boots and reach out to The North Face at 800 297-9150, available from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recall_wshellistaV@thenorthface.com or visit https://www.thenorthface.com/en-us/help/product-recall for further details. Additional information can be found by clicking on 'Product Recall' at the bottom of thenorthface.com.

Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,200 (In addition, about 5,900 were sold in Canada)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 28 reports of the top hook catching on the lace of the other boot, including 15 reports of minor injuries in the United States.

Retailers.Name: The North Face stores, Dick's Sporting Goods, Nordstrom, REI, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at thenorthface.com and other websites, from September 2024 through January 2025 for approximately $165.

Importers.Name: The North Face, a division of VF Outdoor LLC, of Denver, Colorado

Hazards.Name: The hook of one boot may catch on the lace of the other boot, creating a fall risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled boots and contact The North Face for instructions on returning them for a full refund, gift card, or check if purchased with cash. After reaching out, consumers will receive a prepaid shipping label to return the boots at no cost.

metaDescription: The North Face recalls Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots due to a fall hazard caused by the top hook catching on the opposite boot's lace.

en.Title: The North Face Recalls Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots Due to Fall Hazard
en.Description: The North Face is recalling Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots because the top hook on one boot can catch on the lace of the other, posing a fall hazard. The boots were sold in multiple colors and sizes 5 to 11. The company has received reports of the hook catching on the lace, including incidents resulting in minor injuries.
en.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled boots and reach out to The North Face at 800 297-9150, available from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recall_wshellistaV@thenorthface.com or visit https://www.thenorthface.com/en-us/help/product-recall for further details. Additional information can be found by clicking on 'Product Recall' at the bottom of thenorthface.com.

Description: Bebamour has recalled its baby bath seats, model T186, due to a risk of drowning caused by instability and wide leg openings. The seats do not meet federal safety standards for infant bath seats. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Bebamour Baby Bath Seats Recalled for Drowning Hazard and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Bebamour via email at amanda@babyleading.com or visit https://bebamour.com/pages/recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to https://bebamour.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bebamour bath seat - green

Retailers.Name: Bebamour.com and Amazon.com from May 2024 through February 2025 for between $20 and $40.

Distributors.Name: Jinjiangshiweixiaobaobeiyingtongyongpin Co.Ltd., dba Bebamour, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of drowning due to instability and wide leg openings

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bath seats immediately and contact Bebamour for disposal instructions and to submit a photo for a full refund.

Description: Fisher-Price has recalled the Brunch & Go Stroller Toy because the egg yolk component can crack and break into small parts, creating a choking hazard. The toy includes a pretend avocado toast and three attachments: a mirror tomato, crinkle bacon, and an egg teether. There have been reports of the yolk cracking, with no injuries confirmed.
Title: Fisher-Price Recalls Brunch & Go Stroller Toy for Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Fisher-Price toll-free at 855-853-6224 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or visit https://service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx. More information is available by clicking on 'Recalls & Safety Alerts' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Brunch & Go Stroller Toy
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 253,000 (In addition, about 4,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Brunch & Go Stroller Toy
Recalled Brunch & Go Stroller Toy
Recalled Brunch & Go Stroller Toy Tag with model number

Injuries.Name: Fisher-Price has received five reports of the yolk of the toy egg cracking and breaking. In two reports, an infant had a small piece in their mouth. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Hobby Lobby, Kohl's, Macy's, Marshalls, Nordstrom, Ross, TJMaxx and Walmart stores nationwide and online at Amazon.com from February 2022 through March 2025 for about $13 for the stroller toy.

Distributors.Name: Fisher-Price Inc., of East Aurora, New York

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to small parts from cracked egg yolk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy, keep it away from children, and contact Fisher-Price for a free replacement. Mark the egg component with 'Recall' and upload a photo at https://service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx. Dispose of the recalled product after receiving a replacement confirmation.

metaDescription: Fisher-Price recalls Brunch & Go Stroller Toy due to choking hazard from small parts that can break off, posing a risk to young children.

Description: Fisher-Price has recalled the tissue box toy included with the 3-in-1 SnugaPuppy Activity Centers due to a choking hazard. The toy can detach, exposing small parts that may be swallowed by young children. One report involved the toy coming apart, but no injuries have been confirmed.
Title: Fisher-Price Recalls Tissue Box Toy from SnugaPuppy Activity Centers for Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Fisher-Price at 855-853-6224 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx or www.service.mattel.com and click on 'Recalls & Safety Alerts' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Tissue Box Toy from Fisher-Price 3-in-1 SnugaPuppy Activity Centers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,300 (In addition, about 2,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Tissue Box Toy in SnugaPuppy Activity Center
Recalled Tissue Box Toy

Injuries.Name: Fisher-Price has received one report of the tissue box toy coming apart and an infant placing the small support bracket in their mouth. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Walmart stores nationwide, and online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com and other websites from November 2022 through February 2025 for about $130.

Distributors.Name: Fisher-Price Inc., of East Aurora, New York

Hazards.Name: Detachable tissue box toy may separate, revealing small parts that pose a choking hazard to young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled tissue box toy immediately, remove it from the activity center, keep it away from children, and contact Fisher-Price for a free replacement. They will need to permanently mark the toy with 'Recall' and a unique identifier, mark 'X' on the sensory cloth tissues, and upload a photo of the marked toy at https://service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx. After receiving confirmation for the replacement, consumers should dispose of the recalled item. Detailed marking and photo upload instructions can be found at https://service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx. Fisher-Price is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Fisher-Price recalls tissue box toy from SnugaPuppy Activity Centers due to choking hazard from detachable small parts posing risk to young children.

Description: Jonas C is recalling Long-Haul 5L ATV fuel containers because they do not have child-resistant closures as required by federal regulations. This defect creates a risk of burns, fires, and poisoning. The recalled containers are black with markings including "LONG-HAUL," "TZYC," and "PATENT PENDING." No injuries have been reported.
Title: Jonas C Recalls Long-Haul 5L ATV Fuel Containers for Burn, Fire, and Poisoning Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out via email at LongHaulRecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Long-Haul Portable Fuel Container with Accessories

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from January 2023 to October 2024, priced around $50.

Distributors.Name: Cheng du shi ri teng mao yi you xian gong si, DBA Jonas C, of London, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant closures leading to burn, fire, and poisoning risks

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gas cans immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Jonas C for return or disposal instructions to receive a full or partial refund.

Description: Kramer Laboratories is recalling Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu 24-count caplet blister packs because the packaging does not comply with federal child-resistant standards. The non-compliant packaging increases the risk of poisoning if accessed by young children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Kramer Laboratories Recalls Safetussin Max Strength Cough, Cold and Flu Packs for Child Poisoning Risk
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Kramer Laboratories at 800-824-4894 from Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, email kramerlabs@emersongroup.com, or visit https://safetussin.com/recall.

Products.Name: Safetussin Max Strength Cough, Cold and Flu Blister Packs

Images.Caption: Recalled Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu, 24-count caplets

Retailers.Name: Available at HEB, Harris-Teeter, and various regional grocery stores and independent pharmacies across the nation from July 2024 to March 2025 for approximately $11.

Distributors.Name: Kramer Laboratories, of Bridgewater, New Jersey (a subsidiary of Arcadia Consumer Healthcare, of Bridgewater, New Jersey)

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant packaging that poses a poisoning risk to children

Remedies.Name: Secure the product away from children and contact Kramer Laboratories for return or disposal instructions to obtain a full refund.

Description: Lil Pick Up has recalled E-Bully Youth Electric 1000W ATVs sold under various brand names due to failure to meet federal ATV safety standards. The recalled ATVs pose laceration risks from handlebars and collision hazards from ineffective parking brakes, creating a risk of serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Lil Pick Up Recalls E-Bully Youth Electric ATVs for Safety Violations and Injury Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Lil Pick Up at 805-570-4860 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at contact@lilpickup.us. More details are available online at www.lilpickup.us/recall-detail.html or by visiting www.lilpickup.us and selecting 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled E-Bully Youth ATV
Recalled Offroad Mall E-Bully Youth ATV
Recalled E-Bully Youth ATV VIN Plate

Manufacturers.Name: Shandong ODES Industry Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Various online platforms, including Amazon.com, Ebay.com, Seangles.com, and others, sold these ATVs from September 2023 through October 2024 for approximately $1,000.

Importers.Name: Lil Pick Up Inc., of Rowland Heights, California

Hazards.Name: The recalled youth ATVs do not meet federal ATV safety standards, presenting laceration risks from handlebars and potential collision hazards due to ineffective parking brakes, which could lead to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled ATVs immediately and contact Lil Pick Up for a complimentary repair at an authorized service center.

Description: Modus Furniture is recalling Kentfield Solid Wood Eight-Drawer Dressers, also sold as Eliza, Elyza, and Westmont Dressers, due to tip-over and entrapment hazards. These dressers do not meet federal safety standards and can cause serious injury or death if not properly anchored. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Modus Furniture Recalls Kentfield Dressers for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Modus Furniture at 888-859-2129 from 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at 8ZU582A@ModusFurniture.com. More information can be found at www.modusfurniture.com/pages/8zu582a-kentfield-dresser-recall or by visiting www.modusfurniture.com and selecting 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Recalled Kentfield Dresser (production date label)

Retailers.Name: Available at Joybird Stores nationwide and online through wayfair.com, allmodern.com, and joybird.com from January 2024 to December 2024, priced between $980 and $2,000.

Importers.Name: Modus Furniture International, of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment hazards leading to potential injuries or fatalities, especially among children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease use of the Kentfield Dressers immediately and contact Modus for a complimentary repair kit, with an option for free in-home installation.

en.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to JinJiang Baimei Co. Ltd via email at xmjytc@gmail.com for assistance.
en.metaDescription: JinJiang Baimei recalls Multifunctional Puzzle Crab Toys due to ingestion hazard from high-powered magnets violating federal toy safety rules.

Recalled Multifunctional Puzzle Crab Toy
Front of the Package of the recalled Multifunctional Puzzle Crab Toy

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Walmart.com from November 2023 through January 2025 for about $27.

Importers.Name: Lucky Bird Inc., of Buffalo, New York

Distributors.Name: JinJiang Baimei Co. Ltd, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to high-powered magnets

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy and contact JinJiang Baimei for a pre-paid return label and full refund

Description: AmesburyTruth is recalling Ashland casement window hinge tracks because the metal sliding arm can disengage from the hinge track, posing a risk of the window sash falling and causing impact injuries. The recall includes 10-inch and 14-inch hinge tracks identified by specific part numbers and markings. Two incidents of disengagement have been reported, but no injuries have occurred.
Title: AmesburyTruth Recalls Ashland Casement Window Hinge Tracks Due to Impact Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach AmesburyTruth at 844-329-9697 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email HingeTrackRecall@AmesburyTruth.com, or visit www.amesburytruth.com/recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Hinge Track with hinge arm attached
Recalled Hinge Track with the location of the size designation

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reported incidents involving a window sliding arm becoming disengaged from the hinge tracks. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Amesbury Industries Inc., d/b/a AmesburyTruth, of Houston, Texas

Retailers.Name: Distributed by AmesburyTruth to window manufacturers and service parts providers nationwide from April 2024 through January 2025, priced around $2.

Hazards.Name: Risk of window sash falling due to disengagement of the metal sliding arm from the hinge track

Remedies.Name: Contact AmesburyTruth for a free professional inspection and replacement of defective hinge tracks

metaDescription: AmesburyTruth recalls Ashland casement window hinge tracks due to risk of window sash falling and impact injuries. No injuries reported.

Description: Five Below has recalled Room2Room LED Iridescent Bear Lights because the USB power switch can overheat, posing fire and burn hazards. The recall follows reports of the USB cord melting and causing burns to fingers, as well as property damage. The lights are identified by the labels "Room 2 Room," "LED Iridescent Bear Light," and model number "LD-0030."
Title: Five Below Recalls Room2Room LED Iridescent Bear Lights for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Five Below at 844-452-3569, available from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional support can be obtained via email at Recalls@FiveBelow.com or by visiting https://www.fivebelow.com/info/five-below-recalls-led-iridescent-bear-light. Consumers can also find more details by clicking on the "Product Recalls" section at the bottom of the Five Below website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Room2Room LED Iridescent Bear Light
Location of label under the recalled Room2Room LED Iridescent Bear Light

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 28 reports of the USB cord overheating and melting, including six reports of burns to fingers. There were also two reports of property damage, including burns to a wall and a headboard.

Retailers.Name: Five Below stores nationwide and online at fivebelow.com from September 2024 through February 2025 for about $12.

Distributors.Name: 1616 Holdings Inc., of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Five Below Inc., of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Overheating power switch on USB cord leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lights immediately and return them to any Five Below store for a full refund, or contact customer service for a refund by submitting a photo of the light with the cord cut.

metaDescription: Five Below recalls Room2Room LED iridescent bear lights due to fire and burn hazards from overheating USB power switches.

Description: Honda is recalling accessory heaters for certain Pioneer and Talon side-by-side vehicles because they can overheat, posing fire and burn hazards. The recall covers specific model years of the Pioneer 700, Pioneer 1000, and Talon 1000. There have been reports of overheating that caused fires and vehicle damage, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Honda Recalls Accessory Heaters for Pioneer and Talon Side-by-Side Vehicles Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact American Honda at 866-784-1870 from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://powersports.honda.com and click on 'Recall Information' for further details.

Products.Name: Accessory Heaters for 2022-2024 Honda Pioneer 1000, 2023-2025 Pioneer 700, and 2022-2024 Honda Talon 1000

Images.Caption: Recalled accessory heater system and control panel
Compatible Honda Pioneer and Honda Talon vehicles

Injuries.Name: Honda has received three reports of the recalled accessory heaters overheating, including two incidents resulting in fires and vehicle damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Authorized Honda Powersports dealers nationwide from January 2022 through September 2024 for between $900 and $1,040.

Distributors.Name: American Honda Motor Co. Inc, of Torrance, California

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heaters and contact an authorized Honda dealer for a free repair

metaDescription: Honda recalls accessory heaters for Pioneer and Talon side-by-side vehicles due to fire and burn hazards from overheating. Free repairs available.

Description: HONEYJOY is recalling multiple convertible and foldable high chair models due to a suffocation hazard linked to improper marketing for infant sleep and an incline angle exceeding federal limits. These products violate safety regulations for infant sleep products and inclined sleepers. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: HONEYJOY Recalls Convertible and Foldable High Chairs for Suffocation Hazard and Federal Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to HONEYJOY via email at honeyjoyrecall@hotmail.com or visit their Amazon store at https://www.amazon.com/stores/page/A401562A-3E1E-43A1-8F23-3AAE28BA14FC?ingress=2&visitId=596d5ffc-9ec0-4624-baae-6aad8d0c70dd&store_ref=bl_ast_dp_brandLogo_sto&ref_=ast_bln. For further details, click "MORE" at the top of the page and select "RECALL HIGH CHAIR" from the dropdown menu.

Images.Caption: Recalled HONEYJOY 3-in-1 Infant Swing, High Chair and Booster Seat model BE10019US-GR in gray
Recalled HONEYJOY 3-in-1 Infant Swing, High Chair and Booster Seat model BE10019US-PI in pink
Recalled HONEYJOY High Chair model AD10025PI in pink
Recalled HONEYJOY Convertible High Chair model AD1004BE in beige
Recalled HONEYJOY 5-in-1 Convertible High Chair model BB4640BL in blue
Recalled HONEYJOY Convertible High Chair model AD10007BE in beige
Recalled HONEYJOY Foldable High Chair AD10014BE in beige with "Forest Friends" decoration.
Sewn-in Label with Model Number and Date of Manufacture

Retailers.Name: Online exclusively at Amazon.com from April 2023 through August 2024 for between $79 and $220.

Distributors.Name: HONEYJOY COMPANY, of Fontana, California

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper marketing for infant sleep and incline angle violations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled high chairs immediately and contact HONEYJOY for a full refund. Consumers must deface the seat by writing "RECALLED" on it and email a photo of the modified product to honeyjoyrecall@hotmail.com. Follow the disposal instructions provided by HONEYJOY. HONEYJOY and Amazon will reach out to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: HONEYJOY recalls convertible and foldable high chairs due to suffocation risk from improper infant sleep use and incline angle violations.

Description: Superb Sports has recalled baby bath seats due to a risk of serious injury or death from drowning and violations of federal safety standards. The seats, made of polypropylene and EVA plastic with suction cups for stability, pose a drowning hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Superb Sports Baby Bath Seats Recalled for Drowning Hazard and Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DualOranges Store at 980-398-2313 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at info@dualoranges.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Superb Sports baby bath seat – light blue (front view)
Recalled Superb Sports baby bath seat – light blue (top view)

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from February 2024 through November 2024 for between $46 and $60.

Distributors.Name: Shen Zhen Cheng Cheng Ke Ji You Xian Gong Si, dba DualOranges Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to drowning hazard and violation of federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled bath seats and contact DualOranges Store via email at info@dualoranges.com for disposal instructions and to submit a photo for a full refund. Both DualOranges Store and Amazon are reaching out to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Superb Sports recalls baby bath seats due to drowning hazard and federal safety violations. No injuries reported in this recall.
