Description: Sakar is recalling Vivitar Blender Bottles because the blades can operate without the bottle attached, posing a laceration hazard. The bottles are clear plastic with a white base and top, marked with specific identifiers on the base. One incident was reported where the bottle detached while blades were engaged, but no injuries have occurred.
Title: Sakar Recalls Vivitar Blender Bottles Due to Laceration Hazard from Exposed Blades
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sakar at 800-592-9541, via email at support@sakar.com, or visit https://www.vivitar.com/pages/recalls. To find more information, go to https://www.vivitar.com and click on "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vivitar Blender Bottle
VIVITAR, "Description: Blender Bottle," "ITEM: ES15-BB-TA" and "MID #5220824" located under the white plastic base of the bottle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report where the consumer was able to remove the bottle from the base while the blades were engaged. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Target stores nationwide and online at Target.com from December 2024 through January 2025 for approximately $5.

Importers.Name: Sakar International Inc., of Edison, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration due to blades operating without the bottle attached.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the affected blender bottles immediately and contact Sakar for a full refund, which can be received as a $5 Target gift card or a check. A pre-paid return shipping label will be provided. The blender must be returned to Sakar to process the refund, and proof of purchase is not necessary. Do not return these items to Target stores. Sakar is reaching out to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Sakar recalls Vivitar Blender Bottles for laceration risk caused by blades operating without the bottle attached. No injuries reported.

Description: APOTHEKE is recalling its 26 oz. Pumpkin Ginger 3-Wick Scented Candles sold in amber glass containers with gold lettering due to fire, burn, and laceration hazards. The candles can produce excessively high flames and the glass container may break, posing safety risks. There have been reports of flames burning too high, causing minor property damage and one minor burn injury.
Title: APOTHEKE Recalls Pumpkin Ginger 3-Wick Scented Candles for Fire, Burn, and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to APOTHEKE via email at recall@apothekeco.com or visit their website at https://apothekeco.com/pages/candlerecall. Search for 'Recall' on their site for further details.

Products.Name: Pumpkin Ginger 3-Wick Scented Candle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 640 (In addition, about 11 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled APOTHEKE's Pumpkin Ginger 3-Wick Scented Candle

Injuries.Name: APOTHEKE has received six reports of the candle's flames burning too high; four resulting in minor property damage, including singed surfaces and one minor burn to a consumer's hand.

Retailers.Name: Available at Crate & Barrel stores across the country and online at crateandbarrel.com from June 2024 to January 2025, priced around $80.

Distributors.Name: APOTHEKE, of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Fire, burn, and laceration risks due to high flames and potential glass breakage.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the candles immediately and contact APOTHEKE for a full refund, either as store credit or a check. Crate & Barrel will directly reach out to all known buyers. Dispose of the candles after requesting a refund.

metaDescription: APOTHEKE recalls Pumpkin Ginger 3-Wick Scented Candles due to fire, burn, and laceration hazards from high flames and glass breakage risks.

Description: George Oliver six-drawer dressers are recalled due to tip-over and entrapment hazards if not anchored to the wall. The dressers also pose tripping risks from metal support legs. These units do not meet federal safety standards for clothing storage furniture. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: George Oliver Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards Violating Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Pliman at 888-436-7781 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email plimangeorgeoliver@gmail.com, or visit https://www.wingwin.top/recall for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled George Oliver dresser (front view)
Recalled George Oliver dresser (side view)
Recalled George Oliver dresser (back view)

Manufacturers.Name: Zhangzhou Jiesi Intelligent Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Wayfair.com from September 2024 to March 2025 for approximately $275.

Importers.Name: Changzhou Dizeng E-commerce Co. Ltd, dba Pliman, of China

Hazards.Name: Tip-over and entrapment risks due to instability if not anchored, along with tripping hazards from metal support legs.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the dressers if they are not anchored, keep them away from children, and contact Pliman for a full refund. Mark the dressers as 'RECALL,' disassemble them, and send a photo of the disposed item to plimangeorgeoliver@gmail.com. Pliman and Wayfair are reaching out to all known buyers.

metaDescription: George Oliver dressers recalled due to tip-over and entrapment hazards from instability and tripping risks. No injuries reported in this safety recall.

Description: Interior Resources has recalled children's steel utility bunk beds because they do not meet federal safety standards. The bunk beds pose entrapment and strangulation hazards due to design flaws. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Interior Resources Recalls Children's Steel Utility Bunk Beds for Entrapment and Strangulation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Interior Resources at 800-397-7776 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email info@interiorressourcesusa.com. More information is available at https://interiorresourcesusa.com/product-recall or by visiting www.interiorresourcesusa.com and selecting 'Product Safety.'

Images.Caption: Recalled Interior Resources children's steel utility bunk bed (front view)
Recalled Interior Resources children's steel utility bunk bed (side view)
Recalled Interior Resources Children's steel utility bunk bed (back view)

Manufacturers.Name: Delweld Industries Corp., of Stoystown, Pennsylvania

Retailers.Name: Sold to institutions in New York by Interior Resources from January 2017 through May 2024 for approximately $1,800.

Distributors.Name: Interior Resources USA LLC, of New York

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and strangulation hazards due to non-compliance with federal bunk bed regulations.

Remedies.Name: Disassemble the bunk beds immediately and contact Interior Resources for a free repair kit and installation when available.

metaDescription: Interior Resources recalls children's steel utility bunk beds due to entrapment and strangulation hazards from non-compliance with safety regulations.

en.Title: Interior Resources Recalls Children's Steel Utility Bunk Beds for Entrapment and Strangulation Hazards
en.Description: Interior Resources has recalled children's steel utility bunk beds because they do not meet federal safety standards. The bunk beds pose entrapment and strangulation hazards due to design flaws. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Interior Resources at 800-397-7776 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email info@interiorressourcesusa.com. More information is available at https://interiorresourcesusa.com/product-recall or by visiting www.interiorresourcesusa.com and selecting 'Product Safety.'
en.metaDescription: Interior Resources recalls children's steel utility bunk beds due to entrapment and strangulation hazards from non-compliance with safety regulations.

Images.Caption: Recalled Interior Resources children's steel utility bunk bed (front view)
Recalled Interior Resources children's steel utility bunk bed (side view)
Recalled Interior Resources Children's steel utility bunk bed (back view)

Retailers.Name: Sold to institutions in New York by Interior Resources from January 2017 through May 2024 for approximately $1,800.

Distributors.Name: Interior Resources USA LLC, of New York

Manufacturers.Name: Delweld Industries Corp., of Stoystown, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and strangulation hazards due to non-compliance with federal bunk bed regulations.

Remedies.Name: Disassemble the bunk beds immediately and contact Interior Resources for a free repair kit and installation when available.

Description: LaRose Industries has recalled multiple models of Cra-Z-Art Gemex and Gel2Gem jewelry kits because the resin contains acrylates that can cause skin, eye, and respiratory irritation or sensitization. The resin violates the Federal Hazardous Substances Act due to its chemical content. Consumers should be aware that uncured resin poses a health risk, while fully cured resin is not hazardous. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: LaRose Industries Recalls Cra-Z-Art Gemex and Gel2Gem Jewelry Kits for Skin, Eye, and Respiratory Irritation Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the jewelry kits and reach out to LaRose Industries for a full refund. For assistance, contact LaRose toll-free at 877-770-7029 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email gemgel@realtimeresults.net, or visit www.recallrtr.com/gemgel or https://cra-z-art.com/ and click "Recall" at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Cra-Z-Art Gemex Shimmer N Sparkle Gel Set

Recalled Disney Little Mermaid Gel2Gem Jewelry Making Kit (UPC 884920745025) - BACK

Retailers.Name: Target, Marshall's, T.J. Maxx, and Joann stores, and other retailers nationwide and online at Amazon.com, Target.com, Walmart.com and Joann.com from January 2023 through September 2024 for between $13 and $36, depending on the kit.

Importers.Name: LaRose Industries LLC, dba Cra-Z-Art, of Randolph, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: The resin in the jewelry-making kits can cause skin, eye, and respiratory irritation or sensitization when inhaled, touched, or ingested. It contains an acrylate (hydroxyethylmethacrylate "HEMA") in amounts that violate the Federal Hazardous Substances Act. Consumers should inspect any created jewelry to ensure it is fully cured; if any liquid or residue remains, the jewelry should not be worn and should be kept away from children. Once fully cured, the resin is no longer hazardous.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the jewelry kits right away and contact LaRose Industries for a complete refund. LaRose is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: LaRose Industries recalls Cra-Z-Art Gemex and Gel2Gem jewelry kits due to resin causing skin, eye, and respiratory irritation hazards. No injuries reported.

Description: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (NFH) is recalling Iron SAP, Heme Iron SAP, and Prenatal SAP dietary supplements because their bottles do not comply with federal child-resistant packaging requirements. The non-compliant packaging increases the risk of poisoning to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: NFH Recalls Iron SAP, Heme Iron SAP, and Prenatal SAP Supplements for Child-Resistant Packaging Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach NFH toll-free at 866-510-3123 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email info@nfh.ca, or visit https://nfh.ca/ and click on 'Recall' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Iron SAP Iron Glycinate (120 capsules 1167U)
Recalled Iron SAP Iron Glycinate (60 Capsules 1029U)
Recalled Heme Iron SAP Superior Absorption and bioavailability (60 capsules 1124U)
Recalled Prenatal SAP Multivitamin (180 capsules 1034U)
Please see bottom of bottle for lot and expiration: (Example)

Manufacturers.Name: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (dba NFH), of Canada

Retailers.Name: Available at various naturopathic and homeopathic clinics nationwide, A Woman's Time, Doctor Supplement stores, and online at WholescriptsInc.com and Fullscript.com from March 2022 through December 2024, priced between $20 and $95.

Importers.Name: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (dba NFH), of Canada

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the products out of children's reach and contact NFH for a free replacement child-resistant bottle.

metaDescription: NFH recalls Iron SAP, Heme Iron SAP, and Prenatal SAP supplements due to child-resistant packaging violations posing poisoning risks to children.

Description: Specialized Bicycle Components has recalled Vado and Como IGH e-bikes because their chain/belt guards may not provide adequate protection, creating a potential fall hazard. These models feature an internal gear hub without a rear derailleur. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Specialized Recalls Vado and Como IGH E-Bikes for Fall Hazard from Chain/Belt Guards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Specialized Bicycle Components at 877-808-8154 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, via email at ridercare@specialized.com, or visit http://www.specialized.com/safety-recall-notices for more details. Live Chat is also available at http://www.specialized.com.

Products.Name: Specialized Vado IGH E-Bike
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 32,400 (In addition, about 3,200 were sold in Canada and about 190 were sold in Mexico)

Recalled VADO 5.0 IGH STEP THROUGH UL bicycle
Recalled VADO 5.0 IGH ST bicycle
Recalled VADO 5.0 IGH bicycle
Recalled VADO 3.0 IGH bicycle
Recalled Vado 3.0 IGH Step Through UL bicycle

Retailers.Name: Authorized Specialized Bicycle retailers across the country and online, available from March 2021 through November 2024, priced between $3,250 and $5,750.

Importers.Name: Specialized Bicycle Components Inc., of Morgan Hill, California

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to inadequate chain/belt guard coverage.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected e-bikes and contact an authorized Specialized retailer for a complimentary installation of an updated chain/belt guard.

metaDescription: Specialized recalls Vado and Como IGH e-bikes due to fall hazard from inadequate chain/belt guards. No injuries reported in this safety recall.

Description: Arsell is recalling Benadryl Liquid Elixir 100 mL because its packaging does not comply with federal child-resistant requirements. This non-compliance increases the risk of accidental poisoning in young children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Arsell Recalls Benadryl Liquid Elixir 100 mL for Non-Compliant Child-Resistant Packaging
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Arsell via email at recall@arsellsupport.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Benadryl Liquid Elixir, 100 mL bottle and paper box with "X003VRIGUL" on the label

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Amazon.com from July 2023 to October 2024, priced between $16 and $19.

Importers.Name: Arsell Inc., of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant child-resistant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the product out of children's reach and contact Arsell for a full refund. A photo showing the disposal of the recalled product and the Amazon order number will be required.

metaDescription: Arsell recalls Benadryl Liquid Elixir 100 mL due to child poisoning hazard from packaging that fails federal child-resistant standards.

Description: Reckitt has recalled 50-fluid-ounce bottles of Woolite Delicates detergent because of potential contamination with Pseudomonas species bacteria. The recall applies to products with specific lot codes printed on the back of the bottle. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Woolite Delicates Detergent Recalled for Risk of Pseudomonas Bacterial Contamination
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Reckitt at 800-228-4722 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email ConsumerCare_USA@reckitt.com. More information is available at https://www.woolite.us/Voluntary-Recall/ or by visiting https://www.woolite.us/ and selecting “Voluntary Recall” at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Woolite Delicates detergent, 50 fluid ounces
Recalled Woolite Delicates detergent, 50 fluid ounces and location of lot code and SKU

Manufacturers.Name: Reckitt Benckiser LLC, of Parsippany, New Jersey

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com in January 2025 for $14 to $17.

Hazards.Name: Risk of exposure to Pseudomonas species bacteria

Remedies.Name: Full refund for recalled Woolite Delicates detergent

Description: Segway is recalling Ninebot Max G30P and G30LP KickScooters because the handlebars or stem can fold unexpectedly during use, posing a fall hazard. The company has received reports of folding mechanism failures resulting in injuries such as abrasions, bruises, lacerations, and broken bones. This defect increases the risk of serious injury while riding. Consumers should stop using the scooters until the folding mechanism is properly adjusted.
Title: Segway Recalls Ninebot Max G30P and G30LP KickScooters Due to Folding Mechanism Failure and Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Segway at 800-914-6110 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Monday through Friday, email recall@segway.com, or visit https://service.segway.com/recall or www.segway.com and navigate to the 'Recall Center' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Segway Max G30P KickScooter
Recalled Segway Max G30LP KickScooter

Injuries.Name: Segway has received 68 reports of folding mechanism failures, including 20 injuries to include abrasions, bruises, lacerations and broken bones.

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, Costco, Walmart, Target, and Sam's Club nationwide, as well as online at Segway.com and Amazon.com from January 2020 through February 2025, priced between $600 and $1,000.

Importers.Name: Segway Inc., of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of handlebars or stem folding during use, leading to falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled scooters and contact Segway for guidance on adjusting the folding mechanism and to obtain a free maintenance kit, which includes tools and instructions for proper maintenance.

metaDescription: Segway recalls Ninebot Max G30P and G30LP KickScooters due to folding mechanism failure causing fall hazards and risk of serious injury.

Description: Sensio has recalled the Bella Pro Series Steam Espresso Maker (Model 90195) and Cooks Steam Espresso Makers (Model 22395) due to a risk of burn and laceration. The hazard involves the forceful ejection of the brew cup handle during use. The company has received multiple reports of handle ejections, including injuries related to burns and cuts.
Title: Sensio Recalls Bella Pro Series and Cooks Steam Espresso Machines for Burn and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Sensio at 855-647-3125 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.bellakitchenware.com and navigate to the 'Recalls' section for additional information.

Products.Name: Bella Pro Series Steam Espresso Maker

Images.Caption: Recalled Bella Pro Series Espresso machine
Recalled Cooks Espresso machine

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 18 reports of the handles ejecting, eight of which reported burn and/or laceration injuries.

Retailers.Name: JCPenney stores nationwide and online at BestBuy.com and JCPenney.com from August 2023 through January 2025 for between $40 and $60.

Importers.Name: Sensio Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn and laceration due to forceful ejection of brew cup handle

Remedies.Name: Stop using the espresso machines and contact Sensio for a refund

metaDescription: Sensio recalls Bella Pro Series and Cooks Steam Espresso Machines due to burn and laceration hazards from forceful brew cup handle ejection.

Description: Tzumi Electronics is recalling the FitRx SmartBell XL Quick-Select Adjustable Dumbbells because weight plates may detach during use, posing an impact injury hazard. The dumbbells, adjustable from 10 to 90 pounds, have a black and red design with model number 9034. Reports include injuries such as bruises and abrasions linked to the detaching weights. The recall offers a free repair with a new handle and storage tray.
Title: Tzumi Electronics Recalls FitRx SmartBell XL Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Impact Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Tzumi Electronics at 866-363-2237 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email XLRecall@tzumi.com. Additional details can be found online at www.myfitrx.com/Recall or www.fitrxrecovery.com by clicking on 'RECALL' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: FitRx SmartBell XL Adjustable Dumbbells (10-90 lbs.)

Images.Caption: Recalled FitRx SmartBell XL, Quick-Select Adjustable Dumbbell (10-90 lbs.), Model 9034
Location of model number and serial number on the side of the unit's storage tray

Injuries.Name: There have been more than 60 reports of weight plates dislodging during use, which have been linked to at least seven injuries, including bruises, contusions and abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at www.walmart.com from September 2023 to June 2024, priced between $150 and $200.

Importers.Name: Tzumi Electronics Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging during use, leading to potential impact injuries.

Remedies.Name: Free repair with a new dumbbell handle and storage tray.

metaDescription: Tzumi recalls FitRx SmartBell XL adjustable dumbbells due to risk of weight plates detaching and causing impact injuries.

Description: VC Group is recalling 5,000 mAh MagSafe wireless power banks with model number ZHX-PB21 and certain units without PO numbers PO-PP001 to PO-PP010. These power banks pose fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries. The recall follows reports of overheating and fire incidents causing minor injuries.
Title: VC Group Recalls MagSafe Wireless Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Hazards

Description: BMC is recalling Kaius 01 bicycles and framesets equipped with V5 forks because the fork steerer tube may crack or break, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes multiple color variants of the Kaius 01 model. BMC has received reports of steerer tube separation but no injuries have been reported.
Title: BMC Recalls Kaius 01 Bicycles and Framesets Due to Fork Steerer Tube Failure Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach BMC USA at 888-262-7755 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email kaius-fork-recall-USA@BMC-Switzerland.com. Additional information is available at www.bmc-switzerland.com/pages/platform/kaius-01-voluntary-recall or by visiting www.BMC-Switzerland.com and selecting "Learn More" under the "Voluntary Recall for Inspection" section.

Importers.Name: BMC USA Corporation, of Scotts Valley, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fork steerer tube cracking or breaking, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease use of the recalled bicycles and contact an authorized BMC dealer for a complimentary inspection and fork replacement. BMC will directly reach out to all known purchasers.

Description: Chews Life has recalled its Silicone Rosary and Decade teething toys because the two-piece safety snap clasp can detach, posing a choking hazard. The recall includes multiple models featuring colorful beads and a cross. Although there have been reports of the clasp detaching, no injuries have been reported.
Title: Chews Life Recalls Silicone Rosary and Decade Teethers Due to Choking Hazard from Detachable Clasp
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Chews Life at 888-742-2276 from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email sandy@chewslife.com, or visit https://chewslife.com/pages/clasp-recall or www.chewslife.com and click on 'Recall Notices' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Rosary teethers
Recalled Decade teethers
Affected safety snap clasp
Replacement safety snap clasp on repaired teethers

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 92 reports of the safety snap clasp detaching. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Online at chewslife.com from January 2024 through April 2024 for between $20 and $38.

Distributors.Name: Chews Life, of Grand Rapids, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Detachable part of the safety snap clasp posing a choking hazard

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled teethers immediately and contact Chews Life for a free repair. A prepaid shipping label will be provided to return the items, and repaired teethers will be sent back at no cost.

Description: Gerolsteiner is recalling specific 750ml sparkling water bottles due to a laceration hazard caused by potential bottle cracking. The recall applies to bottles with lot numbers 11/28/2024 L and 11/27/2024 L, identified on the lower part of the label. These bottles feature a white, blue, and red label with the Gerolsteiner brand name. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Gerolsteiner 750ml Sparkling Water Bottles Recalled for Laceration Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Gerolsteiner at 800-777-0633 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email customerservice@consup.us or visit https://www.gerolsteiner.de/en/recall for more information.

Hazards.Name: Laceration risk due to potential bottle cracking

Remedies.Name: Stop using the affected bottles and return them to the place of purchase for a full refund, no proof of purchase required.

Description: HabiLife Direct has recalled retractable safety mesh gates due to a serious entrapment hazard. The design allows a child's torso to fit through the gap between the gate and the floor, violating federal safety regulations. No injuries have been reported. Consumers should stop using the gates immediately and follow recall instructions for a refund.
Title: HabiLife Direct Retractable Safety Gates Recalled for Child Entrapment Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact HabiLife Direct at 771-232-2236 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email service@hblife.fans.

Recalled HabiLife safety gate - gray

Label location

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from September 2024 through January 2025 for between $35 and $45.

Distributors.Name: Yiwushihengchuansanyeyouxiangongsi, dba HabiLife Direct, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to design flaw allowing child's torso to fit through the gap

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gates, destroy them, and send a photo of the destroyed gates for a refund

Description: Onasti toddler tower stools are recalled because they can collapse or tip over, posing serious fall and injury hazards to children. The stools feature an adjustable platform and come in multiple colors. There have been reports of injuries, including bruising and a brain injury. Blissful-Time is providing free repair kits to address the safety issue.
Title: Onasti Toddler Tower Stools Recalled for Fall and Injury Hazards by Blissful-Time
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Blissful-Time at 833-999-9327, email support@onasti.net, or visit https://onasti.com/pages/product-recall-information or https://onasti.com/ and select 'Product Recall Information'.

Images.Caption: Recalled Onasti toddler tower stool - beige/white
Recalled Onasti toddler tower stool - blue/gray
Recalled Onasti toddler tower stool - green
Recalled Onasti toddler tower stool - light blue
Recalled Onasti toddler tower stool - pink
The Onasti logo appears on the warning label on the side of the stool
The Onasti logo appears on the warning label on the side of the stool
Onasti toddler tower repair kit
Onasti toddler tower stool with repair kit installed

Injuries.Name: Blissful-Time has received four reports of the stool collapsing or tipping over, including two injuries, with one causing bruising and the other a brain injury to a toddler.

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from March 2024 through December 2024 for between $72 and $90.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshiyaxinrankejiyouxiangongsi, dba Blissful-Time, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of collapse or tipping during use, leading to serious fall and injury hazards for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the recalled stools and keep them out of children's reach until repaired. Contact Blissful-Time to obtain a free repair kit, which includes two steps, one base, and one guardrail. Blissful-Time will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Onasti toddler tower stools recalled due to risk of collapse and falls causing serious injuries to children. Repair kits available from Blissful-Time.

Description: Sublue is recalling black lithium-ion batteries used in Mix underwater scooters because they pose a fire hazard from potential overheating and ignition. The recall follows reports of thermal incidents, swelling, fires, and one injury linked to these batteries. The affected batteries are installed in scooters labeled 'Sublue' and 'Whiteshark Mix Underwater Scooter.'
Title: Sublue Recalls Black Lithium-Ion Batteries for Mix Underwater Scooters Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Sublue at 855-206-8698 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Sunday, email support.us@sublue.com, or visit www.sublue.com/recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sublue Mix Underwater Scooter in arctic white
Recalled Sublue Mix Underwater Scooter in aqua blue
Recalled Sublue Mix Underwater Scooter in space blue
Recalled Sublue Mix Underwater Scooter with “Whiteshark Mix” label
Recalled Sublue Mix battery

Injuries.Name: Sublue has received 161 reports of thermal and swelling incidents involving the lithium-ion batteries, including eight reports of fire, one report of injury, and two reports of property damage totaling $651,400.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.amazon.com, www.costco.com, www.sublue.com, www.westmarine.com, and other platforms from June 2018 to February 2025, priced around $550 for scooters and $150 for replacement batteries.

Importers.Name: Sublue Technology Inc., of Walnut, California

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential overheating and ignition of lithium-ion batteries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the scooters with the recalled batteries and reach out to Sublue for a complimentary replacement battery (gray). Sublue will send a battery collection kit to return the recalled battery and will provide the new battery upon receipt of the recalled one. Note: Recalled batteries should not be discarded in regular trash or standard recycling bins; they must be disposed of according to local regulations.

metaDescription: Sublue recalls black lithium-ion batteries for Mix underwater scooters due to fire hazard from overheating and ignition risks.

Description: Black Diamond Equipment is recalling BD Recon LT avalanche transceivers because corrosion in the switch mechanism may cause malfunction. This defect can impair emergency communication, increasing the risk of serious injury or death during an avalanche. Six reports of switch failures have been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Black Diamond Recalls BD Recon LT Avalanche Transceivers for Communication Failure Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Black Diamond at 866-306-0865 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email 2025reconltrecall@bdel.com or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ for more information.

Products.Name: BD Recon LT Avalanche Transceivers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,660 (In addition, about 206 were sold in Canada). The transceivers were recalled in 2023 for a different hazard

Images.Caption: Recalled Black Diamond Recon LT avalanche transceiver (front)
Recalled Black Diamond Recon LT avalanche transceiver (back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the switch mechanism malfunctioning due to corrosion. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Liberty Mountain, Recreational Equipment Inc. (REI) and other specialty outdoor stores nationwide and online at www.blackdiamondequipment.com, www.backcountry.com and www.evo.com from May 2021 through February 2025 for between $350 and $500.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Corrosion of the metal contact in the switch mechanism may lead to malfunction, risking the ability to locate victims in an avalanche, potentially causing serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled transceivers and contact Black Diamond for a full refund, replacement, or free repair. No proof of purchase is required, and a pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returns. Black Diamond will reach out to known purchasers. To participate in the recall, visit https://warranty.bdel.com/beaconRecall2025/StepOne. The repair involves replacing the original zinc-coated steel insert with a corrosion-resistant stainless-steel version. Consumers can also exchange their BD Recon LT transceiver for a BD Guide BT or BD Recon X model, which feature a different switch design, or opt for a full refund.

metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls BD Recon LT avalanche transceivers due to corrosion risk causing emergency communication failure and potential serious injury hazard.

ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Love Our Home via email at loveourhomerecall@163.com.

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to violation of federal crib bumper ban

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bumpers, cut them in half, take a photo, and email it for a full refund

Description: Luv n' care has recalled Nuby Stroller Fans because the fan blades can be easily accessed, posing a laceration hazard to children. The recall follows reports of children’s fingers reaching the blades, resulting in multiple laceration injuries. These fans are designed for use on strollers and feature adjustable tripod legs and a rechargeable USB port.
Title: Luv n' care Recalls Nuby Stroller Fans Due to Laceration Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Luv n' care at 800-588-6227 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email customers@nuby.com, or visit https://us.nuby.com/pages/fanrecall or www.nuby.us.com and select Important Safety Information for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Nuby Stroller Fan (model and lot numbers on back of fan)
Recalled Nuby Stroller Fan (front)
Recalled Nuby Stroller Fan (side)
Recalled Nuby Stroller Fan (top)

Injuries.Name: Luv n' care has received seven reports of children's fingers accessing the fan blade, resulting in six laceration injuries.

Retailers.Name: Baby Express, Burlington Coat Factory, Target, and Unique Photo stores nationwide, and online at Amazon.com and us.nuby.com from May 2024 through June 2024 for about $15.

Importers.Name: Luv n' care, of Monroe, Louisiana

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration injury due to fan blade exposure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the fan immediately and contact Luv n' care for a free replacement return procedure. The company will reach out to all known customers directly.

metaDescription: Luv n' care recalls Nuby Stroller Fans due to risk of laceration injuries from exposed fan blades. Stop use immediately to prevent injury.

Description: Nanchang Zhongcangjishi E-commerce is recalling Lolanta dinosaur-themed children's bathrobes made of 100% polyester flannel. The bathrobes violate federal flammability standards, posing a burn hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Lolanta Dinosaur Children's Bathrobes Recalled for Burn Hazard and Flammability Violations
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Nanchang Zhongcangjishi E-commerce via email at business@lolanta.com, or visit https://lolanta.com/recalls or https://lolanta.com/ and select 'Product Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Lolanta children's dinosaur flannel bathrobe

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from November 2021 through November 2024 for about $29.

Importers.Name: Nanchang Zhongcangjishi E-commerce Co. Ltd, of China

Distributors.Name: Nanchang Zhongcangjishi E-commerce Co. Ltd, of China

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to violation of federal flammability regulations for children's sleepwear.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bathrobes immediately, remove them from children, and reach out to Nanchang Zhongcangjishi E-commerce for a full refund. Consumers will need to cut the garments in half and send a photo of the destroyed item to business@lolanta.com to receive their refund. Amazon and Nanchang Zhongcangjishi E-commerce are directly notifying all known buyers.

metaDescription: Lolanta dinosaur children's bathrobes recalled due to burn hazard from flammability regulation violations. No injuries reported.

Description: Marie-Chantal has recalled children's nightgowns, pajama sets, and robes that do not comply with federal flammability standards. These garments pose a risk of serious burn injuries or death. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Marie-Chantal Recalls Children's Nightgowns, Pajama Sets, and Robes for Flammability Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to immediately stop using the recalled garments and keep them away from children. For a full refund, contact Marie-Chantal at 888-824-0940 from 8 a.m. to 1:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@mariechantal.com. More information is available at https://www.mariechantal.com/pages/product-recalls or by visiting www.mariechantal.com and clicking on "Recall" at the top of the page.

Importers.Name: Marie-Chantal Ltd., of United Kingdom

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious burn injuries or death due to violation of federal flammability standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the garments, take them away from children, and contact Marie-Chantal for a refund. Description: Taylor Water Technologies is recalling Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles containing sulfuric acid, sold with phosphate test kits or separately. The bottles do not comply with federal child-resistant packaging regulations, posing a risk of chemical burns. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Taylor Water Technologies Recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 Bottles for Chemical Burn Hazard and Packaging Non-Compliance
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Taylor Water Technologies at 800-837-8548 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit their website at https://www.taylortechnologies.com for more information on the recall.

Products.Name: Phosphate Reagent #1 Bottles from Taylor Phosphate Test Kits

Images.Caption: Recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle sold within the Taylor Phosphate Test Kits
Taylor Phosphate Test Kit with recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Taylor Water Technologies LLC, of Sparks, Maryland

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.taylortechnologies.com and in pool and aquarium retail stores across the country from August 2022 through August 2024, priced around $13 for the reagent bottles and $56 for the complete kits.

Hazards.Name: Risk of chemical burns due to sulfuric acid in non-child-resistant packaging

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bottles, secure them away from children, and contact Taylor Water Technologies for disposal instructions and a free replacement.

metaDescription: Taylor Water Technologies recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles due to chemical burn hazard from sulfuric acid and failure to meet child-resistant packaging standards.

Description: Canyon Bicycles USA is recalling Speedmax CF triathlon bicycles equipped with V21 aerostems due to a risk of fall and injury from possible cracking or breaking of the fork steerer tube. The affected bikes include various color combinations and are identified by model numbers R073 and R41. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Canyon Bicycles USA Recalls Speedmax CF Triathlon Bikes Due to Aerostem Fork Steerer Tube Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Canyon Bicycles USA at 833-226-9661 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. They can also visit www.canyon.com/en-us/contact/ for chat support or access further details at https://www.canyon.com/en-us/customer-service/repair-spares-warranty/speedmax-cf.html. Additionally, consumers can click on 'Speedmax Recall' at the bottom of the Canyon homepage for more information.

Products.Name: Canyon Speedmax CF Triathlon Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,400 (In addition, about 380 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Canyon Speedmax CF bicycle with V21 aerostem
The model number can be found on a sticker at the top of the seat tube, just below the saddle

Retailers.Name: Available at Canyon.com from May 2017 through July 2024, priced between $4,000 and $5,000.

Importers.Name: Canyon Bicycles USA Inc., of Carlsbad, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to potential cracking or breaking of the aerostem fork steerer tube.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the affected bicycles immediately and contact a Canyon Authorized Service Partner for a complimentary repair. The repair process will include the removal and inspection of the fork steerer tube, a replacement of the fork if necessary, and reinforcement of the clamping area of the fork steerer tube. Canyon will also reach out directly to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Canyon Bicycles recalls Speedmax CF triathlon bikes for fall hazard caused by potential cracking or breaking of the aerostem fork steerer tube.

Description: Costway is recalling Babyjoy high chairs because their design poses entrapment and suffocation hazards. The chairs have an incline angle that exceeds federal safety standards for high chairs. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Costway Recalls Babyjoy High Chairs for Entrapment and Suffocation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Costway at 844-242-1885 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recall@costway.com, or visit https://www.costway.com/recall-high-chair for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Babyjoy high chair model BB5656PI
Recalled Babyjoy high chair model BB5656BE
Recalled Babyjoy high chair model BB5656GR

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Costway.com and Shein.com from November 2022 through November 2024 for between $70 and $110.

Distributors.Name: Goplus Corporation, dba Costway, of Fontana, California

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and suffocation risks due to design flaws and incline angle exceeding federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the high chairs immediately and contact Costway for a full refund and return instructions.

metaDescription: Costway recalls Babyjoy high chairs due to entrapment and suffocation hazards from design flaws and noncompliance with safety regulations.

Description: Forever 21 has recalled 20 styles of children's pajamas that do not comply with federal flammability regulations, posing a fire hazard. The recalled items include various pants, shorts, and two-piece sets labeled with the Forever 21 brand and specific item numbers. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Forever 21 Recalls Children's Pajamas for Fire Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Forever 21 at 888-219-4074 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, email recall@forever21.com, or visit www.forever21.com/recall for more information.

Products.Name: Forever 21 Children's Pajamas
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 24,700 (In addition, about 80 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Forever 21 Children's Pajama Pants
Recalled Forever 21 Children's Pajama Shorts
Recalled Forever 21 Children's Pajama Pants Set
Recalled Forever 21 Children's Pajama Shorts Sets
The pajama's item number is located on a sewn-in, side-seam label below the bar code

Retailers.Name: Available at Forever 21 stores nationwide and online at https://www.forever21.com

Distributors.Name: F21 OpCo LLC, dba Forever 21, of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to noncompliance with flammability regulations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pajamas and contact Forever 21 for a full refund. Consumers must destroy the pajamas by cutting them in half and send a photo of the destroyed items to recall@forever21.com.

metaDescription: Forever 21 recalls children's pajamas for fire hazard risk due to failure to meet federal flammability standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Goal Zero is recalling the Solar Series Combiner Cable model 98715 because it poses a potential fire hazard when used with Nomad-series solar panels. The cable connects multiple solar panels to charge batteries and features multiple high power port connectors. There have been reports of fires causing property damage, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Goal Zero Recalls Solar Series Combiner Cables Model 98715 Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled cables immediately and reach out to Goal Zero LLC at 888-794-6250 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. They can also email support@goalzero.com or visit https://goalzero.com/pages/notices-recalls for further assistance.

Products.Name: Goal Zero Solar Series Combiner Cable Model 98715

Images.Caption: Recalled Goal Zero Solar Series Combiner Cables for Solar Panels
The recalled Goal Zero Solar Series Combiner cable may have been sold with Goal Zero Nomad Solar Panels or Goal Zero Yeti PRO 4000

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of fires involving property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.goalzero.com and other retailers from October 2023 through September 2024, priced around $70 individually or as part of a kit.

Importers.Name: Goal Zero LLC, of Draper, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential fire hazard due to malfunction with Nomad-series solar panels

Remedies.Name: Consumers can obtain a refund in the original payment method by registering on the Goal Zero website and disposing of the recalled cable as per local regulations.

metaDescription: Goal Zero recalls Solar Series Combiner Cables model 98715 due to fire hazard risk when used with Nomad-series solar panels.

Description: Kichler Lighting has recalled Ellerbeck Wall Sconces because damaged electrical wires at the adjustable joints can cause electric shock. The recall applies to sconces manufactured on or after June 16, 2023, installed as plug-in fixtures without an electrical box. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Kichler Ellerbeck Wall Sconces Recalled for Electric Shock Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Kichler at 866-558-5706, available from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information can be found online at www.kichler.com/legal/safety-information/recall-information-43115 or by visiting www.kichler.com and selecting 'Safety Recall Information' at the bottom of the page. Additionally, consumers can use the 'Contact Us' webform at www.kichler.com/customer-care/contact-us to submit inquiries regarding the product.

Products.Name: Ellerbeck Wall Sconces
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,760 (In addition, about 215 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Ellerbeck Wall Sconce Light
Location of the Production date code underside of the sconce wall plate

Retailers.Name: Available at lighting showrooms across the country and online at Amazon.com and Lowes.com from June 2023 to December 2024, priced between $165 and $240.

Importers.Name: Kichler Lighting LLC, of Solon, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Risk of electric shock due to damaged electrical wires at adjustable joints.

Remedies.Name: Inspect the wall sconce installation as a plug-in fixture without an electrical box, unplug immediately if recalled, and follow instructions for destruction and replacement.

metaDescription: Kichler recalls Ellerbeck Wall Sconces due to electric shock risk from damaged wires in adjustable joints. No injuries reported.

More information can be found online at www.kichler.com/legal/safety-information/recall-information-43115 or by visiting www.kichler.com and selecting 'Safety Recall Information' at the bottom of the page. Additionally, consumers can use the 'Contact Us' webform at www.kichler.com/customer-care/contact-us to submit inquiries regarding the product.

Description: P.J. Salvage is recalling children's pajama sets and robes that do not meet federal flammability standards, creating a burn hazard. The recall includes various plush and fleece sleepwear items in sizes S-XL. These products were sold exclusively at Nordstrom Rack. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: P.J. Salvage Recalls Children's Pajama Sets and Robes for Flammability Violation and Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact P.J. Salvage at recall@pjsalvage.com or visit https://www.pjsalvage.com/pages/kids-recall. You can also find details by clicking on the "Recall" link at the bottom of their homepage.

Hazards.Name: Violation of flammability regulations for children's sleepwear, posing a burn risk.

Remedies.Name: Immediately remove the recalled items from children, destroy them by cutting in half, and complete the online registration form at www.pjsalvage.com/pages/recall-information to confirm destruction.

Description: Triangle Tube is recalling its Prestige Solo, Prestige Excellence, and Aerco Esteem gas boilers due to a risk of carbon monoxide poisoning caused by delayed ignition or damaged components. The recall covers multiple models produced between 2011 and 2019. Two deaths and multiple reports of carbon monoxide leaks have been linked to these boilers. The units are wall-hung condensing boilers with a white metal casing labeled 'Prestige' or 'Aerco' on the front panel.
Title: Triangle Tube Recalls Prestige Solo, Prestige Excellence, and Aerco Esteem Gas Boilers for Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Triangle Tube at 877-574-5036 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at productrecall@triangletube.com. More information is available at https://triangletube.com/products/product-recall/ or by visiting www.triangletube.com and selecting 'PRODUCT RECALL.'

Products.Name: Prestige Solo Gas Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: Approximately 60,000 (In addition, about 7,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Prestige Excellence Boiler
Recalled Prestige Solo Boiler
Recalled Prestige Solo Trimax Boiler
Recalled Aerco Esteem Boiler
Recalled Aerco Esteem Watts Brand Boiler

Injuries.Name: Triangle Tube has received two reports of death due to CO poisoning associated with the recalled boilers. In 2015, a 47-year-old woman died from CO poisoning associated with a Prestige Solo 175 boiler, and in 2016, an 85-year-old man died from CO poisoning associated with a Prestige SOLO TriMax 175 boiler, following a repair. Triangle Tube has also received 25 reports of CO leaks from the recalled boilers.

Manufacturers.Name: Burnsen SA, of Belgium

Retailers.Name: These boilers were produced by Burnsen SA in Belgium and sold through wholesale distributors and independent contractors across the country, with prices ranging from $3,400 to $9,700.

Importers.Name: Triangle Tube/Phase III Co. Inc., of West Deptford, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to potential delayed ignition or component failure.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Triangle Tube for a free inspection and, if needed, free installation of a new ignition system. Those using the recalled boilers should have functioning carbon monoxide alarms in their homes and can request a free alarm from Triangle Tube.

metaDescription: Triangle Tube recalls Prestige Solo, Prestige Excellence, and Aerco Esteem gas boilers due to carbon monoxide poisoning risk from delayed ignition or component failure.

Description: Wayfair is recalling Style Life Eleven baby loungers because they pose suffocation and fall hazards that violate federal safety regulations for infant sleep products. These loungers include a head pillow and fold into a portable handbag, and are marked with the brand name in cursive on a white square. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Wayfair Recalls Style Life Eleven Baby Loungers for Suffocation and Fall Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled loungers and reach out to Wayfair at 888-549-1625 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or via email at AdvocacyTeam@wayfair.com. Additional information can be found online at https://www.wayfair.com/product_recall/ or by visiting www.Wayfair.com and clicking on "Product Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Recalled Style Life Eleven Portable Baby Loungers in the Pattern of Gray/Brown Crowns
Recalled Style Life Eleven Portable Baby Loungers in the Pattern of Pink/Blue Zoo Animals

Retailers.Name: Online exclusively at Wayfair.com from June 2022 through September 2024 for between $34 and $144.

Distributors.Name: Bespoke Clothing Valley Inc., (Royalbelle), d/b/a Zoomie Kids, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation and fall hazards due to violations of federal safety regulations for infant sleep products.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the loungers and contact Wayfair for disposal instructions to receive a full refund.

Description: DR Power Equipment is recalling Tow Behind Field and Brush Mowers because the blade carrier assembly can detach, posing a laceration hazard. The recall affects specific models with certain serial numbers and manufacture dates. The company has received reports of blade carrier detachments but no injuries have been reported. A free repair is offered to address the hazard.
Title: DR Power Equipment Recalls Tow Behind Field & Brush Mowers for Laceration Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled mowers and contact DR Power at 800-458-4362 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be obtained via email at recalls@DRPower.com or online at http://drpower.com/recalls.

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Equipment Tow Behind Field and Brush Mower

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of blade carrier detachments. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: DR Power Equipment, of South Burlington, Vermont, a subsidiary of Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Retailers.Name: Available at various retailers nationwide

Hazards.Name: Laceration risk due to detachment of blade assembly

Remedies.Name: Free repair of the blade carrier assembly

Description: Fjorden Electra AS has recalled the Fjorden iPhone Camera Grip due to a risk of battery ingestion. The device’s battery tray can detach, exposing a lithium coin battery that may be swallowed by children. No injuries have been reported. This recall addresses a violation of federal safety regulations for products containing coin batteries.
Title: Fjorden iPhone Camera Grips Recalled for Battery Ingestion Hazard Due to Detachable Battery Tray
ConsumerContact: For more details, consumers can reach out to Fjorden Electra AS via email at hello@fjorden.co or visit shop.fjorden.co/us-recall or shop.fjorden.co/en-us and click on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Fjorden iPhone Camera Grip
Recalled Fjorden iPhone Camera Grip with Battery Tray

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at shop.fjorden.co from March 2024 to May 2024 for approximately $122.

Distributors.Name: Fjorden Electra AS, of Norway

Hazards.Name: Risk of battery ingestion due to accessible lithium coin battery if the battery tray separates

Remedies.Name: Stop using the camera grip and keep it away from children. Contact Fjorden Electra AS for a free replacement battery tray and instructions.

metaDescription: Fjorden recalls iPhone Camera Grips because the detachable battery tray poses a battery ingestion hazard to children, violating federal safety rules.

Description: Nazzaro is recalling children’s bowling pin sipper cups because the red tape on the cups contains excessive lead, violating federal lead content limits. The cups are white with red tape around the neck, a red plastic cap, and a label with item number PG1045. No injuries have been reported related to this recall.

ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Nazzaro at 800-777-2235 from 10 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at service@rebeccas.com. Additional details are available online at https://www.rebeccas.com/recall.html or by visiting www.rebeccas.com and clicking on the "Recall" link at the bottom of the page.

Description: OdorStop has recalled its Boot and Shoe Dryers and Deodorizers, models OSOBSDD2 and OSOBSDD, due to a potential fire hazard. The devices may experience a short circuit in the heat or ozone on-off switch, which can cause electrical arcing. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: OdorStop Boot and Shoe Dryers and Deodorizers Recalled for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using the recalled products immediately and reach out to OdorStop LLC at 800-414-2191, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. For further assistance, email support@odorstop.com or visit www.OdorStop.com/recall for detailed information.

Products.Name: OdorStop Boot and Shoe Dryer Model OSOBSDD2

Images.Caption: Recalled OdorStop Boot and Shoe Dryers and Deodorizers, models OSBSDD2 & OSBSDD

Retailers.Name: Available online through Amazon.com, Odorstop.com, Lowes.com, Globalindustrial.com, and webrestaurantstore.com from December 2018 to October 2023, priced between $100 and $130.

Importers.Name: OdorStop LLC, of Hamburg, New York

Hazards.Name: Potential fire hazard due to short circuiting in heat or ozone switch

Remedies.Name: Free repair by installing a new in-line fuse

Description: Sauder Woodworking is recalling tip-over restraint straps included with certain dressers and 4-drawer chests manufactured between July 2020 and August 2023. These units, sold under Sauder and Sauder Beginnings brands, pose tip-over and entrapment hazards that could cause serious injury or death to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Sauder Woodworking Recalls Tip-Over Restraint Straps for Dressers and 4-Drawer Chests Due to Child Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sauder at 866-218-8312, Monday through Friday, from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET. More information is available online at www.Sauder.com by clicking on 'Product Safety Recall' or visiting www.sauder.com/About/Public-Notices/recall.

Products.Name: Sauder Dressers and 4-Drawer Chests Tip-Over Restraint Straps

Images.Caption: Recalled Tip-Over Restraint Strap
424263 BEGINNINGS 4-DRAWER CHEST Sold with Recalled Restraint Straps
428205 NORTH AVENUE DRESSER Sold with Recalled Restraint Straps
422806-01 BEGINNINGS DRESSER Sold with Recalled Restraint Straps
422805-01 BEGINNINGS 4-DRAWER CHEST Sold with Recalled Restrained Straps
Location of Manufacture Date

Retailers.Name: Available at Cook Brothers, Homemakers Furniture Plaza, Menards, and online through Amazon.com, BestBuy.com, CYMAX.com, HomeDepot.com, Menards.com, Sauder.com, Target.com, UrbanOutfitters.com, Walmart.com, Wayfair.com, and other retailers from July 2020 to December 2024, priced between $200 and $335.

Importers.Name: Sauder Woodworking Co., of Archbold, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment hazards that may lead to serious injury or death to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to verify if their unit has a date of manufacture sticker dated between July 2020 and August 2023. If it does, they should contact Sauder to receive a complimentary replacement tip-over restraint kit. It is important to remove the old restraint and install the new one upon arrival, while ensuring children are kept away from the unit during this period.

Description: SEJOV is recalling five-drawer dressers sold in white and black with gold-colored knobs due to tip-over and entrapment hazards. These dressers do not comply with federal safety regulations and may tip over if not anchored, posing risks especially to children. The company has received reports of instability but no injuries have been reported.
Title: SEJOV Five-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact SEJOV at 800-331-9670, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or email SEJOVrecall@aliyun.com. More information is available at https://sejov.com/pages/us-recall or by visiting https://sejov.com and selecting 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sejov five-drawer dresser in white
Recalled Sejov five-drawer dresser in black

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the dressers being unstable. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from September 2023 through August 2024, priced between $100 and $180.

Distributors.Name: Dongguanshi Yanxiang Keji Youxiangongsi, dba SEJOV, of China

Hazards.Name: The dressers may tip over if not anchored to the wall, creating significant risks of entrapment and injury, particularly to children. They do not meet the safety standards outlined in the STURDY Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the dressers immediately if they are not wall-anchored and move them out of children's reach. Contact SEJOV for guidance on obtaining a full refund and provide a photo of the disposed product. Amazon is reaching out to all known buyers.

metaDescription: SEJOV recalls five-drawer dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that violate federal safety standards for clothing storage units.

en.Title: SEJOV Five-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
en.Description: SEJOV is recalling five-drawer dressers sold in white and black with gold-colored knobs due to tip-over and entrapment hazards. These dressers do not comply with federal safety regulations and may tip over if not anchored, posing risks especially to children. The company has received reports of instability but no injuries have been reported.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact SEJOV at 800-331-9670, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or email SEJOVrecall@aliyun.com. More information is available at https://sejov.com/pages/us-recall or by visiting https://sejov.com and selecting 'Recall' at the top of the page.
en.metaDescription: SEJOV recalls five-drawer dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that violate federal safety standards for clothing storage units.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sejov five-drawer dresser in white
Recalled Sejov five-drawer dresser in black

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the dressers being unstable. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from September 2023 through August 2024, priced between $100 and $180.

Distributors.Name: Dongguanshi Yanxiang Keji Youxiangongsi, dba SEJOV, of China

Hazards.Name: The dressers may tip over if not anchored to the wall, creating significant risks of entrapment and injury, particularly to children. They do not meet the safety standards outlined in the STURDY Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the dressers immediately if they are not wall-anchored and move them out of children's reach. Contact SEJOV for guidance on obtaining a full refund and provide a photo of the disposed product. Amazon is reaching out to all known buyers.

Description: Super73 is recalling specific Z Miami SE and Z Adventure Core electric bicycles due to a risk of brake failure caused by a dislodged retaining pin in the disc brake calipers. This defect can lead to crashes and injuries. The company has received reports of loose retaining pins and related brake failures, including one minor injury.
Title: Super73 Recalls Z Miami SE and Z Adventure Core Electric Bicycles for Brake Failure Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Super73 at 888-841-3584 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email safety@super73.com. More details can be found at https://super73.com/pages/product-recall or by visiting https://super73.com and clicking on the 'Product Safety Advisory' link at the bottom of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Super73 Z Miami SE e-bike - Model Year 2024
Recalled Super73 ZX Adventure Core e-bike - Model Year 2024
Disc brake caliper on the recalled e-bikes

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 21 reports of loose retaining pins and/or related brake failure, resulting in one minor injury.

Manufacturers.Name: Super73 Inc., of Irvine, California

Retailers.Name: Available at Super73's Irvine, California store, various bicycle retailers nationwide, and online at https://Super73.com from April 2024 through September 2024, priced between $2,300 and $2,500.

Hazards.Name: Risk of brake failure due to a dislodged retaining pin in the disc brake calipers, leading to potential crashes and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected e-bikes immediately and reach out to Super73 for a complimentary repair kit, which includes a new retaining pin and reimbursement of up to $50 for professional installation. Receipts for installation must be submitted for reimbursement. Super73 and its retailers will directly notify all affected purchasers.

Description: SuperATV is recalling specific carrier bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S recreational off-highway vehicles due to fire and burn hazards caused by potential fuel tank leakage. The affected bearings, marked with 'SuperATV' on the housing, can lead to fuel tank damage from contact with the prop shaft. There have been reports of fuel tank cracks and one fire, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: SuperATV Recalls Carrier Bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S Over Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact SuperATV at 855-728-8256 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also visit https://form.jotform.com/243023918871055 to verify if your part is affected, or check https://www.superatv.com/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: SuperATV Carrier Bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S

Images.Caption: Recalled SuperATV Carrier Bearing Model BEA01-001
Recalled SuperATV Carrier Bearing Model BEA01-002
Recalled SuperATV Carrier Bearing Model BEA01-005 (Rev A00 and A01)
Location of SuperATV Brand Name on Carrier Bearing

Injuries.Name: SuperATV has received 24 reports of the fuel tank contacting the prop shaft, including 19 reports that a hole or crack had formed in the fuel tank, including one reported fire. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: SuperATV, of Madison, Indiana

Retailers.Name: Online at www.superatv.com and at off-road vehicle dealers nationwide from April 2017 until September 2022 for between $85 and $250 (for one carrier bearing) and between $170 and $420 (set of 2 carrier bearings).

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential fuel tank leakage

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled carrier bearings, remove them, and register online for a return label to receive free replacements.

Description: Trek is recalling certain Electra Navigator Go! and Ace of Spades Go! e-bike models because the rear fender can detach, posing a crash hazard. There have been reports of the fender coming loose and contacting the rear wheel, resulting in one injury. The defect can cause loss of control and potential crashes.
Title: Trek Recalls Electra Navigator Go! and Ace of Spades Go! E-Bikes Due to Rear Fender Crash Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the affected Electra e-bikes immediately and reach out to Trek at 800-373-4594 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday. More information can be found online at www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/company/legal_policies/safety_recalls/ or by visiting www.trekbikes.com and selecting 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Electra Navigator Go! (2021 & 2022)
Recalled Electra Ace of Spades Go! (2021 & 2022) and Recalled Electra Ace of Spades Go! (2018)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the rear fender coming loose and making contact with the rear wheel, including one injury that involved a fractured shinbone, meniscus tear and scrapes.

Retailers.Name: Available at Trek-owned bicycle stores and independent bicycle retailers across the country, as well as online at www.electra.trekbikes.com from June 2018 through August 2023, priced around $3,000.

Importers.Name: Trek Bicycle Corporation, of Waterloo, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of rear fender detachment leading to loss of control and potential crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled e-bikes and contact an authorized Trek or Electra retailer for a free repair to replace the rear fender bolts. Trek will also be reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Trek recalls Electra Navigator Go! and Ace of Spades Go! e-bikes due to rear fender detachment risk that can cause crashes and injuries.

Description: Walmart is recalling Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Commuter Electric Scooters due to fire and burn risks from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Reports include battery overheating, smoking, melting, and ignition, with one incident causing a burn injury and property damage. The scooters are identified by model numbers on the deck and the "SWAGGER 5" label on the handlebars.
Title: Walmart Recalls Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Electric Scooters Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT daily or visit www.walmart.com/help. More details on the recall can be found at https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls.

Products.Name: Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Electric Scooter

Images.Caption: Recalled Black Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Commuter Electric Scooter (side view)
Recalled Black Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Commuter Electric Scooter (folded)
Recalled Silver Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Commuter Electric Scooter (side view)
Recalled Silver Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Commuter Electric Scooter (folded)
Location of model and serial number
Close-up of model and serial number
Location of battery pack beneath scooter deck

Injuries.Name: There have been seven reports of the lithium-ion batteries in the electric scooters overheating, smoking, melting or igniting, with one ignition resulting in a fire, causing a burn injury and substantial property damage to a residential apartment building.

Manufacturers.Name: Swagtron, of South Bend, Indiana

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart and Sam's Club locations nationwide and online at Walmart.com and SamsClub.com from May 2018 through October 2024, priced between $175 and $450.

Distributors.Name: Walmart, of Bentonville, Arkansas

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burn from overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using and charging the scooters, cut the throttle cord, and dispose of them according to local hazardous waste guidelines. Customers will receive an email with refund instructions.

metaDescription: Walmart recalls Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost electric scooters for fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries.

Description: AFTCO has recalled Youth Solitude Rain Jackets because the retractable drawstring in the hood poses a strangulation hazard. These jackets are available in youth sizes XS to L and feature the AFTCO logo. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: AFTCO Recalls Youth Solitude Jackets with Hood Drawstrings Due to Strangulation Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact AFTCO at 877-489-4278 from 7 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@aftco.com, or visit www.aftco.com/recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled AFTCO Youth Solitude Jacket
The label inside of the recalled AFTCO Youth Solitude Jacket

Retailers.Name: Online at www.aftco.com, and at fishing, camping, and outdoor equipment stores nationwide from November 2018 through August 2024 for between $80 and $100.

Importers.Name: AFTCO Mfg. Co., of Santa Ana, California

Hazards.Name: Strangulation hazard due to drawstring in the hood

Remedies.Name: Return for a full refund or submit a photo without the drawstring for a $25 gift card

metaDescription: AFTCO recalls Youth Solitude Jackets with hood drawstrings due to strangulation hazard. No injuries reported. Return or receive a gift card.

Description: BlockBlueLight has recalled the Multi-Mode Sleep Lamp (model BBL-MM-LAMP) because its lithium-ion battery may overheat, posing fire and burn hazards. The lamp offers three color modes: Noblue Amber, Sunset Mixed, and Twilight Red. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: BlockBlueLight Recalls Multi-Mode Sleep Lamps for Fire and Burn Hazards from Lithium-Ion Batteries
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach BlockBlueLight Limited at 917-277-8422 from 2 p.m. to 10 p.m. ET, Sunday through Thursday, email at product@blockbluelight.com, or visit https://www.blockbluelight.com/pages/multi-lamp-recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled BlockBlueLight Multi-Mode Sleep Lamp
Model number printed on base of the recalled lamp

Retailers.Name: Available online at https://www.blockbluelight.com from December 2023 to November 2024 for approximately $80.

Importers.Name: BlockBlueLight Limited, of New Zealand

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lamps, request a full refund, store credit, or a replacement lamp, and dispose of them per local regulations.

metaDescription: BlockBlueLight recalls Multi-Mode Sleep Lamps due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. No injuries reported.

Description: The 6-in-1 Pounding Game by DMITOY has been recalled because a strong magnet inside the toy can detach, creating an ingestion hazard. The toy includes various components such as wooden carrots and worms with metal heads, and a bird containing the magnet. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: DMITOY Recalls 6-in-1 Pounding Game for Ingestion Hazard from Loose Magnet
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact DMITOY via email at dmitoy202405@163.com for further assistance.

Recalled 6-in-1 Pounding Game Sold by Dmitoy (Front of Box)
Recalled 6-in-1 Pounding Game Sold by Dmitoy (Game Contents)

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Temu.com from May 2024 through July 2024 for about $20.

Distributors.Name: DMITOY, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to loose magnet

Remedies.Name: Stop using the game, dispose of it safely, and send a disposal photo to DMITOY for a full refund

metaDescription: DMITOY recalls 6-in-1 Pounding Game due to ingestion hazard from a loose magnet that can detach and pose serious safety risks to children.

Description: SHEIN is recalling EVRYDAY Kids two-piece pajama sets made of polyester and elastane because they do not meet federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear. The pajamas pose a burn hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: SHEIN Recalls EVRYDAY Kids Pajama Sets for Violation of Federal Flammability Standards
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach SHEIN at 833-853-8668 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email uscsteam@shein.com, or visit https://us.shein.com/campaigns/productrecalls or www.SHEIN.com and click on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Recalled SHEIN EVRYDAY Kids Children's Pajama Set - purple
Recalled SHEIN EVRYDAY Kids Children's Pajama Set - orange

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at www.SHEIN.com from August 2023 through November 2024 for approximately $20.

Distributors.Name: SHEIN Distribution Corporation, of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Violation of federal flammability regulations for children's sleepwear, posing a burn injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the pajamas immediately, keep them away from children, and contact SHEIN for a full refund. SHEIN will reach out to known purchasers.

Description: Target is recalling Spritz Resin Hanukkah Dino Menorahs because they can catch fire when lit candles are used. The menorahs are made of polymer resin and hold up to nine tapered candles in metal cups. Reports include instances of scorching, charring, melting, or catching fire, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Target Recalls Spritz™ Resin Hanukkah Dino Menorahs Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled menorahs immediately and return them to any Target store for a full refund via a Target gift card or the original payment method. For mail returns, consumers can contact Target at 800-440-0680 for in-store purchases or 800-591-3869 for online purchases, or visit https://help.target.com/help/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: Spritz Taper Resin Hanukkah Dino Menorah

Images.Caption: Recalled Taper Resin Hanukkah Dino Menorah
Recalled Taper Resin Hanukkah Dino Menorah

Injuries.Name: Target has received 58 reports of the menorahs scorching, charring, melting or catching on fire. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Target stores nationwide and online at Target.com from October 2024 to January 2025 for approximately $15

Importers.Name: Target Corporation, of Minneapolis, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential for burning or catching fire with lit candles

Remedies.Name: Full refund available through in-store return or prepaid return label for mail return

metaDescription: Target recalls Spritz Resin Hanukkah Dino Menorahs due to fire hazard risk from candles catching fire during use. No injuries reported.

Description: LG is recalling Slide-In and Freestanding Electric Ranges because front-mounted knobs can be unintentionally activated, creating a fire risk. The affected models were manufactured in Mexico and Korea. The company has received reports of fires causing property damage but no injuries have been reported.
Title: LG Recalls Slide-In and Freestanding Electric Ranges for Fire Hazard from Front-Mounted Knobs
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to LG at 800-399-3265, available from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday. You can also email lgrange.recall@lge.com or visit https://lgecares.com/rangerecall to request a free warning sticker and receive instructions on its placement and usage of the Control Lock feature.
