Description: Pella is recalling sliding patio doors and windows equipped with automated shades and Insynctive technology due to an ingestion hazard from accessible button cell batteries. The recall includes Pella Reserve and Pella Lifestyle sliding doors and windows with specific models and date codes. No injuries have been reported related to this hazard.
Title: Pella Recalls Sliding Patio Doors and Windows with Insynctive Technology Due to Button Cell Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to contact Pella at 844-807-2219 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.pella.com and click on 'Recall Notice' for further details. For more information, you can also go to https://www.pella.com/recall-notice.

Description: UVIPC Baby Gates have been recalled because their design poses an entrapment hazard, violating federal safety regulations for gates and enclosures. The pressure-mounted gates, available in multiple versions including some with a pet door, can open inward and outward, increasing risk. No injuries have been reported. The recall addresses these safety concerns to prevent potential harm.
Title: UVIPC Baby Gates Recalled for Entrapment Hazard and Federal Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Xianshengyouli at 626-529-6048 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, or email xianshengyouli@outlook.com.

Distributors.Name: Shangchengxianxianshengyouliguojimaoyishanghang, dba Xianshengyouli Storefront, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to violation of federal safety regulations

Description: Sunkids Factory is recalling sling carriers with style number 0007-66, featuring white plastic buckles, adjustable straps, and mesh fabric in green, gray, and pink. The recall addresses violations of federal safety standards related to structural integrity and occupant retention, creating a fall hazard for infants. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Sunkids Factory Sling Carriers Recalled for Fall Hazard Due to Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled sling carriers immediately. To receive a full refund, take a photo of the sling carrier cut into pieces and email it to 1251530867@qq.com or message Sunkids Factory on Temu.com. The company is also reaching out to known purchasers directly.

Description: TravisMathew is recalling women's half-zip sweatshirts and men's and women's hoodies because they do not meet federal flammability standards, posing a burn hazard. The recall includes multiple styles and colors in sizes S to XXL. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: TravisMathew Recalls Women's Sweatshirts and Men's and Women's Hoodies for Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to TravisMathew at 877-969-1952, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Additional support can be found via email at info@travismathew.com or by visiting https://www.travismathew.com/recallnotice. More information is also accessible on their website under 'Recall Information' in the Customer Support section.

Importers.Name: TravisMathew LLC, of Huntington Beach, California

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to violation of federal flammability standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the garments and contact TravisMathew for a full refund or store credit. Consumers must destroy the items and send a photo of the destroyed garment to receive compensation.

Description: Cabinet Health is recalling certain 4 oz. refillable medicine bottles because their lids may fail to maintain child resistance. These clear glass bottles with colored ABS plastic lids were sold filled or empty. The lids can degrade over time, increasing the risk of children accessing the contents and potential poisoning. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Cabinet Health Recalls 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottles for Child-Resistance Failure
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Cabinet Health at 908-242-6108 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@wearecabinet.com. More information is available at https://cabinethealth.com/voluntaryrecall or by visiting https://cabinethealth.com and clicking on "Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Cabinet Health Inc., of Washington, D.C.

Retailers.Name: Available online at cabinethealth.com, Amazon.com, and Grove.co, as well as at CVS stores from March 2023 through July 2024, priced between $10 and $20.

Hazards.Name: Child safety risk due to potential failure of lid closure

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the recalled bottles away from children and contact Cabinet Health for a free replacement lid. A photo of the destroyed lid will be required. Cabinet Health and Amazon are reaching out to all known purchasers. The recall does not affect the medication inside the bottles.

metaDescription: Cabinet Health recalls 4 oz. refillable medicine bottles due to child-resistance failure posing a child poisoning hazard. No injuries reported.

Description: Greater Goods is recalling multiple models of its Digital Kitchen Scales due to violations of federal safety standards related to lithium coin batteries and child-resistant packaging. The scales contain accessible CR3032 lithium coin batteries that pose a serious ingestion hazard to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Greater Goods Recalls Digital Kitchen Scales for Ingestion Hazard and Battery Packaging Violations
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to immediately remove the battery from the scale and keep it out of children's reach. For assistance, contact Greater Goods at 866-991-0397 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email recall@greatergoods.com, or visit www.greatergoods.com and click the recall banner or go to https://shop.greatergoods.com/pages/replaceyourbatterydoor for further details.

Description: Johnson Health Tech North America has recalled specific Matrix-brand Training Cycles because the adjustable seat can unexpectedly lower during use, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes models CXP-03, CXC-02, CXM-03, CXV, and CXM-02 with certain serial number prefixes. The company has received reports of seats lowering unexpectedly, including incidents where users fell, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Johnson Health Tech Recalls Matrix Training Cycles Due to Fall Hazard from Seat Lowering
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. at 866-218-3674 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email cxrecall@matrixfitness.com. More information can also be found online at https://www.matrixfitness.com/us/eng/recalls or by visiting www.matrixfitness.com and selecting 'Recalls'. The company is actively contacting all known purchasers.

Products.Name: Matrix CXP-03 Training Cycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,885 (In addition, about 140 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXC-02
Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXM-02, Model CXM-03
Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXP-03
Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXV
Location of Serial Number on Rear of Cycle Frame

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 63 reports of seats unexpectedly lowering, including two reports that users fell off the cycle when the seat lowered. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Primarily sold to commercial fitness facilities directly by JHTNA and through third-party distributors from January 2021 to October 2024, priced between $2,530 and $7,305.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. ("JHTNA"), of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to unexpected lowering of the adjustable seat during use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled cycles immediately and contact Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. to arrange for a service technician to install a complimentary repair kit.

metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech recalls Matrix training cycles due to fall hazard from unexpected seat lowering during use. No injuries reported.

en.Description: Johnson Health Tech North America has recalled specific Matrix-brand Training Cycles because the adjustable seat can unexpectedly lower during use, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes models CXP-03, CXC-02, CXM-03, CXV, and CXM-02 with certain serial number prefixes. The company has received reports of seats lowering unexpectedly, including incidents where users fell, but no injuries have been reported.
en.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. at 866-218-3674 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email cxrecall@matrixfitness.com. More information can also be found online at https://www.matrixfitness.com/us/eng/recalls or by visiting www.matrixfitness.com and selecting 'Recalls'. The company is actively contacting all known purchasers.
en.metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech recalls Matrix training cycles due to fall hazard from unexpected seat lowering during use. No injuries reported.

Products.Name: Matrix CXP-03 Training Cycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,885 (In addition, about 140 were sold in Canada)

Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXM-02, Model CXM-03
Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXP-03

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 63 reports of seats unexpectedly lowering, including two reports that users fell off the cycle when the seat lowered. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Primarily sold to commercial fitness facilities directly by JHTNA and through third-party distributors from January 2021 to October 2024, priced between $2,530 and $7,305.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. ("JHTNA"), of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to unexpected lowering of the adjustable seat during use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled cycles immediately and contact Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. to arrange for a service technician to install a complimentary repair kit.

Description: Pearhead is recalling its Learning Toy Sets for infants aged 0-6 months due to a choking hazard from the wooden egg-shaped rattle. The toy sets include multiple developmental items such as a cow stroller toy, crinkle sheep toy, tummy time book, plush activity cube, and wooden egg shaker rattle. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Pearhead Recalls Infant Learning Toy Sets Due to Choking Hazard from Wooden Egg Rattle
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Pearhead at 888-308-4928 from 9:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email service@pearhead.com. Additional details are available at https://www.pearhead.com/pages/product-recall or by visiting www.pearhead.com and selecting 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pearhead, Inc. Learning Set
Recalled Pearhead Learning Set Model Number

Manufacturers.Name: Pearhead, of Brooklyn, New York

Retailers.Name: Kohls stores nationwide and online at Kohls.com, Oyaco.com, Faire.com, Pearhead.com and Amazon.com from August 2024 through October 2024 for about $30.

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to wooden egg-shaped rattle

Remedies.Name: Stop using the egg shaker and contact Pearhead for a free replacement and refund.

Description: Duluth Trading Company is recalling two-piece children’s holiday pajama sets because they do not meet federal flammability standards, creating a burn hazard. The pajamas are made of 100% cotton and come in four festive designs. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Duluth Trading Company Recalls Children’s Holiday Pajama Sets for Flammability Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Duluth Trading at 866-300-9719 from 7 a.m. to 10 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email ProductRecallK0040@duluthtrading.com. More details are available at https://www.duluthtrading.com/content/recall or by visiting www.duluthtrading.com and clicking on "Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Duluth Trading Children's Holiday Pajama Sets
HBT, "CRE," "CBV" or "MNG" is printed on the pajama's side-seam label

Retailers.Name: Available at Duluth Trading stores nationwide and online at www.duluthtrading.com from October 2019 through November 2024 for approximately $35.

Distributors.Name: Duluth Holdings Inc., dba Duluth Trading Company, of Mt. Horeb, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Violation of flammability regulations for children's sleepwear, posing a burn injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled pajamas, keep them away from children, and contact Duluth Trading for a full refund or store credit. They will need to destroy the garments by cutting them in half and send a photo of the destroyed items to ProductRecallK0040@duluthtrading.com. Duluth Trading will notify all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Duluth Trading recalls children’s holiday pajama sets due to flammability violations posing a burn hazard. No injuries reported.

Description: NQDTPBOR has recalled multi-purpose helmets for teens due to non-compliance with federal bicycle helmet safety standards. The helmets, sold in small and medium sizes and multiple colors, pose an increased risk of head injury in crashes. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmets Recalled for Head Injury Risk and Federal Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For further details, contact TPBOR via email at nqdtpbor@outlook.com or visit their Seller Profile on Amazon.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmet (Blue)
Recalled NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmets (Green)
Recalled NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmet (Black)
Recalled NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmet (Pink)
Recalled NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmet (Purple)

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from May 2024 to August 2024, priced around $23.

Importers.Name: CLSYS International Inc., of New York

Distributors.Name: Yiwu Baqianliu Trading Co. Ltd., dba TPBOR, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with federal safety regulations for bicycle helmets, increasing the risk of head injury during crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the helmets immediately and reach out to TPBOR for a full refund. To process the refund, destroy the helmet by cutting the straps, take a photo of the destroyed helmet, and email it to nqdtpbor@outlook.com. After submitting proof of destruction, dispose of the helmet responsibly. TPBOR and Amazon will contact all known buyers directly.

metaDescription: NQDTPBOR recalls multi-purpose helmets due to federal safety violations posing a head injury hazard. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: HallyBee is recalling its Farmhouse 5-Drawer Dressers because they pose tip-over and entrapment hazards if not anchored to a wall. These dressers do not comply with federal safety regulations for clothing storage units. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: HallyBee Farmhouse 5-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach HallyBee at 973-281-2585 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at yueke113@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled HallyBee Farmhouse Dresser (front view)
Recalled HallyBee Farmhouse (back view)
Recalled HallyBee Farmhouse (side view)

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from September 2023 to October 2024, priced around $210.

Distributors.Name: YKHallyBee, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment hazards if not anchored to the wall, posing potential injuries or fatalities to children.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the dressers if they are not anchored, move them out of children's reach, mark them as 'recalled' with a permanent marker, and contact YKHallyBee for a full refund. A photo of the disposed dresser must be submitted to yueke113@163.com.

metaDescription: HallyBee recalls Farmhouse 5-Drawer Dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that violate federal safety standards.

Description: Ivy City has recalled multiple styles of children's pajamas due to violations of federal flammability standards that pose a burn hazard. The recalled items include various sizes and styles labeled with the Ivy City brand. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Ivy City Recalls Children's Pajamas for Flammability Violations and Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Ivy City at 888-915-8609 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email hello@ivycityco.com, or visit https://ivy-city-co.gorgias.help/en-US/recall-information-936646 or https://ivycityco.com/ and select 'FAQ' followed by 'Recall' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Mini Petra Nightgowns
Recalled Mini Camille Pajamas with and without Feathers
Recalled Mini Bella Bunny Pajamas without Feathers
Recalled pajamas neck and side seam labels

Retailers.Name: Ivy City store in Riverton, Utah

Importers.Name: Ivy City Co., of Riverton, Utah

Hazards.Name: Flammability violations posing burn risks to children

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pajamas, keep them away from children, and contact Ivy City for a full refund. Consumers should destroy the pajamas by cutting them in half and disposing of them. To receive a refund, email a photo of the destroyed garment to Ivy City at hello@ivycityco.com. Ivy City will also reach out to known purchasers directly.

Description: Lexmark is recalling the MS725dvn and MX725adve specialty printers because a metal part inside the printers can become dislodged, creating a potential fire hazard. The company has received reports of overheating and one fire incident, but no injuries or damage have been confirmed. These models are used for printing on materials such as paper and vinyl and are identifiable by their model numbers on the top cover and door.
Title: Lexmark Recalls MS725dvn and MX725adve Specialty Printers Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Lexmark at 866-470-1574 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email them at safety@lexmark.com or visit http://www.lexmark.com/recall for more details.

Products.Name: Lexmark MS725dvn Specialty Printer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 43,510 (In addition, about 4,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled MS725dvn Specialty Printer
Recalled MX725adve Specialty Printer

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the printers overheating including one reported fire. No damage or injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at lexmark.com, CDW Logistics, Tech Data Product Management Inc., and Insight Direct USA Inc. from June 2018 to November 2024, priced between $1,000 and $4,200.

Importers.Name: Lexmark International Inc., of Lexington, Kentucky

Hazards.Name: Potential fire hazard due to dislodging metal part inside the printer

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the printers and contact Lexmark for a free repair part that installs easily inside the rear access panel.

metaDescription: Lexmark recalls MS725dvn and MX725adve specialty printers due to a fire hazard from a dislodging metal part inside the device.

Description: Three61 is recalling its Samurai Mini Smoke Alarms, model SM1, because they may fail to alert consumers in the event of a fire. These compact alarms were sold in sets and include lithium-ion batteries and mounting accessories. The failure to activate creates a risk of smoke inhalation or death. No injuries have been reported related to this issue.
Title: Three61 Recalls Samurai Mini Smoke Alarms Due to Fire Alert Failure
ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach Three61 via email at recall@samuraibrands.com or visit www.samuraibrands.com/recall for further assistance. A recall banner is also available on the homepage for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm - front
Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm - back
Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm – packaging
Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm - packaging

Injuries.Name: Three61 has received eight reports of the alarms failing to activate in the presence of smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively through HSN and online at HSN.com from July 2020 to November 2024, priced between $40 and $50.

Importers.Name: Three61 LLC, of Palm Harbor, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of smoke inhalation or death due to failure to alert consumers of a fire.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Three61 for a free replacement alarm. To initiate the process, they must mark the recalled smoke alarm with 'Recalled' using a permanent marker and submit a photo of it on the recall website. The replacement will be shipped immediately upon registration.

metaDescription: Three61 recalls Samurai Mini Smoke Alarms for failure to alert consumers of fires, posing a serious fire hazard. No injuries reported.

Description: Veyer is recalling WorkPro Momentum office chairs because a bolt connecting the chair back to the base can fail, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes black and gray models with specific model numbers located under the seat. There have been reports of falls, including two minor injuries.
Title: Veyer Recalls WorkPro Momentum Office Chairs Due to Fall Hazard from Bolt Failure
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Veyer at 833-881-0087 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email support@momentumwarranty.com, or visit https://help.officedepot.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/7555/kw/recall or www.officedepot.com and navigate to the "Recall Notices" section at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled WorkPro Momentum Office Chair

Injuries.Name: Veyer has received seven reports of consumers falling out of the chair, resulting in two minor injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Office Depot and OfficeMax locations nationwide, as well as online through www.amazon.com, www.officedepot.com, www.walmart.com, www.ebay.com, and other retailers from October 2021 to September 2024, priced around $430.

Importers.Name: Veyer LLC, of Boca Raton, Florida

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to bolt failure connecting chair back to base.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled chairs immediately and reach out to Veyer for a complimentary repair kit, which includes shipping and installation instructions. The kit contains a back bracket, three bolts, an Allen wrench, and a decorative cover. Veyer will directly contact all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Veyer recalls WorkPro Momentum office chairs due to a fall hazard caused by bolt failure connecting the chair back to the base.

Description: Vornado is recalling VH2 Whole Room Heaters marked with date codes "JUL24" or "AUG24" due to risks of electric shock and fire caused by potential cord detachment. These off-white heaters feature thermostatic temperature control and two heat settings. No injuries have been reported in connection with this hazard.
Title: Vornado VH2 Whole Room Heaters Recalled for Electric Shock and Fire Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled heaters and contact Vornado at 844-205-7978 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, or via email at vh2recall@vornado.com. More information is available online at www.vornado.com/recalls/vh2 or by visiting www.vornado.com and selecting "Product Recalls" at the top of the page to verify if their heater is affected.

Images.Caption: Recalled VH2Whole Room Heater
Silver rating label on the underside of the heater

Manufacturers.Name: Vornado Air LLC, of Andover, Kansas

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from August 2024 through October 2024 for approximately $90

Hazards.Name: Risk of electric shock and fire due to potential cord detachment

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the heaters and contact Vornado for a free replacement

Description: Wemfg is recalling its children's multi-purpose bike helmets because they do not meet federal safety regulations, creating a risk of head injury in crashes. The helmets are size small and come in multiple color combinations with model number MD-710-S. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Wemfg Recalls Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmets for Head Injury Risk
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Wemfg via email at 961837339@qq.com or visit their Amazon Storefront page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Wemfg Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmet – green and blue
Recalled Wemfg Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmet – pink/purple/pink
Recalled Wemfg Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmet – pink/yellow/blue
Recalled Wemfg Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmet – blue and pink
Recalled Wemfg Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmet – pink/blue/green
Recalled Wemfg Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmet – green/blue/purple
Recalled Wemfg Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmet – purple/pink/yellow
Recalled Wemfg Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmet – purple/pink/blue

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from April 2024 to July 2024, priced between $26 and $32.

Importers.Name: Shenzhenshimofanggekejiyouxiangongsi, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with safety regulations, posing a risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the helmets and contact Wemfg for a full refund. To obtain a refund, destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, take a photo of the detached straps, and email it along with the Amazon order number to 961837339@qq.com. After submitting proof of destruction, dispose of the helmet.

metaDescription: Wemfg recalls children's bike helmets due to non-compliance with safety standards posing a head injury hazard. No injuries reported.

Description: Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil is recalled because it contains methyl salicylate and does not have child-resistant packaging, posing a poisoning risk to children. The product violates the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil Recalled for Poisoning Hazard and Packaging Noncompliance
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact DLWL-US via email at gzzlhus@163.com or visit www.euqee.net/page9. Click on the "Recall" section at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from December 2023 through August 2024 for approximately $8.

Distributors.Name: DingLinWangLuoKeJiGuang ZhouYouXianGongSi, dba DLWL-US, of China

Hazards.Name: The essential oil contains methyl salicylate and lacks child-resistant packaging, increasing the risk of poisoning if ingested by children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately keep the recalled essential oil out of children's reach and contact DLWL-US for a full refund. A pre-paid label and free packaging will be provided for the return of the product. DLWL-US and Amazon.com are reaching out to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil recalled due to poisoning hazard from lack of child-resistant packaging violating safety regulations.

Description: Yaomiao children's jewelry sets are recalled because they contain lead and cadmium levels that exceed federal safety limits. The sets include multiple pieces with designs such as unicorns, butterflies, and rainbows, packaged in a lavender box labeled "A Special Gift for the Charming You." No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Yaomiao Children's Jewelry Sets Recalled for Lead and Cadmium Poisoning Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact LordRoads at simlordroadple@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Yaomiao Unicorn Children's Jewelry Set
Recalled Yaomiao Unicorn, Butterfly, and Rainbow Necklace and Earring Children's Jewelry Set
Recalled Yaomiao Unicorn Children's Necklaces and Rings Jewelry Set
The recalled Yaomiao jewelry comes inside a lavender box with "A Special Gift for the Charming You" printed on it
Recalled Yaomiao Unicorn Jewelry Set - Unboxed

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from January 2022 to March 2024, priced between $8 and $21.

Distributors.Name: Wuhannuoyunxindianzikejiyouxiangongsi, (dba LordRoads), of China

Hazards.Name: Lead and cadmium levels exceed federal safety standards, posing health risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the jewelry sets, keep them away from children, and reach out to LordRoads for a full refund. They will need to dispose of the jewelry and provide a photo of the disposed items to simlordroadple@163.com. LordRoads and Amazon are reaching out to all known buyers.

metaDescription: Yaomiao children's jewelry sets recalled due to hazardous lead and cadmium levels posing poisoning risks to children.

Description: BCI Burke has recalled Inclusive Orbit merry-go-rounds due to a crushing hazard caused by a gap between the rotating platform and the stationary outer rim. The product includes three handrails and seating for up to six children, with a platform level to the ground. The firm reported two finger crushing injuries linked to this hazard. The recall applies to model numbers 560-0042 and 560-0051.
Title: BCI Burke Recalls Inclusive Orbit Merry-Go-Rounds for Finger Crushing Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BCI Burke Company at 800-266-1250 from 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email orbit@bciburke.com, or visit https://www.bciburke.com/recall or www.bciburke.com and select 'Recall' at the top of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Inclusive Orbit merry-go-round
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 415 (In addition, about 6 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Inclusive Orbit merry-go-round

Injuries.Name: The firm has received reports of two incidents of fingers getting trapped in the gap between the rotating platform and the outer rim, resulting in two finger crushing injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: Playdale Playgrounds LTD, of United Kingdom

Retailers.Name: Sold through factory representatives directly to entities such as municipalities, schools and churches from April 2019 through October 2024 for between $11,900 and $14,550.

Importers.Name: BCI Burke Company Inc., of Fond Du Lac, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Crushing hazard due to gap between rotating platform and stationary rim

Remedies.Name: Stop using the merry-go-rounds and contact the equipment owner for inspection and repair. Schedule a free onsite inspection with BCI Burke.

metaDescription: BCI Burke recalls Inclusive Orbit merry-go-rounds due to a finger crushing hazard from gaps between the rotating platform and outer rim.

Description: ONME Direct has recalled GIKPAL 12-drawer dressers sold exclusively on Walmart.com because they pose tip-over and entrapment hazards. The dressers do not comply with federal safety regulations designed to prevent tip-over incidents. No injuries have been reported in connection with these products.
Title: GIKPAL 12-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards by ONME Direct
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact ONME Direct at 833-449-5777 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT on weekdays, and from 1 p.m. to 2 p.m. PT on weekends. You can also reach them via email at Greenservice88@outlook.com or visit https://onmeus.com/pages/recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled GIKPAL Dresser (front view)
Recalled GIKPAL Dresser (back view)
Recalled GIKPAL Dresser (side view)

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Leader-Union Technology Co. LTD, of China

Retailers.Name: Walmart.com from January 2024 through November 2024 for about $91.

Distributors.Name: ONME Direct, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment, especially for children, due to lack of proper wall anchoring. The dressers do not meet the safety standards outlined in the STURDY Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the dressers if they are not anchored to a wall and to relocate them out of children's reach. Contact ONME Direct for refund instructions and to submit a photo of the disposed product. ONME Direct and Walmart will reach out to all known buyers.

metaDescription: GIKPAL 12-drawer dressers recalled for tip-over and entrapment hazards due to failure to meet federal safety standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Precor is recalling the Resolute™ Cable Multi-Station exercise equipment due to a risk of impact injury from the pulley carriage unexpectedly dropping. The recall addresses reports of the pulley dropping during use, which resulted in minor head injuries. The equipment includes an adjustable pulley carriage for cable resistance exercises and is used in gyms. A free on-site repair with a redesigned pop-pin and warning label is provided to mitigate the hazard.
Title: Precor Recalls Resolute™ Cable Multi-Station Exercise Equipment for Impact Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Precor Incorporated at 800-347-4404, via email at support@precor.com, or visit www.precor.com/recall. You can also find more details by clicking on 'RECALL INFORMATION' at the top of the homepage.

Products.Name: Precor Resolute Cable Multi-Station Exercise Equipment

Images.Caption: Recalled Resolute Cable Multi-Station

Injuries.Name: Precor has received nine reports of the pulley dropping during use, including two minor head injuries.

Retailers.Name: Sold to fitness facilities nationwide from July 2023 to October 2024, priced between $3,020 and $3,340.

Importers.Name: Precor Incorporated, of Woodinville, Washington

Hazards.Name: Potential for impact injury due to pulley carriage drop

Remedies.Name: Free on-site repair including redesigned pop-pin and warning label installation

metaDescription: Precor recalls Resolute Cable Multi-Station exercise equipment due to impact injury hazard from pulley carriage dropping during use.

Description: Chervon North America is recalling SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries due to overheating that can lead to fire and burn hazards. The recall applies to batteries marked "SKIL" and "PWRCORE 40," sold separately and with SKIL 40V Brushless mowers. The firm has received reports of thermal incidents including overheating, melting, smoking, fire, minor burns, and property damage.
Title: Chervon Recalls SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries for Fire and Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Chervon North America at 833-476-5325 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email BY8708-00recall@na.chervongroup.com. More information is available at www.skil.com/recalls or by visiting www.skil.com and selecting "PRODUCT RECALLS" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Battery, model BY8708-00
Nameplate located on housing of the recalled battery shows model number and manufacturing date code (first 3 digits of serial number)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 100 reports of thermal incidents involving the batteries including overheating, melting, smoking and fire. These included eight reports of minor burns and/or smoke inhalation and 49 reports of related property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's and various hardware and home improvement stores nationwide, as well as online through Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and Lowes.com from October 2019 through December 2024, priced around $170 for individual batteries and about $400 for combination kits.

Importers.Name: Chervon North America Inc., of Naperville, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled batteries immediately and contact Chervon for a free replacement or refund. Register for the recall at www.skil.com/recalls. Chervon will send a collection kit for returning the recalled battery.

metaDescription: Chervon recalls SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries due to overheating risks that can cause fire and burns. Affected batteries were sold with SKIL outdoor tools.

Description: Melii Baby is recalling silicone spoons designed for infants because the spoon tips can break, posing a choking hazard. The recall includes spoons sold in three- and four-packs with specific date codes, as well as those in the five-piece feeding sets. The company has received two reports of spoon tip breakage but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Melii Baby Silicone Spoons Recalled for Choking Hazard Due to Breakage
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Melii Baby Inc. toll-free at 855-492-4470 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or visit https://meliibaby.com/pages/recall or https://meliibaby.com and click on the 'Recall' link on the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Melii Baby Silicone Spoons (3-Pack)
Recalled Melii Baby Silicone Spoons (4-Pack)
Recalled Melii Baby 5-Piece Silicone Feeding Set (Cat and Dino) – spoons only
Recalled Melii Baby 5-Piece Silicone Feeding Set (Dog and Shark) – spoons only

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the spoon tip breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Aldi Grocery Stores and Ross and online at buybuyBaby.com, Amazon.com, Sierra.com, MotherandDaughterCreations.com, Just Between Friends (jbfsale.com), MacroBaby.com, TrendyLilTreats.com, TheBrickKitchenStore.com and AdventureSnacks.com from April 2024 through August 2024 for between $8 and $9.

Importers.Name: Melii Baby Inc., of Canada

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to potential breakage of the silicone spoon

Remedies.Name: Stop using the spoons, keep them away from children, and contact Melii Baby Inc. for a free replacement set. Take photos of the spoons, dispose of them, and email the images to care@meliibaby.com to receive the replacement.

metaDescription: Melii Baby recalls silicone spoons for infants due to choking hazard from potential breakage. No injuries reported. Stop use immediately.

Description: Charmast has recalled its Model W1056 power banks due to fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The recall covers units in multiple colors with the brand name on the front and model number on the back. There have been reports of the batteries expanding, igniting, melting, overheating, or smoking, including incidents causing burns or blisters. Consumers should stop using the recalled power banks immediately.
Title: Charmast Model W1056 Power Banks Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Charmast at 929-636-0293, Monday through Friday, from 10 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET, via email at rcus@charmast.com, or visit https://www.charmast.com/pages/recall-info-page. More details can also be found by clicking on 'Recalls' at the top of www.charmast.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Charmast W1056 power banks (front view)
Recalled Charmast W1056 power bank in black (back view)

Injuries.Name: Charmast has received 44 reports of the recalled power banks expanding, igniting, melting, overheating or smoking, including four reports of consumers receiving burns or blisters.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from December 2018 to September 2024, priced between $14 and $25.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Charmast Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Charmast for a full refund, providing necessary photographs and disposing of the product according to regulations.

metaDescription: Charmast recalls Model W1056 power banks due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Stop use and seek a refund.

Description: HTRC has recalled its C240 battery chargers because they pose fire and burn hazards. These chargers, used for lithium-ion batteries, have caused thermal incidents including property damage and injury. The risk includes serious injury or death from potential ignition. Users should discontinue use immediately.
Title: HTRC C240 Battery Chargers Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled chargers immediately and reach out to HTRC at 951-215-8389 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, email servicesupport@ht-rc.com, or visit www.ht-rc.com/recalls.asp for further instructions on returning the chargers for a full refund.

Images.Caption: Recalled HTRC C240 battery charger

Injuries.Name: HTRC has received 32 reports of fire and other thermal incidents, including five reports of significant property damage and one report of injury.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Haitan Technology Co. Ltd., d/b/a HTRC, of China

Retailers.Name: Available at AliExpress.com, Amazon.com, Banggood.com, eBay.com, HTRC-Direct.com, and RCGoing.com from December 2017 through June 2024, priced between $65 and $290.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Return for a full refund

Description: Mr. Christmas has recalled wireless decorative tree light controllers because the wireless receiver can overheat, posing a fire hazard. The recalled products include models 39611, 39612, and 39630, sold under different brandings and designs. The company has received reports of overheating and one fire, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Mr. Christmas Recalls Wireless Decorative Tree Light Controllers Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Mr. Christmas at 800-489-6328 any day from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, email recall@mrchristmas.com, or visit www.mrchristmas.com/recall.

Description: Stanley is recalling Switchback and Trigger Action stainless steel travel mugs because the lids can shrink with heat and detach during use, posing a burn hazard. The recall includes mugs in various sizes and colors with polypropylene lids. There have been reports of lids detaching, resulting in burn injuries, some requiring medical attention.
Title: Stanley Recalls Switchback and Trigger Action Travel Mugs Due to Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, call Stanley's toll-free number at 866-792-5445 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also visit www.Stanley1913TMrecall.expertinquiry.com or go to www.stanley1913.com to submit a recall claim or find more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Stanley Switchback Travel Mug
Recalled Stanley Trigger Action Travel Mug
The product identification number is located on the bottom of the mug

Injuries.Name: Stanley has received 91 reports worldwide, including 16 in the U.S., of the recalled travel mugs' lids detaching during use, resulting in 38 burn injuries worldwide, including two burn injuries in the U.S., with 11 consumers worldwide requiring medical attention.

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon.com, Walmart, Dick's Sporting Goods, Target, and various other retailers nationwide, from June 2016 through December 2024, priced between $20 and $50.

Importers.Name: PMI WW Brands LLC, dba Stanley 1913, of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Lid threads may shrink with heat, leading to detachment and burn risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive a free replacement lid, including shipping, upon contacting Stanley.

metaDescription: Stanley recalls Switchback and Trigger Action travel mugs due to lid detachment causing burn hazards. Replacement lids are provided free of charge.

Description: Touch of Class is recalling Wall Teester Bed Crowns because the welded mounting tabs can break, causing the crowns to fall and pose an injury hazard. The recall includes various styles and sizes of these decorative wall-mounted crowns. There has been one reported injury involving a scratch from a falling bed crown.
Title: Touch of Class Recalls Wall Teester Bed Crowns Due to Risk of Falling Injury
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Touch of Class at 800-457-7456 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email customerservice@touchofclass.com, or visit www.touchofclass.com/product-recall-details/a/85/ for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Wall Teester Bed Crown (AT36 Victoriana)
Recalled Wall Teester Bed Crown (AT37 Viviana)
Recalled Wall Teester Bed Crown (AW41 Valeria)
Recalled Wall Teester Bed Crown (AW42 Valensia)
Recalled Wall Teester Bed Crown (AW43 Victoria Rose Antique Ivory)
Recalled Wall Teester Bed Crown (AW43 Victoria Rose Satin Gold)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of incidents of the bed crowns failing due to broken mounting tabs, including one incident of the bed crown detaching from the wall. One injury has been reported, resulting in a scratch to the consumer's cheek.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Touchofclass.com and Amazon.com from March 2022 through August 2024 for approximately $149.

Importers.Name: Parke-Bell Ltd. Inc., dba Touch of Class, of Huntingburg, Indiana

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury from falling bed crown

Remedies.Name: Full refund or free replacement offered

Description: Yoto Mini portable speakers for children have been recalled due to the risk of overheating lithium-ion batteries, which can cause fire and burn hazards. The recall follows multiple reports of the speakers overheating or melting, though no injuries have been reported. A free battery replacement kit is now offered to address the safety concern.
Title: Yoto Mini Speakers Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards Due to Overheating Battery
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yoto Ltd. via email at minihelp@yotoplay.com, visit us.yotoplay.com/recall, or go to us.yotoplay.com and click on 'RECALL INFORMATION' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Yoto Mini Speakers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 251,165 (In addition, about 18,932 were sold in Canada) (The speakers were previously recalled in April 2024.)

Images.Caption: Recalled Yoto Mini Speaker

Injuries.Name: Yoto has received nine reports from U.S. consumers and three from a UK consumer of the speaker overheating or melting. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Online at us.yotoplay.com, Amazon.com, Target.com and maisonette.com, and in toy and gift stores nationwide from November 2021 through April 2024 for about $70.

Importers.Name: Yoto Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating lithium-ion battery leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the speakers immediately, keep them away from children, and request a free battery replacement kit from Yoto. Dispose of lithium-ion batteries according to local regulations.

metaDescription: Yoto Mini speakers recalled for fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. Free battery replacement kits are available.

Description: Belkin has recalled the BoostCharge Pro Fast Wireless Charger portable 10K power banks due to overheating lithium-ion batteries that pose a fire hazard. The recalled units include model number BPD005 with a white magnetic charger for smart watches and a black power bank. One incident of a fire causing property damage has been reported.
Title: Belkin Recalls BoostCharge Pro 10K Wireless Chargers for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Belkin at 800-223-5546 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit http://www.belkin.com/bpd005recall. More information is also available at https://www.belkin.com under the 'Product Recalls' section.

Products.Name: Belkin BoostCharge Pro 10K Wireless Charger
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,000 (In addition, about 62 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Belkin BoostCharge Pro Fast Wireless Charger and Power Bank 10K

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the recalled power bank catching fire, resulting in a house fire involving property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, belkin.com, and other retailers from May 2023 through June 2024, priced around $100.

Importers.Name: Belkin International Inc.¸ of El Segundo, California

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion batteries posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the power banks immediately and contact Belkin for a full refund or store credit. Submit photos of the front and back of the power bank with your recall submission. Dispose of the lithium-ion battery according to local regulations.

metaDescription: Belkin recalls BoostCharge Pro 10K wireless chargers due to overheating lithium-ion batteries posing a fire hazard. Immediate use is unsafe.

Description: QVC is recalling more than one million Temp-tations Oven Gloves because they do not provide adequate heat protection, creating a burn hazard. The gloves come in various colors and sizes and are labeled "Temp-tations by Tara." There have been reports of minor burns linked to this issue.
Title: QVC Recalls Over One Million Temp-tations Oven Gloves Due to Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to QVC at 888-770-7119 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email ovengloves@realtimeresults.net, or visit www.recallrtr.com/ovengloves or www.qvc.com and click on "Product Recall Info" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Temp-tations Oven Gloves
Recalled Temp-tations Oven Gloves (various colors)

Injuries.Name: QVC has received 162 reports of insufficient heat protection including 92 reports of minor burns.

Retailers.Name: Available on QVC.com, through QVC televised shows, and QVC digital shopping platforms from August 2018 to August 2024, priced between $4 and $13 per pair and $14 to $26 for bundled sets.

Importers.Name: QVC Inc., of West Chester, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Insufficient heat protection leading to burn risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gloves and contact QVC for a refund

Description: Anker Innovations has recalled specific Soundcore Bluetooth Speakers due to overheating lithium-ion batteries that pose a fire hazard. The recall covers models A3102016, A3302011, and A3302031 sold exclusively on Amazon. There have been reports of smoking, small fires, and one minor burn injury related to these batteries.
Title: Anker Soundcore Bluetooth Speakers Recalled for Fire Hazard from Lithium-Ion Batteries
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Anker at 800-988-7973 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email support@anker.com, or visit https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus. A Live Chat option is also available at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us. More details can be found at https://www.anker.com/product-recalls or by visiting www.anker.com and clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Anker Soundcore Bluetooth Speaker (Model A3102)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 69,000 (In addition, about 9,764 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Anker Soundcore Bluetooth Speaker Model A3102016
Recalled Anker Soundcore Bluetooth Speaker Model A3302011r
Recalled Anker Soundcore Bluetooth Speaker Model A3302031

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 33 reports of incidents involving the lithium-ion batteries in the speakers overheating, some of those involve incidents of smoking and small fires, including one minor burn injury in connection with an overheating unit.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from March 2023 to October 2023, priced between $28 and $130.

Importers.Name: Anker Innovations Limited, of Hong Kong

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion batteries posing fire hazards in Anker Soundcore and PowerConf Bluetooth Speakers.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled speakers immediately, ensuring they are powered off and disconnected from any chargers. They should visit Anker's website to verify the SN code and complete the recall form. Anker will provide instructions for obtaining a free replacement speaker. Due to the nature of lithium-ion batteries, consumers should dispose of the speakers according to local regulations and not throw them in the trash.

metaDescription: Anker recalls Soundcore Bluetooth Speakers due to fire hazard from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Check if your speaker model is affected by the recall.

Description: The Legendary 'VACATION' Black Label Scented Candles have been recalled because high flames can cause the glass container to break, posing fire and laceration hazards. The recall follows multiple reports of excessive flames and glass breakage. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Recall of The Legendary 'VACATION' Black Label Scented Candles for Fire and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Vacation Inc. via email at recall@vacation.inc or visit www.vacation.inc/candlerecall. Additional details can be found by navigating to the 'Recalls' section on their website.

Products.Name: The Legendary 'VACATION' Black Label Scented Candles

Images.Caption: Recalled “The Legendary ‘VACATION’” by Vacation® Black Label Scented Candles

Injuries.Name: The firm has received seven reports of high candle flames, some of which resulted in the glass container breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Vacation Inc., of Miami

Retailers.Name: Available online at vacation.inc, Amazon, Ulta.com, and various beauty and fashion retailers nationwide from November 2023 until September 2024 for approximately $42.

Hazards.Name: Fire and laceration hazards due to high flames causing the glass to break.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled candles immediately and contact Vacation Inc. for a refund of $42. Instructions for registration, including submitting a photo of the candle and disposal confirmation, are available at https://vacation.inc/candlerecall.

metaDescription: The Legendary 'VACATION' Black Label Scented Candles are recalled due to fire and laceration hazards from high flames causing glass breakage.

Description: Dorel Home Furnishings is recalling full- and queen-sized wall beds sold under multiple brand names and finishes due to a risk of the beds detaching and falling. This defect poses serious impact and crush hazards. The recall follows reports of beds falling and causing injuries such as concussions, cuts, and bruises.
Title: Dorel Home Furnishings Recalls Full- and Queen-Sized Wall Beds for Impact and Crush Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled wall beds immediately. For further assistance, contact Dorel Home Furnishings at 888-828-8620 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email wallbed@ameriwood.com, or visit www.ameriwoodhome.com/pages/recalls. More information can also be found by clicking on 'Dorel Home Safety Recall' at the top of the Ameriwood Home website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Dorel Wall Bed

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 22 reports of the beds falling, resulting in 10 injuries including concussions, contusions, cuts and bruises.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Wayfair.com, Overstock.com, Cymax.com, Amazon.com, HomeDepot.com, Walmart.com, Lowes.com, Real-Rooms.com, Target.com, BedBathandBeyond.com, MathisBrothers.com, BaileysFurnitureOutlet.com, Rona.com, BigLots.com, and NebraskaFurnitureMart.com from May 2021 through April 2024, priced around $900.

Importers.Name: Dorel Home Furnishings Inc., of Wright City, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Risk of wall beds detaching and falling, leading to serious impact and crush injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled wall beds and reach out to Dorel for guidance on arranging a reimbursable professional inspection and reinstallation, after providing their professional quote.

metaDescription: Dorel recalls full- and queen-sized wall beds due to risk of detachment causing serious impact and crush injuries. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: South Shore is recalling Balka and Olena cribs because the spacing between the crib slats exceeds federal safety regulations, posing an entrapment hazard. These full-sized cribs come in white and natural wood finishes. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: South Shore Balka and Olena Cribs Recalled for Entrapment Hazard and Federal Safety Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact South Shore at 800-290-0465 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email service@southshorefurniture.com. More details can also be found at www.southshorefurniture.com/us-en/recall or by visiting www.southshorefurniture.com/us-en and selecting 'Recall information' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Balka Baby Crib with Adjustable Height - Pure White

Images.Caption: Recalled South Shore Balka Baby Crib with Adjustable Height (13825) and (14408)
Recalled South Shore Balka Baby Crib with Adjustable Height (13824)
Recalled South Shore Balka Baby Crib with Adjustable Height (12937)
Recalled South Shore Balka Baby Crib with Adjustable Height (12938)
Recalled South Shore Crib Label

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com, Cymax.com, Walmart.com, Southshorefurniture.com, and other websites from July 2020 through February 2024 for about $415 to $475.

Importers.Name: South Shore Industries LTD, of Canada

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to excessive spacing between crib slats

Remedies.Name: Stop using the cribs immediately and contact South Shore for disassembly instructions. To claim a full refund, photograph the disassembled crib in an outdoor waste container and email the image to service@southshorefurniture.com. South Shore, Amazon.com, and Walmart.com will reach out to all known purchasers.

Description: Segway is recalling Ninebot P100 KickScooters manufactured in 2023 with specific serial numbers due to a risk of front fork breakage that can cause falls and injuries. The company has received reports of broken front forks resulting in injuries such as fractures, cuts, scrapes, bruises, and shoulder pain. This defect creates a significant fall hazard for riders.
Title: Segway Recalls Ninebot P100 KickScooters Due to Front Fork Breakage Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Segway at 866-473-4929 from 5:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recall@segway.com or visit https://service.segway.com/recall or www.segway.com and select the 'Recall Center' link at the bottom of the page for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Segway Ninebot P100 KickScooter
Location of Serial Number
Sample Serial Number

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 31 reports of broken front forks, including six reports of injuries, including a fracture, cuts, scrapes, bruises and shoulder pain.

Retailers.Name: Broward Motorsport locations in Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, Hobe Sound, and West Palm Beach, Florida; Mad Dog Cycles in Orem, Utah; NYC Bike and Scooter in New York; Scooter Mutter in Belmont, Massachusetts; Urban Motion in Culver City, California; and online at Amazon.com, BestBuy.com, store.segway.com, Indiegogo.com (Longham Tech Co. Limited), and https://www.ebay.com/str/skytechgaming?_trksid=p4429486.m3561.l161211 (SupTech), sold from October 2022 through June 2024 for approximately $2,000.

Importers.Name: Segway Inc., of Los Angeles

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to potential front fork breakage

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled KickScooters and contact Segway for a free repair. Segway will arrange for repairs at a local store where possible or provide a pre-paid shipping label for sending the product to a national service center. All known purchasers will be contacted directly by Segway.

metaDescription: Segway recalls Ninebot P100 KickScooters due to fall hazard from potential front fork breakage. Free repairs are available to address this safety risk.

Description: Todson is recalling Nerf Barrage Youth bicycle helmets because they do not comply with federal safety regulations for helmet retention systems. This defect increases the risk of head injury during a crash. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Todson Recalls Nerf Barrage Youth Bicycle Helmets for Head Injury Risk and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled helmets and contact Todson at 800-278-2565, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT Monday through Friday. For further assistance, email NerfHelmetRecall@Todson.com or visit www.Todson.com/NerfHelmetRecall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Nerf Barrage Youth Bike Helmet
Recalled Nerf Barrage Youth Bike Helmet (Side view)
The lot number (LOT#) and reference number (REF#) can be found on the same label on the inside of the helmet

Manufacturers.Name: Jingzhou Taike Sport Equipment Co. Ltd, of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart locations nationwide and online at Walmart.com from January 2022 through May 2024, priced around $30.

Importers.Name: Todson Inc., of Foxboro, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet federal safety regulations for helmet retention systems, increasing the risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the helmets and contact Todson for a full refund. To claim a refund, destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, take a photo of the destroyed helmet, and email it to NerfHelmetRecall@Todson.com. After submitting the photo, dispose of the helmet. Todson will reach out to known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Todson recalls Nerf Barrage Youth Bicycle Helmets due to failure to meet federal safety standards, posing a head injury hazard in crashes.

Description: Hallmark is recalling Cad Bane Keepsake Christmas Tree Ornaments due to a mold exposure hazard caused by improperly dried paint. The ornaments feature the Cad Bane character from Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett and were sold in collectible storage boxes. The company has received multiple reports of mold found on the ornaments when removed from the packaging. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Hallmark Recalls Cad Bane Keepsake Christmas Tree Ornaments for Mold Exposure Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Hallmark at 800-425-5627 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email through the "Contact Us" section at https://care.hallmark.com/s/, or visit www.hallmark.com/product-recalls for more details.

Products.Name: Cad Bane Keepsake Christmas Tree Ornament

Images.Caption: Recalled Hallmark Keepsake Christmas Tree Ornament - Cad Bane - Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett (front)
Recalled Hallmark Keepsake Christmas Tree Ornament - Cad Bane - Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett (back)
Collectible Box for recalled Hallmark Keepsake Christmas Tree Ornament - Cad Bane - Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of 16 consumer reports of the recalled tree ornaments having mold on them when they were taken out of the box. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Hallmark Gold Crown stores and online at Hallmark.com from September 2024 to October 2024 for approximately $22.

Importers.Name: Hallmark Cards, of Kansas City, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Mold exposure risk due to improperly dried paint

Remedies.Name: Return the ornament for a full refund

metaDescription: Hallmark recalls Cad Bane Keepsake Christmas Tree Ornaments due to mold exposure risk from improperly dried paint. No injuries reported.

Description: Medical King is recalling about 222,000 adult bed assist rails due to serious entrapment and asphyxiation hazards. The recall covers adjustable height models 7007 and 7057, and model 7037 without legs. One death has been reported involving the adjustable height model 7007. These bed rails pose a risk of users becoming trapped between the rail and mattress.
Title: Medical King Recalls 222,000 Adult Bed Assist Rails Due to Entrapment and Asphyxia Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Medical King at their toll-free number 888-715-2630, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information can also be found online at https://medicalkingusa.com/products/recalls or by visiting https://medicalkingusa.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' from the top menu.

Products.Name: Bed Assist Rail with Adjustable Heights

Images.Caption: Recalled Bed Assist Rail with Adjustable Heights (model 7007)
Recalled Bed Assist Rail with Adjustable Heights (model 7057)
Recalled Bed Assist Rail Without Legs (model 7037)

Injuries.Name: Medical King has received one report of a death involving the Bed Assist Rail with Adjustable Heights, model number 7007. In November 2023, at a residential care facility in South Carolina, a 66-year-old man died after becoming entrapped between his mattress and the bed rail.

Retailers.Name: These bed rails were sold online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target Plus, eBay, Kohls.com, and medicalkingusa.com from January 2020 through March 2024 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: Home 4U, Inc., dba. Medical King, of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risk when users become trapped between the bed rail and mattress.

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit or replacement bed rail upon contacting Medical King.

metaDescription: Medical King recalls 222,000 adult bed assist rails due to entrapment and asphyxia hazards, including one reported fatality linked to the product.

Description: New Cosmos USA is recalling the DeNova Detect DD622NCV combination natural gas and carbon monoxide alarms due to a failure to consistently alert users. The alarms may enter sleep mode after a short alert, preventing detection of new gas or carbon monoxide incidents. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: New Cosmos USA Recalls DeNova Detect DD622NCV Natural Gas and Carbon Monoxide Alarms for Alert Failure
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach New Cosmos USA Inc. at 888-447-6016 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email safety@DeNovaDetect.com, or visit www.denovadetect.com and click on "Recalls" or go directly to www.denovadetect.com/safetynotice-dd622.

Products.Name: DeNova Detect Combination Natural Gas and Carbon Monoxide Alarms

Images.Caption: Recalled Natural Gas + CO Alarm
Location of Model Number of Alarm
Location of Serial Number of Alarm

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's stores nationwide and online at Amazon.com, Lowes.com, and Denovadetect.com from December 2023 through July 2024, priced between $90 and $102.

Distributors.Name: New Cosmos USA Inc., of Lisle, Illinois

Hazards.Name: The alarm may enter sleep mode after a short alert, failing to detect new gas or carbon monoxide incidents, increasing the risk of poisoning.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should register online at www.denovadetect.com/safetynotice-dd622 to receive a prepaid shipping package and a replacement alarm. They should continue using the recalled alarms until the replacements are installed and return the recalled units using the provided shipping package.

metaDescription: DeNova Detect natural gas and carbon monoxide alarms may fail to alert users, posing a carbon monoxide poisoning hazard. Learn about the recall details.

Description: Petidoux has recalled Celebration Girl's Summer Pajama Sets because they do not meet federal flammability standards, posing a burn hazard. The pajamas are made of 100% cotton with a firework print and include a short-sleeve top and shorts. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Petidoux Recalls Celebration Girl's Summer Pajama Sets for Burn Hazard Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Petidoux at 888-381-7171 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email customerservice@petidoux.com, or visit https://www.petidoux.com/pages/cpsc-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Celebration Girl's Summer Pajama Set
Label printed on the recalled pajama set
Hangtag on the recalled pajama set

Retailers.Name: Addison Olivia Children's Boutique, The Best Dressed Child, The Spiffy Student, Trunk Shows for Kids, Yellow Turtle, Seaside Associated Stores, Hop, Skip, and A Jump, The Beach Plum, Lake Pharmacy, Broomtail, Wiggles & Giggles, Pride + Joy, Homespun, Lemondrop, Doodlebugs Boutique, Ooh La Shoppe, and online at maisonette.com, faire.com, and petidoux.com.

Importers.Name: Petidoux, of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn injuries due to flammability regulation violations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the pajamas, keep them away from children, and contact Petidoux for a full refund. They must destroy the garments by cutting them in half and send a photo of the destroyed items to customerservice@petidoux.com to receive either a full refund or store credit worth 1.25 times the purchase price.

metaDescription: Petidoux recalls Celebration Girl's Summer Pajama Sets due to burn hazard from flammability regulation violations in children's sleepwear.

Description: Stokke has recalled certain YOYO³ strollers because the parking brake may disengage unexpectedly, posing an injury risk. The recall applies only to strollers with specific serial numbers starting with '142AA' and below '0062239.' No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Stokke Recalls YOYO³ Strollers Due to Brake Disengagement Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Stokke at 877-978-6553 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. Monday through Friday, email info-usa@stokke.com, or visit https://recall.stokke.com/yoyo3 for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled YOYO³ Stroller (olive)
Location of Tracking Label with Serial Number on Recalled YOYO³ Stroller

Retailers.Name: In store and online at Bloomingdale's, Crate and Barrel, Nordstrom, Albee Baby and Macro baby and online at stokke.com, Amazon.com and Babylist.com from September 2024 through October 2024 for about $450.

Importers.Name: Stokke LLC, of Stamford, Connecticut

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to potential brake disengagement

Description: woom Bikes USA is recalling certain OFF and ORIGINAL 6 children's bicycles due to a risk of crank failure that can cause loss of control, leading to falls and crashes. The recall covers specific models intended for children aged 10 to 14. The company has received reports of crank breakage, including one injury. Affected bicycles feature the woom brand on the downtube and headtube.
Title: woom Bikes USA Recalls Children's OFF and ORIGINAL 6 Bicycles Due to Crank Failure Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to woom Bikes USA at 855-966-6872 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at productsafetyUS@woom.com. Additional information is available at https://faq.us.woombikes.com/category/88-recall-notice or by visiting www.woom.com/en_US/ and selecting 'Safety and Recalls' at the bottom of the page to use the online crank checker tool.

Images.Caption: The recalled cranks have no space between the well with the woom logo and the pedal thread
Recalled OFF 5 – Black with silver carbon front fork, 24-inch wheels
Recalled OFF 6 – Black with silver carbon front fork, 26-inch wheels
Recalled OFF AIR 5 – Black with silver suspension front fork, 24-inch wheels
Recalled OFF AIR 6 – Black with silver suspension front fork, 26-inch wheels
Recalled ORIGINAL 6, 26-inch wheels

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the crank breaking, including one report of an injury to a child.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at http://www.woom.com in the U.S. from September 2016 through October 2020, priced between $500 and $900.

Importers.Name: woom Bike USA LLC, of Austin, Texas

Hazards.Name: Crank failure leading to loss of control, resulting in fall and crash hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled bicycles and contact woom Bikes USA for a free replacement crankset. An online crank checker tool is available at woom.com to check if their bike is affected. Affected cranksets will be replaced at no cost, and installation should be performed by a qualified bike repair technician. Consumers can submit their repair receipt for reimbursement of the crank replacement cost. woom will directly contact all known purchasers.

metaDescription: woom Bikes USA recalls children's OFF and ORIGINAL 6 bicycles due to crank failure risk causing fall and crash hazards. Check your bike for safety.

Description: Baofali 4-in-1 Microfiber Crib Safety Bumpers in white, gray, and pink have been recalled for posing a suffocation risk and violating the federal ban on crib bumpers. These rectangular bumpers measure about 52 inches long and 10 inches tall and include fabric ties. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Baofali 4-in-1 Microfiber Crib Bumpers Recalled for Suffocation Hazard and Federal Ban Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Baofali Trading via email at 2540535966@qq.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Baofali 4-in-1 Microfiber Crib Safety Bumper (White) Model number: JJ25B-A1
Recalled Baofali 4-in-1 Microfiber Crib Safety Bumper (Gray) Model number: JJ25B-A2
Recalled Baofali 4-in-1 Microfiber Crib Safety Bumper (Pink) Model number: JJ25B-A3

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Temu.com from June 2024 to August 2024, priced between $25 and $30.

Distributors.Name: Baofali Trading Co. Ltd., dba Unique Person Home Goods, of China

Hazards.Name: These crib bumpers are in violation of the federal crib bumper ban, creating a suffocation risk for infants.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the crib bumpers immediately and remove them from cribs. To obtain a full refund, take a photo of the crib bumper cut in half and send it to 2540535966@qq.com or contact Baofali Trading on Temu.com. Dispose of the destroyed bumper according to local regulations. Baofali Trading will reach out to all known buyers directly.

metaDescription: Baofali 4-in-1 Microfiber Crib Bumpers recalled due to suffocation hazard and violation of federal crib bumper ban.

Description: Dreamgro is recalling Mermaid Lullaby Travel Soothers for infants because the soft gold star attached to the head can detach, posing a choking hazard. The soothers come in light blue and pink and include a detachable hook for attaching to car seats and strollers. One report of the star detaching has been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Dreamgro Recalls Mermaid Lullaby Travel Soothers Due to Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Dreamgro at 888-202-0442 from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email Recall@dreamgro.com. Visit www.dreamgro.com/recall.html or www.dreamgro.com and select 'Product Recall,' or reach out to the retailer where the product was purchased.

Images.Caption: Recalled Dreamgro Lullaby Travel Soothers (blue)
Recalled Dreamgro Lullaby Travel Soothers (pink)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the soft star detaching from the toy. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Beall's Outlet Stores, Burlington Merchandising, DD'S Discounts, Macy's Backstage, Marshalls, Nordstrom Rack, and TJ Maxx stores nationwide from January 2024 through May 2024 for between $6 and $9.

Importers.Name: DreamGro Enterprises LLC, of New York

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to detachable gold star

Remedies.Name: Stop using the soothers and return them for a full refund

Description: GoveeLife and Govee smart electric space heaters have been recalled because of fire and burn hazards caused by overheating. The recall includes models H7130, H7131, H7132, H7133, H7134, and H7135, which display the brand name on the front or side. There have been reports of overheating, fires, and a minor burn injury linked to these heaters.
Title: GoveeLife and Govee Smart Electric Space Heaters Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact GoveeLife or Govee at 833-772-5360 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, or visit https://us.govee.com or www.goveelife.com and select 'Recall Information' at the top of the page. More details can be found at https://recall.goveelife.com/heater-recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled Model H7130
Recalled Model H7131
Recalled Model H7132
Recalled Model H7133
Recalled Model H7134
Recalled Model H7135

Injuries.Name: The firms have received 113 reports of overheating, including seven reports of fires and one report of a minor burn injury.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, https://us.govee.com, the Govee Home App, and TikTok Shop from September 2021 to September 2024, priced between $30 and $150.

Importers.Name: Govee Moments Trading Limited, of China and Govee Moments Limited, of China

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heaters, cut the power cord, and submit a photo of the destroyed unit for a refund

Description: Guava Family is recalling Roam First Generation three-wheel foldable strollers due to brake failures that can lead to falls and injuries. The recall affects strollers manufactured between July 2020 and December 2021. The firm has received multiple reports of the brake lock disengaging and the brake pedal not engaging, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Guava Family Recalls Roam First Generation Strollers Due to Brake Failure Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled strollers immediately and reach out to Guava Family Inc. at 833-408-0493 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at rs@realtimeresults.net. More information can be found online at https://www.guavafamily.com under 'Product Recall' or directly at https://www.guavafamily.com/pages/stroller-recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled Guava Roam Stroller
Recalled strollers tracking label location on the cross bar below seat

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 67 reports of the brake lock disengaging. The firm has also received 152 reports of the brake pedal not engaging when pushed down. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Online at www.guavafamily.com and www.babylist.com from August 2020 through October 2023 for between $500 and $550.

Importers.Name: Guava Family Inc., of Solana Beach, California

Hazards.Name: Brake failure leading to potential falls and injuries for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the strollers and contact Guava Family for a free repair kit and installation guidance.

metaDescription: Guava Family recalls Roam strollers for brake failure hazards that can cause falls. No injuries reported. Stop use until repaired.
