Description: Kyte Baby has recalled its infant Slumber Suits because they do not comply with federal flammability standards, posing a burn hazard. The recall includes multiple sizes and colors of these one-piece sleepwear garments. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Kyte Baby Recalls Infant Slumber Suits for Burn Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Kyte Baby at 817-381-8723 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email hello@kytebaby.com. Additional details can be found at https://kytebaby.com/pages/recall or by visiting https://kytebaby.com/ and clicking on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kyte Baby Slumber Suit
Recalled Kyte Baby Slumber Suits
Recalled Kyte Baby Slumber Suits
Neck label showing "Kyte Baby" brand, size, and "Made in China"

Retailers.Name: Online at www.Kytebaby.com from November 2022 through March 2024 for about $75.

Importers.Name: Kyte Baby, of South Lake, Texas

Hazards.Name: Burn risk due to violation of federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled suits immediately and contact Kyte Baby for guidance on returning or destroying the items for a full refund or store credit, including free shipping and handling. Kyte Baby is reaching out to all known buyers directly.

Description: The Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack, a wireless portable charger with rechargeable lithium-ion batteries, has been recalled due to overheating that poses a fire risk. Reports include incidents of fire, smoking, melting, cracking, and swelling, with some resulting in property damage. Users should stop using the affected battery packs and follow proper disposal guidelines for lithium-ion batteries.
Title: Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack Recalled for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Quad Lock at 833-362-9855 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email recall@quadlock.com.au. More information is available at quadlockcase.net/recall or by visiting www.quadlockcase.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 13,700 (In addition, about 1,580 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack (front)
Recalled Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack (rear)

Injuries.Name: Quad Lock has received 39 reports of incidents in the United States, including three reports of fire and 36 reports of smoking, melting, cracking or swelling, resulting in two reports of property damage. Quad Lock has received 170 reports of incidents internationally, including four reports of fire and 166 reports of smoking, melting, cracking or swelling, resulting in one report of property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.quadlockcase.com, www.amazon.com, and various third-party sellers from April 2023 through September 2024, priced between $60 and $80.

Importers.Name: Annex Products Pty Ltd., dba Quad Lock, of Australia

Hazards.Name: Overheating battery pack posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled battery packs, remove them from the charger, and contact Quad Lock for a full refund or store credit. Dispose of lithium-ion batteries per local regulations.

metaDescription: Quad Lock MAG Battery Packs are recalled due to overheating and fire hazards associated with their lithium-ion batteries.

Description: Tala North America is recalling Muse Portable Lamps because of a fire hazard caused by overheating lithium batteries and lightbulb failure. One incident of battery overheating resulted in property damage, but no injuries have been reported. The lamps are battery-powered and come in multiple colors with specific batch codes on the base.
Title: Tala North America Recalls Muse Portable Lamps Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Tala North America at 800-408-2226 from 4 a.m. to 1 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email productquality@tala.co.uk or visit talalighting.com/pages/the-muse-bulb-replacement-program-safety-recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Tala Muse Portable Lamp – Brass
Recalled Tala Muse Portable Lamp – Blue
Recalled Tala Muse Portable Lamp – White
Recalled Tala Muse Portable Lamp – Black
Recalled Tala Muse Portable Lamp – Red
Recalled Tala Muse Portable Lamp – Pink
Recalled Tala Muse Portable Lamp – Green
Batch code can be found on the base of the product

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a battery overheating, causing a fire that resulted in property damage to a side table and bed. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at West Elm, One Kings Lane, Lumens, Lulu & Georgia, Lightology, and Anthropologie from September 2021 to July 2024, priced between $275 and $495.

Importers.Name: Tala North America Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to overheating lithium battery and lightbulb failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lamps and contact Tala North America for a free replacement bulb. A replacement request form must be completed, including a photo of the product and the number of recalled bulbs. Consumers should dispose of the bulbs safely, and replacement bulbs will be sent at no cost.

Description: Wolf Appliance is recalling Dual Fuel ranges with infrared griddles due to burn and fire hazards linked to potential short-circuiting from spilled liquids. The recall covers stainless steel ranges in 36, 48, and 60-inch widths with specific model and serial numbers. Description: HALO is recalling reusable tumblers with metal straws sold exclusively at Wawa stores due to a risk of laceration. The metal straws can cause cuts to the hand and mouth. The tumblers come in four designs and include a tan lid with a 24-ounce capacity. The recall follows reports of injuries linked to the metal straws.
Title: HALO Recalls Tumblers with Metal Straws Sold at Wawa Due to Laceration Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach HALO at 855-425-6266 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at halo.safety@halo.com, or visit https://halo.com/safety-recall-24oz-mug/ or http://www.halo.com and select 'Safety Recall' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Tumblers with a metal straw

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of incidents, resulting in four laceration injuries to the hand and mouth.

Retailers.Name: Wawa locations across Alabama, Washington D.C., Delaware, Florida, North Carolina, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Maryland during August 2024 for approximately $13.

Importers.Name: HALO Branded Solutions Inc., of Sterling, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration from the metal straw

Remedies.Name: Stop using the metal straw and contact HALO for a free silicone straw replacement or return the tumbler for a $15 gift card refund.

Description: Humane has recalled the Charge Case Accessory for the Ai Pin due to a fire hazard caused by an overheating lithium polymer battery. The accessory may overheat and pose a risk of fire. One report of the charge case overheating and melting during charging has been received, with no injuries or property damage reported. This recall applies only to the Charge Case Accessory and not to other Humane products.
Title: Humane Recalls Charge Case Accessory for Ai Pin Due to Lithium Battery Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Humane at 844-481-1900 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recall@humane.com, or visit https://humane.com/ccrecall or https://humane.com/aipin and select "Charge Case Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Humane Charge Case Accessory for Ai Pin

Images.Caption: Recalled Humane Inc Ai Pin Charge Case
Recalled Humane Inc Ai Pin Charge Case

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a charge case overheating and melting during charging. No injuries or property damage have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase at humane.com from November 2023 through May 2024. The Charge Case Accessory was sold separately for about $150 and as part of the Humane Ai Pin Complete System for between $700 and $800.

Importers.Name: Humane Inc., of San Francisco, California

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium battery posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the Charge Case Accessory immediately and contact Humane for a full refund or a free replacement. Refunds will be $149 for the accessory purchased alone and $129 for those who bought it as part of the Ai Pin Complete System. A replacement will be available in approximately 3 to 6 months. Please dispose of the accessory according to local regulations.

metaDescription: Humane recalls Charge Case Accessory for Ai Pin due to fire hazard from overheating lithium polymer battery in the charging case accessory.

Description: Polaris Industries is recalling Ranger XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicles due to loose high voltage cables on the motor controller unit that may cause arcing or overheating. This defect creates fire and crash hazards. The company has received reports of unintended acceleration, fire, and melting near the motor controller unit, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Polaris Recalls Ranger XP Kinetic Off-Road Vehicles for Fire and Crash Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747, available from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. For more information, visit www.polaris.com and navigate to "Product Safety News" in the "Safety" section at the bottom of the page, or check https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to see if your vehicle's VIN is affected. Additionally, Polaris' owner connections team can be contacted at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online help center at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,400 (In addition, about 70 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 RANGER XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicle – White
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 RANGER XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicle – Camouflage
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 RANGER XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicle – White
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 RANGER XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicle – Camouflage
Recalled Polaris RANGER VIN Location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of unintended acceleration and fire and six reports of melting on or around the motor controller unit. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Polaris dealers nationwide from May 2023 through September 2024 for between $24,900 and $37,800.

Hazards.Name: Loose high voltage cables on the motor controller unit may lead to arcing or overheating, creating fire and crash hazards, which could result in serious injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled ROVs and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a complimentary inspection of the motor controller unit. If any high voltage cables are found to be loose or damaged, Polaris will arrange for a free repair at the dealer's location. Registered owners will be contacted directly by Polaris.

Description: Sanven Technology has recalled Vevor Baby Gates because they pose an entrapment hazard and do not meet federal safety standards for gates and enclosures. The gates are pressure-mounted, swing both inward and outward, and are made of powder-coated iron and plastic. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Sanven Technology Recalls Vevor Baby Gates for Entrapment Hazard and Federal Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Sanven Technology at 888-559-1726 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at csr@vevor.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vevor baby gate and accessories

Retailers.Name: eBay.com, AliExpress.com, and Overstock.com from February 2024 through May 2024 for about $45.

Importers.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Distributors.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to violation of federal safety regulations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gates immediately and contact Sanven Technology for disposal instructions and refund processing.

Description: CFMOTO is recalling 2024 CFORCE 800 and 1000 Touring all-terrain vehicles due to a throttle lever defect that may prevent it from returning to idle. This malfunction can cause unintended acceleration, increasing the risk of crashes. There have been reports of incidents, including one minor injury, related to this issue.
Title: CFMOTO Recalls 2024 CFORCE 800 and 1000 Touring ATVs for Throttle Lever Malfunction
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact CFMOTO Powersports at 888-823-6686, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, reach out via email at info@cfmotousa.com or visit https://www.cfmotousa.com/vehicle-recall or www.CFMOTOUSA.com and navigate to "More" and then "Vehicle Recall" for further details.

Recalled CFMOTO CFORCE 1000 Touring ATV ROV (black)
VIN Location on the recalled 2024 CForce 800 Touring ATV

Injuries.Name: CFMOTO has received four reports of the throttle failing to return to the idle position and including one report of the vehicle crashing, including one minor impact injury.

Retailers.Name: CFMOTO Powersports dealers nationwide from May 2024 through August 2024 for between $9,400 and $10,500.

Importers.Name: CFMOTO Powersports Inc., of Plymouth, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Throttle lever may not return to idle, causing unexpected acceleration and crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ATVs and contact an authorized CFMOTO dealer for a free repair. CFMOTO has issued a stop ride notice and is reaching out to all registered owners and dealers.

Description: Ricky Powersports is recalling Tumble Weed youth ATVs and TGB Blade 600 SE adult ATVs due to noncompliance with federal safety standards. The youth models have mechanical suspension defects that increase crash risk, while the adult models have brake failures that may prevent proper stopping. Handlebar design also poses a laceration hazard, raising the risk of serious injury or death. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Ricky Powersports Recalls Youth and Adult ATVs for Safety Regulation Violations and Injury Risks
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Ricky Powersports toll-free at 844-250-2199 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email at RickyPowerSports@gmail.com, or visit www.Rickypowersports.net/Recall or www.RickyPowerSports.net and click on the "RECALL" button.

Products.Name: Ricky Powersports Tumble Weed Youth ATV

Images.Caption: Recalled Ricky Powersports RPS TK110ATV-1 Tumble Weed Youth ATV
The youth ATV's model number appears on a plate located at the front of the vehicle between the two front tires
Recalled Ricky Powersports RPS Blade 600 SE Adult ATV
The adult ATV's model number appears on a plate located next to the vehicle's front right tire
Identification plates on both the youth and the adult models

Manufacturers.Name: Jiangsu Tiking Sports Vehicle Manufacturing Co. Ltd., of China (youth ATV)
Taiwan Golden Bee Co. Ltd., of Taiwan (adult ATV)

Retailers.Name: Ricky Powersports dealers nationwide from January 2021 through May 2024 for between $850 and $1,100 for the youth model, and for about $9,300 for the adult model.

Importers.Name: Ricky Powersports LLC, of Carrollton, Texas

Hazards.Name: The recalled ATVs do not meet federal ATV safety regulations. The youth models have a mechanical suspension that poses a crash risk, while the adult models' brakes may fail to stop the vehicle adequately, also creating a crash hazard. Additionally, the handlebars could cause lacerations if impacted by the rider's body or head, increasing the risk of serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled ATVs immediately and contact Ricky Powersports to arrange for a full refund.

metaDescription: Ricky Powersports recalls youth and adult ATVs due to crash hazards and risk of serious injury from brake and suspension defects.

Description: Oliva Cigars is recalling promotional cigar slim lighters from the Nub, Serie V, and Cain brands due to the absence of child-resistant mechanisms. These lighters pose fire and burn hazards, especially to children under five who may ignite them. No injuries have been reported. Consumers should stop using and dispose of these lighters safely.
Title: Oliva Cigars Recalls Promotional Cigar Slim Lighters for Missing Child Safety Feature Posing Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Oliva Cigars via email at recall@olivacigar.com or visit https://olivacigar.com/lighter-recall. You can also find details by clicking on the 'Recall' link at the top of their homepage.

Description: Anker has recalled certain Power Bank models (A1642, A1647, A1652) because their lithium-ion batteries can overheat, posing fire and burn hazards. Reports include incidents of batteries overheating, exploding, and catching fire, resulting in two burn injuries. These portable chargers are used for smartphones, tablets, and laptops and display the Anker logo on the front with model numbers on the back.
Title: Anker Power Banks Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards Due to Overheating Batteries
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Anker at 800-988-7973, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at support@anker.com, or visit https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus. A LiveChat option is available at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us. For more details, check https://www.anker.com/product-recalls or visit www.anker.com and click on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Anker Power Bank A1642
Recalled Anker Power Bank A1647
Recalled Anker Power Bank A1652

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 28 reports of incidents of the batteries overheating, exploding, and the power bank catching on fire. Two injuries which included first and second degree burns to the consumer's hands have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Anker Innovations Limited, of Hong Kong, China

Retailers.Name: Best Buy

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion battery posing fire and burn risks

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled power banks and contact Anker Innovations to obtain a free replacement. Visit https://www.anker.com/product-recalls to register for the recall and ensure proper disposal of batteries per local regulations.

metaDescription: Anker recalls specific Power Bank models due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Stop use immediately to prevent injury.

These portable chargers are used for smartphones, tablets, and laptops and display the Anker logo on the front with model numbers on the back.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Anker at 800-988-7973, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at support@anker.com, or visit https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus. A LiveChat option is available at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us. For more details, check https://www.anker.com/product-recalls or visit www.anker.com and click on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.
en.metaDescription: Anker recalls specific Power Bank models due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Stop use immediately to prevent injury.

Images.Caption: Recalled Anker Power Bank A1642
Recalled Anker Power Bank A1647
Recalled Anker Power Bank A1652

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 28 reports of incidents of the batteries overheating, exploding, and the power bank catching on fire. Two injuries which included first and second degree burns to the consumer's hands have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Best Buy

Manufacturers.Name: Anker Innovations Limited, of Hong Kong, China

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion battery posing fire and burn risks

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled power banks and contact Anker Innovations to obtain a free replacement. Visit https://www.anker.com/product-recalls to register for the recall and ensure proper disposal of batteries per local regulations.

Description: Fisher-Price has recalled over two million Snuga Infant Swings due to a suffocation hazard. The swings are not intended for infant sleep, and adding bedding or using the product for sleep increases the risk. Reports include five infant deaths involving unrestrained infants and added bedding. The recall covers multiple models with similar designs.
Title: Fisher-Price Recalls Snuga Infant Swings Due to Suffocation Hazard Linked to Infant Deaths
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Fisher-Price at 855-853-6224 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.service.mattel.com and select 'Recall & Safety Alerts' or go to www.service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx for detailed information.

Products.Name: All Models of Fisher-Price Snuga Swings
Products.NumberOfUnits: Approximately 2.1 million swings were sold in the United States, about 99,000 swings were sold in Canada and about 500 swings were sold in Mexico

Images.Caption: Recalled My Little Snugabunny Cradle 'n Swing (V0099)
Recalled My Little Snugabear Cradle 'n Swing (CHM56)
Recalled Snow Leopard Swing (HBM23)
Recalled My Little SnugaMonkey™ Cradle 'n Swing (X7051)
Recalled My Little Snugapuppy™ Cradle 'n Swing (X7345)
Recalled My Little Snugabear Cradle 'n Swing (X7347)
Representative recalled Snuga Swing headrest
Representative recalled Snuga Swing body support insert

Injuries.Name: Between 2012 and 2022, there have been reports of five deaths involving infants 1 to 3 months of age when the product was used for sleep. In most of those incidents, the infants were unrestrained and bedding materials were added to the product.

Retailers.Name: The Snuga Swings were sold at Amazon, Toys R Us, Walmart/Sams Club, and Target nationwide from October 2010 through January 2024 for about $160.

Distributors.Name: Fisher-Price of East Aurora, New York

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper use for sleep and added bedding materials.

Remedies.Name: Remove the headrest and body support insert; Fisher-Price offers a $25 refund for compliance.

metaDescription: Fisher-Price recalls Snuga Infant Swings for suffocation hazard after infant deaths related to improper sleep use and added bedding materials.

Description: Gimars Playpens, models GWL-01 to GWL-06, are recalled for failing to meet federal safety standards. The basketball hoop accessory poses a head entrapment risk, and children may become trapped under the mattress, creating suffocation hazards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Gimars Playpens Recalled for Entrapment and Suffocation Hazards Due to Federal Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Shenzhen Jingdu Technology via email at Gimars-Aftersales@outlook.com, or visit https://gimarsrecall.com/ or https://gimarsbaby.com and select "Safety Recalls" for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Gimars Playpen
Recalled Gimars Playpen basketball hoop accessory
Recalled Gimars Playpen reversible mattress
Recalled Gimars Playpen reversible mattress
Recalled Gimars Playpen label

Retailers.Name: Online exclusively atwww.Amazon.com from May 2023 through January 2024 for about $80.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Jingdu Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Violation of federal safety regulations; head entrapment and suffocation hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the playpen, disassemble it, cut the fabric floor, mattress pad, and basketball hoop into two pieces, and submit photos for a refund.

Description: NPW Group has recalled Halloween Projector Flashlights because they can overheat, causing the outer casing to melt and posing fire and burn hazards. The flashlights feature three interchangeable Halloween-themed projector covers and are powered by two AA batteries. The recall follows reports of overheating incidents, including minor burn injuries.
Title: NPW Group Recalls Halloween Projector Flashlights for Fire and Burn Hazards

Injuries.Name: The firm has received nine reports of overheating including six reports of smoke or fire, resulting in four reports of minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Target stores nationwide and online at www.target.com, sold during September 2024 for approximately $3

Importers.Name: NPW Group, of Cincinnati, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to fire and burn risks

Remedies.Name: Return to Target for a full refund

ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled flashlights immediately and return them to any Target store for a full refund. Before returning, please turn off the flashlight, remove the batteries, and label the product as 'recalled' with a permanent marker. Target will reach out to all known purchasers directly.
metaDescription: NPW Group recalls Halloween Projector Flashlights due to overheating risks that can cause fire and burns. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: Reyhee Group and Jingling International have recalled Droyd Fury Youth All-Terrain Vehicles (ATVs) due to a laceration hazard caused by handlebars that do not meet federal safety standards. The recalled ATVs pose a risk of injury upon impact. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Reyhee Group and Jingling International Recall Droyd Fury Youth ATVs for Laceration Hazard and Regulatory Non-Compliance
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Reyhee Group at 800-333-3729 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email support@go-droyd.com, or visit www.droyd.com/pages/safety-recall or www.droyd.com and select 'Safety Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Droyd Fury Youth ATV
Recalled Droyd Fury Youth ATV (side view)
Recalled Droyd Fury Youth ATV Identification Plate

Manufacturers.Name: Yongkang Ruihe Metal Products Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Online at Droyd.com, Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target.com, Purchasingpower.com, Wellbots.com and Rideextremedirt.com from May 2024 through June 2024 for about $600.

Importers.Name: Reyhee Group Inc., dba RGI Global, of Ontario, California

Distributors.Name: Jingling International Inc., of Jasper, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Laceration risk due to non-compliance with federal ATV regulations; handlebars can cause injury upon impact.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ATVs immediately and contact Reyhee Group for a complimentary repair kit, which includes a replacement handlebar cover and updated safety warning labels.

metaDescription: Reyhee Group recalls Droyd Fury Youth ATVs due to laceration hazard from handlebars and violation of federal ATV safety regulations.

Description: Reyhee Group has recalled Droyd multi-purpose helmets for youths due to non-compliance with federal bicycle helmet safety regulations, posing a risk of head injury in crashes. The helmets are black with black padding, straps, and a buckle, and have the brand name "DROYD" on the back. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Reyhee Group Recalls Droyd Multi-Purpose Helmets for Head Injury Risk and Federal Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Reyhee Group at 800-333-3729 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email support@droyd.com, or visit www.droyd.com/pages/safety-recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.droyd.com and click on "Safety Recall" for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Droyd helmet – top view
Recalled Droyd helmet – back view with logo
Recalled Droyd helmet – side view
Example of photo with straps cut off from recalled helmet, rendering helmet unusable
Example of photo with straps cut off and recalled helmet inside a trash can, rendering helmet unusable

Manufacturers.Name: Yongkang Xinhao Industry and Trade Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Droyd.com from October 2023 through July 2024, priced around $40.

Importers.Name: Reyhee Group Inc., dba RGI Global, of Ontario, California

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with federal safety regulations for bicycle helmets, posing a risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled helmets immediately and contact Reyhee for a full refund. To obtain the refund, consumers must cut the helmet straps and submit two photos: one showing the cut straps and another of the helmet with the cut straps in a trash can, along with their name and address to Reyhee Group at support@droyd.com. Reyhee Group will also reach out to all purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Reyhee Group recalls Droyd multi-purpose helmets due to head injury hazard from non-compliance with federal bicycle helmet safety standards.

Description: Liberty Hardware is recalling Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars sold under the Peerless, Safety First, and Delta brands because the plastic grip pads can become dislodged, creating a fall hazard. Fourteen reports include one fall with a minor injury. The bars are made of powder-coated aluminum with two gripping surfaces spaced about 8 and 15 inches apart.
Title: Liberty Hardware Recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars for Fall Hazard Due to Dislodged Grip Pads
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Liberty Hardware at 844-811-4541 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@libertyhardware.com, or visit www.libertyhardware.com/recall-notices/tub-safety-bar. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.libertyhardware.com and click on 'RECALL NOTICE' at the bottom right of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bar

Injuries.Name: Liberty Hardware has received fourteen reports of the plastic sticker grip pads becoming dislodged after installation or otherwise needing replacement, including one fall with a minor injury reported.

Retailers.Name: Ace Hardware, The Home Depot, Lowe's, Menards, Walmart, and other hardware and plumbing stores and suppliers nationwide, and online at Amazon.com and FlyingBulldogs.com from January 2011 through May 2024 for between $16 and $55.

Importers.Name: Liberty Hardware Manufacturing Corp., of Winston-Salem, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Potential for instability due to dislodged grip pads, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the safety bars immediately and contact Liberty Hardware for a pre-paid return label and packaging to receive a $35 refund.

metaDescription: Liberty Hardware recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars due to fall hazard from dislodged grip pads that can cause instability and falls.

Description: Caframo Limited has recalled model 9421 Cabinet Heaters because of a potential electrocution hazard caused by improperly finished wire crimping. These heaters are used to maintain warmth in cabinets and parking ticket dispensers. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Caframo Model 9421 Cabinet Heaters Recalled for Electrocution Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Caframo Limited at 800-567-3556 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email contactus@caframo.com. More details can be found online at https://www.caframo.com/recall/ or by visiting www.caframo.com and clicking on 'RECALL' at the top of the homepage.

Products.Name: Caframo 9421 Cabinet Heater
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,140 (In addition, 200 units were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Caframo 9421 Cabinet Heater
Top view showing location of Product Model & Serial number
Detailed view showing serial number and model number locations

Manufacturers.Name: Caframo Limited, of Canada

Retailers.Name: Graybar Electric Co., St Louis, Missouri; Keystone Automotive Operations Inc., Exeter, Pennsylvania; Hamilton Manufacturing Corp., Holland, Ohio; Controlled Access of the Midwest, Pleasant Hill, Iowa; and available online at comfortsolutions.caframobrands.com and Amazon.com from October 2023 through May 2024 for approximately $150.

Hazards.Name: Potential electrocution risk due to improperly finished wire crimping.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled heaters, unplug them, and contact Caframo Limited for free repair or replacement instructions. Caframo will provide a pre-paid shipping label for product returns. If repair is not possible, a replacement will be sent. Caframo is reaching out to all known purchasers.

ConsumerContact: For further details, contact Inforce at xchan524@outlook.com or visit their storefront on Amazon.com.

Distributors.Name: Yangkeqiang, dba Inforce, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risks due to violation of federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled bed rails immediately. To obtain a refund, send a photo of the disposed bed rail and proof of purchase to xchan524@outlook.com. Refunds are available only for products purchased after August 21, 2023.

Description: CVB is recalling LUCID Platform Beds with upholstered square tufted headboards because the wooden support beams and legs can sag, break, or collapse during use. This defect creates a fall and injury hazard. The recall includes beds in multiple sizes and colors. The firm has received reports of injuries related to these failures.
Title: CVB Recalls LUCID Platform Beds with Upholstered Square Tufted Headboards Due to Fall and Injury Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Lucid at 888-910-0236, available from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Thursday, and from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET on Fridays. Additionally, inquiries can be sent via email to recall@lucidmattress.com or through their website at https://lucidmattress.com/recall/upholsteredplatformbed/ or http://www.lucidmattress.com by clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: LUCID Platform Beds with Upholstered Square Tufted Headboards
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 137,000 (In addition, about 890 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Lucid Upholstered Square Tufted Platform Bed (Charcoal)
Recalled Lucid Upholstered Square Tufted Platform Bed (Stone)
The federal law label is located on the backside of the headboard

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 245 reports of the beds breaking, sagging or collapsing during use, causing 18 injuries, including contusions and bruises.

Retailers.Name: Available online through Amazon.com, Bed Bath & Beyond, Belk.com, Brookside, eBay, Home Depot, JC Penney, Lowes.com, Lucid, Macys, Menards, Overstock, QVC, Sears.com, Target.com, Malouf VIP, Wayfair, and Walmart, as well as in stores nationwide from September 2019 to April 2024, priced between $150 and $250.

Importers.Name: CVB Inc., Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of sagging, breaking, or collapsing during use, leading to potential fall and injury hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected beds immediately and contact Lucid for a complimentary replacement bed frame. To facilitate this, they should mark 'recalled' on the bed's support rails with a permanent marker and email a photo of the bed along with a photo of the support rails or the law label to recall@lucidmattress.com.

metaDescription: CVB recalls LUCID Platform Beds with upholstered headboards due to risk of collapse causing fall and injury hazards. Consumers should stop use immediately.

en.Title: CVB Recalls LUCID Platform Beds with Upholstered Square Tufted Headboards Due to Fall and Injury Hazards
en.Description: CVB is recalling LUCID Platform Beds with upholstered square tufted headboards because the wooden support beams and legs can sag, break, or collapse during use. This defect creates a fall and injury hazard. The recall includes beds in multiple sizes and colors. The firm has received reports of injuries related to these failures.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Lucid at 888-910-0236, available from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Thursday, and from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET on Fridays. Additionally, inquiries can be sent via email to recall@lucidmattress.com or through their website at https://lucidmattress.com/recall/upholsteredplatformbed/ or http://www.lucidmattress.com by clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.
en.metaDescription: CVB recalls LUCID Platform Beds with upholstered headboards due to risk of collapse causing fall and injury hazards. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Products.Name: LUCID Platform Beds with Upholstered Square Tufted Headboards
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 137,000 (In addition, about 890 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Lucid Upholstered Square Tufted Platform Bed (Charcoal)
Recalled Lucid Upholstered Square Tufted Platform Bed (Stone)
The federal law label is located on the backside of the headboard

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 245 reports of the beds breaking, sagging or collapsing during use, causing 18 injuries, including contusions and bruises.

Retailers.Name: Available online through Amazon.com, Bed Bath & Beyond, Belk.com, Brookside, eBay, Home Depot, JC Penney, Lowes.com, Lucid, Macys, Menards, Overstock, QVC, Sears.com, Target.com, Malouf VIP, Wayfair, and Walmart, as well as in stores nationwide from September 2019 to April 2024, priced between $150 and $250.

Importers.Name: CVB Inc., Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of sagging, breaking, or collapsing during use, leading to potential fall and injury hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected beds immediately and contact Lucid for a complimentary replacement bed frame. To facilitate this, they should mark 'recalled' on the bed's support rails with a permanent marker and email a photo of the bed along with a photo of the support rails or the law label to recall@lucidmattress.com.

Description: Jo-Ann Stores is recalling Place & Time® battery-operated desk lamps because the battery compartment can overheat, creating a fire hazard. The recall includes lamps in green, pink, and cream with specific model numbers. There have been reports of the battery compartment melting or popping during USB charging, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Jo-Ann Stores Recalls Place & Time Battery-Operated Desk Lamps Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Jo-Ann Stores at 833-931-0230 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email customer.service@joann.com, or visit www.joann.com/product-recalls. Alternatively, visit www.joann.com and click on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Place & Time battery-operated desk lamp with USB cord (green)
Recalled Place & Time battery-operated desk lamp with USB cord (pink)
Recalled Place & Time battery-operated desk lamp with USB cord (cream)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the battery compartment melting or popping while the lamp was charging with the USB cord. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Jo-Ann Stores nationwide and online at www.joann.com from May 2024 to July 2024 for approximately $23.

Importers.Name: Jo-Ann Stores LLC, of Hudson, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Overheating battery compartment posing a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lamps and return them for a full refund. Without a receipt, customers will receive a $23 store credit. For those unable to return in-store, email a photo of the receipt to customer.service@joann.com with the subject 'Desk Lamp Recall Refund' for an electronic gift card or $23 store credit.

Description: Newmemo has recalled children's ring sets because they contain lead and cadmium levels that exceed federal safety limits. The sets include 36 rings in various designs and come in a pink heart-shaped display case. No injuries have been reported related to this recall.
Title: Newmemo Children's Ring Sets Recalled for Lead and Cadmium Poisoning Risk
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled rings immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Newmemo for a full refund. Reach out via email at cly2167@163.com or visit the seller's profile on Amazon at https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=AVVUCA891OMBV and select "Ask a question."

Images.Caption: Recalled Newmemo Ring Set
Recalled Newmemo Ring Sets
Newmemo and "Amazon FNSKU X0034COQMP" appear on a label on the underside of the ring's packaging

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Amazon.com from January 2022 through March 2024 for about $12.

Importers.Name: Memovan Technology Industrial Co. Limited, (dba Newmemo), of China

Hazards.Name: Lead and cadmium levels exceed federal safety standards, posing a risk of poisoning in children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the rings, keep them away from children, and request a full refund from Newmemo. Dispose of the jewelry and send a photo of the disposed product to cly2167@163.com.

metaDescription: Newmemo children's ring sets recalled due to hazardous lead and cadmium levels posing poisoning risks. No injuries reported.

Description: Polaris is recalling certain 2023-2024 Sportsman 850, XP 1000, and XP 1000 S ATVs and related fuel pump kits and fuel tank assemblies due to a fire hazard. The issue involves potential fuel leaks at the pump flange outlet, which could lead to fire and injury. Polaris has received reports of fuel leaks and fires but no injuries. A free fuel pump replacement is offered to address the risk.
Title: Polaris Recalls 2023-2024 Sportsman ATVs and Fuel Components for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Visit www.polaris.com and navigate to 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section, or go to https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to verify if your VIN is affected. For further inquiries, contact Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2023-2024 Polaris Sportsman 850, XP 1000, XP 1000 S ATVs and Fuel Components
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 10,300 ATVs and 3,158 fuel pump kits and fuel tank assemblies (In addition, about 1,500 ATVs were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 Sportsman 850 ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 Sportsman XP 1000 ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 Sportsman XP 1000 S ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 Sportsman 850 ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 Sportsman XP 1000 ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 Sportsman XP 1000 S ATV
Recalled Fuel Pump Kit (part numbers 2208595 and 2208596)
Recalled Fuel Tank Assembly (part numbers 2522378 and 2522379)
Recalled Polaris Sportsman ATVs VIN Location (1)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 14 reports of fuel leaking from the recalled ATVs and three reports of fire. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Polaris dealers nationwide from July 2023 through June 2024 for between $10,600 and $18,000.

Hazards.Name: Potential fuel leak at the pump flange outlet, creating a fire hazard and risk of injury.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ATVs immediately and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a free fuel pump replacement. Polaris will reach out to registered owners directly.

Description: Wee Gallery has recalled the Baby Tummy Time Gallery because the clear plastic pockets contain phthalate levels that exceed federal safety standards. The product is a black and white trifold fabric gallery with clear pockets for art cards. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Wee Gallery Recalls Baby Tummy Time Gallery for Excessive Phthalates in Plastic Pockets
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Wee Gallery at 800-282-5149 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at tummytimerecall@weegallery.com, or visit weegallery.com/tummytimerecall. More details can also be found by navigating to the "Recalls" section at the bottom of the weegallery.com homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Wee Gallery Tummy Time Gallery – Partially Opened
Recalled Wee Gallery Tummy Time Gallery – Opened

Retailers.Name: Independent toy stores nationwide and online at weegallery.com, amazon.com, bloomingdales.com and babylist.com from October 2022 through July 2024 for about $38, when sold separately, and approximately $54 when sold as part of a bundle.

Importers.Name: Wee Gallery Inc., of St. Petersburg, Florida

Hazards.Name: The Baby Tummy Time Gallery contains clear plastic pockets with phthalates that exceed federal safety limits, posing health risks to young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the Baby Tummy Time Gallery immediately, contact Wee Gallery for disposal instructions, and request a refund of $39.95. Wee Gallery and Amazon.com will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

Description: Glen Dimplex Americas is recalling Cadet Apex72 high wall electric heaters due to a potential fire hazard caused by fan malfunction leading to overheating. The recall includes specific models such as HW128, HW132, HW151, and HW162. One incident of the heater igniting has been reported, but no injuries have occurred. Glen Dimplex is providing free replacement heaters and installation.
Title: Glen Dimplex Americas Recalls Cadet Apex72 Electric Heaters for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately cease using the recalled heaters and reach out to Glen Dimplex Americas at 866-673-9880 from 8 a.m. to 6:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email apex@glendimplexamericas.zendesk.com or visit https://www.glendimplexamericas.com/en-us/product-safety-notices/2024-APEX72-Important-Recall-Information for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Cadet Apex Electric Heater
Model and manufacturing date location
Cadet, the model and the manufacturing date, in DD-MM-YY format, appear on the rating label visible through the bottom of the air outlet grill

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a heater fan malfunctioning and causing the unit to ignite. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Glen Dimplex Americas Ltd., of Vancouver, Washington

Retailers.Name: Available through Platt, Crescent Electric, CED, and other electrical distributors nationwide from February 2014 to February 2024, priced around $190.

Hazards.Name: Potential fire hazard due to overheating from fan malfunction

Remedies.Name: Free replacement heater and installation provided by Glen Dimplex

Description: Intellio Toys is recalling Bright Builder Fort Kits that include flashlights with button cell batteries posing an ingestion hazard. The recall affects specific models marked on the packaging. One report of batteries coming loose from a flashlight was received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Intellio Toys Recalls Bright Builder Fort Kits Due to Button Cell Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Intellio Toys at 888-571-0196 from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@intelliotoys.com. More information can be found online at https://intelliotoys.com/pages/recall-information?_pos=1&_sid=c95ece1a9&_ss=r or by visiting www.intelliotoys.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Bright Builder Fort Kits with Flashlights

Images.Caption: Recalled Bright Builder Fort Kit
Recalled Bright Builder Fort Kit - box

The recalled Intellio Toys' Bright Builder fort kit has 100 pieces and a pack of five flashlights with button cell batteries. A photo of the flashlights appears on the back of the recalled Bright Builder fort kits.
Five-flashlight package sold with the recalled fort kits or as an add-on component

Injuries.Name: Intellio Toys has received one report of the batteries coming out the flashlight while handling it. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com and Intelliotoys.com from December 2022 through August 2024, priced between $38 and $49 for the kits and $14 to $15 for the flashlights.

Importers.Name: Intellio Toys, of Houston, Texas

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to easily accessible button cell batteries in flashlights

Remedies.Name: Stop using the flashlights, keep them away from children, and contact Intellio Toys for a refund of $10 to $15. Dispose of button cell batteries properly.

Description: OleOleToy has recalled Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Sets because the bunny ear headbands contain accessible button cell batteries that pose an ingestion hazard. These batteries can cause serious injury if swallowed. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: OleOleToy Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Sets Recalled for Button Cell Battery Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, reach out to OleOleToy via email at degong99@yeah.net or visit their Amazon.com Seller Profile: OleOleToy Direct-High Quality.

Products.Name: Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Sets

Images.Caption: Recalled Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Set Bunny Ear Headbands
Packaging of the recalled Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Set (Front of Package)
Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Set

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from December 2023 to March 2024 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: Yiwu Shanshi Trading Co. Ltd., of China

Distributors.Name: OleOleToy, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible button cell batteries in Bunny Ears Headbands, violating federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the Bunny Ears Headbands, remove them from children, cut off the battery compartment, and contact OleOleToy for a full refund. Dispose of button cell batteries responsibly according to local hazardous waste guidelines. Amazon is reaching out to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: OleOleToy recalls Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Sets due to ingestion hazard from accessible button cell batteries in bunny ear headbands.

Description: The HALO 1000 Portable Power Stations are being recalled because of fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. These units include multiple USB and AC outputs, an LCD display, and a built-in flood light. The recall follows reports of fires, including one fatal incident involving smoke inhalation. No additional injury details are provided.
Title: HALO 1000 Portable Power Stations Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards Following Fatal Incident
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please call the HALO 1000 recall hotline at 888-345-0481 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email halo5528@sedgwick.com. More information is available online at https://halo1000recall.expertinquiry.com/.

Products.Name: HALO 1000 Portable Power Station - Stone Gray

Images.Caption: Recalled HALO 1000 Portable Power Station
Recalled HALO 1000 Portable Power Station

Injuries.Name: A 79-year-old man in Bradenton, Florida died from smoke inhalation from a fire involving the recalled portable power station in his home in June 2022. In addition to the death, the firms have collectively received four reports of fires involving the HALO 1000 Portable Power Station, including two reports of smoke inhalation, one of which included burn injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen DBK Electronics Co. Ltd.,of China

Retailers.Name: Available from ZAGG, ACG, and QVC, approximately 5,740 units were sold between October 2021 and March 2022, priced between $1,000 and $1,300.

Importers.Name: ZAGG Inc. (ZAGG), of Midvale, Utah

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the power stations and contact the HALO Recall hotline for a free replacement

Description: Shawshank LEDz is recalling Plush Ball Monster and Easter Squeezable toys due to a risk of injury from liquid and glitter splashing if the toy's membrane is damaged. The recall follows reports of incidents, including one where a child required medical treatment after glitter exposure to the eyes. These toys pose a safety hazard and should no longer be used.
Title: Shawshank LEDz Recalls Plush Ball Monster and Easter Squeezable Toys Due to Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Shawshank LEDz Inc. at 480-802-1085 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email robert@shawshankledz.com or visit https://shawshankledz.com/Disclosures-recall.html for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Squeeze Plush Ball Monsters toys
Recalled Easter Squeezable toys
Removable hang tag on the recalled Squeeze Plush Ball Monsters Toy
Back of removable hang tag on the recalled Easter Squeezable Toy
Lot number is printed on the top right corner of the permanent sewn-on tag

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of incidents. One incident involved a child having their eyes flushed out by a medical professional to remove glitter.

Retailers.Name: Ace Hardware Stores nationwide from March 2024 through July 2024 for about $5.

Importers.Name: Shawshank LEDz Inc., of Gilbert, Arizona

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury from liquid and glitter splashing if the toy's membrane is damaged.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the toys immediately and return them to the point of purchase for a full cash refund of $5. If returning to the store is not feasible, Shawshank can provide a postage-paid label for mailing the toys back in exchange for a refund check.

metaDescription: Shawshank LEDz recalls Plush Ball Monster and Easter Squeezable toys due to injury hazard from liquid and glitter exposure if damaged.

Description: ALDI is recalling Ambiano Single Serve Coffee Makers because they can expel hot water from the top, posing a burn hazard. The recall includes models in multiple colors with Ambiano branding. There have been reports of burns linked to this issue.
Title: ALDI Recalls Ambiano Single Serve Coffee Makers Due to Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact ALDI at 888-770-7124 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.recallrtr.com/aldicoffeemaker or corporate.aldi.us, navigating to "Newsroom" and then "Product Recalls" for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Ambiano Single Serve Coffee Maker (Blue)
Recalled Ambiano Single Serve Coffee Maker (Gray)
Recalled Ambiano Single Serve Coffee Maker (Pink)
Recalled Ambiano Single Serve Coffee Maker (Purple)

Injuries.Name: ALDI has received 25 reports of the recalled coffeemakers expelling hot water from the top of the machine, including three reports of burns.

Retailers.Name: Aldi grocery stores in 38 states and the District of Columbia from July 2023 through September 2023 for about $50.

Importers.Name: ALDI Inc., of Batavia, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of hot water expulsion leading to burns

Remedies.Name: Stop using the coffee maker immediately and return it to ALDI for a full refund or opt for a $75 electronic gift card by registering online.

metaDescription: ALDI recalls Ambiano Single Serve Coffee Makers for burn hazard from hot water expulsion. Stop use immediately to prevent injury.

Description: IKEA is recalling two VARMFRONT Power Bank models, E2037 and E2038, because of a fire hazard caused by overheating. The recall applies to specific date codes printed on the devices. No injuries have been reported in the U.S., though three incidents occurred globally. Consumers should stop using the recalled power banks to prevent fire risk.
Title: IKEA Recalls VARMFRONT Power Banks Models E2037 and E2038 Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach IKEA at 888-966-4532 from 9 a.m. to 10 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information is available online at https://www.ikea.com/us/en/customer-service/product-support/recalls or by visiting https://www.ikea.com/us/en and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: VARMFRONT Power Bank Model E2037
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,600 (In addition, about 1,690 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled IKEA VARMFRONT Power Bank (E2037)
Recalled IKEA VARMFRONT Power Bank (E2038)

Injuries.Name: IKEA has received three incident reports globally. No injuries have been reported. Additionally, no incidents or injuries have been reported in the U.S.

Retailers.Name: Available at IKEA stores nationwide and online at www.IKEA.com from July 2023 through March 2024, priced between $17 and $30.

Importers.Name: IKEA Supply AG, of Switzerland

Distributors.Name: IKEA US Retail LLC, of Glen Allen, Virginia

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to fire risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and return them to any IKEA store for a free replacement or full refund. If returning to a store is not possible, contact IKEA for disposal instructions to receive a full refund. No proof of purchase is needed.

metaDescription: IKEA recalls VARMFRONT Power Banks models E2037 and E2038 due to overheating and fire hazard. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: Mamibaby and Cosy Nation have recalled their baby loungers because of suffocation and fall hazards. The loungers have low sides and thick sleeping pads that pose a suffocation risk, as well as openings that increase the risk of falls and entrapment. No injuries have been reported in connection with these products.
Title: Recall of Mamibaby and Cosy Nation Baby Loungers for Suffocation and Fall Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to MEIXIA Shop via email at recallemail@163.com.

Description: Trader Joe's is recalling Mango Tangerine Scented Candles because the flame can grow larger than expected, creating a fire hazard. The candles have white wax and a cotton wick in a tin container. There have been reports of high flames causing minor burns and property damage.
Title: Trader Joe's Recalls Mango Tangerine Scented Candles Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Trader Joe's Company at 833-771-0299 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.traderjoes.com/home/contact-us/product-feedback for product feedback. Additional information can be found at https://www.traderjoes.com/home/announcements?category=recalls or on the Trader Joe's website under the "Announcements" section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Trader Joe's Mango Tangerine Scented Candle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 14 reports of incidents involving high flames with the recalled candles, including three reports of minor property damage and two reports of minor burns.

Retailers.Name: Available at Trader Joe's locations nationwide in June 2024 for approximately $4.

Distributors.Name: Trader Joe's Company, of Monrovia, California

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential for larger than expected flames

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled candles immediately and return them to any Trader Joe's store for a full cash refund of $4, or fill out the Product Feedback Form online at https://www.traderjoes.com/home/contact-us/product-feedback to receive a $4 Trader Joe's gift card by mail. Customers may be asked to provide a receipt or a photo of the candle.

metaDescription: Trader Joe's recalls Mango Tangerine Scented Candles due to fire hazard from unexpectedly large flames posing a safety risk.

Description: Samsung is recalling certain Slide-In Electric Range models because the front-mounted knobs can be accidentally activated by humans or pets, posing a fire hazard. The defect has led to numerous fires, property damage, injuries, and pet fatalities. The recall includes free knob locks or covers to prevent unintended operation.
Title: Samsung Slide-In Electric Ranges Recalled for Fire Hazard from Front Knob Activation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Samsung at 833-775-0120 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email rangesupport@sea.samsung.com, or visit samsung.com/us/support/range-knob-kit to request free knob locks.

Images.Caption: Samsung Slide-In Electric Range Model NE58F9500SS/AA
Samsung Slide-In Electric Range Model NE63A8711QN/AA
Samsung Electric Range Model NE58K9850WS/AA
Samsung Electric Range Model NE63T8111SS/AA

Injuries.Name: Samsung has received over 300 reports of unintentional activation of the front-mounted knobs by humans or pets since 2013. These ranges have been involved in approximately 250 fires. At least 18 fires caused extensive property damage. Approximately 40 injuries have been reported, eight of which required medical attention, and there have been reports of seven fires involving pet deaths.

Manufacturers.Name: Samsung Electronics America Inc., of Ridgefield Park, N.J.

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, Costco, The Home Depot, Lowe's, and other appliance retailers nationwide, as well as online at Samsung.com. The ranges were sold from May 2013 to August 2024, priced between $1,250 and $3,050.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to accidental activation of front-mounted knobs

Remedies.Name: Free knob locks or covers for safe operation

metaDescription: Samsung recalls Slide-In Electric Ranges due to fire hazard from accidental front knob activation causing fires and injuries.

Description: SMEG USA is recalling FAB38U model retro refrigerators because the refrigerator door can detach, posing an injury hazard. The recall covers various colors and configurations with left or right-opening doors. The company has received reports of doors falling off but no injuries have been reported.
Title: SMEG USA Recalls FAB38U Retro Refrigerators Due to Door Detachment Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to SMEG USA at 888-763-4782, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email recall@smegusa.com or visit https://www.smeg.com/us/recall or www.smeg.com and navigate to the "Recalls" section for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled SMEG refrigerator
"Made in Italy", the model, and serial numbers are printed on a white label inside of the refrigerator's top door

Injuries.Name: The firm has received nine reports of the refrigerator door falling off. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Independent kitchen and appliance stores nationwide and online at www.williams-sonoma.com, https://www.ajmadison.com and www.wayfair.com from September 2020 through January 2024 for about $4,300.

Importers.Name: SMEG USA Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of refrigerator door detachment and potential injury

Remedies.Name: Stop using the refrigerators and contact SMEG USA for a free repair by a certified technician. Consumers will need to email a photo of the refrigerator's model and serial number to recall@smegusa.com.

metaDescription: SMEG USA recalls FAB38U retro refrigerators due to risk of door detachment causing potential injury. No injuries reported so far.

Description: Attom Tech has recalled its LED light-up jelly ring toys because the button cell batteries inside are easily accessible, posing an ingestion hazard to children. The toys are sold in sets of 30, feature the "NiToy" logo, and come in various shapes and colors. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Attom Tech Recalls LED Light-Up Jelly Ring Toys for Ingestion Hazard from Accessible Batteries
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Attom Tech at 800-252-8968 from 3 a.m. to 11 a.m. CT Monday through Friday, email Support@attomtech.com, or visit https://www.attomtech.com/pages/attom-tech-recall or www.attomtech.com and click "Recall" at the top of the page.

Description: Avocado is recalling its Organic Cotton Mattress Pad Protectors because they do not meet federal mattress pad flammability regulations, posing a fire hazard. The recall includes various sizes and styles of quilted ivory cotton pads labeled with model number SFMPAVORG. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Avocado Organic Cotton Mattress Pads Recalled for Fire Hazard Due to Flammability Regulation Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Avocado Mattress at 844-326-5009 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email safety@avocadomattress.com, or visit www.avocadogreenmattress.com/pages/recall for more information.

Products.Name: Avocado Organic Cotton Mattress Pad Protectors

Images.Caption: Recalled Avocado Organic Cotton Mattress Pad Protector
Product Law label showing Model SFMPAVORG

Manufacturers.Name: Avocado Mattress LLC, of Mexico

Retailers.Name: Available at Avocado stores nationwide and online at www.avocadogreenmattress.com from January 2019 to March 2024, priced between $200 and $370.

Importers.Name: Avocado Mattress LLC (d/b/a Avocado Green Mattress), of Fullerton, California

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to violation of federal flammability regulations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattress pads and contact Avocado for a free replacement. Cut the pad in half, date and initial the law label, and upload photos of the cut pad and label to www.avocadogreenmattress.com/pages/recall.

metaDescription: Avocado Organic Cotton Mattress Pads recalled for fire hazard due to non-compliance with federal flammability standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Beberoad has recalled the New Moon Travel Bassinets because they pose a fall hazard and do not comply with federal safety regulations for infant sleep products. The recall applies to bassinets manufactured on or after June 23, 2022, identified by model number TB999-1 and a sewn-in date tag. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Beberoad Love New Moon Travel Bassinets Recalled for Fall Hazard and Federal Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Beberoadlove at 334-316-0066 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email service@beberoad.com, or visit https://beberoad.com/pages/new-moon-travel-bassinets-safety-recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to https://beberoad.com/ and click on 'NEWMOON TRAVEL BASSINET RECALL' for further details.

Importers.Name: Zhongshanboshangkedianzishangwuyouxiangongsi, dba beberoadlove, of China

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to lack of stand violating federal safety regulations for Infant Sleep Products

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bassinets immediately and contact Beberoadlove for a full refund and disposal instructions. Beberoadlove and Amazon are reaching out to all known purchasers.

Description: Brompton Bicycle is recalling T-Line foldable bicycles because of a fall hazard caused by possible misalignment between the handlebar and front wheel. This issue can lead to loss of steering control. The company has received reports of steering problems but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Brompton Recalls T-Line Foldable Bicycles Due to Steering Misalignment Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Brompton Bicycle Inc. at 833-507-7975 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email support@brompton.co.uk. More information can be found online at us.brompton.com/legal/t-line-recall or visit https://us.brompton.com and click on "Recall."

Products.Name: Brompton T-Line Foldable Bicycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,080 (In addition, about 31 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Brompton T-Line Foldable Bicycle
Recalled Brompton T-Line Foldable Bike in Folded Position
T-Line Logo on Label of Recalled T-Line Foldable Bike

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 13 reports of incidents of steering misalignment or loss of steering control worldwide. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bike dealers and Brompton Junction Stores across the country, as well as online at us.brompton.com from February 2024 through June 2024, priced between $5,225 and $5,500.

Importers.Name: Brompton Bicycle Inc., of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Potential loss of steering control due to misalignment between the handlebar and front wheel, creating a fall hazard

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled bicycles and reach out to an authorized Brompton dealer for a complimentary repair to secure the steerer clamp bolts on the handlebar stem. Brompton will directly contact all registered owners and dealers.

metaDescription: Brompton recalls T-Line foldable bicycles for a fall hazard caused by potential steering misalignment. No injuries reported.

Description: Peace Industry Group has recalled the Peace Sports 125CC Utility and Sport Youth ATVs, models 512 CY125ATV-1 and 518 CY125ATV-6, for failing to meet federal ATV safety standards. Hazards include potential lacerations from handlebars, inadequate parking brakes, missing safety reflectors, and footguard risks on the 518 model. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Peace Industry Group Recalls Youth Peace Sports 125CC ATVs for Safety Violations and Injury Risks
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Peace Industry Group at 888-855-6031 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.peacesports.com/ATV-Recall or www.peacesports.com and select "RECALL" at the top of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Peace Sports 512 CY125ATV-1 Utility Youth ATV
Recalled Peace Sports 518 CY125ATV-6 Sport Youth ATV
Recalled Peace Sports Youth ATV VIN plate

Manufacturers.Name: Zhejiang Changying Car Industry Co. LTD, of China

Retailers.Name: AutoWorx Sales LLC, Daryl's Motorcycle Service, Hialeah Scooters, LJS Repairs LLC, M&M Small Engines, Marobo Motors, Motor Werks LLC, One Stop Automotive Repair LLC, Scooters & Small Engine Repairs, Solano Cycles, Unique Motorsports, Wild Hogs Scooters and Motorsports LLC, and other authorized dealerships nationwide from April 2019 through March 2021 for about $1,110.

Importers.Name: Peace Industry Group, of Norcross, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet federal ATV safety standards, potential laceration risk from handlebars, inadequate parking brakes leading to collision risk, absence of required safety reflectors, and footguard hazards on model 518 CY125ATV-6.

Remedies.Name: Immediate cessation of use and full refund from Peace Industry Group.

Description: Papablic Archie Infant Swings have been recalled because their incline angle exceeds federal safety limits, creating a suffocation hazard. The swings do not comply with infant sleep product regulations. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Papablic Archie Infant Swings Recalled for Suffocation Hazard and Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled swings and reach out to Shenzhen Two Pony at 855-214-2723 between 9 a.m. and 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email info@papablic.com or visit www.papablic.com/pages/recall-information for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Papablic Archie Swing Model PAPABLIC61A
Label located on back of swing, showing model and batch information

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com and Papablic.com from July 2023 through April 2024 for about $150.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Two Pony Tech Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to incline angle exceeding 10 degrees and violations of federal safety regulations for infant sleep products.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the swings and contact Shenzhen Two Pony for disposal instructions and proof of destruction to receive a full refund.

metaDescription: Papablic Archie Infant Swings are recalled due to suffocation risk from unsafe incline angles and violations of infant sleep product regulations.

Description: RH Baby & Child is recalling Jeune French Contemporary Upholstered Panel Cribs because loose cylindrical metal inserts in the crib frame can detach, creating a choking hazard. The cribs have wooden frames with upholstered panels and come in several oak finishes. Two reports of metal inserts loosening have been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: RH Baby & Child Recalls Jeune French Contemporary Upholstered Panel Cribs Due to Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled cribs immediately and reach out to RH at 833-611-1775, available from 6 a.m. to 7 p.m. PT on weekdays and 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekends. They can also email recall@rh.com or visit https://rhbabyandchild.rh.com/us/en/customer-experience/safety-recalls for further assistance.

Products.Name: Jeune French Contemporary Upholstered Panel Cribs
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 480 (In addition, about 10 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled RH Baby & Child's Jeune French Contemporary Upholstered Panel Crib
The SKU numbers are printed on the receipt, and on a label located on the inner-lower portion of the upholstered panel

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of a metal insert loosening and detaching. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at RH Baby & Child stores, RH outlets across the country, and online at https://rhbabyandchild.rh.com from January 2021 through March 2024, priced between $600 and $2,900.

Importers.Name: RH US LLC, of Corte Madera, California

Hazards.Name: Loose cylindrical metal inserts in the crib's frame may detach, creating a choking hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled cribs and contact RH for a full refund or a replacement crib with free shipping. If no receipt is available, a $1,260 cash refund, credit, or a replacement crib will be provided at no charge. RH will arrange for free pickup of the recalled crib and will contact all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: RH Baby & Child recalls Jeune French Contemporary Upholstered Panel Cribs due to loose metal inserts posing a choking hazard to children.

Description: Clark Associates is recalling 12 models of Lancaster Table & Seating Standard Height Plastic Restaurant High Chairs because the front T-bar can loosen or detach, posing a fall hazard. The chairs were sold assembled or as kits in brown, black, or gray. There have been reports of the T-bar breaking, including two injuries involving minor scrapes and bruises from falls.
Title: Clark Associates Recalls Lancaster Table & Seating Plastic High Chairs Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Clark Associates at 800-285-8172 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email highchairs@lancastertableandseating.com or visit https://www.lancastertableandseating.com/recalls/plastic-high-chairs-recall for more information.

Products.Name: Lancaster Table & Seating Black Plastic Highchair with Tray and Wheels, assembled
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 27,140 (In addition, about 69 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled High Chair (Black)
Recalled High Chair (Black)
Recalled High Chair (Brown)
Recalled High Chair (Gray)
Location of Model Number

Injuries.Name: The firm has received nine reports of the T-bar located at the front of the chair coming loose or breaking off. Two injuries have been reported, including one report of a child falling out of the high chair, resulting in minor scrapes and bruises.

Retailers.Name: The Restaurant Store nationwide and online at www.therestaurantstore.com, www.webstaurantstore.com, www.clarkfoodserviceequipment.biz, www.quicksupply.com, and www.hometownprovisions.net from June 2014 through January 2024 for between $65 and $135.

Importers.Name: Clark Core Services LLC, d/b/a Clark Associates, of Lancaster, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: The T-bar at the front center of the chair may loosen and detach, creating a fall hazard for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled high chairs immediately and contact Clark Associates for a full refund. They should also register their product at https://www.lancastertableandseating.com/recalls/plastic-high-chairs-recall and dispose of the item according to local recycling regulations.

metaDescription: Clark Associates recalls Lancaster Table & Seating plastic high chairs due to a fall hazard from a loose T-bar that can cause children to fall.

Description: DGL Group is recalling Hover-1 Dynamo e-scooters due to brake failures that pose a serious injury and crash hazard. The recall applies to scooters with specific serial numbers containing 'WY' in the seventh and eighth positions. Reports include multiple brake failures resulting in injuries such as abrasions, contusions, and fractures.
Title: DGL Group Recalls Hover-1 Dynamo E-Scooters for Brake Failure Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DGL Group at 844-451-7300 from 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.hover-1.com/pages/dynamo_recall. Alternatively, they can go to www.hover-1.com and select 'Important Recall Information' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Hover-1 Dynamo e-scooter
Serial number with the letters "WY" as the seventh and eighth letter appears on a label on the underside of the recalled e-scooter

Injuries.Name: DGL Group has received 22 reports of the e-scooter's brakes failing, which resulted in seven injuries, including abrasions, contusions, a dislocated shoulder, fractured teeth, lacerations and strains.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Walmart.com from November 2021 to July 2022, priced around $200.

Importers.Name: DGL Group Ltd., of Edison, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Brake failure leading to serious injury and crash risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the e-scooter immediately and contact DGL Group for a full refund, providing photos of the serial number and severed motor cable.

metaDescription: DGL Group recalls Hover-1 Dynamo e-scooters due to brake failure risk that can cause serious injury and crashes. Check serial numbers for affected units.
