en.metaDescription: DGL Group recalls Hover-1 Dynamo e-scooters due to brake failure risk that can cause serious injury and crashes. Check serial numbers for affected units.

Images.Caption: Recalled Hover-1 Dynamo e-scooter
Serial number with the letters "WY" as the seventh and eighth letter appears on a label on the underside of the recalled e-scooter

Injuries.Name: DGL Group has received 22 reports of the e-scooter's brakes failing, which resulted in seven injuries, including abrasions, contusions, a dislocated shoulder, fractured teeth, lacerations and strains.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Walmart.com from November 2021 to July 2022, priced around $200.

Importers.Name: DGL Group Ltd., of Edison, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Brake failure leading to serious injury and crash risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the e-scooter immediately and contact DGL Group for a full refund, providing photos of the serial number and severed motor cable.

Description: Grand Rapids Chair Company is recalling Brooke model wood dining chairs because they may shift, break, or collapse, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes all styles with partially or fully upholstered seats and backs. The company has received reports of chairs breaking but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Grand Rapids Chair Company Recalls Brooke Wood Dining Chairs for Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled chairs immediately and contact Grand Rapids Chair Company at 866-453-4748 Ext. 147 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also reach out via email at retrofits@grandrapidschair.com or visit www.grandrapidschair.com/customer-service/product-safety-recall-brooke/ for further assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled Woodseat Chair (model W520-11)
Recalled partially upholstered seat chair (model W520-21)
Recalled fully upholstered seat & back (model W520-33)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports report of the chair breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Grand Rapids Chair Company, of Byron Center, Michigan

Retailers.Name: Available through special orders made between January 2020 and June 2024, priced between $450 and $1,015

Hazards.Name: Fall Hazard due to potential shifting, breaking, or collapsing

Remedies.Name: Free repair service provided by Grand Rapids Chair Company personnel at the consumer's location

Description: Ophanie has recalled large pink fluffy/shag area rugs sold in two sizes due to non-compliance with federal flammability regulations. These rugs pose a fire hazard. One fire incident has been reported, but no injuries have occurred.
Title: Ophanie Pink Fluffy Area Rugs Recalled for Fire Hazard Due to Flammability Regulation Violation
ConsumerContact: Ophanie toll-free at 888-281-8150 from 11:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email at recall@liyi99.com, or online at http://www.ophanie.com/recall/index.html or www.ophanie.com and click "Recall" at the top of the page for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Large Ophanie Pink Rug
Front of Label on recalled rug
Back of Label on recalled rug

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a fire. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Amazon.com from April 2024 through June 2024 for between $42 to $65.

Importers.Name: Ophanie, of China

Hazards.Name: The recalled area rugs violate the mandatory federal flammability regulations for carpets and rugs, posing a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled rugs immediately and contact Ophanie for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to destroy the product by cutting it in half, and to provide proof of destruction by sending a photo of the cut product to recall@liyi99.com to receive a full refund. Ophanie is contacting all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Ophanie pink fluffy area rugs are recalled for violating federal flammability standards, posing a fire hazard. No injuries reported.

Description: Razor is recalling Icon electric scooters because the downtube can separate from the floorboard during use, creating a fall hazard. The recall includes various models and serial numbers, with scooters available in multiple colors. There have been reports of downtube separation and two injuries involving bruising. This defect increases the risk of accidents while riding.
Title: Razor Recalls Icon Electric Scooters for Fall Hazard from Downtube Separation
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Razor at 866-467-2967 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.razor.com/iconrecall for additional information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Razor Icon electric scooter
Serial Number sticker located on front left side of the deck with serial number beginning with "31"

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 34 reports of partial or complete downtube separation with two reported injuries, including bruising.

Manufacturers.Name: Razor USA LLC, of Cerritos, Calif.

Retailers.Name: Target, Walmart, Best Buy, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Razor.com, and additional websites

Importers.Name: Razor USA LLC, of Cerritos, Calif.

Hazards.Name: Fall Hazard due to downtube separation

Remedies.Name: Refund or coupon for future purchase

Description: Torquay eTrading LLC has recalled Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip & Rip re-gripping kits because their labels falsely claim the products are safe and non-toxic. The absence of required cautionary warnings for petroleum distillates creates a poisoning hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
URL: https://www.cpsc.gov/Recalls/2024/Torquay eTrading Recalls Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and-Golf-Grip-Kits-Due-to-Risk-of-Poisoning-Violation-of-the-Federal-Hazardous-Substances-Act
Title: Torquay eTrading Recalls Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits for Poisoning Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Torquay eTrading LLC at 347-587-8964 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@essentialvalues.us. Additional information can be found online at https://www.essentialvalues.us/pages/recall or by visiting https://www.essentialvalues.us and clicking 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 26,000 (In addition, about 3,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Golf Grip Solvent, 8-Ounce
Recalled Grip and Rip Re-Gripping Solution Kit with 14 Strips of Grip Tape
Back of the Recalled Golf Grip Solution

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, eBay.com, Walmart.com, and www.essentialvalues.com from December 2017 through May 2024, priced around $9, with kits including golf grip tape priced at approximately $13.

Distributors.Name: Torquay eTrading LLC (dba Essential Values), of New York (formerly sold by Mocer LLC and Bright Ventures LLC)

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to misleading safety labeling

Remedies.Name: Stop using the products and contact Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label

Description: Stiger has recalled the Besrey Twins Strollers, Model BR-C708S, because they pose entrapment, fall, and choking hazards. The front seat design can cause entrapment, while the restraint system and rear seat enclosure may lead to falls. Additionally, foam padding on the grab bar presents a choking risk. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Stiger Recalls Besrey Twins Strollers for Entrapment, Fall, and Choking Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to cease using the stroller immediately and reach out to Stiger for guidance on product disposal and to secure a full refund. For assistance, contact Besrey at 888-310-6255 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email support@besrey.com or stigerstg@outlook.com. More information can also be found online at https://www.besrey.com/pages/product-recalls-safety-alerts or by visiting www.besrey.com and clicking on "Product Recalls" at the bottom of the homepage.

Importers.Name: Stiger Inc., of Diamond Bar, California

Hazards.Name: Entrapment, fall, and choking hazards due to safety regulation violations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the stroller and contact Stiger for disposal instructions and a full refund

Description: BISSELL has recalled over 3 million Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaners from the 39N7 and 2994 series due to a burn hazard. The products can expel hot water or steam unexpectedly, posing a risk of burns. There have been reports of minor burn injuries associated with this issue.
Title: BISSELL Recalls Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaners Due to Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately cease using the recalled steam cleaners and reach out to BISSELL at 855-417-7001 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, and 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET on Saturday. Email inquiries can be sent to RecallNA@bissell.com, or visit www.BISSELL.com/steamshotrecall for further details.

Products.Name: BISSELL Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaners
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3.2 million (In addition, about 355,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled BISSELL Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaner (color may vary)
Model number is printed on label located on the bottom of the product

Injuries.Name: BISSELL has received 183 reports of hot water or steam escaping from the product, including 157 reports of minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Target, Walmart, and various department and home goods stores nationwide, as well as online at www.bissell.com, www.amazon.com, www.hsn.com, and other retailers from August 2008 through May 2024, priced between $35 and $40, or $70 for a two-pack.

Importers.Name: BISSELL Homecare Inc., of Grand Rapids, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Risk of burns due to hot water or steam expulsion

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive a $60 credit for BISSELL.com purchases or a $40 refund for each recalled steam cleaner. Visit www.BISSELL.com/steamshotrecall for registration and instructions on how to cut the cord and upload a photo showing the model number.

metaDescription: BISSELL recalls Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaners due to risk of burns from hot water or steam expulsion. Affected models include 39N7 and 2994 series.

Description: Sassy Baby has recalled the Manhattan Toy Brilliant Bee Rattle because the wooden base can detach from the rod, potentially releasing plastic rings that pose a choking hazard. The toy includes a plastic light-up bee, three plastic rings, and a baby-safe mirror. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Sassy Baby Recalls Manhattan Toy Brilliant Bee Rattles Due to Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Manhattan Toy at 800-541-1345 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Thursday and 8 a.m. to 12 p.m. CT on Friday, email help@manhattantoy.com, or visit www.manhattantoy.com/pages/recall-information or www.manhattantoy.com and click on 'Recall Information' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Manhattan Toy Brilliant Bee Rattle

Retailers.Name: Independent specialty toy stores nationwide and online at www.manhattantoy.com from May 2024 through June 2024 for about $16.

Importers.Name: Sassy Baby Inc., of Gonzales, Louisiana

Hazards.Name: Detachment of the wooden base from the rod, potentially releasing plastic rings and creating a choking hazard for young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled rattles immediately, keep them away from children, and return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. Alternatively, contact Manhattan Toy for return instructions to obtain a full refund. No proof of purchase is necessary.

metaDescription: Sassy Baby recalls Manhattan Toy Brilliant Bee Rattles due to choking hazard from detaching parts that may pose risk to young children.

Description: Atomi has recalled several Smart Heater models because they can unexpectedly turn on, creating fire and burn hazards. The recall includes tower, tabletop, and wall heater models in various colors. One incident of unexpected activation has been reported, but no injuries have occurred.
Title: Atomi Smart Heaters Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards Due to Unexpected Activation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Atomi at 888-770-7140 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.atomiheaterrecall.com or www.atomismart.com and click on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Atomi Smart Tower Heater Model AT1323
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 99,400 (In addition, about 10,700 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Atomi Smart Tower Heater model AT1323
Recalled Atomi Smart Tower Heater models AT1481 and AT1520
Recalled Atomi Smart Tabletop Heater models AT1482 and AT152
Recalled Atomi Smart Tower Heater model AT1632
Recalled Atomi Smart Wall Heater model AT1635

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the heater turning on without user input. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon, Ace Hardware, Atomiusa.com, Best Buy, BJ’s Wholesale Club, Costco, Hammacher Schlemmer, Home Depot, Lowes.com, Menards, Sam's Club, and Walmart from October 2019 to April 2024, priced between $80 and $150 based on the model.

Importers.Name: Atomi Smart, of New York

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to unexpected activation

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the heaters, unplug them, and follow online instructions at www.atomiheaterrecall.com to obtain a full refund. They will need to cut the power cord of the unplugged unit and upload photos of the cut cord and the manufacturer's label during the registration process.

Description: Lovely Angel has recalled children's lace nightgowns sold in multiple colors and sizes due to a burn hazard. The nightgowns do not comply with federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Lovely Angel Children's Nightgowns Recalled for Burn Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Lovely Angel via email at wangmaxime@163.com or visit their online store at Temu.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Black Nightgown
Recalled Lavender Nightgown
Recalled Watermelon Nightgown
Recalled Beige Nightgown
Recalled Green Nightgown
Recalled Red Nightgown
Label of the Recalled Nightgown

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Temu.com from June 2023 to May 2024 for approximately $10.

Importers.Name: Tong Tai Clothing Co. Ltd., dba Lovely Angel, of China

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to violation of federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the nightgowns immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Lovely Angel for a full refund. To dispose of the garments, cut them in half and follow local recycling laws. A photo of the destroyed garment should be sent to wangmaxime@163.com or through Lovely Angel's online store to process the refund. Lovely Angel will also reach out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Lovely Angel children's nightgowns recalled for burn hazard due to failure to meet federal flammability standards for sleepwear.

en.Title: Lovely Angel Children's Nightgowns Recalled for Burn Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violation
en.Description: Lovely Angel has recalled children's lace nightgowns sold in multiple colors and sizes due to a burn hazard. The nightgowns do not comply with federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
en.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Lovely Angel via email at wangmaxime@163.com or visit their online store at Temu.com.
en.metaDescription: Lovely Angel children's nightgowns recalled for burn hazard due to failure to meet federal flammability standards for sleepwear.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Temu.com from June 2023 to May 2024 for approximately $10.

Importers.Name: Tong Tai Clothing Co. Ltd., dba Lovely Angel, of China

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to violation of federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the nightgowns immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Lovely Angel for a full refund. To dispose of the garments, cut them in half and follow local recycling laws. A photo of the destroyed garment should be sent to wangmaxime@163.com or through Lovely Angel's online store to process the refund. Lovely Angel will also reach out to all known purchasers directly.

Description: Claire's is recalling Halloween witch hats made of 100% polyester with a blue mesh scarf and star decorations. These hats fail to comply with federal flammability standards, creating a burn hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Claire's Halloween Witch Hats Recalled for Burn Hazard and Flammability Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Claire's at 888-252-4737 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email NAProductRecall@claires.com, or visit www.claires.com/us/recall-notices/ or www.claires.com and select "Product Recalls" in the Help section.

Products.Name: Halloween Witch Hats
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5,700 (In addition, about 450 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Halloween Witch Hat
"CBI Distributing Corporation" is printed on the sewn-in tag inside the hat

Retailers.Name: Available at Claire's and Icing stores nationwide from July 2023 through December 2023 for approximately $15.

Distributors.Name: Claire's and Icing Stores, of Hoffman Estates, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Violation of federal flammability standards, posing a burn injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the hats immediately and return them to any Claire's or Icing store for a full refund.

Description: Ross Stores has recalled wicker-style armchairs with plastic cross weave seats and dark metal frames due to a risk of leg bending or breaking. This defect can cause falls and injuries. The recall follows reports of chair legs breaking, resulting in minor injuries such as soreness and contusions.
Title: Ross Stores Recalls Wicker-Style Armchairs Due to Fall and Injury Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ross Stores at 800-335-1115, available from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at customer.inquiries@ros.com or visit www.rossstores.com/recalls. For more details, click on 'Legal Notices & Recalled Products' at the bottom of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Armchair – green
Recalled Armchair – gray

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the chair legs breaking; four resulting in minor injuries, including soreness and contusions.

Retailers.Name: Available at Ross stores across the nation from February 2024 to April 2024 for approximately $50.

Importers.Name: Ross Procurement Inc., of Dublin, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of leg bending or breaking, leading to falls and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the armchairs immediately and return them to any Ross store for a full refund or a $50 store credit, no receipt required.

metaDescription: Ross Stores recalls wicker-style armchairs due to risk of leg breakage causing falls and injuries. Immediate use should be stopped for safety.

Description: Hatch Baby is recalling AC power adapters sold with Rest 1st Generation Sound Machines because the plastic casing can detach when unplugged, exposing electrical prongs and posing a shock hazard. The recall involves adapters with model number CYAP05 050100U. The company has received reports of the casing detaching, including incidents of minor electrical shock.
Title: Hatch Baby Recalls Power Adapters for Rest 1st Generation Sound Machines Due to Shock Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Hatch Baby Inc. at 888-918-4614 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@hatch.co, or visit www.hatch.co/adapterrecall for further details.

Products.Name: Power Adapters for Rest 1st Generation Sound Machines
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 919,400 (In addition, about 44,352 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Rest 1st Generation Sound Machine sold with recalled Power Adapter
Recalled Power Adapter for Rest 1st Generation Sound Machines
Location of model number on recalled adapter

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 19 reports of the plastic housing surrounding the AC power adapter coming off, including two reports of consumers who experienced a minor electrical shock.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Hatch.co and Amazon.com, as well as in-store at BuyBuyBaby, Target, Walmart, Nordstrom, Pottery Barn Kids, and BestBuy locations nationwide from January 2019 to September 2022, priced between $60 and $70 for certain Rest 1st Generation sound machines. Additionally, sold on Amazon.com from January 2019 to May 2024 for $60 to $200 with specific Rest 1st Generation sound machines.

Importers.Name: Hatch Baby Inc., of Palo Alto, California

Hazards.Name: The plastic casing of the AC power adapter may detach when unplugged, exposing the prongs and creating a shock hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled power adapters and contact Hatch for a complimentary replacement. They need to unplug the adapter, cut the cord, take a photo showing the model number and cut cord, and upload this photo along with their name and mailing address at www.hatch.co/adapterrecall. Hatch will also reach out to all registered owners.

metaDescription: Hatch Baby recalls power adapters for Rest 1st Generation Sound Machines due to shock hazard from detachable plastic casing exposing prongs.

Description: MGA Entertainment is recalling Miniverse Make It Mini Sets containing unused liquid resins that pose risks of skin, eye, and respiratory irritation and sensitization. The resins can cause burns, irritation, and respiratory issues, including triggering asthma. These hazards occur when the resins are inhaled, touched, or ingested. Once cured, the resins no longer present these risks.
Title: MGA Entertainment Recalls Miniverse Make It Mini Sets Due to Skin, Eye, and Respiratory Irritation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact MGA Entertainment at 800-222-4685 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or 9 a.m. to 2 p.m. ET Saturday. You can also email mvcustomer_care@mgae.com or visit https://www.mgae.com/customer-care/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Miniverse Make It Mini Sets with Liquid Resins
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 21 million (In addition, about 1 million in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – Food
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – Appliances
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini - Lifestyle
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – Kitchen
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – All You Can Eat
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini Appliances
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini Lifestyle
Recalled Make It Mini Food – 18 Pack
Recalled Make It Mini Food Ice Cream Social - Front
Recalled Make It Mini Food Ice Cream Social - Opened

Injuries.Name: MGA has received 26 reports of incidents with this product from children and adults, including reports of skin burns and irritation, and respiratory irritation, with one report that a consumer's asthma was triggered.

Retailers.Name: Target, Walmart, Family Dollar, Dollar General, ALDI, Hobby Lobby, and various other stores nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, shop.mgae.com, Target.com, and Walmart.com.

Importers.Name: MGA Entertainment, of Chatsworth, California

Hazards.Name: The recalled Make It Mini sets contain resins that can cause skin, eye, and respiratory irritation or sensitization when inhaled, touched, or ingested by children or adults.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using any units with unused resins and contact MGA to obtain a pre-paid label for returns, either for the entire unopened product or the unused resins along with a photo of the opened product.

Description: Baseus has recalled specific magnetic wireless charging power banks due to a fire hazard caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The affected models include PPCXM06 and PPCXW06, which feature a magnetic side for phone attachment. The recall follows reports of battery swelling, fires, and burn injuries. Consumers should stop using these power banks immediately.
Title: Baseus Magnetic Wireless Charging Power Banks Recalled for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Baseus at 855-215-5824 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email care@baseus.com, or visit https://baseuspowerbankrecall.expertinquiry.com. More details can also be found at www.baseus.com under 'Product Recall Information' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Baseus Magnetic Wireless Charging Power Bank PPCXM06

Images.Caption: Recalled Magnetic Wireless Charging Power Banks
Recalled PPCXM06 Magnetic Power Bank
Recalled PPCXW06 Power Bank

Injuries.Name: Baseus has received 171 reports of incidents, including 132 reports of bulging or swelling batteries and 39 reports of fires, resulting in 13 burn injuries and about $20,000 in property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, AliExpress.com, and Baseus.com from April 2022 to April 2024, priced between $18 and $55.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Baseus Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Baseus for a full refund with proof of purchase, or a $36 cash refund without proof. Submit a photo showing the model number for recall registration. Those without an order number must provide a photo of the power bank along with their name and date written in permanent marker. Baseus and Amazon will notify all known purchasers. Dispose of the battery according to local regulations.

metaDescription: Baseus recalls magnetic wireless charging power banks due to fire hazard from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Stop use and seek refund if affected.

Injuries.Name: Baseus has received 171 reports of incidents, including 132 reports of bulging or swelling batteries and 39 reports of fires, resulting in 13 burn injuries and about $20,000 in property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, AliExpress.com, and Baseus.com from April 2022 to April 2024, priced between $18 and $55.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Baseus Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Baseus for a full refund with proof of purchase, or a $36 cash refund without proof. Submit a photo showing the model number for recall registration. Those without an order number must provide a photo of the power bank along with their name and date written in permanent marker. Baseus and Amazon will notify all known purchasers. Dispose of the battery according to local regulations.

Description: REI is recalling Co-op Cycles REV children's bicycles with training wheels because the training wheel assembly knob can loosen, posing a fall hazard. The recall includes 12-, 16-, and 20-inch models from recent years with specific serial numbers. The company has received reports of training wheels detaching, resulting in scrapes, bruises, and a broken arm.
Title: REI Recalls Co-op Cycles REV Children's Bicycles with Training Wheels Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact REI at 800-426-4840 from 6 a.m. to 8 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, or from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekends. Additional support is available via email or chat at rei.com/help, or by visiting https://www.rei.com/help?a=REI-Co-op-Cycles-REV-Bikes---id--He33EfUtSkKpMKyK7ZX27A or https://www.rei.com/help/product-safety-and-recalls.html and selecting 'Co-op Cycles REV 12-, 16-, or 20-inch Kid's Bicycles with Training Wheels Recall' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Co-op Cycles REV 12 Kids Bike (color: Pencil)
Recalled Co-op Cycles REV 16 Kids Bike with training wheels (color: Catch Up)
Recalled Co-op Cycles REV 20 Kids Bike with training wheels (color: Plum Burst)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of the training wheels falling off. Injuries included reports of scrapes, bruising, and one involving broken arms.

Manufacturers.Name: RECREATIONAL EQUIPMENT INC. (REI), of Sumner, Washington

Retailers.Name: REI stores nationwide and online at REI.com from March 2022 through May 2024 for between $200 and $300.

Hazards.Name: Loosening training wheel assembly knob can lead to falls and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycle if training wheels are attached, contact REI for a free repair or kit, or continue using if riding without training wheels.

Description: Babboe B.V. has recalled all models of their two-wheeled and three-wheeled cargo bicycles, including electric and non-electric versions. The recall addresses a risk of frame cracking and bending, which can lead to falls. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Babboe Cargo Bicycles Recalled for Risk of Frame Cracking and Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Babboe at their toll-free number 833-220-7867, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at service.us@babboe.com. More details can also be found on their website at www.internationalcargobike.com.

Products.Name: Babboe Cargo Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 300 (In addition, about 445 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Babboe Cargo Bicycle
Recalled Babboe Cargo Bicycle

Manufacturers.Name: Babboe B.V., of the Netherlands

Retailers.Name: Various bicycle retailers across the U.S., including 718 Cyclery in New York City, A Street Bike Named Desire in Palo Alto, Going Dutch Bikes in Old Greenwich, Aika Trading LLC in Pacific Palisades, Bicycle Space in Washington, D.C., Clever Cycles in Portland, Dockside Boat in Princess Anne, Human Electric Hybrids LLC in Ypsilanti, La Dolce Velo in San Jose, PressWell in Hinsdale, and the Bike Center in Santa Monica, sold these bicycles from January 2011 through March 2024, priced between $3,500 and $7,000.

Hazards.Name: Risk of frame cracking and bending, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled bicycles and contact Babboe for a full refund and bicycle pickup.

metaDescription: Babboe recalls cargo bicycles due to risk of frame cracking that can cause falls. No injuries reported. Users should stop use immediately.

Description: Cinmar is recalling Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Aluminum and Newport Teak Chaise Lounge Chairs due to a risk of finger crushing and amputation. The adjustable backrest can unexpectedly drop, trapping fingers between the backrest and chair frame. There have been reports of injuries, including partial finger amputations and a finger-crushing injury requiring surgery.
Title: Cinmar Recalls Frontgate Chaise Lounge Chairs for Finger Crushing and Amputation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Cinmar at 888-298-4651, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET and Saturday from 9:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. Alternatively, visit www.frontgate.com/newportchaiserecall or navigate to the "Product Recalls" section on www.frontgate.com for further details.

Products.Name: Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Aluminum Chaises

Images.Caption: ecalled Cinmar Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Aluminum Chaise
Recalled Cinmar Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Teak Chaise

Injuries.Name: Cinmar has received three reports of incidents involving consumers' fingers becoming entrapped between the adjustable backrest and the chair frame, resulting in two partial finger amputation injuries, and one finger-crushing injury requiring surgery.

Retailers.Name: Available at Frontgate retail locations in Georgia, Kentucky, North Carolina, Ohio, and Texas, as well as online at Frontgate.com and in Frontgate catalogs from February 2014 through December 2023, priced between $600 and $1,400.

Importers.Name: Cinmar LLC, of West Chester, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Risk of finger crushing and amputation from backrest adjustment

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit including new backrest support bar and assembly instructions

metaDescription: Cinmar recalls Frontgate Chaise Lounge Chairs due to finger crushing and amputation hazards from adjustable backrest. Immediate safety risk reported.

Description: Global Home USA has recalled the Cedona Natural View six-drawer dressers due to tip-over and entrapment hazards. These dressers do not comply with federal safety standards for clothing storage units. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Global Home USA Recalls Cedona Natural View Six-Drawer Dressers for Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should immediately stop using the dresser and contact Rooms To Go at 855-688-0919, available Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET. Alternatively, they can email productcare@roomstogo.com or visit www.cedonadresserrecall.com.

Importers.Name: Global Homes USA, of Charlotte, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Tip-over and entrapment hazards

Remedies.Name: Free replacement and removal of the recalled dresser

Description: Goal Zero is recalling Yeti Link Expansion Modules and EC8 power cables because overheating and melting connections can cause fire and burn hazards. These products are used to connect and expand Yeti Power Stations in homes and vehicles. The recall follows reports of cables melting inside a vehicle, resulting in minor property damage. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Goal Zero Recalls Yeti Link Expansion Modules and EC8 Cables Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Goal Zero at 888-794-6250 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email support@goalzero.com, or visit www.goalzero.com and click on 'Notices/Recalls' in the Support section for further details.

Products.Name: Yeti Link Expansion Module
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 34,460 (In addition, about 815 were sold in Canada) (The EC8 cables were previously recalled in April 2021)

Recalled Male EC8 to Ring Cable
Recalled Female EC8 to Ring Cable
Recalled MPPTx to Tank Cable with EC8 Connector

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the cables melting inside a vehicle, causing minor property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: REI

Importers.Name: Goal Zero LLC, of Draper, Utah

Hazards.Name: Overheating and melting connections leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled products and request a free replacement kit from Goal Zero

Description: MM Products has recalled JoyJolt Declan Single-Wall Glass Coffee Mugs, model JG10242, due to risks of burns and cuts. The mugs can break or crack at the base when filled with hot liquids, posing burn and laceration hazards. Multiple incidents and injuries have been reported related to this defect.
Title: MM Products Recalls JoyJolt Declan Glass Coffee Mugs Due to Burn and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach MM Products at 888-569-5680, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recall@joyjolt.com or visit https://joyjolt.com/pages/product-notices-recalls or www.joyjolt.com and click on 'Safety Recalls' for further information.

Products.Name: JoyJolt Declan Single-Wall Glass Coffee Mugs

Images.Caption: Recalled Declan Single-Wall Glass Coffee Mug

Injuries.Name: There have been 103 incidents of the recalled coffee glasses breaking at the base, resulting in 56 injuries, including 35 burns across the body from spilled hot liquids, and 21 cuts. Seven incidents required medical attention, including surgery and stitches.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.amazon.com and MM Products Inc.'s website www.joyjolt.com from September 2019 to May 2022, priced between $20 and $25 for a set of six.

Importers.Name: MM Products Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn and laceration from breaking or cracking when filled with hot liquids.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mugs immediately and contact MM Products for a full refund.

metaDescription: MM Products recalls JoyJolt Declan Glass Coffee Mugs due to burn and laceration hazards from breaking when used with hot liquids.

Description: myCharge has recalled POWER HUB All-In-One 10,000mAh portable chargers due to fire and burn hazards from overheating while charging. The affected models include AO10FK-A, AO10FK-B, and AO10FK-C with specific date codes ending in 21, 22, or certain codes ending in 23. Reports include incidents of melting, smoking, fire, and sparking, with no injuries requiring medical attention reported.
Title: myCharge Recalls POWER HUB All-In-One Portable Chargers for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled chargers immediately and contact myCharge at 888-251-2026 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional assistance can be obtained via email at compliance@mycharge.com or by visiting https://mycharge.com/pages/power-hub-all-in-one-recall or www.myCharge.com and selecting "RECALL INFORMATION" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: myCharge POWER HUB All-In-One 10,000mAh Portable Charger

Images.Caption: Recalled myCharge POWER HUB All-In-One 10,000mAh portable charger, models AO10FK-A, AO10FK-B, and AO10FK-C
Location of WWYY date code printed on wall prong

Injuries.Name: There have been a total of 120 reports involving the portable chargers overheating. myCharge received five reports of incidents, including two reports of residential fires, resulting in approximately $165,000 in reported property damage; and Costco received 115 returns of the portable chargers, mentioning melting, expanding, smoking, fire, burning, exploding, or sparking. There have been no reports of injuries requiring medical attention.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at Costco stores nationwide and online at Costco.com from January 2022 through November 2023 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: RFA Brands LLC, d/b/a myCharge, of Birmingham, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating while charging

Remedies.Name: Free replacement portable charger

metaDescription: myCharge recalls POWER HUB portable chargers due to fire and burn hazards caused by overheating during charging. No injuries reported.

Description: Orly Corporation is recalling lavender scented candles contained in round wooden bread bowls because excessive flame height can ignite the bowl, creating fire and burn hazards. The candles have three wicks and weigh about 8 ounces, with a label marked 'CBOCS Distribution, Inc.' and UPC 197712076826. The recall follows reports of flames reaching excessive heights and incidents of minor burns.
Title: Orly Recalls Lavender Scented Candles in Wooden Bread Bowls Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach Orly Corporation at 800-917-2573, email candlerecall@orly-corp.com, or visit https://www.orly-corp.com/cracker-barrel-candle-recall and www.orly-corp.com and select 'Candle Recall'.

Products.Name: Lavender Scented Candle in Wooden Bowl

Images.Caption: Recalled Lavender scented candle in a round wooden bread bowl
Label located on the underside of the recalled candle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of flames reaching excessive heights, including three reports of minor burns.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at Cracker Barrel Old Country Store® from November 2023 to May 2024 for approximately $13.

Importers.Name: CBOCS Distribution Inc., of Lebanon, Tennessee

Hazards.Name: Excessive flame height can ignite the bowl, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled candle immediately and return it to any Cracker Barrel Old Country Store® for a full refund.

Description: StyleCraft has recalled its Instinct Cordless Hair Clippers, model SC607M, because of fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The recall affects clippers in blue, red, and black with specific lot numbers printed on the back. Reports include incidents of battery overheating and one minor burn injury.
Title: StyleCraft Instinct Cordless Hair Clippers Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact StyleCraft at 888-864-6409 from 8:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, via email at CSteam@stylecraftus.com, or visit www.stylecraftus.com/recall for more information.

Products.Name: Instinct Cordless Hair Clippers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 50,000 (In addition, about 1,320 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled StyleCraft Instinct Professional Vector Motor Cordless Hair Clippers (blue)
Recalled StyleCraft Instinct Professional Vector Motor Cordless Hair Clippers (blue)
Model and Lot number location on the recalled clippers

Injuries.Name: StyleCraft has received six reports of the batteries overheating or causing a fire, including one minor burn.

Retailers.Name: Available at professional beauty supply stores nationwide and online at www.stylecraftus.com and www.amazon.com from January 2023 to May 2024, priced around $250.

Importers.Name: StyleCraft LLC, of Boca Raton, Florida

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using and charging the clippers immediately. Follow StyleCraft's instructions for battery removal and receive a free replacement. Batteries can be replaced at authorized service centers or by mailing the unit back in a pre-paid shipping package. Dispose of the battery according to local regulations.

metaDescription: StyleCraft recalls Instinct Cordless Hair Clippers due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Immediate safety precautions advised.

en.Description: StyleCraft has recalled its Instinct Cordless Hair Clippers, model SC607M, because of fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The recall affects clippers in blue, red, and black with specific lot numbers printed on the back. Reports include incidents of battery overheating and one minor burn injury.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact StyleCraft at 888-864-6409 from 8:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, via email at CSteam@stylecraftus.com, or visit www.stylecraftus.com/recall for more information.
en.metaDescription: StyleCraft recalls Instinct Cordless Hair Clippers due to fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Immediate safety precautions advised.

Products.Name: Instinct Cordless Hair Clippers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 50,000 (In addition, about 1,320 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled StyleCraft Instinct Professional Vector Motor Cordless Hair Clippers (blue)
Recalled StyleCraft Instinct Professional Vector Motor Cordless Hair Clippers (blue)
Model and Lot number location on the recalled clippers

Injuries.Name: StyleCraft has received six reports of the batteries overheating or causing a fire, including one minor burn.

Retailers.Name: Available at professional beauty supply stores nationwide and online at www.stylecraftus.com and www.amazon.com from January 2023 to May 2024, priced around $250.

Importers.Name: StyleCraft LLC, of Boca Raton, Florida

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using and charging the clippers immediately. Follow StyleCraft's instructions for battery removal and receive a free replacement. Batteries can be replaced at authorized service centers or by mailing the unit back in a pre-paid shipping package. Dispose of the battery according to local regulations.

Description: Thousandshores has recalled Theefun Kids Gardening Tools sets because the brown handle strings contain phthalates exceeding federal limits. These chemicals pose health risks if ingested by children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Thousandshores Recalls Theefun Kids Gardening Tools Sets for Excessive Phthalates in Handle Strings
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Thousandshores via email at support@theefun.com or visit their customer service page at https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=A3G27BXXOHYJZ1.

Images.Caption: Recalled Theefun Kids Gardening Tools, model number HJL-001

Manufacturers.Name: JiuDing Tools Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from January 2024 through April 2024 for between about $27 and $29 for the bundle of five tools.

Importers.Name: Thousandshores Inc., of Newark, California

Hazards.Name: Excessive phthalates in handle strings pose health risks if ingested by children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should remove these gardening tools from children immediately and contact Thousandshores for a full refund. Additionally, cut off the brown handle strings and dispose of them per local recycling regulations. Thousandshores and Amazon will notify all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Thousandshores recalls Theefun Kids Gardening Tools sets due to excessive phthalates in handle strings posing health risks to children.

Description: Vitamix has expanded its recall of 8-ounce and 20-ounce blending containers and blade bases from the Ascent and Venturist Series due to a laceration hazard. The recall includes units previously repaired in 2018. Consumers have reported injuries caused by contact with exposed blades. A repair kit with a protective shroud and instructional labels is offered to address the hazard.
Title: Vitamix Expands Recall of Ascent and Venturist Series 8-oz and 20-oz Blending Containers and Blade Bases for Laceration Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Vitamix at 855-215-5178 from 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email service@vitamix.com, or visit www.BlendingCupBowlRecall.expertinquiry.com or https://www.vitamix.com/us/en_us and navigate to the "Product Recalls" section for further details.

Products.Name: Vitamix Ascent Series 8-ounce Blending Container
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 569,000 (In August 2018, 105,000 of the 569,000 blending containers and blade bases were previously recalled) (In addition, about 121,950 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Vitamix Ascent Series and Venturist Series 8-ounce Blending Container
Recalled Vitamix Ascent Series and Venturist Series 20-ounce Blending Container
Recalled Vitamix Ascent Series and Venturist Series Blade Base

Injuries.Name: Vitamix has received 27 reports of lacerations, including 11 reports from the prior 2018 recall, when consumers' hands came in contact with exposed blades.

Manufacturers.Name: Vita-Mix Corporation, of Cleveland, Ohio

Retailers.Name: Costco, Best Buy, Crate & Barrel, Macy's, Target, Williams Sonoma, Walmart, and various specialty stores across the country, as well as online at Vitamix.com, Amazon.com, and QVC.com from April 2017 through May 2024, priced between $30 and $990 depending on the configuration.

Hazards.Name: Laceration risk due to blade exposure

Remedies.Name: Repair kit with protective shroud and instructional labels

metaDescription: Vitamix recalls Ascent and Venturist Series blending containers and blade bases due to laceration hazard from exposed blades. Repair kit available.

Description: Adven Group is recalling all sizes and thicknesses of Nap Queen Sleep Victoria Hybrid Mattresses because they do not meet federal flammability regulations, creating a fire hazard. The recalled mattresses have specific model numbers and distinct labeling. No injuries have been reported. The company offers a free fitted cover to bring the mattresses into compliance with safety standards.
Title: Adven Group Recalls Nap Queen Sleep Victoria Hybrid Mattresses for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Nap Queen Sleep at 866-387-6239 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email recall@napqueens.com, or visit https://www.napqueensleep.com/pages/napqueen-victoria-hybrid-mattress-recall-program. Alternatively, visit www.napqueensleep.com and click on "Product Recall" for more details.

Products.Name: Nap Queen Sleep Victoria Hybrid Mattresses

Images.Caption: Recalled Nap Queen Sleep Victoria Hybrid Mattress
Nap Queen Sleep, "Victoria Hybrid," the model number, and manufacture date appear on a white label sewn onto the mattress cover

Retailers.Name: Available at various retailers nationwide and online, including Amazon.com, Walmart, Home Depot, Wayfair, Target, Bed Bath and Beyond, and Napqueensleep.com from February 2020 through October 2023, priced between $160 and $375.

Importers.Name: Adven Group, dba Nap Queen Sleep, of Missouri City, Texas

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to violation of federal mattress flammability regulations

Remedies.Name: Free fitted cover to ensure compliance with federal safety standards

metaDescription: Adven Group recalls Nap Queen Sleep Victoria Hybrid Mattresses due to fire hazard from non-compliance with federal flammability standards.

Description: Bambu Lab has recalled its A1 3D printers because of electric shock and fire risks caused by short-circuiting heatbed cables. The recall applies to units with a serial number featuring 'A' as the sixth digit on the QR code sticker. The company has received reports of damaged cables, including one sparking incident, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Bambu Lab A1 3D Printers Recalled for Electric Shock and Fire Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach Bambu Lab via email at a1recall@bambulab.com, or visit https://bambulab.com/en-us/support/A1recall for more details. Alternatively, go to https://bambulab.com/en-us and select 'A1 Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bambu Lab A1 Printer
Bambu Lab and "A1" are located on the x-axis motor
QR code sticker on the rear of recalled 3D printer has "A" in the sixth digit

Injuries.Name: The firm has received a total of 19 reports of damaged cables, including one cable sparking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Micro Center stores nationwide and online at us.store.bambulab.com from December 2023 through January 2024, priced between $400 and $560.

Importers.Name: Bambu Lab USA Inc., of Austin, Texas

Hazards.Name: Electric shock and fire hazards due to short-circuiting heatbed cable.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled printers and contact Bambu Lab for a full refund or a free replacement of the heatbed and cable through a local repair facility. Refund requests will include a prepaid shipping label for printer return. Self-repair is not advised.

metaDescription: Bambu Lab recalls A1 3D printers due to electric shock and fire hazards from short-circuiting heatbed cables. No injuries reported.

Description: Daikin Comfort Technologies is recalling packaged air conditioning and heat pump units under the Amana, Daikin, and Goodman brands due to a fire hazard. Incorrect electrical ratings on the units' serial plates may cause improper wiring, increasing the risk of fire. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Daikin Comfort Technologies Recalls Amana, Daikin, and Goodman Air Conditioning and Heat Pump Units for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact DCT at 833-324-5460 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.goodmanmfg.com/package-recall for further details.

Products.Name: Amana Air Conditioning and Heat Pump Units

Images.Caption: Recalled Goodman branded air conditioning and heat pump
Recalled air conditioning and heat pump model number label location

Manufacturers.Name: Daikin Comfort Technologies Manufacturing L.P. (f/k/a Goodman Manufacturing Company L.P.), of Houston, Texas

Retailers.Name: Heating and cooling equipment independent dealers nationwide from January 2024 through March 2024 for between $4,500 to $7,500.

Hazards.Name: Incorrect electrical ratings on the unit's serial plate may lead to improper wiring, creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should refrain from opening the outside electrical panel and should immediately reach out to Daikin Comfort Technologies for a complimentary repair and inspection. DCT will directly contact all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Daikin recalls Amana, Daikin, and Goodman air conditioning and heat pump units due to fire hazard from incorrect electrical ratings on serial plates.

Description: Retrospec is recalling Beaumont Plus ST city bikes equipped with disc brakes because of a front disc brake rotor and brake pad misalignment. This defect can lead to brake failure, increasing the risk of crashes. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Retrospec Recalls Beaumont Plus ST Bikes with Disc Brakes Due to Brake Misalignment Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Retrospec at 888-488-2369 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recall@retrospec.com, or visit https://retrospec.com/pages/retrospec-beaumont-plus-w-disc-brakes-recall for further details.

Products.Name: Beaumont Plus ST City Bikes with Disc Brakes

Images.Caption: Recalled Beaumont Plus ST with Disc Brakes in Bluebird
Recalled Beaumont Plus ST with Disc Brakes in Eggshell
Recalled Beaumont Plus ST with Disc Brakes in Mint
Recalled Beaumont Plus ST Bike label on bike frame

Retailers.Name: Bicycle retailers across the country from February 2024 to March 2024, priced around $400.

Importers.Name: Xander Bicycle Corp., dba Retrospec, of Perris, California

Hazards.Name: Misalignment of the front disc brake rotor and brake pads, leading to potential brake failure and crash risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the affected bicycles immediately and return them to the authorized dealer for a complimentary installation of a new front fork. Locate a dealer at https://retrospec.com/pages/dealer-locator/.

metaDescription: Retrospec recalls Beaumont Plus ST bikes due to front disc brake misalignment that can cause brake failure and crash hazards.

Description: Medline Industries is recalling its adult portable bed rails, model numbers MDS6800BA and MDS6800BAH, due to serious entrapment and asphyxiation risks. The recall follows two reported deaths involving these bed rails. The products feature black metal tubing, a foam rubber grip handle, and a fabric pouch, with identifying labels on the support frame.
Title: Medline Industries Recalls Adult Portable Bed Rails Due to Entrapment and Asphyxiation Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Medline at 866-359-1704 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at recalls@medline.com, or visit https://recalls.medline.com or https://athome.medline.com and select "Product Recalls" at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Medline Adult Portable Bed Rails
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1.5 million (In addition, about 5,522 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Bed Assist Bar (shown with retention strap included with product)
Location of label printed with model number
Model number printed next to the letters "REF" on the label

Injuries.Name: Medline has received two reports of entrapment deaths associated with the Bed Assist Bars. The deaths occurred in July 2019 and November 2023 and involved a 76-year-old woman at a senior nursing facility in Iowa and an 87-year-old woman at a residential care facility in South Carolina.

Retailers.Name: Available through Medline's websites, Medline.com and athome.medline.com, as well as major online retailers such as Amazon and Walmart.com, online pharmacies like CVS.com and Walgreens.com, and various online medical supply stores including Cascade Healthcare Solutions and Magic Medical, from July 2009 until March 2024, priced between $32 and $64.

Importers.Name: Medline Industries LP, of Northfield, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bed rails and contact Medline for a full refund.

metaDescription: Medline recalls adult portable bed rails due to serious entrapment and asphyxiation hazards. Two fatal incidents have been reported.

Description: Padded crib bumpers sold on AliExpress have been recalled because they pose a suffocation hazard to infants. These bumpers, which come in various colors and lack identifying labels, violate federal regulations banning crib bumpers. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Padded Crib Bumpers Recalled for Suffocation Hazard and Federal Ban Violation Sold on AliExpress
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can visit AliExpress online at https://customerservice.aliexpress.com/home?language=en&from=byr_common&hcMapRule=aeMapRule and select "online service" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Padded Crib Bumper in the Color of Teddy Bear
Recalled Padded Crib Bumper in the Color of Gray with White Clouds
Recalled Padded Crib Bumper in the Color of Light Pink
Recalled Padded Crib Bumper in the Color of White

Retailers.Name: Available online at AliExpress.com from October 2023 to February 2024 for approximately $174 (Henan Ouchang Trading Co., Ltd.) and from December 2023 to February 2024 for about $26 (Xinxiang Junshun Trading Co., Ltd.)

Distributors.Name: Henan Ouchang Trading Co. Ltd.
Xinxiang Junshun Trading Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk for infants due to obstruction of breathing

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled crib bumpers immediately and reach out to AliExpress for a full refund. To process the refund, consumers should destroy the bumpers by cutting the straps and padding, then provide a photo as proof of destruction by replying to AliExpress's email. The company is reaching out to all known buyers.

Description: CHZHVAN has recalled its JKD-512 combination smoke and carbon monoxide detectors because they may fail to activate during a fire. These detectors, sold exclusively online, do not reliably alert users to smoke, creating a significant fire hazard. There have been reports of alarm failure, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: CHZHVAN JKD-512 Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detectors Recalled for Fire Alert Failure
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Haikouhuidishangmaoyouxiangongsi via email at l18276710356@163.com, or visit www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=A4INNIJC6B92S&asin=B0C2ZCTCZ8&ref_=dp_merchant_link&isAmazonFulfilled=1 or www.haikouhuidi.com and click on 'Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: CHZHVAN Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector

Images.Caption: Recalled CHZHVAN Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector (Front with Packaging)
Recalled CHZHVAN Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector (Back with Packaging)

Injuries.Name: There are five reports of the detectors failing to alarm in the presence of smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from August 2023 to January 2024, priced between $18 and $59.

Importers.Name: Haikouhuidishangmaoyouxiangongsi, of China

Hazards.Name: Failure to activate, risking lack of fire alert for consumers.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled detectors immediately and contact Haikouhuidishangmaoyouxiangongsi for a full refund.

Description: Electrolux Group has reissued a recall for Frigidaire and Kenmore Elite smooth-top electric ranges due to fire and burn hazards caused by potential heating element malfunctions. These issues include spontaneous activation, failure to turn off, or incorrect temperature settings. The recall covers specific models with rear panel rotary knobs and digital displays in multiple colors. Reports include fires and injuries related to burns and smoke inhalation.
Title: Electrolux Group Reissues Recall of Frigidaire and Kenmore Electric Ranges for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Electrolux Group at 888-845-8226 from 8:30 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at potentiometerrecall@electrolux.com, or visit https://www.ema-recall.com/potentiometer or www.potentiometerrecall.com for further details. Additional information is available at www.frigidaire.com by clicking on 'Recall Information'.

Products.Name: Frigidaire and Kenmore Smooth-top Electric Ranges
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 203,000 (The ranges were previously recalled in August 2009)

Images.Caption: Recalled Frigidaire Smooth-top Freestanding Electric Range: Model PLEFMZ99GC

Injuries.Name: Since the recall was announced in August 2009, Electrolux has received at least 212 reports of the ranges behaving erratically, including 14 reports of fires and eight reports of injuries involving burns to hands or arms, as well as smoke inhalation.

Manufacturers.Name: Electrolux Home Products Inc., of Charlotte, North Carolina

Retailers.Name: Available at Sears and independent appliance retailers across the country from June 2001 to August 2009, priced between $1,000 and $2,500.

Hazards.Name: Potential spontaneous activation of heating elements, failure to turn off, or incorrect temperature settings, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Contact Electrolux or register online for recall participation. Depending on the serial number, consumers will be informed if their range is repairable. Those with affected ranges should avoid leaving items on the range when not in use. Repairable units will receive a free inspection and repair, while non-repairable ranges will qualify for a $50 electronic gift card and reimbursement for haul-away fees up to $60 upon proof of purchase for a new range.

metaDescription: Electrolux recalls Frigidaire and Kenmore electric ranges due to fire and burn hazards from heating element malfunctions. Check if your range is affected.

Description: Kano Laboratories is recalling several Super Lube products, including Syncopen Synthetic Penetrant, Fire Resistant Hydraulic Fluid, and Metal Protectant and Corrosion Inhibitor. These products contain ethylene glycol or low-viscosity petroleum distillates and are packaged in containers that do not meet child-resistant standards, posing a poisoning risk to children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Kano Laboratories Recalls Super Lube® Products for Poisoning Hazard Due to Non-Child-Resistant Packaging
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately secure these recalled Super Lube products out of children's reach and contact Kano Laboratories for a full refund. Reach them at 800-253-5823 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at info@super-lube.com. More details can be found online at www.super-lube.com/recall.

Images.Caption: Front Labels of Recalled Super Lube Syncopen Synthetic Penetrant
Rear Labels of Recalled Super Lube Syncopen Synthetic Penetrant

Manufacturers.Name: Kano Laboratories LLC, of Bohemia, New York

Retailers.Name: Available at Grainger Parts, E & R Industrial Sales, Home Depot, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com from January 2018 through January 2024.

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging containing ethylene glycol or low-viscosity petroleum distillates.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the products and contact Kano Laboratories for a full refund by providing proof of possession.

metaDescription: Kano Laboratories recalls Super Lube products for poisoning hazard from non-child-resistant packaging containing hazardous chemicals.

Description: Cotton On USA has recalled promotional toy pinwheels because the fastener cap can become loose and detach, creating a choking hazard for young children. The pinwheels are multi-colored and marked with 'KIDS' and 'COTTON ON' in white lettering. One incident involving a child swallowing the fastener cap has been reported.
Title: Cotton On USA Recalls Promotional Toy Pinwheels Due to Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Cotton On USA at their toll-free number 844-319-5970, available from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, inquiries can be sent via email to customerenquiries@cottonon.com or through their website at cottonon.com/US/product-recall.html. More details can also be found by visiting www.cottonon.com/US and clicking on 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pinwheel

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the fastener cap coming loose and being swallowed by a child.

Retailers.Name: Given away as promotional items at Cotton On Kids Stores in Scottsdale, Arizona; Christiana, Delaware; Willowbrook, Texas; Florida Mall, Orlando, Florida; Cool Springs, Tennessee, and Mall of Louisiana, Franklin, Louisiana, from April 2023 through January 2024 for free.

Importers.Name: Cotton On USA Inc., of La Mirada, California

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to loose fastener cap

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the pinwheels and dispose of them. No refunds or replacements will be issued as the items were free.

metaDescription: Cotton On USA recalls toy pinwheels due to choking hazard from loose fastener caps that can detach and be swallowed by children.

Description: Korimefa multi-purpose helmets for teens and adults are recalled for not complying with federal bicycle helmet safety standards. These helmets pose an increased risk of head injury in crashes. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Korimefa Multi-Purpose Helmets Recalled for Non-Compliance with Bicycle Helmet Safety Standards
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Yangxi via email at marketing.us@hotmail.com or visit the Yangxi Storefront on Amazon.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Korimefa Multi-Purpose Helmet
Recalled Korimefa Multi-Purpose Helmet

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from April 2022 to November 2022 for approximately $24.

Importers.Name: Shanghai Yangxi Dian Zi Shang Wu You Xian Gong Si, DBA Yangxi, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with CPSC safety standards for bicycle helmets, increasing the risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the helmets and contact Yangxi for a full refund. To process the refund, destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, take a photo of the destroyed helmet, and email it to marketing.us@hotmail.com. After providing proof of destruction, dispose of the helmet. Yangxi will reach out to all purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Korimefa multi-purpose helmets recalled due to risk of head injury from failure to meet federal bicycle helmet safety standards.

Description: Article has recalled Spin Swivel Chairs because the base can break, creating a fall hazard. The chairs have gold-colored stainless steel swivel legs and come in multiple colors. Although there have been reports of base failures, no injuries have been reported.
Title: Article Recalls Spin Swivel Chairs Due to Fall Hazard from Base Failure
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Article at 888-746-3455 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, and 7 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekends. Alternatively, they can email service@article.com or visit https://www.article.com/c/spin-recall for more details.

Products.Name: Spin Swivel Chairs
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,500 (In addition, about 140 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Spin Swivel Chair in Calcite Ivory
Recalled Spin Swivel Chair in Desert Gray
Recalled Spin Swivel Chair in Sunset Orange
Recalled Spin Swivel Chair in Ink Blue

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 190 reports of the base of the swivel chair breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at article.com from July 2021 to June 2023, priced around $700.

Importers.Name: Trademango Solutions Inc., dba Article, of Canada

Hazards.Name: Risk of base breaking, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the chairs immediately and contact Article for a complimentary replacement base and installation guidance. Article will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

Description: SIORO has expanded its recall of 100% cotton terry cloth children's robes due to violations of federal flammability standards, posing a burn hazard. The recalled robes include multiple colors and sizes, all labeled as 100% cotton and made in China. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: SIORO Children's Cotton Terry Cloth Robes Recalled for Burn Hazard and Flammability Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For further information, email amazonus@sioro.com or visit the SIORO Amazon store at https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=AUPGGE9NR4SBV and select 'Ask a question'.

Products.Name: SIORO Children's Robes
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5,000 (About 950 children's robes were previously recalled in June 2021)

Images.Caption: Recalled Rose Pink Children's Robe
Recalled Light Pink Children's Robe
Recalled Pure White Children's Robe
Recalled Falcon Gray Children's Robe
Recalled Green Mist Children's Robe
Recalled Light Blue Children's Robe
Recalled Navy Children's Robe
Recalled Peacock Blue Children's Robe
Recalled Gray Blue Children's Robe
Recalled Plum Children's Robe

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from November 2021 through July 2023 for between $20 and $27.

Importers.Name: SIORO, of China

Hazards.Name: Violation of flammability standards for children's sleepwear, increasing the risk of burn injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must remove the recalled robes from children, cease using them, and contact SIORO for a full refund. Garments should be destroyed by cutting them in half, and a photo of the destroyed item should be sent to amazonus@sioro.com. Upon receiving the photo, SIORO will issue a refund. Finally, dispose of the destroyed robes in accordance with local recycling regulations.

Description: Dixon Ticonderoga has recalled Creativity Street Foam Pattern Rollers due to lead levels that exceed federal safety standards. Description: Igloo is recalling its 12 oz. Youth Sipper Bottles due to a choking hazard from a detachable silicone cover on the sipper. The bottles, made of stainless steel and marked with the "IGLOO" brand, were sold in multiple color combinations. Description: SKIMS is recalling fleece children's pajama sets that do not comply with federal flammability regulations, posing a burn hazard. The pajamas are two-piece, button-up sets with long sleeves and wide-legged pants, sold in various plaid patterns and sizes 2T to 10. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: SKIMS Recalls Children's Pajama Sets for Burn Hazard and Flammability Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Skims Body at 877-206-1172 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email pajamasets@realtimeresults.net. More information can be found at https://www.recallrtr.com/pajamasets or by visiting www.skims.com and selecting "Product Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: SKIMS Fleece Children's Pajama Sets
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,200 (In addition, 23 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Skims Body Pajama Set - Blue Plaid
Recalled Skims Body Pajama Set – Multi-colored Plaid
Recalled Skims Body Pajama Set – Green and Black Plaid
Recalled Skims Body Pajama Set – Black and Burgundy Plaid

Retailers.Name: Available online at skims.com and in-store at the SKIMS location in Austin, Texas, from November 2023 to January 2024, priced around $54.

Importers.Name: Skims Body Inc., of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet flammability regulations for children's sleepwear, posing burn injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the pajamas immediately, keep them away from children, and contact SKIMS for a full refund. They will need to destroy the garment by cutting it in half and upload a photo of the destroyed items to https://www.recallrtr.com/pajamasets to receive a refund and future purchase credit. SKIMS will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: SKIMS recalls children's pajama sets due to burn hazard from failure to meet flammability standards. No injuries reported in this safety recall.

Description: True Manufacturing is recalling commercial refrigerators with Secop compressors because of a fire hazard caused by potential compressor overheating. The recall affects multiple models and serial numbers used primarily in commercial food service. The company has received reports of fires causing property damage but no injuries. The remedy involves a free repair replacing the original compressor starter with a safer configuration.
Title: True Manufacturing Recalls Commercial Refrigerators with Secop Compressors Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact True Manufacturing toll-free at 855-203-0185 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, or email info@true-secopcompressor-retrofit.com. Visit www.true-secopcompressor-retrofit.com or www.true-secopcompressor-recall.com for further details, or go to www.truemfg.com and select the 'Recall' quick link.

Products.Name: Commercial Refrigerators with Secop Compressors
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 29,200 (In addition, about 50 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled True Commercial Refrigerator with Secop Compressors, model number T-49G
Recalled True Commercial Refrigerator with Secop Compressors, model number GDIM-49NT
Recalled True Commercial Refrigerator with Secop Compressors, model number GDM-72
Recalled True Commercial Refrigerator with Secop Compressors, model number GDM GDM-49
Recalled True Commercial Refrigerator with Secop Compressors, model number TSD-47
SECOP Compressor with model number NLE11MN

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 14 reports of fires resulting in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Secop GmbH, of Germany (compressors
True Manufacturing Co. Inc., of O'Fallon, Missouri (refrigerators)

Retailers.Name: Directly to commercial food service businesses by dealers and distributors from January 2018 through June 2020 for between $3,000 and $8,000.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential compressor overheating

Remedies.Name: Free repair by replacing the original Secop ePTC starter with a CSIR motor configuration

Description: 3M is recalling Peltor X4 series earmuffs because cracks in the earmuff cups can reduce noise protection, leading to overexposure to loud noise. These earmuffs are used in industrial and occupational settings and come in black with green or orange accents. No injuries have been reported related to this issue.
Title: 3M Recalls Peltor X4 Series Earmuffs for Risk of Overexposure to Loud Noise
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact the 3M Technical Service Team at 800-243-4630 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Thursday and from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT on Fridays. Additional information can be found online at https://www.3m.com/X4-Peltor-Earmuff-Recall or by visiting www.peltor.com and selecting 'Peltor X4 Earmuffs Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Description: Crate & Barrel has recalled Hampshire Cribs because of a fall hazard caused by loose mattress support pins. The issue involves the mattress support potentially separating, which can lead to falls. The company has received reports of this defect but no injuries have been reported. The recall covers multiple color options and specific SKU numbers.
Title: Crate & Barrel Recalls Hampshire Cribs for Fall Hazard from Mattress Support Failure
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Crate & Barrel at 800-967-6696, available from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, and from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT on weekends. Additional information can be found at www.crateandbarrel.com/customer-service/product-recalls or www.crateandkids.com under the 'Recalls' section.

Products.Name: Crate & Barrel Hampshire Cribs
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,200 (In addition, about 120 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Crate & Barrel Hampshire Crib - Blush
Recalled Crate & Barrel Hampshire Crib - Gray
Recalled Crate & Barrel Hampshire Crib - White
Recalled Crate & Barrel Hampshire Crib - Olive
Recalled Crate & Barrel Hampshire Crib - Natural Brown
Recalled Crate & Barrel Hampshire Cribs – label on the mattress support board with SKU number

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 12 reports of incidents of the mattress support pins becoming loose. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Crateandbarrel.com from June 2022 to November 2023, priced between $600 and $800.

Importers.Name: Euromarket Designs Inc., d/b/a Crate & Barrel, of Northbrook, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to separation of the hydraulic cylinder rod from the piston.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the cribs immediately and reach out to Crate & Barrel for a free repair or a full refund. The company is also contacting all known purchasers directly.

Description: Chau River Sports Outdoors is recalling small-sized kids' bike helmets that do not meet federal safety standards, posing a risk of head injury. These helmets feature various designs and an adjustable black plastic knob. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Chau River Sports Outdoors Recalls Kids' Bike Helmets for Head Injury Risk and Safety Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Chau River Sports Outdoors via email at lexi_kuyou@163.com or send a message through the "message" icon on their Temu store page.

Recalled kids' bike helmet – orange with a dinosaur print
Recalled kids' bike helmet – blue with a sea world print
Recalled kids' bike helmet – pink with a sea world print

Retailers.Name: Available on Temu.com from June 2023 to January 2024, priced between $16 and $40.

Importers.Name: Chau River Sports Outdoors, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with CPSC safety standards for bicycle helmets, increasing the risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets immediately and contact Chau River Sports Outdoors for a full refund. To process the refund, cut off the straps of the helmet, take photos as proof, and send them via the onsite messaging system on Temu.com, then dispose of the helmet.
