Description: Sant and Abel has recalled two styles of children’s pajama sets made from 100% cotton because they do not meet federal flammability standards for children’s sleepwear. The recalled items include long-sleeve Henley tops and pants in various prints and sizes. This failure creates a burn risk for children wearing the pajamas. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Sant and Abel Recalls Children’s Pajama Sets for Burn Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Sant and Abel at 213-328-2448 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, email returns@santandabel.com, or visit www.santandabel.com/pages/us-product-recall. Alternatively, visit www.santandabel.com and click on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Sant and Abel Children's Cotton Pajama Sets

Images.Caption: Recalled Sant and Abel Children's Henley Two-Piece Pajama Sets (Sky Blue Stripe, Peony Stripe, Red Stripe, Navy Stripe, Wreath, Horse, Christmas, Candy Cane and Daisy)

Recalled Sant and Abel Children's Traditional Collared Two-Piece Pajama Sets (Boys' White Cambric, Girls' White Cambric, Girls' Billie Neon, Gingham Pink, Gingham Green, Gingham Blue, Palm Tree Blue, Palm Tree Pink, Palm Tree Green, Banana Leaf Pink, Banana Leaf Green, Banana Leaf Blue and Houndstooth)

Retailers.Name: Online at santandabel.com, neimanmarcus.com, maisonette.com, SAKS.com and Flip.com from January 2021 through February 2024 for about $59.

Importers.Name: Sant and Abel, of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet flammability standards for children's sleepwear, creating a burn risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the pajamas immediately, remove them from children, and contact Sant and Abel for a full refund. They should destroy the pajamas by cutting them in half and disposing of them according to local recycling laws. A photo of the destroyed item should be sent to returns@santandabel.com to receive the refund. Sant and Abel will reach out to all known purchasers.

en.Title: Sant and Abel Recalls Children’s Pajama Sets for Burn Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violation
en.Description: Sant and Abel has recalled two styles of children’s pajama sets made from 100% cotton because they do not meet federal flammability standards for children’s sleepwear. The recalled items include long-sleeve Henley tops and pants in various prints and sizes. This failure creates a burn risk for children wearing the pajamas. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

Alternatively, visit www.santandabel.com and click on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Description: Full Speed Ahead is recalling Gossamer Pro AGX+ bicycle cranksets, model CK-6037, because the crank arms may loosen or detach, posing a fall hazard. These cranksets were sold individually and on bicycles from several brands. Only cranksets with serial numbers starting with B1, B2, or B3 are affected. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Full Speed Ahead Recalls Gossamer Pro AGX+ Cranksets Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected bicycles immediately and contact Full Speed Ahead at 877-743-3372 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be obtained via email at Recall-USA@fullspeedahead.com or by visiting www.fullspeedahead.com/en/support/recall. For updates, check the "Gossamer Pro AGX+ crankset recall" section on their website.

Products.Name: Gossamer Pro AGX+ Crankset
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,300 (In addition, about 250 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled FSA Gossamer Pro AGX+ Bicycle Crankset
Recalled FSA Gossamer Pro AGX+ Bicycle Crankset

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 277 reports of bicycle crankarms loosening or detaching. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle retailers nationwide and online through brands such as Cannondale, Canyon, Marin, Haro, Fuji, and Pinarello from January 2022 to June 2023, priced around $190 for the crankset and between $1,700 and $4,000 for complete bicycles.

Importers.Name: Full Speed Ahead Inc., of Mukilteo, Washington

Hazards.Name: Risk of crank arms loosening or detaching, leading to falls and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles with the recalled cranksets and contact Full Speed Ahead for free dealer installation of a replacement crankset.

Description: Gasaciods is recalling children's multi-purpose helmets that do not comply with federal safety standards for bicycle helmets. The helmets fail to meet requirements for stability, retention system strength, and impact protection, increasing the risk of head injury. No injuries have been reported in connection with these helmets.

ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled helmets immediately and reach out to Fengwang Sports for a refund. Contact them via email at george66305@163.com or through the message feature on their Temu seller profile.

Description: Delta Cycle and Dimension have recalled specific models of bicycle stem raisers because they can shift during use, causing the handlebars to move unexpectedly. This defect creates a fall hazard for riders. One incident of stem raiser shifting has been reported, but no injuries have been confirmed.
Title: Delta Cycle Recalls Stem Raisers for Bicycles Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Delta Cycle at 800-474-6615 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email stemrecall@designbydelta.com or visit www.designbydelta.com/stemrecall for more details.

Products.NumberOfUnits: About 500,000 (In addition, about 8,510 were sold in Canada)

Importers.Name: Delta Cycle, of Foxborough, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to unexpected handlebar shift

Remedies.Name: Stop using the stem raisers and contact Delta Cycle for a free repair

Description: Johnson Health Tech North America is recalling Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm commercial treadmills because a loose power cord at the power socket can cause sparks, smoke, or fire. The recall includes units produced between March 2018 and January 2024. There have been reports of fires and property damage, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Johnson Health Tech Expands Recall of Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm Commercial Treadmills Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Johnson Health Tech North America at 866-218-3674 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email reworks@johnsonfit.com, or visit https://matrixfitness.com/us/eng/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Matrix T1xe Commercial Treadmill
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 9,700 (About 19,900 were previously recalled in January 2022)

Images.Caption: Recalled Matrix T3xe Treadmill
Recalled Matrix T3xm Treadmill
Recalled Matrix T1xe MAX Treadmill
Location on recalled treadmill of serial number
Location of model name and the serial number on the connecting bar at the bottom of the treadmill

Injuries.Name: Matrix Fitness has received one report of a fire in a fitness facility since the last recall, in addition to the 58 reported in the January 2022 recall, of the treadmill's power cord and socket sparking, smoking, and/or melting, including seven reports of fire; two resulting in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Johnson Health Tech North America and Johnson Fitness & Wellness stores across the country from March 2018 to January 2024, priced between $3,400 and $8,900.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech North America Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wis.

Hazards.Name: Loose power cord from the treadmill's power socket posing a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Inspect treadmills for power cord bracket installation; ensure power cord is securely connected before use. Contact Johnson Health Tech North America for a free power cord bracket installation or to order one for self-installation.

metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech recalls Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm treadmills due to fire hazard from loose power cords posing risk of sparks and fire.

Description: Red Land Cotton is recalling quilts and quilted shams because broken needle pieces were found inside some items, posing a laceration hazard. The company has received reports of consumers being jabbed by sharp metal needles, including one injury that broke the skin. The affected products include various sizes and colors of quilts and shams. Consumers should stop using these items to avoid injury.
Title: Red Land Cotton Recalls Quilts and Shams for Laceration Hazard from Broken Needles
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Red Land Cotton at 877-478-3467 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email customer service at info@redlandcotton.com. More information can be found by visiting www.redlandcotton.com/pages/voluntary-quilt-recall or by checking the "Voluntary Recall" tab on their website.

Products.Name: Red Land Cotton Quilts and Quilted Shams

Images.Caption: Recalled "Bankhead Basic Classic Quilt" white
Recalled "Euro Sham" natural
Recalled "Ticking Stripe Classic Quilt"

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of consumers being jabbed by a sharp metal needle from the product, including one breaking the skin on a consumer's hand.

Manufacturers.Name: Red Land Cotton, Moulton, Alabama

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from October 2018 through December 2023 for approximately $15.

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration from broken needle pieces found in some quilts and quilted shams.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled items and visit www.redlandcotton.com/pages/voluntary-quilt-recall for recall participation instructions. Red Land Cotton is providing either a replacement product of equal value or a refund via store credit or check. Proof of purchase is required, which can include photos of the recalled items, receipts, or other payment documentation. The company is reaching out to all known purchasers of the affected products.

Description: Yoto Ltd. has recalled the Yoto Mini portable speaker for children due to an overheating lithium-ion battery that poses fire and burn hazards. The device is designed for children aged three to twelve and operates using Yoto cards to play audiobooks, music, radio, and podcasts. Several reports of the speaker overheating or melting have been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Yoto Recalls Yoto Mini Speakers for Children Due to Overheating Battery Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yoto Ltd. at 844-370-0426 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, via email at minihelp@yotoplay.com, or visit https://yotoplay.com/recall. You can also go to https://us.yotoplay.com/ and select 'RECALL INFORMATION' at the top for further details.

Products.Name: Yoto Mini Speaker
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 251,165 (In addition, about 18,932 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Yoto Mini
Base of Recalled Yoto Mini

Injuries.Name: Yoto has received six reports from U.S. consumers and one from a UK consumer of the speaker overheating or melting. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Online at us.yotoplay.com, Amazon.com, Target.com and maisonette.com, and in toy and gift stores nationwide from November 2021 through April 2024 for about $70.

Importers.Name: Yoto Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion battery posing burn and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the speakers, keep them away from children, and contact Yoto for a free replacement charging cable. Send a photo of the cut old cable to receive the new one.

Description: Procter & Gamble is recalling specific lots of Tide Pods, Gain Flings, Ace Pods, and Ariel Pods liquid laundry detergent packets because the flexible film bags may open near the zipper track. This defect creates a serious risk of ingestion, which can cause severe injury, especially to children and vulnerable individuals. No confirmed injuries have been directly linked to this packaging issue.
Title: Procter & Gamble Recalls Tide, Gain, Ace, and Ariel Laundry Detergent Packets Due to Packaging Defect
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Procter & Gamble at 833-347-5764 from Monday to Friday, 9 AM ET to 6 PM ET, and Saturday, 9 AM ET to 5:30 PM ET. More information is available online at pg.com/bags.

Products.Name: Tide Pods
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8.2 million (In addition, about 56,741 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Tide Pods
Recalled Gain laundry packets
Recalled Ace Pods
Recalled Ariel laundry packets

Injuries.Name: No confirmed cases directly relating to this packaging defect. The firm has received four reports of children in the United States accessing the liquid laundry packets, three of which reported ingestion during the time period that the recalled lots were sold, but it is not known if these laundry packets came from recalled bags.

Manufacturers.Name: The Procter & Gamble Company, of Cincinnati, Ohio

Retailers.Name: Big Lots

Hazards.Name: The packaging may open near the zipper track, posing a serious risk of ingestion and potential injuries to children and vulnerable individuals. Ingesting large amounts of these products can be fatal, especially for those with pre-existing health conditions.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the recalled bags away from children and contact Procter & Gamble for a full refund and a complimentary child-resistant storage bag. A cabinet lock for securing laundry products is also available. To check if their product is affected, consumers can verify the lot code on the bottom of the package at pg.com/bags. Photos of the recalled product with the lot code can be submitted to participate in the recall.

metaDescription: Procter & Gamble recalls Tide, Gain, Ace, and Ariel laundry detergent packets due to packaging defects posing ingestion hazards to children and vulnerable individuals.

Description: Essential Medical Supply has recalled several models of adult portable bed rails due to entrapment and asphyxiation hazards. The recall includes the Hand Bed Rail models P1410, P1410-P, P1411, and P1411-P, which are made of metal tubing with black padding. Fatalities have been reported involving these products, highlighting the serious safety risk. The recalled rails are identified by the brand name and model number on the grip handle label.
Title: Essential Medical Supply Recalls Adult Portable Bed Rails for Entrapment and Asphyxia Hazards with Additional Fatalities Reported
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Essential Medical Supply at 800-826-8423 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit essentialmedicalsupply.com and select 'Important Safety Notice' at the top of the page. More details can also be found at https://www.essentialmedicalsupply.com/important-safety-information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Essential Medical Supply Endurance® Hand Bed Rail (P1410)
Recalled Essential Medical Supply Endurance® Hand Bed Rail with Pouch (P1410-P)
Recalled Essential Medical Supply Endurance® Hand Bed Rail with Floor Support (P1411)
Recalled Essential Medical Supply Endurance® Hand Bed Rail with Floor Support with Pouch (P1411-P)

Injuries.Name: In December 2022, an 86-year-old man died at an assisted living facility in Connecticut, and in August 2023, a 99-year-old man died at his home in California. CPSC previously announced the recall on December 22, 2021, following the entrapment death of an 86-year-old man in California. He died at his home in December 2012, reportedly from the Endurance Hand Bed Rail (model P1410).

Retailers.Name: Available at medical supply stores across the country and online through www.amazon.com and www.walmart.com. The bed rails were sold from October 2006 to December 2021, priced between $36 and $98.

Importers.Name: Essential Medical Supply Inc., of Orlando, Florida

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risks when users become trapped between the bed rail and mattress.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease use of the recalled bed rails immediately. Those who purchased the affected models after November 1, 2015, will be eligible for a pro-rated refund based on the product's age.

metaDescription: Essential Medical Supply recalls adult portable bed rails due to entrapment and asphyxia hazards linked to multiple fatalities involving these products.

Description: Excalibur Crossbow has recalled the C2 Crank cocking aids because the plastic cocking hooks can break during use, posing impact injury and laceration hazards. Reports include incidents where broken hooks became projectiles, causing injuries such as a permanent eye injury. The product is identified by its black color and the 'Excalibur Crossbow' logo.
Title: Excalibur Crossbow Recalls C2 Crank Cocking Aids Due to Impact and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach Excalibur Crossbow at 888-689-1289, Monday through Thursday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at warranty@excaliburcrossbow.com. More information is available online at www.excaliburcrossbow.com under the 'Recalls' section or directly at https://excaliburcrossbow.com/c2-crank-recall/.

Products.Name: Excalibur C2 Crank Cocking Aid
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,000 (In addition, about 3,920 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Excalibur Crossbow C2 Crank with cocking hooks

Injuries.Name: Excalibur has received 538 reports of the C2 Crank cocking aid hooks breaking during use, resulting in plastic projectiles flying and hitting consumers, including one injury where a consumer reportedly suffered a permanent eye injury.

Manufacturers.Name: Excalibur Crossbow US LLC, of Canada

Retailers.Name: Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's, and other stores nationwide and online at excaliburcrossbow.com, ebay.com and amazon.com from January 2008 through December 2021 for about $190.

Hazards.Name: Risk of impact injury and laceration from breaking cocking hooks

Remedies.Name: Free replacement rope and hooks upon contacting Excalibur Crossbow

metaDescription: Excalibur Crossbow recalls C2 Crank cocking aids due to risk of impact injury and lacerations from breaking hooks. Use caution if you own this product.

Description: Lovey & Grink is recalling various children's sleepwear items, including two-piece pajama sets and nightgowns in multiple prints and sizes. The pajamas fail to meet federal flammability regulations, creating a burn risk. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Lovey & Grink Recalls Children's Pajamas for Flammability Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Lovey & Grink at 877-360-5470, via email at recall@loveyandgrink.com, or visit www.loveyandgrink.com/pages/product-safety. Click on 'Product Safety' for additional details.

Importers.Name: Koala Tree Enterprises, DBA Lovey & Grink, of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Violation of flammability regulations for children's sleepwear, creating a burn risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the pajamas, keep them away from children, and contact Lovey & Grink for a full refund. They will need to destroy the garments by cutting them in half and disposing of them according to local recycling laws, then send a photo of the destroyed item to recall@loveyandgrink.com to receive their refund.

Description: Empower Brands has expanded the recall of BLACK+DECKER Model HGS011 Easy Garment Steamers due to a burn hazard from hot water expulsion, spraying, or leaking during use. The recall includes all units, even those previously repaired or featuring updated designs. Reports include multiple burn injuries, some second-degree. A full refund is now offered for all recalled steamers.
Title: BLACK+DECKER Model HGS011 Garment Steamers Recalled for Burn Hazard by Empower Brands
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Empower Brands at 800-990-5298 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email hgsrecall@brandprotectplus.com, or visit www.prodprotect.com/recall or www.blackanddeckerappliances.com and click on 'Safety Notices' for further details.

Products.Name: BLACK+DECKER HGS011 Easy Garment Steamer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1.6 million (518,500 units were previously recalled in November 2022)

Images.Caption: Recalled BLACK+DECKER HGS011 Easy Garment Steamer
Recalled BLACK+DECKER HGS011F Easy Garment Steamer
Recalled BLACK+DECKER HGS011S Easy Garment Steamer

Injuries.Name: Since the recall was announced in November 2022, Empower Brands has received 317 reports of hot water expelling from the recalled steamers, including 82 reports of burn injuries, seven of which were second-degree burns. 94 of these incident reports involved units that were repaired as part of the original recall or models featuring the updated design, including 19 burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart, Target, Amazon, Bed Bath & Beyond, and various other retailers nationwide, including online at www.walmart.com, www.target.com, www.amazon.com, www.bedbathandbeyond.com, and www.blackanddeckerappliances.com from June 2021 through February 2024, priced between $14 and $23.

Importers.Name: Spectrum Brands Inc., of Middleton, Wis., owns subsidiary Empower Brands LLC

Hazards.Name: Risk of burns due to hot water expulsion, spraying, or leaking during use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled steamers and contact Empower Brands for a full refund. Reports of burn injuries have continued, even for units repaired in the previous recall. The remedy has been updated to a full refund for all units, including those that received a replacement upper assembly.

metaDescription: BLACK+DECKER garment steamers recalled due to burn hazard from hot water expulsion; full refunds offered by Empower Brands for all affected units.

Description: Thule has recalled its RideAlong rear-mounted child bike seats because the harness padding contains excessive levels of the flame-retardant chemical DecaBDE, posing a chemical ingestion hazard. The recall affects model number 100107 with date codes from March 2021 to October 2023. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Thule Recalls RideAlong Rear-Mounted Child Bike Seats for Chemical Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Thule at 888-816-0228, available from 8:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email thuleridealong.americas@thule.com or visit www.thule.com/recallthuleridealongamer for more details. Additional information is accessible on their website under the "Product Support" section by selecting "Product Recall."

en.Title: Thule Recalls RideAlong Rear-Mounted Child Bike Seats for Chemical Hazard
en.Description: Thule has recalled its RideAlong rear-mounted child bike seats because the harness padding contains excessive levels of the flame-retardant chemical DecaBDE, posing a chemical ingestion hazard. The recall affects model number 100107 with date codes from March 2021 to October 2023. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Thule at 888-816-0228, available from 8:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email thuleridealong.americas@thule.com or visit www.thule.com/recallthuleridealongamer for more details. Additional information is accessible on their website under the "Product Support" section by selecting "Product Recall."

Description: Walmart is recalling Mainstays Electric Mini Choppers because the blades can unexpectedly operate during assembly or when not enclosed, posing a laceration hazard. The product features a rechargeable, cordless design with a blade attachment of three curved blades. There have been reports of lacerations, including injuries requiring medical attention.
Title: Walmart Recalls Mainstays Electric Mini Choppers Due to Laceration Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT daily, or visit https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Mainstays Electric Mini Chopper container with blade and lid
Recalled Mainstays Electric Mini Chopper assembled
Recalled Mainstays Electric Mini Chopper assembled

Injuries.Name: Walmart has received five reports of lacerations, two of which required medical attention, including stitches. Injuries occurred during assembly of the product or during cleaning/handling of the product.

Manufacturers.Name: Shuangma Plastic Manufacturing Inc., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart locations across the U.S. and online at Walmart.com from August 2022 to October 2023, priced between $10 and $15.

Importers.Name: Walmart Stores Inc., of Bentonville, Arkansas

Hazards.Name: Unexpected blade operation during assembly or when not enclosed, leading to a laceration risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled choppers immediately and contact Walmart for a full refund. Return the chopper to any Walmart store for a refund.

metaDescription: Walmart recalls Mainstays Electric Mini Choppers for laceration hazard from unexpected blade operation during assembly or handling.

Description: Zazaba International is recalling Grão de Gente baby nests because they pose suffocation, fall, and entrapment hazards. The products do not meet federal safety regulations due to issues like low side height, thick sleeping pads, and openings that could trap or allow infants to fall. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Zazaba International Recalls Grão de Gente Baby Nests for Suffocation, Fall, and Entrapment Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Zazaba International Inc. at 855-425-4640 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email at recall@graodegente.com, or visit https://graodegente.com/pages/baby-nest-recall. Additional information can be found by clicking 'Recall Notices' on their website.

Description: Zazaba International is recalling Grão de Gente padded crib bumpers sold with multi-piece crib bedding sets. These bumpers pose a suffocation hazard if infants roll onto them, blocking airflow. The products violate the federal ban on crib bumpers under the Safe Sleep for Babies Act of 2021. No injuries have been reported.
Title: Zazaba International Recalls Grão de Gente Crib Bumpers for Suffocation Hazard and Federal Ban Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Zazaba International Inc. at 855-425-4640 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@graodegente.com, or visit https://graodegente.com/pages/crib-bumper-recall. Additional information is available by clicking 'Recall Notices' on their website.

Description: Forias has recalled its Pack and Play mattresses due to violations of federal crib mattress safety standards that create a suffocation hazard for infants. These portable memory foam mattresses are marked with Forias branding on the underside. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Forias Pack and Play Mattresses Recalled for Infant Suffocation Hazard and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Forias Direct at customer.care@forias.com or visit https://forias.com/pages/recall-information. You can also find details by clicking 'Recall Information' under 'HELP&SUPPORT' at the bottom of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Forias Waterproof Pack and Play Mattress
Recalled Forias Waterproof Pack and Play Mattress Label
Recalled Forias Waterproof Pack and Play Mattress Warning Label

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from May 2023 through August 2023 for about $36.

Distributors.Name: Forias Direct, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard for infants due to safety standard violations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattress, cut it in half, and send a photo for a refund

Description: Stateside Bead Supply has recalled its 5mm high-powered magnetic beads because they pose a serious ingestion hazard. These small, strong magnets can attract each other or metal objects inside the body, risking severe digestive injuries. The beads are gun metal gray and sold in clear plastic packaging labeled with the company name. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Stateside Bead Supply Recalls 5mm High-Powered Magnetic Beads Due to Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further information, contact Stateside Bead Supply via email at info@statesidebeadsupply.com or call their toll-free number at 888-644-3448, available Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT. More details can also be found at https://www.statesidebeadsupply.com/recall-notice.html or by visiting www.statesidebeadsupply.com and selecting 'Recall Notice.'

Images.Caption: Recalled Magnetic Beads HIGH POWER 5mm AAA Grade
Recalled Magnetic Beads HIGH POWER 5mm AAA Grade (Magnetic Beads are sold on a strand in a plastic bag)

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths since 2005 involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Manufacturers.Name: Boss Magnet, of Taiwan

Retailers.Name: Online at www.statesidebeadsupply.com from November 2022 through June 2023 for between $7 and $9.

Distributors.Name: Stateside Bead Supply Inc., of Issaquah, Washington

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to strong magnets that can cause serious digestive injuries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the beads, keep them away from children, and contact Stateside Bead Supply for a pre-paid return label to receive a full refund.

metaDescription: Stateside Bead Supply recalls 5mm high-powered magnetic beads due to ingestion hazard that can cause serious digestive injuries. No injuries reported.

Description: Crown Boiler is recalling Aruba IV (AWR Series) gas-fired hot water boilers used at altitudes above 2,000 feet due to a risk of carbon monoxide poisoning. The hazard arises if the vent system becomes blocked and the blocked vent switch fails. No injuries have been reported. The recall includes a free repair service to replace the main burner gas orifice and blocked vent switch if needed.
Title: Crown Boiler Recalls Aruba IV Gas-Fired Hot Water Boilers for Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Crown Boiler Company at 888-583-5624 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email AKP@velocityboilerworks.com or visit www.velocityboilerworks.com/news/2024/01/product-modification-required-crown-aruba-iv-models-above-2000-ft-recall/ for further details. Additionally, information is available at www.velocityboilerworks.com under "Product Recall Alerts Important Information" on the homepage.

Products.Name: Crown Aruba IV (AWR Series) Gas-Fired Hot Water Boilers

Images.Caption: Recalled Crown Boiler gas-fired AWR Series
Label location on recalled Crown Boiler gas-fired AWR Series
Model number location on rating plate of recalled Crown Boiler

Manufacturers.Name: Crown Boiler Company LLC, of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

Retailers.Name: Available through heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors nationwide from July 2012 to December 2023, priced between $2,000 and $4,000

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to potential failure of the blocked vent switch

Remedies.Name: Free repair service including replacement of the main burner gas orifice and blocked vent switch if necessary

Description: Culture Kings has recalled two models of Sneaker Basel Magnetic Levitation Displays due to laceration and ingestion hazards from strong magnets. The recalled models include the CXF-22 and CXF-24B, both designed to levitate two sneakers and equipped with magnets, counterweight beads, LED lighting, and a power adaptor. Reports include magnets colliding and shattering during use, causing minor injuries in some cases. No injuries were reported in the U.S. incident.
Title: Culture Kings Recalls Sneaker Basel Magnetic Levitation Displays for Laceration and Ingestion Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Culture Kings at 888-520-1885 from 12 p.m. to 11 p.m. PT Sunday to Tuesday, 12 p.m. to 2 a.m. PT Wednesday to Thursday, and 2 p.m. to 10 p.m. PT on Fridays. They can also email sneakerdisplays@realtimeresults.net or visit www.recallrtr.com/sneakerdisplays and www.culturekings.com, clicking on 'Recall' for further details.

en.Title: Culture Kings Recalls Sneaker Basel Magnetic Levitation Displays for Laceration and Ingestion Hazards
en.Description: Culture Kings has recalled two models of Sneaker Basel Magnetic Levitation Displays due to laceration and ingestion hazards from strong magnets. The recalled models include the CXF-22 and CXF-24B, both designed to levitate two sneakers and equipped with magnets, counterweight beads, LED lighting, and a power adaptor. Reports include magnets colliding and shattering during use, causing minor injuries in some cases. No injuries were reported in the U.S. incident.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Culture Kings at 888-520-1885 from 12 p.m. to 11 p.m. PT Sunday to Tuesday, 12 p.m. to 2 a.m. PT Wednesday to Thursday, and 2 p.m. to 10 p.m. PT on Fridays. They can also email sneakerdisplays@realtimeresults.net or visit www.recallrtr.com/sneakerdisplays and www.culturekings.com, clicking on 'Recall' for further details.

Description: Fisher-Price is recalling the Donald Duck and Daisy Duck figures from the Little People Mickey and Friends pack because their heads can detach, creating a choking hazard. The recall addresses concerns about the detachable heads posing a risk to young children. There have been reports of heads detaching, including one instance where a detached head was found in a child's mouth, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Fisher-Price Recalls Little People Mickey and Friends Donald and Daisy Duck Figures Due to Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Fisher-Price at 855-853-6224 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More details are available online at www.service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx or www.service.mattel.com under 'Recalls & Safety Alerts'. You can also reach them via email at https://consumersupport.mattel.com/mattelsupport/s/create-case.

Products.Name: Fisher-Price Little People Mickey and Friends Figures
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 204,000 (In addition, about 11,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Donald Duck Figure
Recalled Daisy Duck Figure
Recalled Donald Duck and Daisy Duck figures were sold in the Fisher-Price Little People Mickey and Friends figure pack: Model HPJ88/ Model HTW75

Injuries.Name: Fisher-Price has received three reports of the heads detaching from the Donald Duck or Daisy Duck figures, including one report of a detached head in the mouth of a young child. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Walmart, Kohls, Meijer, HEB and Kroger stores nationwide, and online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com and other various eCommerce sites from May 2023 through February 2024 for about $20.

Distributors.Name: Fisher-Price Inc., of East Aurora, New York

Hazards.Name: Detachable heads of the Donald Duck and Daisy Duck figures pose a choking risk for young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately cease use of the Donald Duck and Daisy Duck figures, keep them away from children, and contact Fisher-Price for a pre-paid return label to send back the figures for a $10 refund. This recall specifically pertains to the Donald Duck and Daisy Duck figures from the pack.

metaDescription: Fisher-Price recalls Little People Donald and Daisy Duck figures due to choking hazard from detachable heads posing risk to young children.

Description: Nestlé USA is recalling metallic-coated ceramic mugs included in Starbucks-branded gift sets because they can overheat or break when microwaved or filled with hot liquids. This defect poses risks of burns and lacerations. Multiple incidents have resulted in injuries, including severe burns and cuts.
Title: Nestlé USA Recalls Starbucks Metallic Mugs Due to Burn and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Nestlé USA at 800-681-1676 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.nestleusa.com/media/pressreleases/metallicmugrecall or https://www.athome.starbucks.com and select 'Metallic Mug Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Metallic Mugs from 2023 Holiday Starbucks Gift Sets

Images.Caption: Recalled Starbucks Holiday Gift Set with 2 Mugs (As Sold)
Recalled Starbucks Holiday Gift Set with 2 Mugs (Close-up of Mug)
Recalled Starbucks Classic Hot Cocoa and Mug
Recalled Starbucks Peppermint and Classic Hot Cocoas and Mug
Recalled Starbucks Holiday Blend Coffee and Mug
Location of the gift set identifier code on the bottom of the mug

Injuries.Name: There have been 12 incidents of the mugs overheating or breaking resulting in 10 injuries, including nine severe burns/blisters on fingers or hands, and one cut on a finger. One incident required medical attention.

Retailers.Name: Available online and in-store at Target and Walmart, as well as through Nexcom (military retail outlets) nationwide from November 2023 to January 2024, priced around $10 to $20 depending on the gift set.

Distributors.Name: Nestlé USA Inc., of Arlington, Virginia

Hazards.Name: Risk of burns and lacerations from overheating or breaking

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mugs immediately and return them for a full refund or contact Nestlé USA for a refund via their website.

metaDescription: Nestlé USA recalls Starbucks metallic mugs from gift sets due to burn and laceration hazards caused by overheating or breaking during use.

Description: BRS and BULin liquid fuel bottles have been recalled because their closures are not child-resistant, creating burn and poisoning hazards for children. The bottles, sold individually in 1000mL size, include an orange BRS model with a black cap and a red BULin model with a matching red cap. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Recall of BRS and BULin Liquid Fuel Bottles for Burn and Poisoning Hazards Due to Non-Child-Resistant Closures
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please email squarebird@aliyun.com or visit their Amazon seller profile at https://www.amazon.com/s?me=A1778LF2VCKZ0J&marketplaceID=ATVPDKIKX0DER.

Images.Caption: Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle – front
Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle – back
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle –front
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle – back

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from January 2023 to December 2023, priced between $10 and $13.

Distributors.Name: Tentock, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant closures leading to burn and poisoning risks for children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bottles immediately, keep them out of children's reach, and contact Tentock for disposal instructions to obtain a full refund.

Description: Twenty Four Six Foods recalled all flavors of Happiness USA liquid rolling candy because the rolling ball applicator can dislodge and pose a choking hazard. The product includes blue raspberry, strawberry, and green apple flavors in resealable bottles. One incident involved a child choking on the rolling ball, requiring emergency surgery.
Title: Twenty Four Six Foods Recalls Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy Due to Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the candy immediately, keep it away from children, and reach out to Twenty Four Six Foods for a full refund. For assistance, contact them toll-free at 833-436-1200 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET Monday through Thursday, or via email at recall246foods@gmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy (front)
Recalled Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy (back)
Recalled Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy (all flavors)

Injuries.Name: Twenty Four Six Foods has received one report of the rolling ball dislodging, resulting in a seven-year-old child choking in New Jersey in July 2023. The child underwent emergency surgery to successfully remove the rolling ball from his throat.

Retailers.Name: Kosher market stores nationwide from January 2018 through July 2023 for about $2.

Importers.Name: Twenty Four Six Foods LLC, of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to dislodging rolling ball

Remedies.Name: Stop using the candy and contact for a refund

Description: Best Buy is recalling Insignia Air Fryers and Air Fryer Ovens because of risks related to overheating that can cause melted or broken handles, fire, burns, and lacerations. The recall covers multiple models with cooking capacities from about 3.4 to 10 quarts. There have been reports of overheating, melting, glass shattering, and fires, but no injuries or property damage have been confirmed.
Title: Best Buy Recalls Insignia Air Fryers and Air Fryer Ovens for Fire, Burn, and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled air fryers and air fryer ovens immediately. For further assistance, contact Best Buy at 800-566-7498 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/airfryer to learn how to submit photos of the unit, model number, and purchase receipt for a refund. Additional information can be found at http://www.bestbuy.com/product/recalls.

Products.Name: Insignia Air Fryers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 187,400 (In addition, about 99,900 were sold in Canada)

Product rating label on the underside of the unit showing INSIGNIA brand and the model number

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 24 reports of overheating/melting or glass shattering, including six reports of air fryers catching on fire. No injuries or property damage have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Best Buy stores nationwide and online at www.bestbuy.com, www.eBay.com, and third-party sellers from November 2021 through November 2023 for between $32 and $180.

Importers.Name: Best Buy Purchasing LLC, of Richfield, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to melted or broken handles, fire and burn risks

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled products and follow the refund process outlined on the recall website

Description: Honeywell is recalling L-Series Low Frequency Sounders, Sounder Strobes, and Compact Sounders because they may fail to alert consumers in a fire. The devices have square white and red housings designed for wall mounting. Two reports noted low or no sound output during installation, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Honeywell Recalls L-Series Low Frequency Fire Alarm Sounders and Strobes for Alert Failure Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact System Sensor at 800-736-7672, Monday through Friday from 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, email LFSounder.recall@systemsensor.com, or visit https://buildings.honeywell.com/us/en/brands/our-brands/system-sensor/resources/recall or www.SystemSensor.com and select 'Recall' at the top for further details.

Description: Textron Specialized Vehicles is recalling Tracker Off Road OX EV light utility vehicles due to a fire hazard caused by water entering the lithium-ion battery pack. The affected vehicles have specific serial numbers located beneath the charger receptacle. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Textron Recalls Tracker Off Road OX EV Utility Vehicles for Fire Hazard from Battery Water Exposure
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Textron Specialized Vehicles at 888-438-3946 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email msizemore@textron.com or visit https://www.trackeroffroad.com and navigate to the "Recalls" section or directly access https://www.trackeroffroad.com/recalls.html for further details.

Products.Name: Tracker Off Road OX EV Vehicles

Images.Caption: Recalled Tracker Off Road OX EV

Manufacturers.Name: Textron Specialized Vehicles, of Augusta, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's stores, and Tracker Marine and off-road dealerships nationwide from January 2023 through December 2023 for between $12,000 and $13,000.

Hazards.Name: Potential fire hazard due to water exposure in the lithium-ion battery pack.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the Tracker OX EV immediately and contact an authorized Tracker Off Road dealer or service center to arrange for a complimentary battery module inspection and repair. Until this service is completed, do not charge the vehicle and ensure it is parked outdoors, at least 15 feet away from any structures or other vehicles. Textron Specialized Vehicles will reach out to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Textron recalls Tracker Off Road OX EV vehicles due to fire hazard from water infiltration in lithium-ion battery packs. No injuries reported.

Description: U.S. Boiler Company is recalling certain gas-fired residential boilers installed above 5,400 feet due to a risk of carbon monoxide poisoning. The hazard involves potential failure of the blocked vent switch, which can lead to unsafe gas buildup. Affected models include various units from U.S. Boiler, New Yorker Boiler, Advantage, FORCE, and Archer brands. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: U.S. Boiler Company Recalls Gas-Fired Residential Boilers for Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach U.S. Boiler at 888-432-8887 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at info@usboiler.net. More information is available online at https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/catsy.782/high+altitude+homeowner+recall+letter3.pdf, usboiler.net, archerboiler.com, or newyorkerboiler.com by selecting "Safety Recall Information: High Altitude Gas Boilers" on the homepage.

en.Title: U.S. Boiler Company Recalls Gas-Fired Residential Boilers for Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Hazard
en.Description: U.S. Boiler Company is recalling certain gas-fired residential boilers installed above 5,400 feet due to a risk of carbon monoxide poisoning. The hazard involves potential failure of the blocked vent switch, which can lead to unsafe gas buildup. Affected models include various units from U.S. Boiler, New Yorker Boiler, Advantage, FORCE, and Archer brands. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
en.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach U.S. Boiler at 888-432-8887 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at info@usboiler.net. More information is available online at https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/catsy.782/high+altitude+homeowner+recall+letter3.pdf, usboiler.net, archerboiler.com, or newyorkerboiler.com by selecting "Safety Recall Information: High Altitude Gas Boilers" on the homepage.

Description: Anker has recalled EverFrost coolers because their lithium-ion battery packs can overheat and pose a fire hazard. These coolers are designed for outdoor use and feature wheels, a drop-down handle, and a blue charging port. The company received reports of overheating, smoke, and fire, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Anker EverFrost Lithium-Ion Battery Coolers Recalled for Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled coolers and reach out to Anker at 800-988-7973 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at support@anker.com. More information is available online at https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus, and a LiveChat option can be found at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us. For details on the recall, visit https://www.anker.com/anker-solix/cooler-battery-recall-pps.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the coolers and contact Anker for a free replacement battery pack. Dispose of recalled batteries according to local regulations.

Description: Fossil Group is recalling the braided black bracelet included in Skechers watch and jewelry gift sets because it contains high levels of lead and cadmium. The bracelet has metal clasps at both ends and is part of a set featuring a watch marked with code IF2812307, Skechers branding, and model number SR9082. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Fossil Group Recalls Skechers Gift Set Bracelet for Elevated Lead and Cadmium Levels
ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Fossil Group at 800-842-8621, available from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. CT on weekdays and from 9:30 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT on weekends. Additional information can be obtained via email at info@fgservices.com or by visiting www.fossilgroup.com/recalls/ or www.fossilgroup.com and selecting 'Recalls'.

Products.Name: Braided Black Bracelet from Skechers Gift Sets

Recalled Bracelet
Recalled Bracelet Clasp
Back of Watch in Recalled Skechers Jewelry Gift Set With Model Number and Code

Manufacturers.Name: Fossil Group Inc., of Richardson, Texas

Retailers.Name: Available at Skechers stores across the country and online through Amazon.com, Watchstation.com, and Skechers.com from August 2023 to January 2024, priced around $45.

Importers.Name: Fossil Partners, L.P. of Richardson, Texas

Hazards.Name: High levels of lead and cadmium in the bracelet's clasp pose health risks, particularly to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the bracelet immediately, dispose of it, and reach out to the retailer for a full refund of the gift set.

Description: GT is recalling Model Year 2019 to 2023 LaBomba bicycles, including various models and framesets, due to a risk of weld damage at the headtube and downtube. This defect can cause the headtube to separate from the frame, creating a fall and injury hazard. The company has received reports of weld damage but no injuries have been reported.
Title: GT Recalls LaBomba Bicycles for Headtube/Downtube Weld Failure Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact GT at 800-843-2453 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at ridersupport@gtbicycles.com or visit www.gtbicycles.com/pages/recalls-safety for more details.

Products.Name: GT LaBomba 24" Bicycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,040 (In addition, about 324 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled GT LaBomba 24"
Recalled GT LaBomba Rigid
Recalled GT LaBomba Pro

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of damage to the bicycle headtube/downtube weld, causing the bicycle headtube to separate from the bicycle frame. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle retailers across the country from October 2018 to September 2023, priced between $400 and $1,450.

Importers.Name: GT Bicycles LLC, of Aliso Viejo, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to potential damage at the headtube/downtube weld.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles immediately and contact an authorized GT dealer for a complimentary replacement frame.

metaDescription: GT recalls LaBomba bicycles due to fall hazard from potential weld damage at the headtube and downtube connection.

Description: Jool Baby has recalled its Nova Baby infant swings due to a suffocation risk caused by improper design and failure to meet federal safety standards. The swings include a cloth seat with safety restraints and hanging toys but do not comply with infant sleep safety regulations. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall. Consumers will receive a free repair kit with a new seat, updated warnings, plush toys, and a remote control.
Title: Jool Baby Recalls Nova Baby Infant Swings for Suffocation Hazard and Safety Violations
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled swings for sleep and contact Jool Baby at 888-788-5665 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Thursday, and from 10 a.m. to 1 p.m. ET on Friday. They can also email recall@JoolBaby.com or visit www.JoolBaby.com/recall for further details.

Products.Name: Jool Baby Nova Baby Infant Swings

Images.Caption: Recalled Jool Baby Nova Baby Infant Swing
Model JBP-NOVA-GRY and the manufacture date are printed on the sewn-in warning label on the back of the swing's seat
New seat warning label to be provided as part of the repair kit

Retailers.Name: Walmart stores and online at www.JoolBaby.com, www.amazon.com, www.babylist.com, www.target.com, www.walmart.com and other websites from November 2022 through November 2023 for about $150.

Importers.Name: Jool Products LLC, dba Jool Baby, of Lakewood, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper design for infant sleep and non-compliance with safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive a free repair kit that includes a new seat with updated warnings, new plush toys, and an updated remote control.

Description: Bubble Bear crib mattresses sold exclusively on Amazon by Coral Island are recalled for violating federal fire safety regulations. The recall involves two models with specific designs that do not meet flammability and labeling requirements, posing a fire hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with these mattresses.
Title: Bubble Bear Crib Mattresses Recalled for Fire Hazard Due to Flammability and Labeling Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Shenzhen Coral Island Furniture via email at coralisland2020@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bubble Bear Gray Style Crib Mattress
Sewn-in label showing 4/2022 manufacture date, model CD-03, and batch number 20220428CD
Recalled Bubble Bear Happy Time Crib Mattress
Sewn-in label showing 4/2022 manufacture date, model CD-02, and batch number 20220428CD
Sewn-in Bubble Bear Label on Side of Mattress

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from May 2022 through July 2022 for about $55.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Coral Island Furniture Co, d/b/a Coral Island, of China

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to failure to meet federal flammability and labeling standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled mattresses and email the company for a full refund and disposal instructions. Shenzhen Coral Island Furniture and Amazon will directly contact all buyers. To process the refund, consumers should cut out the top fabric from both sleep surfaces and send photos of each side to coralisland2020@outlook.com.

Description: This recall involves 5mm neodymium rare-earth magnetic balls sold in sets of 226, packaged with storage cases and bags. The magnets are small and powerful, creating a serious ingestion hazard if swallowed. No injuries have been reported in connection with this product. The sets do not comply with federal safety standards for toy magnets.
Title: Recall of 5mm High-Powered Magnetic Ball Sets Sold by Joybuy Due to Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Joybuy Marketplace Express at 302-426-4543 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email oversea-service@jd.com, or visit https://www.walmart.com/seller/16214 for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled 226pcs 5mm (0.2 inch) Magnetic Stones Toy Creativity Development DIY Magnetic Beads

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths since 2005 involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Walmart.com from August 2022 through April 2023 for about $14.

Distributors.Name: Shanghai Beiyong Trading Co., of China, through JD-E Commerce America Limited (dba Joybuy Marketplace Express), of Irvine, California

Hazards.Name: Ingestion risk due to strong magnets that can attract each other or metal objects, leading to serious health issues.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the magnetic ball sets immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Joybuy Marketplace Express for a prepaid return label to obtain a full refund. Joybuy is directly notifying all known buyers.

metaDescription: Recall of 5mm magnetic ball sets due to ingestion hazard from strong neodymium magnets posing serious health risks to children.

Description: Yamaha Corporation of America has recalled PA-10 AC Power Adapters used with certain Yamaha analog mixing consoles and sold separately. The adapters may have a casing defect that can expose electrical wiring, creating a risk of electrical shock and electrocution. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Yamaha PA-10 AC Power Adapters Recalled for Electrical Shock and Electrocution Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the PA-10 AC Power Adapter immediately and reach out to Yamaha for a complimentary replacement. Contact Yamaha at 844-703-5446 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email at pa10recall@yamaha.com, or visit www.yamaha.com/us/support/pa10recall/ for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled PA-10 AC Power Adaptor
Back of the Recalled PA-10 AC Power Adaptor Showing Date Code
A crack can occur between the recalled PA-10 AC Power Adapter's upper and lower cases

Manufacturers.Name: Yamaha Corporation, of Japan

Retailers.Name: Available at Guitar Center, Amazon.com, Sweetwater Sound, Sam Ash, Musicians Friend, B & H Foto & Electronics, American Musical Supply, and other authorized Yamaha retailers across the country; also sold directly by Yamaha online at www.usa.yamaha.com and www.shop.usa.yamaha.com/ from June 2021 through November 2023 for approximately $60.

Importers.Name: Yamaha Corporation of America, of Buena Park, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of electrical shock and electrocution due to potential separation of adapter casing, exposing electrical wiring.

Remedies.Name: Free replacement AC Power Adapter for consumers who purchased the defective adapter or a mixing console with it.

Description: Cannondale is recalling 2021 to 2023 Model Year 26" Dave bicycles and framesets due to a potential defect in the headtube/downtube weld. This weld may fail and cause the headtube to separate from the frame, creating a fall hazard. The company has received reports of weld damage but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Cannondale Recalls 2021-2023 Dave Bicycles for Weld Failure Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Cannondale at 800-245-3872, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, or via email at ridersupport@cannondale.com. More information is available at www.cannondale.com/en/safety-and-recalls or by visiting www.cannondale.com and selecting "Recalls & Notices" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: 2021 to 2023 Cannondale Dave Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 660 (In addition, about 113 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Cannondale 26" Dave bicycle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of damage to the bicycle headtube/downtube weld, causing the bicycle headtube to separate from the bicycle frame. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle stores nationwide and on Cannondale.com from June 2021 to September 2023, priced around $1,300.

Importers.Name: Cycling Sports Group Inc., dba Cannondale, of Wilton, Connecticut

Hazards.Name: Risk of falls and injuries due to potential headtube/downtube weld failure.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles and contact an authorized Cannondale dealer for a free frame replacement.

Description: Oso & Me has recalled children's 100% cotton pajama sets due to failure to meet federal flammability standards, creating a burn hazard. The recalled pajamas feature an animal print design and are sized for children ages 3 to 8 years. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Oso & Me Recalls Children's Pajama Sets for Flammability Hazard and Burn Risk
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Oso & Me at 415-742-5168 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or via email at ProductRecall@osoandme.com. Additional details can be found online at https://www.osoandme.com/pages/product-recall or by visiting www.osoandme.com and clicking on 'Product Recalls' under 'Support'.

Images.Caption: Recalled Oso & Me "Animal Party" Pajama Set

Retailers.Name: Available at various stores nationwide and online through Oso & Me, The Little, Mud Pie, Marigold Modern, Nellie George, Pitt Street Kids, Swaddle AL, and Bea Colette from January 2020 to November 2023 for approximately $98. Some products were also gifted directly by Oso & Me to influencers and friends.

Importers.Name: Oso Children LLC, of San Francisco, California

Hazards.Name: Flammability risk leading to potential burn injuries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pajamas and contact Oso & Me for a full refund. Consumers must destroy the garment and send a photo of the destroyed item to receive their refund.

metaDescription: Oso & Me recalls children's pajama sets due to flammability risk posing a burn hazard. These pajamas do not meet federal safety standards.

Description: Heritage Store Hydrogen Peroxide Mouthwash in Wintermint and Eucalyptus Mint flavors is recalled because its packaging does not meet child-resistant requirements mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The mouthwash contains ethanol at levels that pose a poisoning risk if accessed by children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Heritage Store Hydrogen Peroxide Mouthwash Recalled for Poisoning Risk and Non-Compliant Child-Resistant Packaging
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Nutraceutical at 800-227-6063 from 7 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT Monday through Friday, email info@nutracorp.com, or visit https://heritagestore.com/pages/recall, www.betterbeing.com/recall, or www.Nutraceutical.com and select "Product Recall" at the bottom right for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Heritage Store Hydrogen Peroxide Mouthwash - Wintermint
Recalled Heritage Store Hydrogen Peroxide Mouthwash - Eucalyptus Mint

Manufacturers.Name: Nutraceutical Corporation, of Salt Lake City, Utah

Retailers.Name: Available at Whole Foods, Fresh Thyme Farmers Market, Mom's Organic Market, New Season's Market, Mother's Market & Kitchen, and various other stores nationwide, as well as online at Amazon, iHerb, HeritageStore.com, and additional websites from October 2010 through December 2023 for approximately $10.50.

Hazards.Name: The mouthwash contains ethanol at a concentration that requires child-resistant packaging under the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (PPPA). The current packaging does not meet this requirement, creating a risk of poisoning if ingested by children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately keep the recalled mouthwash out of reach of children and contact Nutraceutical Corporation for a full refund or a replacement product, including shipping, based on availability. Consumers will be instructed to dispose of the product safely. Nutraceutical will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Heritage Store Hydrogen Peroxide Mouthwash recalled due to poisoning hazard from non-compliant child-resistant packaging under federal safety standards.

Description: Liverpool Football Club has recalled 17 styles of children's pajamas that do not comply with federal flammability standards, creating a burn hazard. The recalled items include sleepsuits and pajama sets for children aged 9 months to 12 years. Each garment is marked with a red neck label featuring the size and 'L.F.C.' The recall addresses potential burn risks, and no injuries have been reported.
Title: Liverpool Football Club Recalls Children's Pajamas for Burn Hazard and Flammability Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should visit Liverpool Football Club's recall page at https://store.liverpoolfc.com/us-product-recall or click on 'USA Product Recall' at the bottom of the Liverpool store website under the 'Customer Service' section. Consumers can also request a phone call through the recall page.

Recalled LFC Blippi Long Pajamas

Retailers.Name: Exclusively on https://store.liverpoolfc.com from January 2022 through November 2023 for between $10 and $34.

Importers.Name: Liverpool Football Club, of United Kingdom (UK)

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet flammability standards for children's sleepwear, increasing burn injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must remove the pajamas from children, cease their use, and contact Liverpool Football Club for a full refund. They should destroy the garments by cutting them in half and dispose of them according to local recycling laws. A photo of the destroyed item should be sent to Liverpool Football Club via https://store.liverpoolfc.com/us-product-recall to receive a full refund.

Description: Sandford Family 6-Player Croquet Sets sold by DOM Sports are recalled for containing lead paint and phthalates above federal limits on mallets, hoops, and posts. These hazardous substances pose risks of lead poisoning and other serious health issues if ingested by children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Sandford Family Croquet Sets Recalled for Lead Paint and Phthalate Violations by DOM Sports
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DOM Sports at 877-725-5056 during business hours from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email support@domsports.com or visit www.domsports.com/recalls.html for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sandford Family 6-Player Croquet Set - Big Game Hunters branded
Recalled Sandford Family 6-Player Croquet Set - Garden Games branded

Manufacturers.Name: Garden Games Ltd, of United Kingdom

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from May 2015 until June 2021 for approximately $225.

Importers.Name: DOM Enterprises & Mfg. Ltd, dba DOM Sports, of Canada

Hazards.Name: The red mallet, blue mallet, red hoop, and winning post have paint that exceeds federal lead paint limits, creating a risk of lead poisoning. Additionally, the coatings on the red and blue mallets contain prohibited levels of phthalates, which are harmful if ingested by young children and can lead to serious health issues.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled croquet sets immediately and contact DOM Sports for instructions on sending a photo of the disposed product to support@domsports.com to obtain free replacement parts. DOM Sports and Amazon are reaching out to all known buyers.

metaDescription: Sandford Family Croquet Sets recalled due to lead paint and phthalate hazards posing poisoning risks to children. No injuries reported.

Description: Vernier Science Education has recalled the Go Direct Charge Station due to an overheating issue that poses a burn hazard. The device charges multiple Go Direct wireless sensors used in educational science experiments. Two reports of overheating were received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Vernier Science Education Recalls Go Direct Charge Stations for Overheating Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are advised to stop using the Vernier Go Direct Charge Station immediately. For further assistance, contact Vernier Science Education at 888-837-6437, available from 6:45 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Thursday and 6:45 a.m. to 3 p.m. PT on Friday. You can also email support@vernier.com or visit www.vernier.com/gdx-crg-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vernier Go Direct Charge Station
Label located at the bottom of the charge ctation

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the chargers overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Vernier.com, Fishersci.com, Flinnsci.com, and Us.vwr.com from November 2017 to December 2023, priced between $90 and $930 based on individual or Teacher Pack purchases.

Importers.Name: Vernier Science Education, of Beaverton, Oregon

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to burn risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the charge station and contact Vernier for return instructions and free repair.

Description: Cascade Designs is recalling specific MSR camping cooking pots because the pot handle can detach or loosen during use, posing burn and scald hazards. The recall covers anodized aluminum pots in 1- to 2-liter sizes, including models sold individually and in Reactor, Trail Lite, and PocketRocket Stove Kits. There have been reports of handle detachment and burn injuries associated with these pots.
Title: Cascade Designs Recalls MSR Camping Cooking Pots Due to Handle Detachment Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Cascade Designs Inc. at 800-531-9531, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at https://www.msrgear.com/pot-recall.html or by visiting https://www.msrgear.com and clicking on the 'Recall Header' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: MSR Camping Cooking Pots
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 60,655 (In addition, about 22,320 were sold in Canada and about 35 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled MSR Reactor Accessory cooking pot (1 liter)
Recalled MSR Trail Lite pot (2 liter)
Recalled MSR Reactor Stove System (1.7 liter)
Recalled MSR PocketRocket Stove Kit

Injuries.Name: The company has received 22 reports of the pot handle either detaching or becoming loose, including two reports of burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Outdoor equipment stores nationwide from January 2008 through October 2023 for between $40 and $290.

Importers.Name: Cascade Designs Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Distributors.Name: Cascade Designs Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Pot handle may detach or loosen, leading to burn and scald hazards from hot food and liquids.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled cooking pot and contact Cascade Designs for instructions on submitting a photo to obtain a free replacement pot handle/lifter, which is easy to install without tools.

Description: Yalande-US has recalled two styles of JURLEA large area rugs sold exclusively on Amazon because they do not comply with federal flammability standards. The rugs, available in neutral and multicolor designs and multiple sizes, pose a fire hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: JURLEA Rugs Recalled by Yalande-US for Fire Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please reach out to Yalande-US via email at yalande_us2021@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled JURLEA Rugs in Light Gray/Ivory
Recalled JURLEA Rugs in Gray Beige/Light Yellow

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Amazon.com from April 2023 through December 2023 for between $60 and $130.

Importers.Name: Kaka Trading Co. LTD, of China

Distributors.Name: Yalande-US, of China

Hazards.Name: Violation of federal flammability regulations for carpets, posing a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the rugs immediately and contact Yalande-US for a full refund. They will need to destroy the product by cutting it in half and send a photo of the destroyed item to yalande_us2021@163.com to receive their refund. Yalande-US is also reaching out to all known customers directly.

metaDescription: JURLEA rugs sold by Yalande-US are recalled for fire hazard risk due to failure to meet federal flammability regulations for carpets.

Description: IKEA is recalling the ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Chargers because of burn and electric shock hazards linked to potential cable damage. The chargers have a detachable power cord with a C7 connector and a plug. No injuries have been reported in the U.S., though incidents have occurred internationally.
Title: IKEA Recalls ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Chargers for Burn and Electric Shock Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact IKEA toll-free at 888-966-4532 from 9 a.m. to 10 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.ikea.com/us/en/customer-service/product-support/recalls/ for further details.

Products.Name: ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Charger
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 22,600 (In addition, about 10,250 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Charger
The product can be identified by the model number ICPSW5-40-1, which can be found on the label on the back side of the USB charger

Injuries.Name: IKEA has received 17 incident reports globally, including five reports of burns and four reports of electric shock. No incidents or injuries have been reported in the U.S.

Retailers.Name: Available at IKEA stores nationwide and online at IKEA.com from April 2020 through December 2023 for approximately $30.

Importers.Name: IKEA Supply AG, of Switzerland

Distributors.Name: IKEA US Retail LLC, of Virginia

Hazards.Name: Burn and electric shock risks due to potential cable damage

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the chargers and return them to any IKEA store for a full refund. If returning in-store is not possible, contact IKEA for disposal instructions to receive a full refund. No proof of purchase is required.

metaDescription: IKEA recalls ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Chargers due to burn and electric shock hazards from potential cable damage. Stop use and return for a refund.

Description: Magik & Kover has recalled pack and play mattresses that do not meet federal crib mattress safety regulations, posing a suffocation hazard to infants. These mattresses have a waterproof cover and specific internal tags including "CertiPUR-US" and batch number 221002US. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Magik & Kover Pack and Play Mattresses Recalled for Infant Suffocation Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, please reach out to Magik & Kover via email at bruandoli@126.com.

Products.Name: Magik & Kover Pack and Play Mattresses

Images.Caption: Recalled Magik & Kover Waterproof Pack and Play Mattress
CertiPUR-US is printed on a tag inside the cover
Batch number 221002US and the manufacture date on the back of that tag
STANDARD 100 is printed on another tag inside the mattress cover

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from December 2022 through August 2023 for about $40.

Distributors.Name: Magik & Kover, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to non-compliance with federal safety standards for crib mattresses.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattresses, destroy them, and contact Magik & Kover for a full refund and disposal instructions.

Description: Worthington Enterprises has recalled EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinders with model number EVAS - M0859 and specific serial and date codes. These cylinders pose a fire hazard due to potential gas leaks. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Worthington Enterprises Recalls EVAS 20-lb. Propane Exchange Cylinders Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Worthington at 888-520-1304, Monday to Friday from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, email at propanecylinders@realtimeresults.net, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/PropaneCylinders.

Images.Caption: Recalled EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinder
Location of model number of recalled EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinder
Scan QR Code for propane exchange retail locations

Retailers.Name: AmeriGas propane cylinder exchange suppliers in New Hampshire and the Boston Metropolitan area from July 2023 to August 2023, or from other propane exchange suppliers thereafter.

Importers.Name: Worthington Enterprises, of Columbus, Ohio

Distributors.Name: AmeriGas, of King of Prussia, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Potential gas leaks leading to fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled cylinders immediately, close the top valve, and return them to any AmeriGas Propane Exchange location for a free replacement.

Description: Spring Spirit and Biloban pack and play mattresses have been recalled because they pose a suffocation hazard to infants. Both products fail to meet federal safety regulations for crib mattresses and lack required warnings and labels. No injuries have been reported in connection with these mattresses.
Title: Recall of Spring Spirit and Biloban Pack and Play Mattresses for Infant Suffocation Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further details, please reach out via email at recall@biloban.com.

Recalled Biloban Pack and Play Mattress - top
Recalled Biloban Pack and Play Mattress - underside
Biloban is printed on the mattress hangtag, and batch number 2304US and the manufacture date in the format "PD DD.MM/YYY" are printed on a tag stapled to the mattress cover

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from August 2022 through August 2023 for about $40.

Distributors.Name: Hangzhou Sharejoy Ecommerce Co. Ltd, dba Biloban, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to violations of federal safety standards for crib mattresses

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattresses immediately, destroy them, and contact Biloban for a full refund and disposal instructions

Description: Dynabook Americas is recalling approximately 15.5 million Toshiba laptop AC adapters because they can overheat and spark, creating fire and burn hazards. The recall follows reports of adapters melting, catching fire, and causing minor burn injuries. These adapters were sold with Toshiba laptops and separately between 2008 and 2014.
Title: Dynabook Recalls 15.5 Million Toshiba Laptop AC Adapters for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Dynabook at 800-457-7777 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email dba-acadapter2024@dynabook.com or visit https://support.dynabook.com/recall2024ax for more details.

Products.Name: Toshiba Laptop AC Adapters
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15.5 million (In addition, about 1.3 million were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Toshiba AC Adapter with model name/number and serial number location
Recalled Toshiba AC Adapter with model name/number and serial number location
Recalled Toshiba AC Adapter with model name/number and serial number location
Recalled Toshiba AC Adapter with model name/number for PA-1750-04 and serial number location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 679 reports of the recalled AC adapters overheating or catching on fire, melting, and burning. This includes 43 reports of minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at various retailers across the country and on Toshiba.com from April 2008 through April 2014, with prices ranging from $25 to $75.

Importers.Name: Dynabook Americas Inc., of Irvine, California (importer at the time of sale was Toshiba America Information Systems, Inc.)

Hazards.Name: Overheating and sparking, leading to burn and fire hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled adapters and reach out to Dynabook for a complimentary replacement. To verify if their adapter is included in the recall, they can visit https://support.dynabook.com/axx2024. A photo of the adapter with the power cord cut must be submitted to dba-acadapter2024@dynabook.com to receive the replacement.

metaDescription: Dynabook recalls 15.5 million Toshiba laptop AC adapters due to overheating and fire hazards posing burn risks to users.
