Description: Bell Sports is recalling Bell Soquel Youth bicycle helmets due to a risk of head injury caused by potential dislodging of the strap anchor. The helmets do not meet federal safety regulations for bicycle helmets. No injuries have been reported. Consumers should stop using the affected helmets immediately.
Title: Bell Sports Recalls Bell Soquel Youth Bicycle Helmets for Head Injury Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bell Helmets at 800-456-2355 (Option 3) from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com, or visit www.thebellgarage.com/bell-sports-inc-is-voluntarily-recalling-the-bell-youth-soquel-helmet for more details.

Products.Name: Bell Soquel Youth Helmet
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,425 (In addition, about 2,400 were sold in Canada)

Importers.Name: Bell Sports Inc, of Irvine, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of head injury due to potential dislodging of the strap anchor, violating CPSC safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets and contact Bell Sports for a refund after destroying the helmet.

Description: Char-Broil has recalled multiple models of its digital electric smokers because of a risk of electric shock caused by potential electrical current leakage. The recalled products include several versions of the Digital Electric Smoker, identifiable by the Char-Broil logo and model information on the back. There have been reports of minor electric shocks, including one case resulting in a finger blister. The recall addresses this safety hazard to prevent further incidents.
Title: Char-Broil Digital Electric Smokers Recalled for Electric Shock Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Char-Broil at 833-206-4647 from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Saturday, or email recall@charbroil.com. More information is available on their website at www.charbroil.com under the 'Product Recalls' section or directly at www.charbroil.com/recalls.

Products.Name: Digital Electric Smoker
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 211,700 (In addition, 3,440 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Char-Broil Digital Electric Smoker
Recalled Char-Broil Digital Electric Smoker w/Window
Recalled Char-Broil Digital Electric Smoker, Black w/Window
Recalled Char-Broil Digital Electric Smoker, XL
Recalled Char-Broil Digital Electric Smoker, Medallion

Injuries.Name: Char-Broil received 79 reports of consumers who experienced a minor electric shock from touching the smokers while in use, including one report of a consumer developing a finger blister after the shock.

Retailers.Name: Lowe's

Importers.Name: Char-Broil LLC, of Columbus, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of electric shock due to potential electrical current leakage

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled smokers and reach out to Char-Broil for a complimentary repair kit, which includes a Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter (GFCI) plug, a clamshell enclosure, and a cable clamp, along with installation instructions.

metaDescription: Char-Broil recalls digital electric smokers due to risk of electric shock from electrical current leakage. Affected models require repair to ensure safety.

Description: Electrolux Group is recalling certain Frigidaire rear-controlled ranges manufactured in late 2023 due to a risk of electric shock and electrocution. The hazard arises from a potential detachment of the electronic control panel. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.

Description: WAOLi is recalling BRS and BULin liquid fuel bottles because they do not meet child-resistant closure requirements, increasing risks of poisoning and burns. The 530mL BRS bottle also lacks a flame mitigation device, creating a flash fire hazard. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: WAOLi Recalls BRS and BULin Liquid Fuel Bottles for Poisoning and Fire Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, email lyuxia3k@163.com or visit WAOLi's Amazon seller profile at Amazon.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle - front
Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle - back
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle - front
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle - back

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from February 2023 through November 2023, priced between $12 and $40.

Distributors.Name: WAOLi, of China

Hazards.Name: The fuel bottles do not comply with child-resistant closure requirements under the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Act, increasing the risk of burns and poisoning in children. Additionally, the 530mL BRS bottle lacks a flame mitigation device, which poses a flash fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled bottles immediately and keep them out of children's reach. To obtain a full refund, WAOLi requests that consumers initial and date the bottles, then submit a photo of them in the trash along with their refund request.

metaDescription: WAOLi recalls BRS and BULin liquid fuel bottles due to poisoning and fire hazards from non-compliant child-resistant closures and missing flame mitigation devices.

Description: CableMod is recalling its 12VHPWR Angled Adapters, models V1.0 and V1.1, because the male connector can loosen, overheat, and melt into the GPU, posing fire and burn hazards. These adapters connect to graphics card power ports at 90-degree or 180-degree angles and come in various colors and designs. The company has received reports of overheating and melting causing property damage, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: CableMod GPU Angled Adapters Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach CableMod at 888-203-3605 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email support@cablemod.com. More information is available online at https://cablemod.com/adapterrecall/ or by visiting https://cablemod.com and clicking on the 'Voluntary Safety Recall' banner.

Images.Caption: Recalled 90-degree V1.0 Angled Adapter - Variant A
Recalled 90-degree V1.0 Angled Adapter - Variant B
Recalled 180-degree V1.0 Angled Adapter - Variant A
Recalled 180-degree V1.0 Angled Adapter - Variant B
Recalled 90-degree V1.1 Angled Adapter - Variant A
Recalled 90-degree V1.1 Angled Adapter - Variant B
Recalled 180-degree V1.1 Angled Adapter - Variant A
Recalled 180-degree V1.1 Angled Adapter - Variant B

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 272 reports of the adapters becoming loose, overheating and melting into the GPU, with at least $74,500 in property damage claims in the United States. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: CableMod Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at small independent stores nationwide and online at vprbrands.com from September 2022 to November 2023 for approximately $18.

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burns due to overheating connectors

Remedies.Name: Stop using the adapters, contact CableMod for safe removal instructions, and receive a full refund or $60 store credit.

Description: Electrolux Group is recalling certain Frigidaire side-by-side refrigerators with Slim Ice buckets because broken plastic parts can cause choking and laceration hazards. The company has received reports of plastic pieces breaking off, including incidents of laceration and ingestion injuries. The recall affects multiple models produced between 2015 and 2019. A free replacement ice bucket assembly component is available to address the hazard.
Title: Electrolux Recalls Frigidaire Side-by-Side Refrigerators with Slim Ice Buckets Due to Choking and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Electrolux Group at 888-377-7563 from 8:30 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email IceBucketRecall@electrolux.com or visit www.icebucketrecall.com or www.frigidaire.com and click on 'Recall Information' for further details.

Products.Name: Frigidaire Side by Side Refrigerators with Slim Ice Buckets

Images.Caption: Recalled Production Year 2015-2019 Frigidaire Branded Side by Side Refrigerators with Slim Ice Buckets
Recalled Production Year 2015-2019 Frigidaire Branded Side by Side Refrigerators with Slim Ice Buckets

Injuries.Name: Electrolux Group has received 343 reports of plastic pieces breaking off, including two incidents resulting in laceration and ingestion injuries when the pieces were dispensed out of the recalled ice bucket.

Retailers.Name: Lowe's, Home Depot and other stores nationwide and online at Frigidaire.com from November 2015 through September 2019 for between $1,400 and $2,400.

Importers.Name: Electrolux Home Products Inc., of Charlotte, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Choking and laceration hazards due to broken ice bucket assembly components

Remedies.Name: Request a free replacement ice bucket assembly component

Description: Vornado has recalled handheld garment steamers from Steamfast, Vornado, and Sharper Image due to a risk of serious burns. The steamers can expel hot water from the nozzle during use, posing a burn hazard. There have been multiple reports of hot water spraying incidents, including burn injuries. The affected models include Steamfast SF-425, SF-435, SF-440, SF-445, SF-447, Vornado VS-410, and Sharper Image SI-428.
Title: Vornado Recalls Handheld Garment Steamers from Steamfast, Vornado, and Sharper Image Due to Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Vornado at 888-240-2768 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.vornado.com/recalls/hhgs. Additional information is available at www.vornado.com, www.steamfast.com, or www.sihomecomfort.com under 'Product Recalls.'

Products.Name: Steamfast Handheld Garment Steamer SF-425
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2 million (In addition, about 13,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-425
Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-435 (multiple colors)
Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-440
Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-445
Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-447

Injuries.Name: Vornado has received 122 reports of hot water spraying or spitting from the steam nozzle, including 23 reports of burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart, Bed Bath & Beyond, and various retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, Vornado.com, sihomecomfort.com, and Steamfast.com from July 2009 to January 2024, priced between $14 and $35.

Importers.Name: Vornado Air LLC, of Andover, Kansas

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious burns due to hot water expulsion from the steam nozzle during use.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the steamers immediately and contact Vornado for a full refund or a free replacement, based on the model.

Description: Box Components is recalling BMX Race Box One Oversized 31.8 Delta bicycle stems because the faceplate can crack, causing the handlebars to loosen. This defect creates a fall hazard by increasing the risk of loss of control while riding. The company has received one report of a cracked faceplate but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Box Components Recalls BMX Race Bicycle Stems Due to Handlebar Loosening Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Box Components at 800-797-6880 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Thursday. You can also email orders@boxcomponents.com or visit https://boxcomponents.com/pages/recall-claim-form-delta-stem to file a recall claim. For additional details, check https://boxcomponents.com/pages/recall-bulletin or go to https://boxcomponents.com and select 'Recalls and Bulletins' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: BMX Race Box One Oversized Bicycle Stems

Images.Caption: Recalled Box One Oversized 31.8 Delta Stem

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a stem faceplate's cracking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through BMX Race dealers across the country and online at https://boxcomponents.com from January 2023 to October 2023, priced around $100.

Distributors.Name: The Cycle Group, Dba Box Components, from Anaheim, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of handlebars loosening due to cracking faceplate, leading to potential loss of control and injury.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the affected stems and contact Box Components for a full refund or a complimentary replacement, including shipping.

Description: Ekouaer is recalling girl's nightgowns that do not comply with federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear, posing a burn hazard. The nightgowns are made of 95% cotton and 5% spandex with a screen print on the chest. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Ekouaer Girl's Nightgowns Recalled for Burn Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ekouaer at 800-608-1432 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, excluding holidays. You can also email walshkelly15@gmail.com or visit https://ekouaer.com/pages/us-recall-nightgown-2023 for more details. Alternatively, go to https://ekouaer.com/ and click on 'Product Recall Information' at the top of the page.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Weite Information Technology Co., Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to non-compliance with federal flammability regulations for children's sleepwear.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the nightgowns immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Ekouaer for a full refund. Description: iFIT is recalling the NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Voice-Controlled Adjustable Dumbbells because the weight plates can detach during use, posing an impact hazard. The dumbbells adjust from 5 to 50 pounds using a motorized selector with voice control or manual knob adjustment. Bluetooth connectivity allows syncing with the iFIT app for workout tracking. There have been reports of injuries including contusions and abrasions caused by dislodged weight plates.
Title: iFIT Recalls NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Voice-Controlled Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Impact Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. MT Monday through Friday, email iselect-recall@ifit.com, or visit www.nordictrack.com/product-recalls or www.nordictrack.com and select 'Voluntary Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,400 (In addition, about 650 sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled iFIT NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Voice-Controlled Adjustable Dumbbells

Injuries.Name: There were 40 reports of weight plates dislodging during use with eight resulting in injuries such as contusions and abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available at NordicTrack.com, Amazon.com, and DicksSportingGoods.com from January 2022 through November 2023, priced between $500 and $600.

Importers.Name: iFIT Inc., f/k/a Icon Health & Fitness, of Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging during use

Remedies.Name: Stop using the dumbbells and contact iFIT for a full refund. iFIT will reach out to all known purchasers.

metaDescription: iFIT recalls NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells due to risk of weight plates dislodging and causing impact injuries.

Description: Lovevery has recalled certain Slide & Seek Ball Runs included in Babbler Play Kits for children 13 to 15 months old. The recall involves wooden knobs on the toy doors that can detach, posing a choking hazard. There have been reports of knobs detaching during and after shipping, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Lovevery Recalls Slide & Seek Ball Runs with Wooden Knobs Due to Choking Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should visit Lovevery's recall page at www.recallrtr.com/ballrun or www.lovevery.com/pages/product-recalls. For assistance, call 888-520-0167 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email ballrun@lovevery.com. More details can be found under the 'Product Recalls' section on Lovevery's website.

Products.Name: Slide & Seek Ball Runs
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 47,000 (In addition, about 4,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Slide & Seek Ball Run
Recalled Slide & Seek Ball Run with The Babbler Play Kit packaging

Injuries.Name: Lovevery has received 33 reports of the wooden knob detaching during shipping and six reports of the wooden knob detaching after shipment. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Online at Lovevery.com as part of the Babbler Play Kit from June 2023 through November 2023 for about $120 for the kit.

Importers.Name: Lovevery Inc., of Boise, Idaho

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to potential detachment of the wooden knob

Remedies.Name: Free replacement after registration and proper disposal of the affected door

metaDescription: Lovevery recalls Slide & Seek Ball Runs with wooden knobs due to choking hazard from potential detachment. No injuries reported.

Description: Stripe & Stare is recalling children’s nightgowns made of 95% modal and 5% spandex because they do not meet federal flammability regulations. The nightgowns pose a risk of burn injuries due to their flammability. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Stripe & Stare Recalls Children’s Nightgowns for Fire and Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Stripe and Stare via email at hellousa@stripeandstare.com or visit https://us.stripeandstare.com/pages/childrens-nightgown-recall. You can also go to https://us.stripeandstare.com/ and select "Product Recall" at the top of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Stripe and Stare Children's Nightgown in Morning Bliss Pink

Retailers.Name: Available online at https://us.stripeandstare.com and https://www.loveshackfancy.com from February 2021 to January 2023, priced at approximately $80.

Importers.Name: Stripe and Stare, of United Kingdom

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn injuries due to violation of federal flammability regulations for children's sleepwear.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the nightgowns, keep them away from children, and contact Stripe and Stare for a full refund. They should cut the garments in half and email a photo of the destroyed product to hellousa@stripeandstare.com. A full refund will be issued upon receipt of the photo.

metaDescription: Stripe & Stare recalls children’s nightgowns due to fire hazard from non-compliance with federal flammability standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Snap has recalled the rechargeable lithium-ion battery used in Pixy Flying Cameras because it can overheat and pose a fire hazard. The battery may bulge and has caused one minor fire and one minor injury. This recall addresses the risk of overheating that could lead to fire.
Title: Snap Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery for Pixy Flying Cameras Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Snap's recall hotline at 800-269-6990, available 24/7, or email support@pixy.com. Additional information can be found online at https://support.pixy.com/recall or by visiting www.pixy.com and clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page. Snap is also directly contacting all known purchasers.

Images.Caption: Recalled battery for Pixy Flying Camera
Pixy Flying Camera with recalled battery on charger

Injuries.Name: Snap has received four reports of the battery overheating and bulging, resulting in one minor battery fire and one minor injury.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Pixy.com and Amazon.com from May 2022 to December 2023, priced between $185 and $250 for the Pixy Flying Camera or 'Flight Pack' bundle, $40 to $50 for the additional charger/battery, and $16 to $20 for the separately sold battery.

Importers.Name: Snap LLC., of Santa Monica, California

Hazards.Name: Overheating battery posing a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Stop using the Pixy Flying Camera, remove and cease charging the battery. Visit www.pixy.com for recall participation details, refund instructions, and proper disposal guidelines.

metaDescription: Snap recalls the lithium-ion battery for Pixy Flying Cameras due to a fire hazard from overheating. Users should stop use immediately for safety.

Description: SRAM is recalling all 12-speed shift brake levers sold as standalone upgrade or replacement kits. The clamp bolt on these brake levers can loosen, creating a crash hazard by compromising brake control. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: SRAM Recalls 12-Speed Shift Brake Levers Due to Crash Hazard from Loose Clamp Bolt
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact SRAM at 800-346-2928, available from 11 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional details can be found at https://www.sram.com/en/service/recalls by selecting 'Recall Notice,' or by visiting www.sram.com and navigating to 'Recalls' under 'Service/Support.'

Remedies.Name: Free brake installation inspection and tightening or replacement of clamp bolt assembly if necessary

Description: Clark Associates has recalled Allegro plastic side chairs sold under Lancaster Table & Seating due to a risk of the chairs breaking apart during use, posing a fall hazard. The recall follows reports of chairs failing and causing injuries. Consumers should stop using the chairs immediately to prevent potential falls.
Title: Clark Associates Recalls Allegro Plastic Side Chairs for Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Clark Associates at 844-895-5694 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, you can email allegro@lancastertableandseating.com or visit lancastertableandseating.com/recalls/allegro-side-chair-recall/ for more details. Additional information is available at www.therestaurantstore.com and www.webstaurantstore.com under the 'Safety Recall' section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Lancaster Table & Seating Allegro plastic side chair
Recalled Lancaster Table & Seating Allegro plastic side chair stamp on underside of plastic side chair

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 16 reports of chairs breaking apart while in use, resulting in two injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available through Clark Pro and Clark National Accounts across the country, as well as online at www.webstaurantstore.com and www.therestaurantstore.com from November 2020 to August 2023, priced between $45 and $66.

Importers.Name: Clark Core Services LLC, d/b/a Clark Associates, of Lancaster, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Risk of breaking apart during use, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the Allegro plastic side chairs immediately and contact Clark Associates for a full refund or store credit. A photograph must be submitted online showing 'DO NOT USE' clearly marked on the chairs, and the product should be disposed of. Clark Associates will directly notify all known purchasers.

metaDescription: Clark Associates recalls Allegro plastic side chairs due to risk of breaking and fall hazard. Immediate discontinuation of use is advised for safety.

Description: Pacific Cycle is recalling Ascend Cabrillo and Minaret electric bikes because of a fire hazard caused by improperly assembled wiring harnesses during charging. The recall includes specific models identified by model numbers and distinct color options. There have been reports of battery fires, including one injury involving second-degree burns.
Title: Pacific Cycle Recalls Ascend Cabrillo and Minaret E-Bikes Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Pacific Cycle at 877-564-2261 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at customerservice@pacificycle.com. More information is available online at www.pacific-cycle.com/safety-notices-recalls or by visiting www.pacific-cycle.com and clicking on 'Safety Notices & Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Recalled Ascend Minaret Electric Bike (black)
Recalled Ascend Cabrillo Electric Bike (serial number location)
Recalled Ascend Minaret or Cabrillo Electric Bike service label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the battery catching on fire, resulting in one injury of second-degree burns.

Retailers.Name: Bass Pro Shops nationwide

Importers.Name: Pacific Cycle Inc., of Madison, Wisconsin

Distributors.Name: Bass Pro LLC, of Springfield, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Improperly assembled wiring harness posing a fire risk during charging

Remedies.Name: Stop using and unplug the recalled electric bikes; confirm recall status online and return for a full refund

Description: BRS Fuel Bottles in 530mL, 750mL, and 1000mL sizes are recalled because they do not have child-resistant closures. This non-compliance creates risks of burns and poisoning for children, violating the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Safety Act. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: BRS Fuel Bottles Recalled for Burn and Poisoning Hazards Due to Missing Child-Resistant Closure
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to OAREA Outdoor Gear via email at kim_ye92@yahoo.com or visit their Amazon seller page at www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=A34L6EV18KW96Q and select "Ask a Question" at the top.

Images.Caption: Recalled BRS Liquid Fuel Bottle

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from December 2020 to October 2023, priced approximately at $12, $15, and $17 for the 530mL, 750mL, and 1000mL sizes, respectively.

Distributors.Name: OAREA Outdoor Gear, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with child-resistant closure requirements, leading to risks of burns and poisoning for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled bottles, keep them out of children's reach, and contact OAREA Outdoor Gear for instructions on returning or disposing of the product for a full refund.

metaDescription: BRS Fuel Bottles recalled for burn and poisoning hazards due to lack of child-resistant closure, violating safety standards designed to protect children.

Description: Visual Comfort & Co. is recalling Maverick Coastal 60-inch outdoor ceiling fans because the fan blades can crack or break, creating an impact injury hazard. The fans have three blades and come in white or black finishes. The model number 3MGMR60XXX is located on a label on the fan motor. Although there have been reports of blade failures, no injuries or property damage have been reported.
Title: Visual Comfort Recalls Maverick Coastal 60-Inch Outdoor Ceiling Fans Due to Impact Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Visual Comfort at 888-475-1136 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email techlightingcs@visualcomfortco.com or visit www.visualcomfort.com/recall for further details.

Products.Name: Maverick Coastal 60-inch Outdoor Ceiling Fan
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 6,400 (In addition, about 200 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Maverick Coastal Outdoor Fan in Black
Recalled Maverick Coastal Outdoor Fan in White

Injuries.Name: The firm has received nine reports of fan blades cracking or breaking. No injuries or property damage have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Home Depot, Circa Lighting, and various other retailers, as well as online at Amazon.com, Build.com, Wayfair.com, and more

Importers.Name: Visual Comfort & Co., of Skokie, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Blade cracking or breaking, posing an impact injury risk

Remedies.Name: Free replacement blades and installation assistance from a certified electrician

Description: The Yaomiao Children's Rhinestone Silver Tiara with red rhinestones has been recalled because its lead content exceeds federal safety limits. This product poses a lead poisoning hazard if ingested by children. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Yaomiao Children's Rhinestone Silver Tiara Recalled for Excessive Lead Content
ConsumerContact: For further details, please reach out to LordRoadS via email at simlordroadple@163.com or visit their Amazon storefront at https://www.amazon.com/s?me=A1ISSCMO2YO2PL&marketplaceID=ATVPDKIKX0DER.

Images.Caption: Recalled Yaomiao Red Rhinestone Silver Tiara

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from January 2021 to November 2023, priced around $19 for the set of four.

Distributors.Name: Wuhannuoyunxindianzikejiyouxiangongsi (dba LordRoadS), of China

Hazards.Name: Lead content in rhinestones exceeds federal safety limits, posing a risk of toxicity if ingested by children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled tiaras immediately, keep them away from children, and contact LordRoadS for a refund. Both LordRoadS and Amazon are reaching out to known purchasers.

Description: Bokser Home is recalling all sizes of its 100% cotton mattress pads produced after October 2022 because they do not meet federal flammability regulations, posing a fire hazard. The mattress pads have a cotton shell and fill, with manufacturer and production details labeled. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Bokser Home Recalls 100% Cotton Mattress Pads for Fire Hazard and Flammability Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bokser Home at 888-216-4403 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at support@bokserhome.com, or visit www.bokserhome.com/mattress-pad-recall/ for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bokser Home 100% Cotton Mattress Pad
Label Attached to Recalled Bokser Home 100% Cotton Mattress Pad

Manufacturers.Name: Anhui Zhongqinghonyu Home Textile Technology Co. LTD, of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Bed Bath & Beyond, Belk, Macy's, Target, Bokser Home Outlet, and Fernish B2B stores nationwide, as well as online at BedBathandBeyond.com, BokserHome.com, and Bokserhomeoutlet.com from November 2022 to October 2023, priced between $25 and $145.

Importers.Name: Bokser Home, of Minneapolis, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to non-compliance with federal flammability standards for mattress pads.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the mattress pads immediately and reach out to Bokser Home for a full refund. To process the refund, consumers must destroy the product by cutting it and send a photo of the destroyed mattress pad to support@bokserhome.com. A full refund will be issued upon receipt of the photo.

metaDescription: Bokser Home recalls 100% cotton mattress pads due to fire hazard from non-compliance with federal flammability standards. No injuries reported.

Description: Home Design is recalling multiple models of Tufted Upholstered Low Profile Beds due to risks of breaking, sagging, or collapsing during use, which can cause falls and injuries. The recall includes various part numbers and color options, with some beds lacking adequate center support. Reports include incidents of bed failure resulting in injuries. Each bed can be identified by a law label on the back of the headboard.
Title: Home Design Recalls Tufted Upholstered Low Profile Beds for Fall and Injury Hazards
ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can visit http://www.homedesign-us.com/recall or navigate to the "Product Recalls" section on the Home Design website. They may also call toll-free at 833-383-2967 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@homedesign-us.com.

Products.Name: Tufted Upholstered Low Profile Standard Bed - Part 80002

Images.Caption: Recalled Part Number 80002 Tufted Upholstered Low Profile Standard Bed
Recalled Part Number 80032 Tufted Upholstered Low Profile Standard Bed
Recalled Part Number 80055 Tufted Upholstered Low Profile Standard Bed
Recalled Part Number 80071 Tufted Upholstered Low Profile Standard Bed
Recalled Part Number 80053 Tufted Upholstered Low Profile Standard Bed
Law Label
Law Label Location

Injuries.Name: Home Design has received 128 reports of the beds breaking, sagging or collapsing during use, including 36 injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Wayfair, Walmart.com, and Overstock.com from July 2018 through November 2023, priced between $100 and $300.

Importers.Name: Home Design Inc., of Silver Lake, Indiana

Hazards.Name: Risk of breaking, sagging, or collapsing during use, leading to potential falls and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled beds immediately and contact Home Design for complimentary replacement slats and side rails. To receive the free repair, consumers must provide an image of the bed along with the law label or proof of purchase to recall@homedesign-us.com. Home Design will also reach out directly to known purchasers.

metaDescription: Home Design recalls Tufted Upholstered Low Profile Beds due to risk of breaking and collapse causing falls and injuries. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: Steamist is recalling its wall-mounted Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats due to corrosion and breakage of mounting brackets and seat rods, which can cause falls and lacerations. The affected models include SBS-101BN, SBS-101PN, SBS-101PC, and SBS-101ORB with various finishes. The company has received reports of the seats breaking and some injuries related to these failures.
Title: Steamist Recalls Wall-Mounted Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats for Fall and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled shower seats immediately and reach out to Steamist at 866-968-2250 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email recall@steamist.com, or visit www.steamist.com/recall-notices for further details.

Products.Name: Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,900 (In addition, about 733 were sold in Canada and about 126 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with brushed nickel finish
Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with polished nickel finish
Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with polished chrome finish
Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with oil-rubbed bronze finish

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 62 reports of the shower seats breaking, including 4 reports of injuries.

Retailers.Name: Specialty plumbing supply outlets in the United States, Canada and Mexico from June 2009 through February 2015 for between $490 and $660.

Importers.Name: Steamist Inc., of East Rutherford, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Corrosion and breakage of wall mounting brackets and seat rods, leading to fall and laceration hazards.

Remedies.Name: Cease use of the recalled shower seats and contact Steamist for recall participation instructions.

metaDescription: Steamist recalls wall-mounted Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats due to corrosion risks causing falls and lacerations. Consumers should stop use immediately.

Description: Boot Royalty is recalling the Men's Dark Brown Keaven WP Met Guard Work Boots because the metatarsal guard may be missing, increasing the risk of foot injury from impact. These slip-on leather boots include a steel toe and are available in men's sizes 7 to 14. One injury involving bruising was reported after a heavy object fell on a consumer's foot.
Title: Boot Royalty Recalls Men's Dark Brown Keaven WP Met Guard Work Boots Due to Crush Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers should reach out to Boot Royalty Company at 800-550-7327 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email customerservice@justinbrands.com. More information is available online at https://www.justinboots.com/ by clicking on the "Safety Recall" tab or visiting www.justinboots.com/safetyrecall.

Products.Name: Justin Boots Keaven WP Met Guard Work Boots

Images.Caption: Recalled Justin's Boots Keaven WP Met Guard Work Boot
Recalled boots manufacture date code label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an injury when a heavy object fell on a consumer's foot, resulting in bruising.

Retailers.Name: Available at Grainger, Safety Shoes Distributors, Saf-Gard Safety Shoe Company, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at www.grainger.com and www.justinboots.com from September 2022 through September 2023, priced between $150 and $200.

Importers.Name: Boot Royalty Company L.P., of Fort Worth, Texas

Hazards.Name: Potential absence of metatarsal guard leading to impact risk for users

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled boots and contact Boot Royalty Company for instructions on obtaining free replacement boots.

Description: Retrospec has recalled Scout model kids' bike helmets in sizes XS and S due to a risk of head injury from potential helmet failure. The helmets come in multiple colors and designs and feature specific branding tags. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Retrospec Recalls Scout Kids' Bike Helmets for Head Injury Risk
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Retrospec at 888-488-2369 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://retrospec.com/pages/retrospec-scout-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Black)
Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Blue)
Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Green)
Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Pink)
Hang tag label on recalled helmet, listing the name “Retrospec,” the Scout brand name, and the helmet size

Retailers.Name: Available at T Scheels, Play it Again Sports, independent bicycle stores nationwide, and online at Retrospec.com and Amazon.com from February 2019 through March 2023, priced between $18 and $23.

Importers.Name: Xander Bicycle Corporation, DBA Retrospec, of Perris, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of head injury due to potential helmet failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets and contact Retrospec for a full refund. Destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, marking it with initials and the date, and upload a photo to the recall registration page to confirm destruction before disposal.

Description: Aventon is recalling Sinch.2 folding electric bicycles because of an unexpected acceleration hazard that can cause loss of control and crashes. The recall affects Class 2 e-bikes with a 500w motor available in silver and blue. Six reports of sudden acceleration have been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Aventon Recalls Sinch.2 Folding E-Bicycles for Unexpected Acceleration Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ride Aventon at 866-300-3311 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or via email at info@aventon.com. Further details can be found online at https://www.aventon.com/pages/recalls or by visiting www.Aventon.com and clicking on "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike (Quicksilver)
Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike (Sapphire)
Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike (Sapphire) with handlebars folded
Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike - portable

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the recalled e-bikes accelerating unexpectedly. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle retailers across the country and online at BestBuy.com and Eriksbikeshop.com from April 2023 to August 2023, priced around $1,800.

Importers.Name: Ride Aventon Inc., of Brea, California

Hazards.Name: Unexpected acceleration leading to loss of control, posing crash and injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected e-bikes and contact a Ride Aventon authorized dealer for a complimentary repair, which includes a software update.

metaDescription: Aventon Sinch.2 folding e-bicycles are recalled due to unexpected acceleration hazard that can cause crashes and injuries. Free repairs available.

Description: Bell Sports is recalling Slope adult bicycle helmets because the strap anchors can become dislodged, posing a risk of head injury in a crash. The recall includes helmets identified by an inner sticker labeled 'Slope' with code B1161X, available in dark green and light blue. No injuries have been reported related to this issue.
Title: Bell Sports Recalls Slope Adult Bicycle Helmets for Head Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bell Helmets at 800-456-2355 (Option 3) from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com or visit www.thebellgarage.com and click on the recall banner for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bell Slope helmet (Dark Green)
Recalled Bell Slope helmet (Light Blue)

Manufacturers.Name: Bell Sports Inc., Irvine, California

Retailers.Name: Available at Target, Fred Meyer, Academy, and other retailers nationwide, as well as various websites from January 2023 to April 2023 for approximately $30.

Importers.Name: Bell Sports Inc., Irvine, California

Hazards.Name: Potential for strap anchor dislodgment, violating CPSC safety regulations and increasing head injury risk in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the recalled helmets and contact Bell Sports for a refund. Consumers must destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, provide photographic proof to consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com, and then dispose of the helmet.

metaDescription: Bell Sports recalls Slope adult bicycle helmets due to risk of head injury from strap anchor failure. No injuries reported in this recall.

Description: H.H. Brown Shoe Company has recalled Redeemer DH 5379 Men's 11" Waterproof Metguard Comp Toe Ropers Work Boots due to a risk of injury from non-puncture-resistant soles. Description: UBBCARE has recalled its play yard mattresses because they do not meet federal safety standards for crib mattresses, creating a suffocation hazard for infants. The mattresses measure 38 by 26 inches and have a white quilted dot cover with brand and care tags. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: UBBCARE Play Yard Mattresses Recalled for Infant Suffocation Hazard Due to Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For more information, reach out to UBBCARE via email at HBoutdoor-us@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled UBBCARE Play Yard Mattress
Recalled UBBCARE Play Yard Mattress tag and instructions

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from October 2022 through August 2023 for about $40.

Distributors.Name: UBBCARE, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to non-compliance with federal safety regulations for crib mattresses

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattresses, dispose of them, and contact UBBCARE for a full refund and disposal instructions.

Description: BlendJet is recalling 4.8 million BlendJet 2 Portable Blenders due to risks of fire and lacerations caused by overheating and blade breakage. The recall includes units with serial numbers between 5201 and 5542. Reports include blade breakage during use, overheating incidents, minor burns, and one laceration injury. Consumers should stop using affected blenders to avoid these hazards.
Title: BlendJet Recalls 4.8 Million BlendJet 2 Portable Blenders for Fire and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach BlendJet at 844-334-0562 from 8 a.m. to 10 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.blendjet.com/safetyandrecall for more details and to access the recall information.

Products.Name: BlendJet 2 Portable Blenders
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4.8 million (in addition, about 117,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled BlendJet 2 Portable Blender
Recalled BlendJet 2 complete packaging set
Recalled BlendJet 2 base of the blender, showing serial number

Remove and cut the rubber seal from the base of the recalled BlendJet 2 into three or more pieces and send a photo showing the serial number on the bottom of the unit and its rubber seal in pieces to BlendJet to receive the free replacement of the base

BlendJet 2 units with serial numbers where the first 4 digits are between 5201 and 5542 may be subject to the recall. Consumers should enter their unit's full serial number into this online tool found at www.blendjet.com/safetyandrecall

Injuries.Name: The firm has received approximately 329 reports of the blades breaking while in use, and an additional 17 reports of overheating or fires resulting in property damage claims of approximately $150,000. The firm has received about 49 reports of minor burns and one report of a laceration injury.

Manufacturers.Name: BlendJet Inc., of Benicia, California

Retailers.Name: Costco, Walmart, Target and other stores nationwide and online at BlendJet.com from October 2020 through November 2023 for between $50 and $75.

Hazards.Name: Fire and laceration risks due to overheating and blender blade breakage.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the affected blenders and contact BlendJet for a complimentary replacement base unit. To facilitate this, they must cut the rubber seal from the base into multiple pieces and provide a photo showing the serial number and the seal pieces via the website or email.

metaDescription: BlendJet recalls 4.8 million BlendJet 2 Portable Blenders due to fire and laceration hazards from overheating and blade breakage.

Description: This recall involves 5mm neodymium magnetic balls sold in sets of 216 with storage cases and bags. These magnets pose a serious ingestion hazard because they do not meet federal safety regulations. No injuries have been reported in connection with this product.
Title: Recall of 5mm High-Powered Magnetic Balls Sold by Joybuy on Walmart.com Due to Ingestion Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Joybuy Marketplace Express at 302-426-4543 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, by email at oversea-service@jd.com, or visit https://www.walmart.com/seller/16214.

Images.Caption: Recalled Relax 5mm Science Kit, Large Hematite Magnets Magnetic Stones Building Blocks

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Walmart.com from February 2022 through April 2023 for between $14 and $15.

Distributors.Name: Shanghai Yanlei Network Technology Co. Ltd., of China, through JD E Commerce America Limited (dba Joybuy), of Irvine, California

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to non-compliance with federal magnet regulations, posing risks of intestinal blockage, infection, and potential death.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the magnetic balls immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Joybuy for a pre-paid return label to obtain a full refund.

metaDescription: High-powered 5mm magnetic balls recalled for ingestion hazard due to non-compliance with federal magnet safety standards.

Description: Brandline has recalled HEAO 4-in-1 high chairs due to risks of suffocation, entrapment, and laceration caused by design flaws and failure to meet federal safety standards. The chairs come in multiple colors and feature adjustable heights, reclining positions, and locking wheels. The firm is aware of one injury involving a child's finger laceration that required medical treatment.
Title: Brandline Recalls HEAO 4-in-1 High Chairs for Suffocation, Entrapment, and Laceration Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Brandline at 302-261-5366 from Monday to Friday, 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, or via email at maxlead.us@gmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled HEAO High Chair with static wheels
Recalled HEAO High Chair with rotating wheels

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of one incident that resulted in a laceration to a child's finger that required medical attention.

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from November 2022 through September 2023 for about $170.

Importers.Name: Max Lead International Ltd., dba Brandline, of Dallas, Texas

Hazards.Name: Suffocation, entrapment, and laceration hazards due to design flaws and non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the high chairs immediately and contact Brandline for disposal instructions and a full refund.

metaDescription: Brandline recalls HEAO 4-in-1 high chairs due to suffocation, entrapment, and laceration hazards posing serious safety risks to children.

Description: DGL Group has recalled specific Hover-1 Helix hoverboards because of a fire hazard linked to overheating lithium-ion batteries. The affected units include serial numbers with 15914, 19203, or 19988 as the fifth digit and come in camouflage and galaxy colors. There have been reports of fires causing property damage and health symptoms from smoke inhalation.
Title: DGL Group Recalls Hover-1 Helix Hoverboards Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected hoverboards immediately and reach out to DGL at 888-556-8426, available from 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at https://www.hover-1.com/pages/important-safety-information or by visiting www.hover-1.com and selecting 'Important Recall Information' at the top of the homepage.

Products.Name: Hover-1 Helix Hoverboard (Camouflage and Galaxy Colors)

Images.Caption: Recalled Hover-1 Helix hoverboard in Camouflage
Recalled Hover-1 Helix hoverboard in Galaxy
Hover-1 Helix label on the bottom of the hoverboard
Hover-1 Helix serial number label on the bottom of the hoverboard

Injuries.Name: DGL has received three reports of fire, which are associated with five reports of people experiencing headaches, nausea, or coughs from smoke inhalation and approximately $25,000 in reported property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.walmart.com and www.amazon.com from September 2020 to December 2021, priced between $170 and $200.

Importers.Name: DGL Group Ltd., of Edison, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Contact Hover-1 for a free replacement hoverboard

metaDescription: DGL Group recalls Hover-1 Helix hoverboards due to fire hazard from overheating lithium-ion batteries. Users should stop use immediately.

Description: Klein Tools and Blackfire have recalled portable rechargeable power stations due to overheating inverter boards that pose fire and burn hazards. The recall includes models PAC1000 (Blackfire) and KTB1000 (Klein Tools), which feature lithium-ion batteries and multiple outlets. Four reports of overheating were received, but no injuries or battery fires have been reported.
Title: Klein Tools and Blackfire Power Stations Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using the recalled power stations immediately and return them to the place of purchase. For further assistance, contact Klein Tools at 800-527-3099 from 7:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.kleintools.com/recall/ktb1000 for Klein Tools units and www.blackfire.com/recall/pac1000 for Blackfire units. More information is also available on their websites under 'Safety Recalls.'

Products.Name: Klein Tools Portable Rechargeable Power Station
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,800 (In addition, about 164 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Klein Tools KTB1000 Portable Rechargeable Power Station
Recalled Blackfire PAC1000 Portable Rechargeable Power Station

Injuries.Name: Klein Tools received four reports of overheating. No injuries have been reported. No lithium-ion battery fires have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through industrial distributors and electrical wholesalers nationwide from November 2021 to November 2023, priced around $1,300

Importers.Name: Klein Tools, of Lincolnshire, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Overheating inverter boards leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power stations and return them to the purchase location

metaDescription: Klein Tools and Blackfire recall portable power stations due to fire and burn hazards from overheating inverter boards. Stop use immediately.

Description: Pella is recalling Architect Series venting wood and aluminum-clad casement windows because the window sash can detach from the frame, posing an injury hazard. The recall includes specific custom-made windows with side pivot hardware and certain size dimensions. One report of sash detachment has been received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Pella Recalls Architect Series Casement Windows for Risk of Sash Detachment
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Pella at 888-276-7624 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information is available at www.pella.com under "Recall Notice," or at www.PellaHingeRecall.expertinquiry.com to check if their window's serial number is affected.

Products.Name: Pella Architect Series Venting Casement Windows
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,000 (In addition, about 305 were sold in Canada and about 10 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled Pella Casement Window – exterior view

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a window sash detaching from the frame and falling. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Pella Corporation, of Pella, Iowa

Retailers.Name: Available through authorized distributors nationwide, including Pella Window and Door stores, Lowe's, ABC Supply, Allied Building Products, Builders First Source, Builders Supply Co, Inc., Hammond Lumber Company, McCoy's Building Supply, National Lumber, and The Carter Companies from January 2021 to July 2023, priced between $700 and $10,000 per window.

Hazards.Name: Risk of window sash detachment leading to potential injuries.

Remedies.Name: Keep the recalled windows closed and locked, and contact Pella for a complimentary repair by an authorized service technician.

metaDescription: Pella recalls Architect Series casement windows due to risk of window sash detachment causing injury. A free repair is available for affected windows.

Description: Litti City and Litti Pritti dress-up playsets, along with Prextex DIY slime eggs, have been recalled for containing phthalate and lead levels that exceed federal safety standards. The recalled items include various playsets with accessories and slime eggs with colorful slime and small components. No injuries have been reported related to these products.
Title: Recall of Litti City and Litti Pritti Dress-Up Playsets and Prextex Slime Eggs for Excessive Phthalates and Lead
ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Perch at 888-359-4676 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email playsets@realtimeresults.net, or visit www.recallrtr.com/playsets, https://www.perchhq.com/ and select 'Recall' at the top of the page, or go to https://www.prextex.com/ and click on 'Recall' for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Litti City Premier Doctor Playset
Recalled Litti Pritti Handbag Playset
Recalled Litti City Teacher Purse Set
Recalled Prextex DIY Take-Along Slime Eggs

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com, with the Litti Pritti handbag playset also sold at Ocean State Job Lot stores in the northeast, from July 2021 through April 2023 for between $9 and $45.

Importers.Name: Whele LLC d/b/a Perch, of Boston, Massachusetts
Amusty Inc. of Staten Island, New York
Bingo Deals of Harriman, New York

Hazards.Name: The dress-up playsets and slime eggs contain phthalate levels that exceed federal regulations. Additionally, the Litti City teacher purse playset, Litti Pritti handbag playset, and slime eggs have lead levels surpassing the federal limit. Both lead and phthalates pose health risks if ingested by young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled items immediately and contact Perch for guidance on disposal and to obtain a full refund upon proof of destruction. Perch, Amazon, and Walmart will reach out to known purchasers directly.

metaDescription: Litti City and Litti Pritti dress-up playsets and Prextex slime eggs recalled due to hazardous phthalates and lead levels exceeding federal safety limits.

Description: TJX has recalled foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs sold under the Nautica and House & Garden brands due to a risk of fall. The wooden chair frames may break or collapse, posing a fall hazard. The recall follows reports of the chair frames breaking while in use, resulting in minor injuries such as bruising and ankle strain.
Title: TJX Recalls Foldable Wood and Rope Bistro Set Chairs Due to Fall Hazard
ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach TJX at 888-520-0157 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/woodandropechair. Customers can also check product recalls on the respective websites of HomeGoods, Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, Homesense, and Sierra.

Products.Name: Foldable Wood and Rope Bistro Set Chairs

Images.Caption: Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs
Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs (blue)
Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs (gray)
Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs (sand)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of the wooden frame of the chair breaking or collapsing when a consumer was seated in the chair, resulting in six reports of minor injuries, including bruising and an ankle strain.

Retailers.Name: Available at Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, HomeGoods, Homesense, and Sierra stores nationwide, as well as online at Homegoods.com, TJmaxx.tjx.com, Marshalls.com, and Sierra.com from March 2020 through September 2023, priced between $130 and $350 for sets and $40 to $80 for individual chairs.

Importers.Name: The TJX Companies Inc., of Framingham, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential breakage or collapse of the wooden chair frame.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled chairs immediately and contact TJX for a full refund and disposal instructions. Alternatively, chairs can be returned to any HomeGoods, Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, Homesense, or Sierra store for a full refund.

metaDescription: TJX recalls foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs due to fall hazard from potential chair frame breakage or collapse.

en.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach TJX at 888-520-0157 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/woodandropechair. Customers can also check product recalls on the respective websites of HomeGoods, Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, Homesense, and Sierra.
en.metaDescription: TJX recalls foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs due to fall hazard from potential chair frame breakage or collapse.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of the wooden frame of the chair breaking or collapsing when a consumer was seated in the chair, resulting in six reports of minor injuries, including bruising and an ankle strain.

Retailers.Name: Available at Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, HomeGoods, Homesense, and Sierra stores nationwide, as well as online at Homegoods.com, TJmaxx.tjx.com, Marshalls.com, and Sierra.com from March 2020 through September 2023, priced between $130 and $350 for sets and $40 to $80 for individual chairs.

Importers.Name: The TJX Companies Inc., of Framingham, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential breakage or collapse of the wooden chair frame.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled chairs immediately and contact TJX for a full refund and disposal instructions. Alternatively, chairs can be returned to any HomeGoods, Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, Homesense, or Sierra store for a full refund.

Description: BRP is recalling certain 2022 and 2023 Can-Am Commander, Defender, and Maverick Trail side-by-side vehicles equipped with HD7 or 700 engines. An incorrect maintenance schedule in the operator's guide may cause clutch failure, leading to ejection of fragments and posing an injury hazard. One clutch malfunction has been reported, but no injuries have occurred.
Title: BRP Recalls 2022-2023 Can-Am Side-by-Side Vehicles for Clutch Failure Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BRP at 888-272-9222 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Sunday, or visit https://can-am.brp.com/off-road/us/en/resources/contact-us.html. Additional recall information can be found at https://can-am.brp.com/off-road/us/en/owner-zone/safety-recalls.html or by navigating to the "Safety Recalls" section on any page of https://can-am.brp.com/off-road/us/en/models/side-by-side-vehicles.html.

Products.Name: Can-Am Commander Series (2022-2023)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 14,555 (In addition, about 2,285 were sold in Canada)

Recalled Maverick Trail Series MY2023

Manufacturers.Name: BRP Mexico, of Mexico

Retailers.Name: BRP Can-Am authorized dealers nationwide from October 2021 through October 2023 for between $13,000 and $36,000.

Importers.Name: BRP US, of Sturtevant, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Incorrect maintenance schedule may lead to clutch failure and ejection of fragments, posing serious injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled vehicles immediately and review the updated maintenance instructions. BRP will send a revised maintenance booklet to registered owners and will contact known purchasers directly.

Description: Empower Brands has recalled two models of Power XL dual-basket air fryers due to a burn hazard linked to a breaking U-Channel connector. The recall includes black and cinnamon 9-quart and 10-quart models identifiable by a silver PowerXL label on the front. The firm received reports of the product breaking during use, resulting in burn injuries. Consumers should be aware of this safety risk.
Title: Empower Brands Recalls Power XL Dual Basket Air Fryers Due to Burn Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled air fryers immediately and contact Empower Brands at 866-704-9370, available from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at www.prodprotect.com/recall/duaf or https://powerxlproducts.com under the "Important Safety Recall Notice" section.

Images.Caption: Recalled PowerXL Air Fryer
Recalled PowerXL Model DUAF-10 Air Fryer

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 41 reports of the product breaking during use, including three reports of burns.

Retailers.Name: Target, Walmart, Kohls and other stores nationwide and online at www.target.com, www.walmart.com, www.kohls.com and other websites from August 2021 through October 2023 for between $60 and $190, depending on the model.

Importers.Name: Empower Brands, LLC of Middleton, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Potential burn hazard from breaking U-Channel connector

Remedies.Name: Full refund for recalled dual basket air fryers

metaDescription: Power XL Dual Basket Air Fryers recalled for burn hazard caused by breaking U-Channel connector. Stop use and seek refund from Empower Brands.

Description: Room & Board is recalling Griffin Duo Bunk Beds made of cherry or maple wood due to a risk of collapse and injury. Some beds may have missing set screws in the side rail brackets, compromising structural integrity. Two collapse incidents have been reported, but no injuries have occurred.
Title: Room & Board Recalls Griffin Duo Bunk Beds for Collapse and Fall Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Room & Board at 800-301-9720, available Monday to Friday from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT and Saturday to Sunday from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT. You can also email shop@roomandboard.com or visit www.roomandboard.com/product-recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Griffin Duo Bunk Beds by Room & Board

Images.Caption: Recalled Griffin Duo Bunk Bed (Cherry)
Recalled Griffin Duo Bunk Bed (Maple)
Griffin Duo Bunk Bed Product Label Located on Footboard
Diagram Showing Location of Set Screw

Injuries.Name: Two incidents of the bunk bed collapsing have been reported. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Room & Board stores nationwide and online at www.roomandboard.com from May 2013 to May 2017, priced between $2,000 and $2,200.

Distributors.Name: Room & Board Inc., of Golden Valley, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Risk of collapse and injury due to improperly installed set screws in side rail brackets.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the bunk beds and check the eight brackets for installed set screws. If any are missing, they need to be added before use. Room & Board will provide free repair kits and can send a representative for inspection and installation at no charge. A helpful repair video is available at https://go.roomandboard.com/037D13.

metaDescription: Room & Board recalls Griffin Duo Bunk Beds due to risk of collapse and injury from missing set screws in side rail brackets.

Description: Savvy Rest has recalled its quilted cotton mattress pads because they do not meet federal flammability standards, creating a fire hazard. The mattress pads are made from unbleached cotton, have corner ties, and come in multiple sizes. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Savvy Rest Recalls Quilted Cotton Mattress Pads for Fire Hazard Due to Flammability Standard Violation
ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the mattress pads immediately and contact Savvy Rest at 866-856-4044, via email at info@savvyrest.com, or visit www.savvyrest.com/product-recall for further details.

Products.Name: Savvy Rest Quilted Cotton Mattress Pads

Images.Caption: Recalled Savvy Rest Quilted Cotton Mattress Pads
Recalled Savvy Rest Quilted Cotton Mattress Pads

Manufacturers.Name: Savvy Rest Inc., of Charlottesville, Virginia

Retailers.Name: Available online at savvyrest.com, thenaturalsleepstore.com, stlbeds.com, and organiquesleep.com, as well as in stores like EcoDwelling, The Clean Bedroom NY, Natural Mattress, Moss Envy, ECO Sleep Solutions, Green Island, Eagles' Rest, Well Rested, and Billibeds across the nation from April 2023 to October 2023, priced between $150 and $280.

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet federal flammability standards, posing a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the mattress pads and request a full refund from Savvy Rest. They will need to destroy the product by cutting it and provide a photo of the destroyed item to info@savvyrest.com to receive their refund.

metaDescription: Savvy Rest recalls quilted cotton mattress pads that fail federal flammability standards, posing a fire hazard. No injuries reported.

Description: Vornado has expanded its recall to include additional Steamfast Travel Steam Irons, specifically models SF-717, SF-720, and SF-727. These irons pose fire, burn, and electric shock hazards caused by damaged power cords and exposed wires. The company has received reports of power cords smoking, sparking, catching fire, overheating, and minor electric shocks. Consumers should be aware of these safety risks.
Title: Vornado Expands Recall of Steamfast Travel Steam Irons Models SF-717, SF-720, and SF-727 for Fire and Shock Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Vornado at 866-827-3362, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information and recall registration can be found at www.steamfast.com/recalls/travelirons. Additional details are also available on www.vornado.com or www.steamfast.com under the "Product Recalls" section.

Products.Name: Steamfast Home & Away Travel Steam Irons
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1.75 million (In addition, about 9,000 in Canada) (About 275,000 Steamfast Home & Away Travel Steam Irons manufactured before March 2017 were previously recalled in April 2023)

Images.Caption: Recalled Steamfast Iron Model Side View
Recalled Steamfast Iron Models SF-717, SF-720, and SF-727
Model number on back of recalled Steamfast irons

Injuries.Name: Vornado has received a total of 74 reports of incidents, including 18 additional reports of the iron's power cord smoking, sparking, catching fire, or overheating at the power cord, or of the power cord becoming damaged. Vornado has received two reports of minor shocks.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart, Bed Bath & Beyond, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, Vornado.com, and Steamfast.com from January 2009 to September 2023, priced between $13 and $30.

Importers.Name: Vornado Air LLC, of Andover, Kansas

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burn due to damaged power cord and shock hazard from exposed wires.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled irons and register for a refund or replacement through the Steamfast or Vornado website.

metaDescription: Vornado recalls Steamfast Travel Steam Irons due to fire and shock hazards from damaged power cords and exposed wires. Safety risk reported by users.

Description: Bugaboo has recalled Dragonfly Seat Stroller seats due to a risk of the backrest moving downward in parent-facing mode, which may cause unrestrained infants to fall. The recall affects seats with specific serial numbers and colors including black, gray, and forest green. No injuries have been reported in the U.S., though incidents were reported elsewhere. Consumers should stop using the affected seats immediately.
Title: Bugaboo Recalls Dragonfly Seat Strollers Due to Backrest Injury Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bugaboo at 800-460-2922 from Monday to Thursday, 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, and Friday, 7 a.m. to 1 p.m. PT. Alternatively, email service.us@bugaboo.com or visit www.bugaboo.com and navigate to the "Recalls" section for further details.

Products.Name: Bugaboo Dragonfly Seat Strollers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,000 (In addition, about 200 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Dragonfly Seat Stroller

Injuries.Name: Bugaboo has received one report in the United States and seven reports in South Korea of the backrest of the seat moving downward during use when in parent-facing mode. No injuries have been reported in the U.S.

Retailers.Name: Children's specialty stores nationwide and online at www.bugaboo.com from May 2023 through June 2023 for about $900 for seat strollers only, and about $1,100 for bassinet strollers with seats.

Importers.Name: Bugaboo North America Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to seat backrest moving downward in parent-facing mode, potentially causing unrestrained infants to fall.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled seats immediately and reach out to Bugaboo for a complimentary replacement.

metaDescription: Bugaboo recalls Dragonfly Seat Strollers because the seat backrest can move downward, posing an injury hazard to infants in parent-facing mode.

Description: Adven Group has recalled all sizes of the Nap Queen Maxima Hybrid Mattresses because they do not meet federal flammability standards, creating a fire hazard. The recall includes multiple model numbers and features a single-sided design with specific fabric and labeling. No injuries have been reported in connection with this issue.
Title: Adven Group Recalls Nap Queen Maxima Hybrid Mattresses Due to Fire Hazard
ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Nap Queen at 866-387-6239 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, or visit https://sleepqueens.myshopify.com/pages/napqueen-maxima-12-twin-size-mattress-recall or www.napqueensleep.com and click on the "Product Recall" banner for further details.

Importers.Name: Adven Group LLC, of Missouri City, Texas

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet federal flammability standards, creating a fire risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the mattresses and contact Nap Queen for a free fitted cover to ensure compliance with safety standards.

Description: Mary Meyer has recalled Bubba Bull Plush Toys (Style 45541) with date code 1945 because the eyes can detach, posing a choking hazard. The toys have a brown body with off-white horns and the Mary Meyer name on the seam label. One report of an eye detaching was received, but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Mary Meyer Recalls Bubba Bull Plush Toys Due to Choking Hazard from Detachable Eyes
ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Mary Meyer Corp. at 800-451-4387 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, via email at Recall@marymeyer.com, or visit www.marymeyer.com/recall/ and click on 'Safety Recall Notices' for further details.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an eye detaching from the plush toy. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Independent specialty toy stores nationwide and only at www.MaryMeyer.com from December 2022 through October 2023 for about $24.

Importers.Name: Mary Meyer Corporation, of Townshend, Vermont

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to detachable eyes

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys, keep them away from children, and contact Mary Meyer for a free replacement.

Description: Primark is recalling the Wooden Music Shaker and DTR M&M Maracas baby rattles due to choking and ingestion hazards caused by parts that can detach. The rattles come in multiple colors and feature Mickey and Minnie Mouse designs. The company has received reports of the rattles breaking apart but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Primark Recalls Wooden Music Shaker and DTR M&M Maracas Baby Rattles for Choking Hazard

Description: Bunkhouse and Lotsa LITES! Connectable Mini String Lights are recalled because their bulbs can overheat, creating fire and burn hazards. The nine-foot strands have 20 white LED bulbs and come in Everyday, Christmas, and Halloween packaging. The firm received reports of overheating but no injuries have been reported.
Title: Bunkhouse and Lotsa LITES! Mini String Lights Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to DM Merchandising at 800-548-6784 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at customerservice@dmmerch.com. More details can be found online at https://247dm.com/globalassets/episerver-forms/0923-recall_string-lights.pdf or by visiting https://247dm.com and selecting 'Important Recall Information.'

Products.Name: Bunkhouse and Lotsa LITES! Connectable Mini String Lights

Images.Caption: Recalled Bunkhouse Connectable Mini String Lights

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the recalled string lights overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: SLIS, of China

Retailers.Name: Available at gift stores and boutiques across the country and online from August 2023 to September 2023, priced between $10 and $13.

Importers.Name: DM Merchandising Inc., of Elmhurst, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Overheating bulbs posing burn and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lights immediately, unplug them, and contact DM Merchandising for a full refund and recall participation details.

Description: Moonseasleep is recalling pack and play mattresses that do not meet federal crib mattress safety regulations, posing a suffocation hazard to infants. The recalled mattresses have a tri-fold design with a diamond pattern and a date code on the tag. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Moonseasleep Recalls Pack and Play Mattresses for Infant Suffocation Hazard
ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Moonseasleep via email at service@moonseasleep.com or visit https://www.moonseasleep.com/pages/product-recalls. You can also go to www.moonseasleep.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Moonsea Pack and Play Mattress
Recalled Folded Mattress and Carrying Bag
Date code tag on recalled mattress

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Amazon.com from January 2023 through August 2023 for about $40.

Importers.Name: Huangshan Yueyang Ecommerce Co. Ltd., of China

Distributors.Name: Huangshan Yueyang Electronic Commerce, D/B/A Moonseasleep, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to non-compliance with federal crib mattress safety regulations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattresses immediately and contact Moonseasleep for a full refund and disposal instructions

Description: Sourceone Ventures recalled the Windsor Queen Xtrabed™ Murphy bed due to risks of the outer case breaking and falling when opened, posing injury hazards. The bed can also tip over if not properly secured to a wall, creating additional safety risks. Reports include multiple injuries from case breakage and one tip-over incident. The recall applies to models manufactured before May 2022 without a specific tray notch.
Title: Sourceone Ventures Recalls Windsor Queen Xtrabed™ for Injury and Tip-Over Hazards
ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to J Grubb Designs at 800-392-0090 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email Service@roomandloft.com, or visit www.roomandloft.com/recalls or www.roomandloft.com and click on "Recalls" at the bottom for further details.

Products.Name: Windsor Queen Xtrabed Mobile Murphy Bed
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,000 (In addition, about 275 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Windsor Queen Xtrabed Mobile Murphy Bed (closed)
Recalled Windsor Queen Xtrabed Mobile Murphy Bed (open)
Left: Models after May 2022 (with notch) - Right: Models prior to May 2022 (without notch)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 19 reports of the Xtrabed's outer case breaking and falling when opened from the front, resulting in 11 injuries to consumers' shoulders, back, legs, knees or feet. The firm has received one report of a tip-over resulting in bruising to the arm.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.wayfair.com from November 2016 to April 2023, priced between $1,500 and $1,860.

Importers.Name: SourceOne Ventures Inc., of Anaheim, California

Hazards.Name: The Xtrabed's outer case or cabinet may break and fall when opening from the front, creating an injury risk. Additionally, the bed can tip over if not secured to the wall, leading to potential tip-over and entrapment dangers.

Remedies.Name: Customers should stop using the recalled Xtrabeds immediately and contact J Grubb Designs to arrange a complimentary home repair by a technician. Alternatively, consumers may choose to receive a full refund instead of the repair. J Grubb Designs is reaching out to all known buyers directly.

metaDescription: The Windsor Queen Xtrabed™ has injury and tip-over hazards due to case breakage and instability if not secured to a wall.

Description: Vibe Bear has recalled portable playyard mattresses that do not meet federal safety regulations for crib mattresses, posing a suffocation hazard to infants. The mattresses fail to comply with firmness requirements and lack required safety labels. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
Title: Vibe Bear Playyard Mattresses Recalled for Infant Suffocation Hazard and Safety Standard Violations
ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Vibe Bear via email at EEERecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vibe Bear mattress
Blue tag with cartoon teddy bear
Model number and date of manufacture tag

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from January 2023 through June 2023 for about $34.

Distributors.Name: Vibe Bear, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to non-compliance with federal safety regulations for crib mattresses, including issues with firmness and missing safety labels.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled mattresses immediately, destroy them, and contact Vibe Bear via email for a full refund and disposal instructions. Vibe Bear and Amazon will reach out to all buyers directly.

metaDescription: Vibe Bear recalls playyard mattresses due to infant suffocation hazard from non-compliance with federal crib mattress safety standards.

Description: Wet & Forget USA is recalling 2.7 million bottles of its Xtreme Reach Outdoor Mold & Mildew Stain Remover because the hose end nozzle clip can dislodge, posing a risk of skin and eye irritation. The recall includes 48 oz. and 68 oz. bottles with specific UPC codes. The company has received reports of nozzle dislodgement and related exposure incidents, including cases of irritation.
Title: Wet & Forget USA Recalls 2.7 Million Bottles of Xtreme Reach Mold & Mildew Stain Remover Due to Skin and Eye Irritation Hazard
ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled product and reach out to Wet & Forget at 888-359-4623 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, or via email at nozzle@realtimeresults.net. More information is available online at www.recallrtr.com/nozzle under the "Safety Recalls" section.

Products.Name: Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Outdoor Mold & Mildew Stain Remover

Images.Caption: Recalled Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover – 48 oz. Bottle
Recalled Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover – 68 oz. bottle
Recalled hose-end nozzle sold with Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 3,188 reports of dislodgement of the nozzle insert, including 157 reports of exposure to the cleaning solution and 28 reports that include complaints of skin or eye irritation.

Retailers.Name: Available at Ace Hardware, Costco, Home Depot, Lowe's, True Value, Walmart, and various home goods stores nationwide, as well as online at www.amazon.com, www.SamsClubs.com, www.walmart.com from February 2018 through November 2023, priced between $30 and $35.

Importers.Name: Wet & Forget (USA) Inc., of Elgin, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of skin and eye irritation due to dislodging hose end nozzle clip

Remedies.Name: Free replacement nozzle for the recalled stain remover; do not use until replacement is installed
